### Tüdeltreff Kiel e.V.

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/communities/tuedeltreff-kiel/

Fact — Tüdeltreff Kiel e.V., 2015–, is documented as a German community in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2015–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Kiel rope-bondage / shibari community and registered German association. The first-party site frames Tüdeltreff Kiel e.V. As a meeting point for adults who enjoy rope, offering a safety-minded environment to try tying, exchange experience and learn from one another. Current formats include monthly Tüdeltreff XL in central Kiel, third-Sunday workshops/events and a smaller fourth-Friday Tüdeltreff; the event pages describe beginner rounds, rope impulses, suspension points, mats and loaner rope. Public Fesselzeit and myKiNK listings preserve earlier third-Friday Tüdeltreff activity, and a 2021 LOFT artist page independently anchors the project to 2015 via Bavragor's leadership. The public record supports a durable local community / association rather than a fixed studio or formal lineage school.

Fact — Yaminawakai, 2000s-, is documented as a Japanese community in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2000s-

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — Tokyo dark / gothic kinbaku salon and rope-community context tied to MK Studio, Mira / Miura Kurumi and Mistress Maya. MyShibari says Miura Kurumi runs Yaminawakai at MK Studio; Clubberia and iFLYER preserve a 2018 Midnight★Mess Earthdom listing with MIRA KURUMI in the Yaminawakai / MK Studio rope-performance slot and Mistress Maya in a Yaminawakai rope-bondage-experience slot. A Finnish Tokyo scene report independently characterizes Yaminawakai as an erotic dark-art kinbaku rope group meeting at Mistress Maya and Mira Kurumi's MK Studio. The atlas keeps this as community / venue infrastructure and does not add standalone Kurumi, Maya, Shimalphas or one-off event entries from the same trail.

### Balkan Shibari Scene

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/balkan-shibari-scene/

Fact — Balkan Shibari Scene, 2010s–2020s, is a context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s–2020s

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Balkan / Belgrade-centered shibari and rope-scene context from the 2010s public Serbian BDSM / performance layer to current dedicated shibari infrastructure and regional festival hosting. Early public traces include Akademija lepih boli as a Belgrade BDSM education / workshop scene, Sergej Pejić's credited shibari work for the 2015 Sajsi MC project, VICE Serbia's 2016 Belgrade BDSM documentary and later Nova.rs commentary on Serbian shibari development. Current traces include Maša Štrbac / Masha Mokosh and House of Mokosh, Azzky's Belgrade teaching / session / performance practice, NekoNeko Studio, KnotCoven and BalkanBound as a Belgrade-centered international shibari festival presented as the first shibari festival in the Balkans.

### Benelux / Amsterdam-Antwerp-Brussels Scene

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/benelux-amsterdam-antwerp-brussels-scene/

Fact — Benelux / Amsterdam-Antwerp-Brussels Scene, 2010s-2020s, is a context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s-2020s

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Benelux scene context for the Amsterdam-Rotterdam-Antwerp-Brussels rope infrastructure layer: RopeMarks / RopeLounge and Knot Here in the Netherlands; current Amsterdam and Rotterdam queer / peer-rope groups; Shibari Lounge and Onawa Asobi Europe in Antwerp; Corneum, Studio TIEY, Nicolas's Dojo, Atelier Mae and International Shibari Film Festival in Belgium. The combined regional context is stronger than separate Netherlands / Belgium country entries in the current lineage view.

Fact — Active: 2010s-2020s

Fact — Era: 2010s

### Contemporary Performance Circuit

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/contemporary-performance-art-circuit/

Fact — Contemporary Performance Circuit, 2010s-2020s, is a context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s-2020s

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — 2010s-2020s context for rope moving via recurring performance, theatre, salon, festival and studio-show circuits. Rope Studies, Study on Falling, shibari / butoh stage work, travelling Shibari Theater formats and performer archives show rope functioning as public performance infrastructure rather than only class, lineage or party material. Museum, film-festival, gallery and institutional public-art crossover is documented separately.

### Curriculum / License / Branch Networks

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/curriculum-license-branch-networks/

Fact — Curriculum / License / Branch Networks, 2010s-2020s, is a context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s-2020s

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — 2010s context for a new kind of legitimacy around curriculum, instructor license, formal branch schools, online certificates and authorized study groups. The entry keeps these signals distinct from deshi lineage: Ichinawakai instructor licensing, Kazami-ryu official schools, Osada / Kinbaku Academy course structures, Rope365 curriculum work and regional branch dojos are documented as pedagogy and authorization infrastructure, not as automatic apprenticeship claims.

### Dedicated Rope-Space Boom

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/dedicated-rope-space-boom/

Fact — Dedicated Rope-Space Boom, 2010s-2020s, is a context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s-2020s

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — 2010s context for the move from travelling workshops and mixed-use kink venues toward persistent rope-specific spaces: studios, ateliers, rope schools, salons and practice rooms with regular classes, open practice, rental, photo, performance or community programming. Schwelle7, Karada House, Place des Cordes, Anatomie Studio, Daruma Berlin, VoxBody and later local studios show the fixed-space layer becoming a major carrier of pedagogy.

### Early Web, Blogs & Podcasts

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/early-web-forums-and-blogs/

Fact — Early Web, Blogs & Podcasts, 2000s-early 2010s, is a context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2000s-early 2010s

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — Early English-language and transnational web layer where rope practitioners found tutorials, podcasts, blogs and history writing before large course platforms became normal. TheDuchy, Graydancer's Ropecast, The Two Knotty Boys, The Kinbakunomicon and early Kinbaku Today show kinbaku / rope learning moving via small independent sites, podcasts, books and online archives rather than only via local workshops or print media.

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/eastern-european-war-diaspora/

Fact — War / Diaspora / Relocation Layer, 2022–, is a context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2022–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Post-2022 context for the Eastern European rope scene after Russia's full-scale invasion of Ukraine. The war created a major displacement crisis and changed where Ukrainian, Russian and Belarusian practitioners could live, teach, gather and organize. On this map it is listed narrowly as migration / diaspora pressure on rope communities: Ukrainian studios and camps continuing under wartime conditions, cross-border Poland / Ukraine camp formats, Russian-scene practitioners appearing via later European / Balkan contexts, Belgrade studio/community infrastructure organized by Eastern European emigrants, and post-2022 European festivals becoming visible meeting points.

### Edo Period Torture & Punishment

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/edo-torture/

Fact — Edo Period Torture & Punishment, 1600–1868, is documented as a Japanese context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 1600–1868

Fact — Era: 1800s

Fact — Formal rope-centered restraint and punishment practices of the Edo / Tokugawa period (1600–1868). State torture techniques involving elaborate rope binding became part of the cultural visual vocabulary later absorbed by kinbaku.

#### Reference links

- [wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Edo_period)

### French Shibari Scene Infrastructure

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/french-shibari-scene-infrastructure/

Fact — French Shibari Scene Infrastructure, 2010s-2020s, is a context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s-2020s

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — 2010s-2020s context for French rope-scene infrastructure: Paris studios and schools such as Place des Cordes, Atelier Simonet, Ecole des Cordes and Port d'attaches; Toulouse / Occitanie association and event formats around ASKA, Shibari Mon Amour and Rope Camp France; Marseille / South France teaching-platform traces such as Attache-Moi and French Shibari Mafia; and the post-2020 spread of local associations in Lille, Lyon, Alsace and Grenoble. The setting complements, rather than replaces, thematic post-pandemic and curriculum contexts.

### Global Workshop Circuit

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/global-workshop-circuit/

Fact — Global Workshop Circuit, 2000s, is a context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2000s

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — 2000s context in which Japanese and Japan-trained teachers began circulating via workshops, studios and performance venues outside Japan. Studio SIX / Osada-ryu, Midori's English-language teaching, early European spaces and figures such as Felix Ruckert helped turn shibari from a Japan-centered subculture into a transnational workshop network.

### Hojōjutsu

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/hojojutsu/

Fact — Hojōjutsu, 1600s–1800s, is documented as a Japanese context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 1600s–1800s

Fact — Era: 1800s

Fact — Traditional Japanese martial art of rope binding, used by samurai and police to restrain prisoners. Developed during the Edo period. A direct technical predecessor to kinbaku.

### Kabuki Theater

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/kabuki-theater/

Fact — Kabuki Theater, 1603–, is documented as a Japanese context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 1603–

Fact — Era: 1800s

Transmission — Traditional Japanese theater featuring prisoner scenes and Onnagata (male actors performing female roles). Kabuki's dramatic rope and restraint staging influenced the aesthetic language of kinbaku.

### Kinbaku Video Archive Era

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/kinbaku-video-archive-era/

Fact — Kinbaku Video Archive Era, 1990s, is documented as a Japanese context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 1990s

Fact — Era: 1990s

Transmission — 1990s video / DVD / photo-publication context that preserved sessions, compared senior bakushi styles and made lineages studyable after the live event. Kinbiken recordings and CineMagic-style productions made Nureki, Yukimura, Akechi and related aesthetics portable spanning later communities.

### Latin American Scene Localization

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/latin-american-scene-localization/

Fact — Latin American Scene Localization, 2010s-2020s, is a context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s-2020s

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Regional localization context for Spanish- and Portuguese-language shibari scenes in Latin America: Argentine teaching / publishing projects, Mexico City schools, Colombian city festivals and communities, Brazil's school / event / online-education layer, and distributed regional community efforts such as Shibari Club Latin America. It is not a single lineage claim; it is a regional infrastructure context that keeps local pedagogy, language, public events and Japanese-teacher visit circuits visible without turning every roster or social tag into a connection.

### Material & Supply Infrastructure

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/material-supply-infrastructure/

Fact — Material & Supply Infrastructure, 2020s, is a context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — 2020s context for mature material infrastructure: Japanese asanawa suppliers, JBO-free rope companies, local stockists, studio shops, regional distributors and rope-care / material-information platforms. Ogawa-linked studio shops, KOUMANAWA, AMATSUNAWA, Torinawa, KoumanawaUK, BondageRope.EU, G-Ropes, Mayunawa and La Gabia Shop show material access becoming part of scene infrastructure rather than a background craft detail.

### Peer-Exchange / Festival Circuit

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/peer-exchange-festival-circuit/

Fact — Peer-Exchange / Festival Circuit, 2010s-2020s, is a context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s-2020s

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — 2010s context for the recurring peer-exchange and festival formats that made shibari learnable via repeated gatherings rather than only via one-off master visits. EURIX, ROPECRAFT, Bound In Boston, BoundCon Munich, Onawa Asobi Europe and later ropeXchange show the circuit form: workshops, performances, peer exchange, presenter rosters, documentation and cross-city travel becoming infrastructure in their own right.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — Context for the early Russian-language shibari / rope-bondage scene spanning Russia, Belarus and nearby post-Soviet networks: translation work, local peer-rope meetings, early Minsk / Saint Petersburg / Moscow communities, Art Co / Nawapedia documentation and later Saint Petersburg show-theater infrastructure. The page presents not a lineage school; it is a regional-language diffusion layer that keeps Belarusian and Russian-source traces visible without inventing direct teacher-student relationships.

### Postwar SM Publishing

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/postwar-sm-publishing/

Fact — Postwar SM Publishing, 1947-1975, is documented as a Japanese context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 1947-1975

Fact — Era: 1950s

Fact — Postwar Japanese pulp and fetish-magazine ecosystem that moved bondage from isolated illustration / theater / private photo sessions into recurring print culture. Kitan Club, KK Tsushin, Fuzoku Soshi, Uramado, Fuzoku Kitan and related magazines made kinbaku images, terminology, essays, how-to fragments and reader/subscriber communication visible to a wider SM readership.

### Safer-Space / Access Infrastructure

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/queer-rope-safer-space-infrastructure/

Fact — Safer-Space / Access Infrastructure, 2010s-2020s, is a context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s-2020s

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Visible 2020s context for reference-supported queer, LGBTQIA+, women / gender-diverse, body-diverse, fat, disability-aware and safer-space rope infrastructure: festivals, conferences, studios and communities whose official public materials make access, pedagogy, safer-space design or community mission part of the rope-facing record. It links only to entries with direct public source support. It is not a taxonomy of queer people, not an identity registry and not a basis for inferring a participant's identity from event co-presence.

### Regional Scene Localization

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/regional-local-language-scenes/

Fact — Regional Scene Localization, 2010s-2020s, is a context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s-2020s

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Cross-era parent context for regional scene localization: shibari scenes becoming locally authored via local-language media, city communities, regional festivals, studios, online surfaces and supply routes rather than reading only as endpoints of Japanese- or English-language diffusion. Child scene contexts carry the geography; this parent links the shared localization layer and keeps direct relationships only for compact regional infrastructure that does not yet need its own scene context.

### Roman Porno SM Cinema

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/roman-porno-sm-cinema/

Fact — Roman Porno SM Cinema, 1970s, is documented as a Japanese context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 1970s

Fact — Era: 1970s

Fact — 1970s Japanese pink-film and Nikkatsu Roman Porno context that pushed SM fiction and kinbaku imagery into cinema. Flower and Snake, based on Oniroku Dan's novel, became the first Nikkatsu Roman Porno film centered on SM themes and helped define the decade's public visual vocabulary for rope.

- [Flower and Snake](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Flower_and_Snake_(1974_film))

### Rope Material Globalization

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/rope-material-globalization/

Fact — Rope Material Globalization, 2000s-2020s, is a context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2000s-2020s

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — Historical transition context for the global supply bridge that made shibari-specific rope more portable spanning scenes: early handmade Western rope businesses, Japanese asanawa suppliers, European craft / supplier routes and the first broadly visible stockist layer. The mature 2020s JBO-free, studio-shop and distributor ecosystem is documented separately as Material & Supply Infrastructure.

### Safety / Consent / Rope-Bottom Pedagogy

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/safety-consent-rope-bottom-pedagogy/

Safety / consent — Safety / Consent / Rope-Bottom Pedagogy, 2010s-2020s, is a context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s-2020s

Fact — Era: 2010s

Safety / consent — 2010s context for rope education becoming explicitly structured around risk awareness, consent, negotiation, body mechanics and rope-bottom agency. Crash Restraint, Rope Study, Rope Connections, Evie Vane's rope-bottoming work, Rope Podcast / Friction Live body-mechanics material, Studio Kokoro's rope-bottom resources and later ropehelp.ch mark this as a pedagogy layer rather than a lineage.

### SM Bar & Salon Performance Circuit

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/sm-bar-and-salon-performance-circuit/

Fact — SM Bar & Salon Performance Circuit, 1980s-2020s, is a context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 1980s-2020s

Fact — Era: 1980s

Fact — Narrow context for recurring bars, salons, studio stages and close-audience live-show formats where rope practice became public performance infrastructure. The entry links only bounded examples: Studio Phantom's Akechi-era shows, Tokyo bar / salon formats such as Bar Frame, DecaBarS and Nawa Cafe, Saint Petersburg show formats such as Bondage Cafe SPb, SIBUI, Kazak Shibari Theater and Vabi-Sabi, and public museum / art-production examples such as the Barcelona live show and BAKUKA.

Fact — Somatic / Embodiment / Retreat Turn, 2010s-2020s, is a context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s-2020s

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — 2020s context, with 2010s roots, for rope pedagogy framed via embodiment, intimacy, somatic awareness, ritual, vulnerability, retreat structure and body-centered learning. Sensual Shibari, Voudou Ropes retreats, L-Corde's connective / somatic teaching, Rina Trevi's ritual framing and Shibari Retreat Poland show this as a current pedagogy and event format rather than a school or lineage.

### Taisho Ero-Guro & Photography

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/taisho-eroguro-photography/

Fact — Taisho Ero-Guro & Photography, 1910s-1930s, is documented as a Japanese context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 1910s-1930s

Fact — Era: 1900s

Fact — Early modern Japanese mass culture context around Taisho / early Showa eroguro, newspaper illustration, theater criticism, staged torture tableaux and photography. The page presents the cultural field in which Seiu Ito transformed Edo punishment, kabuki and ukiyo-e references into explicitly modern kinbaku imagery.

Fact — Active: 2000s-2020s

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — UK and London-centered scene context for the English-language European rope infrastructure layer: early web / workshop bridges via Esinem, London Festival of Rope Art and BOUND London; Anatomie Studio's class, guest-teacher and shop ecosystem; Studio Kokoro UK, Studio Yukari, Studio Ma and Rope Jam Studio as London studio succession / expansion traces; and current UK community, festival and studio entries. The page presents a regional infrastructure context, not a claim that the UK scene is one lineage.

### Ukiyo-e / Shunga

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/ukiyo-shunga/

Fact — Ukiyo-e / Shunga, 1600s–1800s, is documented as a Japanese context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 1600s–1800s

Fact — Era: 1800s

Transmission — Ukiyo-e woodblock print tradition and its erotic sub-genre Shunga, which depicted bondage and rope scenes. A major aesthetic influence on kinbaku imagery and composition.

- [wikipedia](https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Shunga)

Fact — 2000s-2010s context for rope-bondage craft moving via photography books, education-video formats, adult-media rigger / director profiles and rope-making projects in North American and English-language scenes. ### Western Shibari Diffusion

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/contexts/western-shibari-diffusion/

Fact — Western Shibari Diffusion, 2000s-early 2010s, is a context in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2000s-early 2010s

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — Historical transition context for how Japanese kinbaku moved into Europe- and North America-facing rope scenes via festivals, studios, English-language publishing, early web media and workshop circuits. This entry is intentionally presented as a bounded 2000s-to-early-2010s diffusion bridge, while later pedagogy, platform, festival, local-scene, safer-space, material and performance layers are documented via narrower context entries.

### Aijō Dojo

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/aijo-dojo/

Fact — Aijō Dojo, 2026–, is a Swiss dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2026–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Swiss shibari / kinbaku school established in 2026 by Jack the Rigger and Shana after many years shaping the Secret56 / Seilschaften teaching and performance context. Aijō frames itself as Jack and Shana's own small rope school and label for private lessons, keiko, rope jams, workshops, shows and shootings; its current home page places the dojo in Lenzburg with public opening from August 2026. Its Osada-Ryū page says Aijō is officially authorized to teach Osada-Ryū and prepare students for kyu exams, with examinations handled together with Kinbaku Studio Vienna and Secret56 under a licensed Osada-Ryū trainer.

### Alive Kinbaku Dojo

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/alive-kinbaku-dojo/

Fact — Alive Kinbaku Dojo, 2023-, is a Czech dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2023-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Prague shibari / kinbaku dojo and community space established and operated by Alive Ropes. Official pages describe Alive Kinbaku Dojo as a live space in Praha 7 dedicated to Japanese Shibari / Kinbaku, with social events, educational workshops, beginner courses, Alive Rope Jams, private lessons and rental. The public history says official activity began with the first Alive Rope Jam in Prague-Dejvice on February 24, 2023, before the project moved in summer 2024 to Praha 7 / Holesovice and expanded its bamboo tying points. AKD's current festival page and ShibariRigger's 2025 report place Alive Kinbaku Festival / Alive Onawa Matsuri at the dojo as a recurring workshop, performance, discussion and rope-jam weekend. Current AKD shop pages and ShibariShop.cz document Ogawa Ropes and safety-gear availability via AKD; The page presents presented as material-support context rather than a separate ShibariShop lineage entry in this pass.

### Athens Rope Dojo

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/athens-rope-dojo/

Fact — Athens Rope Dojo, 2012-2017, is a Greek dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2012-2017

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Athens local rope-bondage school / dojo shell associated with Rope Shadow and Rope Studies in the 2010s. ShibariNews says Rope Shadow ran the local rope bondage school in Athens, Athens Rope Dojo, teaching Japanese shibari techniques from 2012. Booze Cooperativa's 2016 Rope Incident pages list Rope Studies and Athens Rope Dojo together as presenters of workshops, rope jams and performances, while SouthernRock's 2013 listing preserves weekly 7 Sins Club seminar programming by Rope Studies around Shibari / Kinbaku history, safety, floorwork and suspension basics.

### Baku Yu Kai

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/baku-yu-kai/

Fact — Baku Yu Kai, 1996–, is documented as a Japanese dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 1996–

Fact — Era: 1990s

Fact — One of the first official rope dojos in Japan, established in 1996 by Akechi Denki and Miura Takumi. Kinbaku Today characterizes Akechi as founder and Miura as head instructor, with Bakuyukai organized around a continuing mixed-level dojo-learning model rather than one-off workshop instruction.

### CORE Ottawa

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/core-ottawa/

Fact — CORE Ottawa, 2017–, is a Canadian dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2017–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Ottawa rope-education collective and Japanese rope art school established in 2017. The current Eventbrite organizer profile frames CORE as a local group focused on rope education and Japanese rope culture, inclusive Ottawa classes, and, as of 2022, an official Kanna-ryu school teaching Nawashi Akechi Kanna style / semenawa; it also says CORE is the only currently active North American school authorized to teach Kanna-style. Shibari.PH and ROPECRAFT identify Mango / AGKT as a CORE co-founder and place CORE in the Collective for Ottawa Rope Education context.

Fact — Active: 2010–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — French kinbaku / shibari school established in 2010 and later directed by Alex DirtyVonP. Its current school page frames it as the first French school dedicated to kinbaku / shibari, with courses, private sessions and workshops in a Naka Ryu-focused pedagogical frame. Earlier Kinbaku Lovers material names Alex DirtyVonP as the main teacher and characterizes the school as offering classes, workshops and performances in Paris-area spaces.

Fact — São Paulo shibari school active in the 2020s. Fact — Santiago shibari school directed by Les / Quiero Sangre. The Shibari Study directory profile frames Escuela de la Sangre as a structured school for studying rope as a technical, artistic and relational practice, with training spanning levels, safety, biomechanics, communication, aesthetics and creative exploration, and says more than 500 students have passed via its classes, workshops and intensives. The public Instagram profile preview for `@quierosangre.c0m` independently frames Les as director of Escuela de la Sangre and routes classes, sessions and training in Santiago.

### Evil Girls HQ

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/evil-girls-hq/

Fact — Evil Girls HQ, 2016–, is an Australian dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2016–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — South East Queensland Kazami-Ryu school and rope venue led by Miss Gen. Evil Girls HQ frames itself as a Kazami-Ryu school since 2016, with regular classes at its Shailer Park venue and a tiered Level 1-4 curriculum. Its Kazami-Ryu page records Miss Gen as Tōkatsu Shihan / Head of School for Oceania and deshi of Kazami Ranki, with Basanos as Shihan Dai, instructor and assistant to Tōkatsu Shihan, and deshi of Miss Gen. The home page places EGHQ in Logan between the Gold Coast and Brisbane and frames it as the home of Kazami-Ryu Shibari in Australia and Oceania.

### Harukumo-Juku

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/harukumo-juku/

Fact — Harukumo-Juku, 2014–, is a Swiss dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2014–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Swiss shibari / kinbaku juku established by Harukumo and Yoko in Zürich Oerlikon in June 2014, later operating in Neuenhof for the 2019 Osada Steve workshop series and announcing a new home in Koblenz, Aargau in 2020. Harukumo-Juku's official site frames the Juku as a private-school / studio model inspired by Kitagawa Salon in Tokyo, Osada Steve's Studio SIX teaching and Yukimura Haruki's Ebisu studio. It programming includes private lessons, small workshops, regular practice / exchange, photography / video work and material-preparation support, with most techniques drawn from Osada-ryu and Yukimura-ryu. The lessons page characterizes Harukumo's Yukimura-ryu teaching as communication- and experience-centered, and says a philosophical / aesthetic / theoretical kyū-system for Yukimura-ryu has been in use since 2020. Official event pages document Osada Steve workshop series in 2018 and 2019, such as Juku and Secret56 collaboration.

#### Reference links

### Heartland Kinbaku

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/heartland-kinbaku/

Fact — Heartland Kinbaku, 2020s–, is documented as an American dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Chicago / Logan Square kinbaku school and rope studio established and led by SweetZephyr. Heartland's official home page characterizes it as a Chicago rope studio offering traditionally inspired Japanese rope-bondage education, while the team page names SweetZephyr as organizational director, founder and lead instructor and ClaraRosa as instructor / bottoming instructor. The FAQ and private-lesson pages document core modules, open studio / practice times, Discord community access and residential private tuition for traveling students. The public curriculum is framed around traditional Japanese rope bondage, semenawa, suffering, surrender, compacting the body, suspension and advanced continuing education; the site explicitly says the curriculum is inspired by Akechi Kanna, Riccardo Wildties and Naka Akira while not claiming official authorization in those styles.

### Ichinawakai

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/ichinawakai/

Fact — Ichinawakai, 2004–, is documented as a Japanese dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2004–

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — Shibari classroom established and run by Hajime Kinoko since 2004. Holds regular group classes, private lessons, rope salons, instructor licensing, and a beginner/intermediate curriculum taught in Tokyo and by licensed instructors abroad. Instructor-recognition wording also uses the style name Ichinawa-ryu / 一縄流.

### Kannawa-kai / Phantom-no-kai

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/kannawa-kai/

Fact — Kannawa-kai / Phantom-no-kai, 2011–, is documented as a Japanese dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2011–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Akechi Kanna's organization and teaching circle. Kannawa-kai was established in October 2011; Kanna presides over Kannawa-kai and Phantom-no-kai and established the Kanna-ryu instructional lineage with official instructors such as Raymond and Sparks. Public Kanna / Kagura biographies also place Kagura in the salon, workshop and event work around Kanna.

### Kazami-Ryu Shibari Perth

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/kazami-ryu-perth/

Fact — Kazami-Ryu Shibari Perth, 2020s–, is an Australian dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Western Australian Kazami-Ryu shibari school centered in Morley / Perth. Its official page characterizes it as the only officially licensed shibari school in Western Australia, under the regular tutelage of Miss Gen, who heads Kazami-Ryu in Oceania, and Kazami Ranki himself. The school programming includes regular classes and workshops around the same four-level Kazami-Ryu curriculum used by Evil Girls HQ; its current events page records Level 1 and Level 2 classes in Morley, while Perth Rope Space records Kazami-Ryu Shibari School of Western Australia as an affiliated group.

### KinbakuMania Dojo

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/kinbaku-mania-dojo/

Fact — KinbakuMania Dojo, 2014–, is an Argentine dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2014–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Kinbaku dojo in Buenos Aires, Argentina, established by HaruTsubaki / Camelia and opened on May 18 2014. The official dojo page frames it as a Latin American / Spanish-language teaching project connected to Tsubaki's study with Osada Steve; a 2023 Latin American shibari genealogy paper treats its 2014 group classes as one of the foundational experiences for Latin American shibari.

### KinbakuOdyssey

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/kinbaku-odyssey/

Fact — KinbakuOdyssey, 2017–, is documented as an American dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2017–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Rope bondage studio in Manassas, Virginia, established by Odys in 2017. KinbakuOdyssey's own resume characterizes Odys as owner and designer of the studio, endorsed by Naka Akira to teach NakaStyle basics in the USA, taught by Naka since 2017 and a Nawa Naka Kai member since 2018. The studio locations private lessons, workshops, study groups and performance opportunities focused on traditional Japanese kinbaku / shibari and Naka-style semenawa.

### Kinbiken

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/kinbiken/

Fact — Kinbiken, 1985–2009, is documented as a Japanese dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 1985–2009

Fact — Era: 1990s

Transmission — Kinbaku Beauty Research Society — study group run by Chimuo Nureki, active 1985–2009. Naka Akira attended for five years, eventually becoming a student of Nureki. TOHJIRO was also a member and produced Nureki-related works, making Kinbiken one of the connections between Nureki-style kinbaku and later SM video production.

### LA Rope Dojo

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/la-rope-dojo/

Fact — LA Rope Dojo, 2011–, is documented as an American dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2011–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Los Angeles / Koreatown dojo and LA Rope teaching space with official-site founding year 2011. LA Rope Dojo programming includes classes, workshops, intensives and events for traditional Japanese kinbaku, with a curriculum centered on Yukimura-ryu / aibu-nawa communication, touch, body position, kata progressions and partial-suspension study. Its official instructor page records Zetsu / Haru Yutaka as head instructor and owner; Master K is preserved as a co-founder / proprietor context from Nawapedia and LA Rope's 2014 Naka Akira visit post.

### Long Beach Shibari Dojo

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/long-beach-shibari-dojo/

Fact — Long Beach Shibari Dojo, 2020s–, is documented as an American dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Safety / consent — Long Beach, California rope dojo and working art studio. The first-party site frames LB Shibari Dojo as a welcoming, safety-focused and inclusive shibari / kinbaku space for all skill levels, open only for scheduled events/classes rather than public drop-in. The site characterizes a spacious 4000-square-foot Long Beach venue; current programming includes 101 beginner classes, intermediate classes, Next Level / 300-level classes, partial suspension, rope-bottom classes, rope jams/socials and the Kinbaku Minus One first-Sunday class series.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Madrid shibari dojo / school co-owned by Zor and Margout Darko. Madrid Shibari Dojo hosts classes, private classes, workshops, performances, jams and Kinbaku Salon events, and its public pages include monthly initiation-course framing plus a small treated-jute-rope sales route. Kinbaku Society of Berlin described it as co-owned by Margout Darko and Zor.

### Nawa Musubi

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/nawa-musubi/

Fact — Nawa Musubi, 2018–, is an Australian dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2018–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Brisbane shibari education and performance partnership of Jake and Laced. Nawa Musubi teaches Hajime Kinoko's Ichinawa-kai curriculum in Brisbane, programming includes private tuition, classes, workshops, photoshoots and performances, and emphasizes education for both the rigger and the model. Its current class pages include First Steps of Shibari plus beginner, intermediate and advanced Ichinawa Kai course layers, while its public workshop archive includes Ozbound 2019, Wellington 2019 and Peer Rope Adelaide 2021.

### Nawa Naka Kai

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/nawa-naka-kai/

Fact — Nawa Naka Kai, 2011–, is documented as a Japanese dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2011–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Naka Akira's Nawa Naka Kai / 縄奈加會, a Tokyo private rope-meeting and study format around Naka-style semenawa. Studio Yukari / Nuitdetokyo characterizes it as a regular Nawa Naka Kai started by Naka in a traditional Roppongi house in 2011, followed by ongoing photographic meetings and the Semenawa Kinbaku Kenkyu Kai. KinbakuOdyssey characterizes Nawa Naka Kai as a rope group created by Naka Akira, and several public bios document direct participation or membership traces for Iroha Shizuki, Naoko, Odys, Riccardo Wildties, Red Sabbath, Kurogami and Shiawase. Gorgone's Nawapedia chronology uses the Nawana Kakai spelling for Naka / Gorgone modeling traces in 2014-2015; those are listed only as appearance context, not membership or deshi status.

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/nawakai-gijon/

Fact — Nawakai Gijón, 2005–, is a Spanish dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2005–

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — Yagami-ryu shibari dojo in Gijón, Asturias, Spain, led by Ryuan (Sr. Interior). The current in-person school website is Escuela de Shibari, while Shibari Dojo remains the online Spanish-language school. The dojo teaches private classes and workshops using the #MSAFE methodology — a framework making rope accessible regardless of physical condition or experience level.

Fact — Montreal shibari / kinbaku school and community hub associated with Isabelle Hanikamu. Hanikamu's official learning page characterizes Nawakai 縄界, formerly Kinbaku Dojo Montreal, as her school and a hub for Japanese-bondage learning, with private, group and hosted workshop options. Nawakai's own official pages present it as a safe, inclusive Montreal space dedicated to teaching and sharing knowledge about shibari, kinbaku and Japanese rope bondage, with current course offerings and peer-rope / collaborative learning context.

### Neko-Do Shibari School

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/neko-do-palermo/

Fact — Neko-Do Shibari School, 2020s–, is an Italian dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Palermo shibari school established and run by Marco / 7Gatto and Wendy. The official Neko-Do site frames it as a space for conscious and artistic shibari practice in Palermo, with Marco and Wendy bringing nearly ten years of practice and offering beginner, intermediate and advanced courses, individual sessions, events and demonstrations. A 2022 Balarm listing characterizes Neko-dō as the Palermo rope school established by Marco 7Gatto and Wendy, historical students of Federico Kirigami and Marta Tenshiko, in the context of a Kirigami / Tenshiko Palermo kinbaku event. That source supports a Kirigami / Tenshiko study-context connection only; Neko-Do is listed as an independent Palermo school, not as a formal Yugen branch, deshi line, rank or certification claim.

### The Boring Dojo

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/nicolas-dojo/

Fact — The Boring Dojo, 2015–, is a Belgian dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2015–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Brussels / Anderlecht rope dojo run in the A-Nicolas / Nicolas Yoroi orbit, now presented publicly as The Boring Dojo. A-Nicolas's official bio records a Brussels dojo opening in 2015 and a fifth Brussels dojo opening in 2024. The current dojo site records Chaussee de Mons 26, 1070 Anderlecht and characterizes a friendly space for physical, emotional and artistic exploration centered on minimalist, organic, movement-centered rope practice. Its 2026-2027 programme continues a regular study path from introductory kinbaku via floor work, suspension, masterclass / research, intensive training and rope jams. The practice draws on Japanese bondage / kinbaku / shibari as well as dance, massage, martial arts, performance, power exchange, safety, medical and art knowledge.

### Osada Kinbaku Dojo Berlin

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/osada-kinbaku-dojo-berlin/

Fact — Osada Kinbaku Dojo Berlin, 2010–, is documented as a German dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Berlin shibari / kinbaku association and dojo also presented publicly as Kinbaku Dojo Berlin / Osada Kinbaku Dojo e.V. The first-party site characterizes it as a place for shibari enthusiasts in Berlin, teaching Japanese rope art via regular classes and workshops with emphasis on physical and emotional health, respect for boundaries, safety, consent, movement optimization and aesthetics. The dojo teaches shibari in theory and practice according to its interpretation of classical Osada Ryu. Prague Shibari Festival's FrlLilly / Seilartig bio corroborates Kinbaku Dojo Berlin as an Osada-ryu school context where they studied and began giving weekly lessons in 2017.

Kazami-Ryu's current official global schools page records `Kazami-Ryu Hong Kong` as one of the three international official schools and links directly to the public `@ropearthk` profile. Instagram profile names the project as `繩縛大同 Rope Art Hong Kong`, calls it `風見流香港支部`, says it was established in 2016, characterizes it as a Kazami-Ryu rope-art studio centered in Hong Kong, and places it in Mong Kok.

### RopeSchool

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/ropeschool-russia/

Fact — RopeSchool, 2012–, is a Russian dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2012–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Russian shibari / kinbaku teaching school established by Boris Mosafir in 2012. RopeSchool frames itself as the first Russian shibari school and teaches Japanese rope-bondage technique via a four-lesson beginner course, author seminars and individual lessons, with regular classes in Moscow and Saint Petersburg. Its current instructor list names Nick Freerider for Moscow and Karol Soulrope for Saint Petersburg; RopeFest partner pages also name Boris Mosafir and Karol Soulrope as school instructors.

### Secret56

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/secret56/

Fact — Secret56, 2009–, is a Swiss dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2009–

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — Swiss rope / shibari / kinbaku space and Osada-ryu dojo established in 2009 by Jack the Rigger, Ibinuphis and Umino. The current Secret56 team page says it was established to give rope in Switzerland a fixed home; by 2026 the site characterizes 18 years of rope activity with workshops, private lessons, Seilschaften meetups, events and Osada-ryu kyu training. Secret56 received permission from Osada Steve in September 2018 to teach Osada-ryu with Kinbaku Studio Vienna, and Ibinuphis / Jack the Rigger were appointed Osada-ryu instructors on 2022-01-01.

### Shibari Bremen Dojo

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/shibari-bremen/

Fact — Shibari Bremen Dojo, 2019–, is documented as a German dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2019–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Bremen / North Germany Osada-ryu shibari school and private dojo. The first-party site characterizes Shibari Bremen as a school for emotional tying, workshops and individual training in the Osada-ryu curriculum; its dojo page dates the dedicated space to November 2019. The about page says the teacher's path began via Osada-ryu Vienna and repeated stays with Osada Steve at Studio SIX Tokyo, with February 2019 authorization to teach all Osada-ryu techniques at Shibari Bremen and August 2020 licensed Osada-ryu instructor status. The official Osada-ryu dojo directory records Shibari Bremen under Andree as a licensed instructor since 2020.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Ravenna / Emilia Romagna branch lesson shell of Davide La Greca's Shibari Dojo Italy network. The official BDSM Shop page for Emilia Romagna Shibari Dojo frames an MBDStudio Bondage and Shibari lesson at The Den in Ravenna, with a 15:00-19:00 format, beginner-to-experienced multilevel framing, adult-only attendance and the same Shibari Dojo safety-video-course requirement used spanning the network. A June 25, 2026 MaestroBD announcement for a July 5 Shibari Dojo Ravenna intensive frames Ravenna as a regional entry and names Max Nevyn as instructor / Ravenna Dojo referent. Current sources support a public Ravenna teaching hub, not a separate lineage or certification claim.

### Shibari Dojo Italy

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/shibari-dojo-it/

Fact — Shibari Dojo Italy, 2012–, is an Italian dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2012–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Italian shibari / kinbaku school network established and run by Davide La Greca, who also uses the MaestroBD alias. The official Shibari Dojo site says the first school was Rome in 2012, with later schools in Bologna, Milan, Pavia, Tuscany, Veneto and Brescia, directly managed by Davide La Greca / MaestroBD with Lucio Pat Foster. Current school and shop pages describe multi-level lesson hubs in Milano, Brescia, Veneto, Bologna, Toscana and an Emilia Romagna / Ravenna lesson shell, more than one instructor, a safety video course / test, online classes, private lessons and practical teaching that includes emotional management. Public pages also place Koguma Dojo inside Spazio Musubi at Castel San Giovanni as Davide's private-lesson and shibari-photo studio base. A public Instagram shell under `@mbdstudioshibaridojo` separately frames the project as a `safe place for ropes`, advertising shibari classes, private lessons and a linked Italian Shibari Dojo photobook Telegram channel. The dedicated five-level page defines an internal curriculum from principiante via esperto, with exams used to verify safety, technical knowledge and level-specific competence; The page presents listed as Shibari Dojo curriculum context, not as external rank or lineage certification.

Fact — Shibari Perth, 2024–, is an Australian dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2024–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Purpose-built Perth shibari rope dojo and community rope space. Its official site characterizes it as Perth's first purpose-built shibari rope dojo, offering weekly lessons, rope nights, jute jams and workshops with local, interstate and international teachers. Current workshop pages connect the space to Nawa Musubi private lessons, Kasumi Hourai's first Perth visit, and Hajime Kinoko / Ichinawakai-style classes taught by a certified Ichinawa instructor.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Munich shibari dojo / learning community with weekly structured training and all-level practice. The first-party site frames Shigetsu as a Munich dojo teaching Japanese rope bondage with regular Wednesday training in Giesing and periodic Monday training near Heimeranplatz. Its about page names ChrisBe and Zoesithe in the team, and the current teacher page gives ChrisBe a full profile while Zoesithe still appears only as `Zoe (aka Zoesithe)` with `More Info Coming Soon!`; ChrisBe's regular Ren Yagami study / Yagami-ryu instructor-license context is now documented separately, while Zoesithe remains source record until a stronger standalone public profile appears.

### Silver State Shibari

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/silver-state-shibari/

Fact — Silver State Shibari, 2024-, is documented as an American dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2024-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Reno rope-community and Japanese rope suspension education project built around a free, application-only 18-month course. The official home page characterizes Silver State Shibari as a community built around a free 18-month Japanese rope suspension course and says the project works to grow the Reno rope community via comprehensive education, rope socials and one-off classes. The official course page characterizes an intensive sequence from zero to safe suspensions in multiple positions and body types, taught out of a Midtown Reno rope studio, and ties the curriculum history to Second City Kinbaku before the instructors moved to Reno in 2024. The Shibari Study directory profile independently corroborates the Reno listing, rope socials and play-party community context.

Fact — Studio TIEY, 2021–, is a Belgian dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2021–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Shibari dojo in Antwerp, Belgium, opened full-time in 2021 by Etheros (Peter Jaspers). Official Yagami-ryu dojo. Programming includes individual sessions, workshops, and retreat intensives integrating rope with budo, yoga, and Zen philosophy. The 2025 Studio TIEY program also records an Akira Naka / Iroha Shizuki visit with a Nawa Kai evening and introductory Naka Style workshop.

Fact — Active: 2011–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Sydney Japanese rope-bondage education dojo established by Hebari / David Taylor in 2011. Sydney Rope Dojo's public pages describe weekly beginner and intermediate / advanced classes, weekend intensives, travel / away teaching, graded students and an instructor team such as Avalon, Hebari and -Zero. The dojo grew from Hebari's Yukimura-ryu / Japanese rope study context and Nawapedia records venue moves to Leichhardt in 2012 and Erskineville in 2015.

### Toronto Kinbaku Salon

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/toronto-kinbaku-salon/

Fact — Toronto Kinbaku Salon, 2013-, is a Canadian dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2013-

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Toronto rope school and social practice space established by Osaka Dan in late September 2013. After Kazami Ranki's March 2014 intensive in Toronto, the salon matured into a Kazami-ryu school with a structured curriculum and weekly events. JD-66 and AJ accepted Deshi titles to Kazami Ranki in 2017 and Shihan titles in 2018; the current instructors page says JD-66 owns and runs the salon, with Kazami-style classes running five days per week.

### Yagami Juku

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/dojos/yagami-juku/

Fact — Yagami Juku, 2014–, is documented as a Japanese dojo in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2014–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Yagami Style / sekibaku teaching structure established by Ren Yagami in 2014. Ren's official bio says he worked independently on his own style from 2014 and established Yagami Juku that year. Public source evidence supports a Tokyo location for Ren / Yagami Juku, while exact class / salon venue details are handled by direct contact rather than a public street address. The official style page frames the curriculum via Shoden, Chuden, Joden and Kaiden levels, with Semenawa treated separately; the official instructors page records six current official Yagami Style dojos worldwide.

### Alive Kinbaku Festival

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/alive-kinbaku-festival/

Fact — Alive Kinbaku Festival, 2025-, is a Czech event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2025-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Prague rope festival organized via Alive Kinbaku Dojo and Alive Ropes. ShibariRigger.cz reports the first Alive Onawa Matsuri / Alive Kinbaku Festival as a three-day September 2025 AKD festival with workshops, discussions, performances and rope jams. The official AKD 2026 page announces the third Alive Kinbaku Festival for September 4-6, 2026 in Praha 7 / Holesovice, with workshop blocks, evening performances, discussion blocks, Alive Rope Jams, capacity around 40 participants plus teachers, and a provisional Czech / international teacher roster. The provisional 2026 roster is treated as programme evidence until the final programme is public.

Fact — Active: 2026

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Two-day Bangkok shibari performance, workshop and rope-jam event organized by Bangkok Kinbaku Lab around Hajime Kinoko's June 2026 return to Bangkok. The official Ticketmelon page schedules a June 20, 2026 show day at G Village Bangkok, with Ropeholics / TheRopeholic, Wataru Shindo and ZIMA & Asame in the first part, followed by Hajime Kinoko's web-installation show. June 21 is listed as a workshop and rope-jam day at Sintear Bar in the spirit of Kinoko's Ichinawa Salon.

### Austin Ropecraft Symposium

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/austin-ropecraft-symposium/

Fact — Austin Ropecraft Symposium, 2005–2006, is documented as an American event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2005–2006

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — Historical rope-education symposium held in Austin in 2005 and 2006. The inaugural December 2005 edition described itself as unaffiliated and not-for-profit, sold out and brought together an international teaching roster; Austin Ropecraft Symposium 2 followed on September 1-3, 2006. Public programmes connect the symposium to Bridgett / Lee Harrington, Lochai, Mark of DV8House, Lew Rubens and Graydancer. This two-edition symposium is distinct from Graydancer's later ROPECRAFT convention brand launched in 2016.

### BalkanBound

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/balkanbound/

Fact — BalkanBound, 2025-, is a Serbian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2025-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Belgrade-centered international shibari festival presented as the first shibari festival in the Balkans. BalkanBound's public Telegram archive and Instagram profile frame the festival as established by Vlada Vedmovskaya in Spring 2025 and list NekoNeko Studio as an organizer / Belgrade shibari-studio partner. The Spring 2025 programme records March 20-24 FestiveWeek workshops and the March 23 KC Grad festival, with Sarca & Manul, Mikhail Karlson & Marina, RomKnots & NatNata, Ludvig Rigger & Frostslavee and Vlada Vedmovskaya & Ksenia in the public programme. The public Fall 2025 archive preserves the November 15-16 SKC Baza edition with a wider November 12-16 festival-week program at AdultLand and KinkyLab, plus workshops, rope jams, talks and performances by RopeMarks & Keira, Vlada Vedmovskaya & Katya, RomKnots / Ded Mazai & NatNata, Andrea Ropes & AngieZiv, Nikita Ropekit & LianaKnot, Karol Soulrope & Kwistis, Goldie Sunshine & Nina, WisDomme & NyxRope, CatsCradle / RopeRat / Feuertänzer, Jaeger & Orsa and others.

### Bogotá Shibari Week

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/bogota-shibari-week/

Fact — Bogotá Shibari Week, 2022–, is a Colombian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2022–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Bogotá shibari / BDSM event week organized by Shibari Night Bogotá and Berilio Art Shibari. The 2023 second edition ran March 14-19 with six days of workshops, performances, talks, photoshoot, Ropebunny / Petplay parade, bazaar, play party, bondage picnic and shibari varieté, with guests from Argentina and Colombian BDSM rope communities. The 2024 third-edition listings show the format continuing with rope jams, talks and international / national presenters. The fourth edition then ran May 18-24, 2026 under the theme `Conversaciones Silenciosas, Conexión, comunicación y activación con cuerdas`, naming Renée de Sans as principal guest together with Liebre de Azul, Charles Ilcoatl and local guests.

### Bondage Cafe SPb

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/bondage-cafe-spb/

Fact — Bondage Cafe SPb, 2012–, is a Russian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2012–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Saint Petersburg recurring shibari / rope-bondage party and show format in the ArtCo ecosystem. The ArtCo explainer characterizes Bondage Cafe SPb as a safe space with a show program, without a free-tying zone, where visitors can watch masters perform, get inspired and socialize; the same source names Karol Soulrope as the person currently responsible for the event. Fetside / public event mirrors describe Bondage Cafe as an open public event for meeting the culture of rope bondage and the Saint Petersburg shibari community, with regular monthly meetings since 2012. Performance traces connect the event to Karol Soulrope, Boris Mosafir, Nick Freerider, Frater, Kazak and Lucretia.

### BondageFest Russia

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/bondagefest-russia/

Fact — BondageFest Russia, 2007–2008, is a Russian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2007–2008

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — Early Moscow / Russian-language bondage arts festival held in 2007 and 2008. The first edition ran on March 3, 2007 at the club-restaurant Europe and Asia, with two podiums, demonstrations and a photo exhibition; its official programme records Mihail_A from Minsk for shibari. The 2008 archive frames the second open all-Russian festival as a public binding-art event with masters from Russia, Ukraine and Belarus, shibari / hojo-jutsu among the represented forms, master classes during breaks and a thematic photo exhibition. The event is a pre-RopeFest public trace for post-Soviet shibari / bondage exchange.

### Bound In Boston

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/bound-in-boston/

Fact — Bound In Boston, 2009–2018, is documented as an American event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2009–2018

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — New England / Boston-area rope-bondage convention and workshop series centered on Shibari / Kinbaku-style rope education. Official archived pages document Bound In Boston XIII for March 23-24, 2013, Sched records Bound In Boston XX for March 24-26, 2017 at Crowne Plaza Hotel, Warwick, RI, and later official pages describe the March 16-18, 2018 Warwick edition as a large-format con with hundreds of people, classes and parties. Matthew / RopeRider's 2018 farewell says the series grew from a January 2009 30-person Sunday education session into 21 cons over nine years at four hotels, with Tethered Together emerging from the same organizer / staff continuity after the final Bound In Boston.

### BOUND London

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/bound-london/

Fact — BOUND London, 2012-, is a British event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2012-

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — London shibari night created to support Japanese rope bondage enthusiasts, public awareness, skill exchange, new performers and rope shows. Venue and ticket archives trace BOUND at The Flying Dutchman from 2012 via the mid-2010s; a 2013 WeGotTickets listing credits Esinem, Nina Russ, Gorgone and MoSaru as creators in collaboration with Flying Dutchman London, and ShibariClasses characterizes BOUND as London's pioneering shibari night established in 2012.

### Bound to Nature

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/bound-to-nature-costa-rica/

Fact — Bound to Nature, 2026–, is an event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2026–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Women's shibari immersion / retreat project in Costa Rica created and hosted by Persephone Ortega. The official Bound to Nature site frames the project as a seven-day women's shibari experience in Uvita where rope is taught via connection, somatic awareness, ritual, nature and creative expression; Flo Retreat Center records the April 27-May 3, 2026 edition hosted by Persephone Ortega Gomez, and the current official about page says the next experience is planned for January 2027.

### BoundCon Munich 2000s

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/boundcon-munich-2000s/

Fact — BoundCon Munich 2000s, 2004–2009, is documented as a German event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2004–2009

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — Founding-decade slice of BoundCon Munich, the international bondage and fetish convention first held at Kleine Olympiahalle in 2004. Official archived pages document BoundCon I-V via 2008, such as BoundCon III — Revenge of the Ropes, BoundCon IV — Back to the Future at Tonhalle / Kultfabrik and BoundCon V — Destination Bizarre at Zenith. Early rope programmes connected German and international show, workshop and exhibitor circuits via figures and projects such as Matthias T. J. Grimme / Bondage Project, Lew Rubens / BoundnDetermined and RopeMarks / Bob Roos. The first documented public convention was held in 2004; later 2003 origin wording likely refers to the project's foundation or planning phase.

### BoundCon Munich 2010s

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/boundcon-munich-2010s/

Fact — BoundCon Munich 2010s, 2010–2019, is documented as a German event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010–2019

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — 2010s slice of BoundCon Munich, separated from the convention's founding 2000s editions and current 2020s programme. BoundCon combines trade fair, stage shows, rope space, workshops, custom photo shoots and international bondage artists. Confirmed 2014-2017 rope-program entries include Vinciens & Kenyade, Matthias Grimme, RopeMarks / Bob Roos, Berlin Ropes, Nawasabi, Umino & Azooka, Ligatio, Nicolas Yoroi, La Quarta Corda and Maksim Kalahari. Andrea Ropes' own event list records BoundCon Munich shows and workshops in 2017-2019.

### BoundCon Munich 2020s

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/boundcon-munich-2020s/

Fact — BoundCon Munich 2020s, 2020s–, is documented as a German event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Munich-centered international bondage and fetish convention, running since 2004 and organized by Minuit Event GmbH / the BoundCon team, with Claudia Linder (Minuit) listed as owner, CEO and exhibitor / artist lead. BoundCon XXI is scheduled for June 5-7, 2026 at Zenithalle and combines exhibitors, international artists, rope space, stage shows, workshops, custom photo shoots, meet-and-greets and a SubRosaDictum aftershow party. Current workshop, artist and main-stage listings include Vinciens, Matthias Grimme / Rope Cat, Steve Freeman, Marie Sauvage / Atypi, Shibaku, Jordi Lucena, Sarca / Winter, Ligatio / Lizanne, RopEmotion, Tengu Shibari Munich, Sakura Rope, UnkownRigger, Viking Shibari and Amabuki Shibari; public evidence keeps present-era partner and presenter connections separate from the pre-2020 Munich convention entry.

Fact — Active: 2026-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Monthly Hudson Valley community ropeshare hosted by Hudson Valley Rope at Less Dead Studio in Poughkeepsie. The public May 2026 listing characterizes Building Tension as a free, registration-required last-Thursday gathering focused on labbing, learning and community, open to newcomers and experienced rope participants, with a Rope 101 lesson and open practice.

### Copenhagen Queer Knots Festival

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/copenhagen-queer-knots/

Fact — Copenhagen Queer Knots Festival, 2020s-, is a Danish event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Copenhagen queer shibari festival established and artistically directed by RedLily / Beatrice Gigliuto. RedLily's own bio characterizes it as an international gathering dedicated to queer perspectives in rope art and relational practices, bringing artists, educators and community members together for performances, workshops and conversations. The 2026 event listing frames the festival as a four-day queer shibari, community and collective-research container with classes, workshops, round tables, performances, rope jams and play-party space.

### Danish Shibari Festival

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/danish-shibari-festival/

Fact — Danish Shibari Festival, 2020s-, is a Danish event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Annual Danish shibari festival hosted by MyShibari at its private rope venue in Sønder Felding / Central Jutland. MyShibari characterizes the venue as Denmark's largest shibari venue, with indoor and outdoor suspension spots, forest and lake tying areas, an 11m tower, international presenters, workshops, rope jams and camping. The 2025 listing names HenRigger & A-TypeSub, Neya & Kameko, Saara Rei & Swen Brandy, Kitty Rea & Rainbow, Roxu & Ena and Vik13 & Gala as presenters; the 2026 listing calls it the fourth Danish Shibari Festival and names documented presenters Joan von Brook, Kameko and Sam Jay / Zero-Gi among a broader roster.

- [2024 edition](https://web.archive.org/web/20250813005507/https://myshibari.dk/en/begivenheder/danish-shibari-festival-2024-3/)

### Elevation Rope

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/elevation-rope/

Fact — Elevation Rope, 2026–, is documented as an American event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2026–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Southeast United reports rope bondage / shibari conference based at Deerfields Retreat in Horse Shoe, North Carolina, near Asheville. The first-party site frames Elevation Rope as a multi-night immersive rope education event in the Blue Ridge Mountains, with beginner-to-masterclass instruction, outdoor / lake retreat context, themed evenings, performances and play parties. The 2026 edition is advertised for July 16-21, with the main four-night conference July 17-21 and optional early arrival on July 16.

### EURIX 2010s

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/eurix-2010s/

Fact — EURIX 2010s, 2012-2019, is documented as a German event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2012-2019

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — European Rigger & Model Exchange — Berlin's bi-annual gathering for international rope artists, established by Felix Ruckert in 2012. This entry represents the 2010s phase, from the schwelle7 editions via the pre-pandemic Berlin exchange years. A primary diffusion vector of contemporary shibari pedagogy spanning Europe; Andrea Ropes' own event list includes EURIX Berlin in September 2016.

### EURIX 2020s

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/eurix-2020s/

Fact — EURIX 2020s, 2020-, is documented as a German event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — European Rigger & Model Exchange in its 2020s phase, continuing as Felix Ruckert's Berlin-centered exchange and research format for contemporary rope artists after the pandemic-era disruption. EURIX XXVII autumn 2025 records Sarca & Kat Schrodinger among the presenters. This decade slice keeps current presenters visually attached to the present era rather than the 2010s founding entry.

Fact — Festival de Cuerdas de Medellín, 2023–, is a Colombian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2023–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Recurring Medellín rope / shibari festival active in the 2020s. An indexed official BDSMColombia page preserves the IV Festival de Cuerdas as a March 2023 city-wide rope-culture program, while Shibari Night Bogotá's 2024 Galería Viva Medellín page places that invitation in the framework of the V Festival de Cuerdas de Medellín.

### Folsom Street Fair Bondage Stage

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/folsom-street-fair-bondage-stage/

Fact — Folsom Street Fair Bondage Stage, 2024–, is documented as an American event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2024–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Rope / bondage performance and education stage inside San Francisco's Folsom Street Fair, a leather / BDSM street fair first held in 1984 in the South of Market neighborhood. Folsom Street Fair itself is broader kink / leather infrastructure, so the map models the rope-specific Bondage Stage rather than turning the whole fair into a high-degree hub. The 2024 stage page frames the Bondage Stage as a Twisted Windows and KinkPOC Bay Area production with four simultaneous rope performances, performance art, education and hands-on rope experiences, produced by Ivy Limieux and Shay Tiziano.

#### Reference links

- [Folsom Street Fair history](https://www.folsomstreet.org/history)
- [Folsom Bondage Stage 2024](https://www.twistedwindows.com/events/folsom-street-fair-bondage-stage-2024)

### FOR YOUR PLEASURE: SHIBARI Photo Exhibition 2025

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/for-your-pleasure-shibari-2025/

Fact — FOR YOUR PLEASURE: SHIBARI Photo Exhibition 2025, 2025, is a Thai event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2025

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Bangkok shibari photography exhibition by Forfun Bangkok Co., Ltd. At Bangkok Art and Culture Centre in November 2025. The official BACC event page frames the show as a shibari photo exhibition about rope as an art form and records the Bangkok Art and Culture Centre Curved Wall, 3rd Floor as the venue. Public public social traces connect the FYP / BACC context to Usagi Shibari rope work, Kathalyn Rojanakongtawin model credits and FilmNetflick photography context, while Lilythothealley's official About page adds a direct artist-work trace.

### Helsinki Shibari

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/helsinki-shibari/

Fact — Helsinki Shibari, 2015-, is a Finnish event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2015-

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Helsinki rope community established in 2015. Organizes peer rope and play nights, workshops, and the Helsinki Shibari festival, with volunteer staff and a safer-space / feminist learning framework. The official workshop archive preserves dated early festival and guest-workshop traces.

Fact — Indy Rope Expo, 2020s-, is documented as an American event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Indianapolis shibari event produced by Rope Daddy Studios at SubSpace Indy. The event frames itself as a premier shibari event with nationally recognized presenters, beginner-to-advanced classes, private classes, play parties, performances, vendors and a raffle, giving the US Midwest a dedicated rope-expo entry beyond the historic ShibariCon / ROPECRAFT axis.

### International Shibari Film Festival

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/international-shibari-film-festival/

Fact — International Shibari Film Festival, 2026-, is a Belgian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2026-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Belgian film festival dedicated to shibari via cinema. The official ISFF site characterizes it as the first cultural event in Europe dedicated to shibari via the medium of cinema, presenting shibari as an artistic language at the crossroads of film, performance and visual arts. The public 2026 site places the festival in Brussels at Cinema Aventure on July 2, 2026, says it serves as the opening night of Graines de Cordes III, and names Atelier Mae ASBL as the organizer.

### Intro to Shibari Luxembourg

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/intro-to-shibari-luxembourg/

Fact — Intro to Shibari Luxembourg, 2025-2026, is an event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2025-2026

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Bounded Luxembourg City Intro to Shibari workshop trace used to locate L-Corde's public Luxembourg scene activity without creating a general venue entry. Visit Luxembourg City / Echo listings document June 14, 2025 and February 7, 2026 Intro to Shibari workshops led by L-Corde at Rainbow Center, covering consent, rope types, knots, frictions and foundational ties for mixed-experience participants; the same public bios describe L-Corde as now centered in Luxembourg and offering workshops, private lessons and performances. The page presents listed as a narrow public workshop connection, not as a recurring festival, studio or broad local platform.

### Jyo-mon / 縄門

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/jyo-mon/

Fact — Jyo-mon / 縄門, 2018–2026, is documented as a Japanese event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2018–2026

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Tokyo shibari performance event associated with Hajime Kinoko and Ichinawakai. A 2018 Tokyo Shibari Aid report characterizes Jyo-mon as a Shinjuku shibari night hosted by Hajime Kinoko, structured as both an Ichinawakai student showcase and a mature performance night with established masters such as Nawashi Kanna, Shishi-waka, Shinobu Fuwa and Maruku. Tokyo Love District's 2025 listing frames Jyo-mon as a three-day Takadanobaba rope-performance event with Grace / Harmony / Beauty themes and a roster naming Hajime Kinoko, ZIMA & Mafuyu, Jake & Laced, VESPER & Miho, KungFurious and Louis Kordex. Louis Kordexe's official 2026 event page confirms continuing Takadanobaba / Studio Kicolit continuity under Hajime Kinoko, documenting a Sunday May 24, 2026 performance pair with Kiyome inside a three-day edition.

Fact — Active: 2025-

Fact — Danish shibari / kinbaku camp near Hadsund. The 2025 Ropejam international Kinbaku Camp listing records a four-day schedule at Ropejam / Fruerlundparken with workshops by Andrea Ropes and Ren Yagami plus show pairings around Hairoguma / SarahAdira and Andrea Ropes / Yagami. ### Kinbaku Dreams

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/kinbaku-dreams/

Fact — Kinbaku Dreams, 2018-, is an Australian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2018-

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Australian Kinbaku Dreams rope camp / performance-team and event umbrella hosted via Kinbaku Australia. The current official site frames Kinbaku Dreams around immersive gatherings, evocative performances and inclusive workshops, and the Humanitix collection characterizes it as a performance team coordinating rope-related events via the year. Public Jungle Love coverage places Kinbaku Dreams in the 2018 performance-art lineup beside a Shibari workshop, while Humanitix records the September 2022 Jungle Love theme camp as an expansion of an earlier popular workshop into performances, intro and beginner workshops, rope experiences and intermediate / advanced workshops. The 2024 Partici-party page records Kinbaku Dreams as a returning Jungle Love feature theme camp, and later Humanitix pages track the shift into capped Natural Bridge / Springbrook rope retreats and camps in 2025-2027.

### Kinbaku Yuga

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/kinbaku-yuga/

Fact — Kinbaku Yuga, 2023–, is a Russian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2023–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Siberian shibari festival in Krasnoyarsk, publicly documented from at least the June 9-12, 2023 Telegram-channel description and the June 2025 / June 2026 public listings. The 2025 VKevent mirror records `Kinbaku Yuga X` for June 13-15, with a lecture, master class, two shibari-show evenings and afterparty, and names Natalia Vishnya / CherryRopes as the ticket and participation contact via the Shibari Së Krasnoyarsk community. The public Telegram profile listed the 2026 edition for June 12-14 in Krasnoyarsk, invited masters, models and speakers, and named @cherryropes for participation / cooperation. 2026 Telegram posts list show days on June 12-13, workshop blocks by Alexey Poryadnov, Rhino, Lisitsa and Kano, and a post-event afterparty recap naming actions by Alexey Poryadnov, Sё and CherryRopes / Vishnya before announcing the next June 2027 edition; a later CherryRopes repost labels the June 2027 festival as the twelfth edition.

### Kinky Breizh Ropecamp

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/kinky-breizh-ropecamp/

Fact — Kinky Breizh Ropecamp, 2025–, is a French event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2025–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — French / Brittany-region kinky rope camp organized by Association Le Manoir. The official 2026 page announces a July 23-26, 2026 edition between Nantes and Rennes, with 26 shibari spaces such as upgraded suspension points in forest / natural areas, a Japanese Torii performance area with sound and light, workshops around rope, BDSM and Sulky practices, mini-munches, an expanded BDSM play zone, camping / lodging options and on-site food booking. The same page says 38 workshops were offered in 2025, so the event is documented as a recurring camp rather than a one-off listing; the private exact address is intentionally not listed.

### La Nuit des Cordes

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/la-nuit-des-cordes/

Fact — La Nuit des Cordes, 2014–, is a Canadian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2014–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Montreal annual rope-bondage performance event established by Ebi McKnotty and Dunter in 2014, inspired by Morpheous' Bondage Extravaganza and tied to Nuit Blanche / Montréal en Lumière. The official current site says NDC has been part of Nuit Blanche every year since, with the 2021 edition virtual and a 2024 return in person at the usual Espace des Arts. Public listings and archives place the 2015, 2016, 2017 and 2019 editions at Espace des Arts / 9 Sainte-Catherine Est; the 2021 page frames the event as a seven-hour digital edition with 100+ artists, simultaneous performances, circus, erotic bondage, acrobatic and decorative rope, and photography exhibition context.

#### Reference links

- [Official site](https://www.nuitdescordes.com/eng)

### La Ofrenda

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/la-ofrenda/

Fact — La Ofrenda, 2024-, is a Chilean event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2024-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Santiago live-performance work mixing shibari with multidisciplinary erotic / ritual stage languages. The project's own profile characterizes La Ofrenda as a living work combining dance, theatre, shibari, happening, acrobatics, eroticism and rituality in one spectacle. La Cava Tensión introduced it in late 2024 as a creation by Demonia Vegan and Knoctopus, and subsequent public FAE programme snippets preserve it as a scheduled performance title.

### London Festival of Rope Art / Japanese Bondage

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/lfra/

Fact — London Festival of Rope Art / Japanese Bondage, 2009–2015, is a British event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2009–2015

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — Historical London rope-art festival lineage spanning the London Festival of the Art of Japanese Bondage and later London Festival of Rope Art. Early Resistance Gallery editions in 2009 / 2010 helped introduce international kinbaku performance and teaching to the UK scene, with Esinem and BeShibari among early participants and Hajime Kinoko appearing in the 2010 edition. The 2015 LFRA edition at Resistance Gallery framed itself as a celebration of shibari, performance art, cultural exchange and international cooperative learning, with performers, teachers, exhibitions, workshops and masterclasses from many countries.
