### MeART Kinoko Live Hong Kong

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/meart-kinoko-live-hong-kong-2024/

Fact — MeART Kinoko Live Hong Kong, 2024, is a Hong Kong-centered event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2024

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — One-night Hong Kong shibari performance showcase curated by MeART at Mondrian Hong Kong on November 22, 2024. Art+ Magazine characterizes the program as The Shibari Connection: Love, Lust, and Beauty, with separate performance segments by Hajime Kinoko, Nawa Shishi and Steve Freeman; Nawa Shishi's Love segment is documented as a rope-and-calligraphy performance with Merryislovely.

Fact — Active: 2025–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Medellín recurring open-rope / shibari jam launched in 2025. Public MedJam profile posts describe it as a space for kinbaku, connection and collective learning in Medellín, welcoming all bodies and all levels via jams, guided practice, community encounters, collaborations and workshops. The current July 19, 2026 post places the session at Academia de baile Químbara Cumbara in Laureles / Estadio and says each meeting starts with a mini introductory workshop before the jam. The current record names Kinky Room and Cuerdas Nómadas as the current organizers.

### Men Roping Men

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/men-roping-men/

Fact — Men Roping Men, 2024-, is documented as an American event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2024-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Recurring US rope conference focused on education and community-building for men, masc-leaning non-binary, trans and gender-nonconforming people interested in rope bondage. The official 2026 site characterizes Men Roping Men as the third annual conference, hosted in New York City on October 9-11, 2026, with direct instruction, performances, skill sharing and play / connection time. Its mission statement frames MRM as a volunteer-run organization emphasizing safety, consent, respect, diversity, education, networking and community building.

### Moscow Knot / MoscowKnot International

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/moscowknot/

Fact — Moscow Knot / MoscowKnot International, 2015–, is a Russian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2015–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — Moscow shibari / bondage festival lineage associated with Vlada Vedmovskaya's Russian-scene organizing work. Public traces use the Moscow Knot name for bondage-festival performances by 2015 / 2016, such as Andrea Ropes and Xa Na, while BalkanBound's history frames MoscowKnot International as the 2018-present international event that connected practitioners and audiences and popularized shibari in Russia and abroad. RopeMarks' own archive records a 2018 Moscow Knot edition at Mona Club with RopeMarks shows, a RopeMarks Ryu workshop and Yagami Ren in the same program, plus an October 2018 follow-up RopeMarks Ryu weekend at Club Predel organized by Vlada and Falco. Ka.sure characterizes Vlada as one of the founders and producers of Moscow Knot for many years. This entry keeps the early Moscow Knot performance traces and the later International format together because they belong to the same 2010s Moscow event lineage rather than separate visual eras.

### NARIX

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/narix/

Fact — NARIX, 2014–, is an event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2014–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — North American Rope Innovation eXchange, a peer-to-peer rope unconference / conference series active since 2014. The first-party site characterizes NARIX as a twice-a-year peer-to-peer conference bringing dedicated rope lovers from spanning North America together to share, learn, discuss and experiment with rope bondage, with regional event support, a digital organizer-resource archive and an online community. A 2022 steering-committee post records a June Montreal event hosted at TensionMtl after a long hiatus and Spring 2023 restart planning. The current homepage says NARIX is on hiatus while staying open to future events.

Fact — Recurring Detroit rope, shibari and BDSM education series operated by Oblige as Learn, Explore, Apply & Play. The first-party page characterizes weekly Thursday classes that alternate rope / shibari and wider kink subjects, with fundamentals, formal instruction, guided practice, open play, livestream access and an archive of past recordings. Current and archived listings repeatedly feature Oblige co-owners Paul / Serene_X and Flynn Racer as rope and suspension instructors alongside guest presenters.

### Obryady — KNOT FEST

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/obryady-festival-of-knots/

Fact — Obryady — KNOT FEST, 2026, is a Russian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2026

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Four-day outdoor rope and body-practice festival announced for July 23–26, 2026 in the Saint Petersburg area. The programme combines camping, shared meals, lake and forest rope photography, a free-tying zone, somatic and related practices, fire and wax play, and evening shibari performances on a forest stage. Aleksandr Kazak's official site gives KNOT-FEST a dedicated event block and routes registration to the standalone festival page; the precise campsite and named performer lineup are not publicly listed.

Fact — Karlsruhe erotic BDSM and fetish fair staged by M&A Messemanagement. Official public pages support recurring 2024-2026 continuity and frame obscene as a community-oriented fair with exhibitors, workshops, stage shows, evening events and the bondage area Rope Xperience. The public 2026 programme names rope workshops and stage performances such as Ren Yagami, Aijō / Shana & Jack, RopuNawa, Sakura Rope, VAEGABOUND x Sapieha, Dan Apus Monoceros, Davide La Greca, Happa & LucyLaCat and Rope Xperience & Friends, making the fair a current regional rope-performance connection rather than only a shopping / fetish-market entry.

Fact — Active: 2009–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Tokyo-centered annual female bakushi festival / high-skilled amateur and professional kinbaku stage established and produced by Yoi Yoshida since 2009. Kinbaku Today's Yoi interview says the first June 2009 Onawa Asobi grew from the Good-Studio orbit and featured Hajime Kinoko, Kasumi Hourai, Shigonawa Bingo, Ero Ouji and others; later interview traces place Ranki Kazami at Onawa Asobi 2010, while Shibari Lounge sources document BeShibari and Shibarista Jess performing at Tokyo Onawa Asobi in 2019. The European edition (Onawa Asobi Europe) was launched from this Tokyo format as a partnership with Shibari Lounge.

### Onawa Asobi Europe

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/onawa-europe/

Fact — Onawa Asobi Europe, 2020, 2023–, is a Belgian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020, 2023–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — European edition of the Tokyo-centered Onawa Asobi kinbaku festival, held at Shibari Lounge in Antwerp. The Tokyo festival has run yearly since 2009 under Yoi Yoshida and is described by Onawa Europe as a stepping stone for many Japanese shibarite, such as Naka Akira, Hajime Kinoko, Ranki Kazami, Ren Yagami, Kasumi Hourai, Shigonawa Bingo and Ero Ouji. Yoi Yoshida teamed with BeShibari / Marc BeShibari and Shibarista Jess to bring the format to Antwerp in 2020; later European editions followed in 2023, 2024, 2025 and 2026. The event frames itself as two days of amateur Japanese and European kinbaku performances, Shibari Night rope parties, friendship, cultural exchange and sincere rope interaction rather than a competition or masterclass convention.

### PAKILIMAS / Take-Off Lithuania

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/pakilimas-take-off-lithuania/

Fact — PAKILIMAS / Take-Off Lithuania, 2020s-, is an event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Vilnius fetish-oriented techno / BDSM night and recurring Take-Off party series with public play-room performance programming. Resident Advisor records PAKILIMAS 20.0% at Kablys in February 2025, promoted by Kablys + ClubTake-Off, with ALFY billed for a shibari suspension essentials demonstration; a current 2026 RA listing for PAKILIMAS X Elastica frames Take-Off as promoter and the event as the start of PAKILIMAS's seventh season.

### ParanFest Yerevan

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/paranfest-yerevan/

Fact — ParanFest Yerevan, 2026, is an Armenian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2026

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Two-day 18+ shibari / Japanese-culture festival held in Yerevan, Armenia on May 23-24, 2026. The first-party site frames PARANFEST as the first Armenian festival of Japanese culture, voluntary bondage and rope bondage / shibari, with performances, educational lectures, workshops, a market zone and open discussions. The public Telegram channel, Instagram announcement and final schedule make it structurally useful for the map because the roster connects multiple existing Russian-language and European scene entries, such as Nick Freerider, Tati Limati, Zevsa, Aleksei Kalatsky, Nik Yourknots, Vasilisa Lesnaya, august, Entangled Witch and Aleksandra Bor / Trojm.

### Pinse Camp Denmark

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/pinse-camp-denmark/

Fact — Pinse Camp Denmark, 2024–, is a Danish event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2024–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Recurring Danish rope / shibari camp hosted by MyShibari at its Central Jutland venue in Sønder Felding. MyShibari's official listings describe Pinse Camp as a multi-day camp with lower-intermediate and intermediate / advanced tracks, workshop days, free rope space, indoor / outdoor tying facilities, camping, full catering and international teachers. RopeMarks' 2024 report names Bo and Ewa of MyShibari as hosts, with RopeMarks / the Shady Lady teaching the intermediate-advanced group and Haag / Zu as co-teachers. The 2025 programme records a GanRaptor / Kiki_x lower-intermediate track, an A-Nicolas / Ssyrene intermediate-advanced track and a Friday GanRaptor / Kiki_x performance; the 2026 programme records Nikoleta / Šembi for lower-intermediate / intermediate, Tifereth / Joan von Brook for intermediate / advanced and a Friday Nikoleta / Šembi performance.

### Prague Shibari Fest 2010s

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/prague-festival-2010s/

Fact — Prague Shibari Fest 2010s, 2016–2019, is a Czech event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2016–2019

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — 2010s slice of Prague Shibari Festival, a three-day rope event held twice a year in Prague since 2016. The first 2016 Prague Karlin edition combined English / Czech workshop tracks with Friday and Saturday performance nights; later 2017-2019 archives keep the pre-pandemic festival circuit visible. This founding-era entry keeps Soptik's Prague organization, Subspace pre-party / local scene context and source-supported early international presenters such as Caritia, Felix Ruckert, Hedwig, Pedro Cordas, Thure Nyyrikki, Miss Eris, RopeMarks, Tamandua, Gestalta, Asiana and Michael RopeKnight attached to the founding decade.

### Prague Shibari Fest 2020s

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/prague-festival-2020s/

Fact — Prague Shibari Fest 2020s, 2020s–, is a Czech event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — 2020s slice of Prague Shibari Festival, continuing the twice-yearly Prague event after the pandemic-era break with English-language workshop tracks, evening bondage jams and Subspace Prague pre-party / scene context. Recent official pages document 2022-2026 presenters and schedules such as Tifereth, Joan von Brook, Gestalta, Haag / Pendragon, Misungui Bordelle, Soptik, Sam Jay / Zero-Gi, Bänana, Stuart, Petite Pretzel, Alexander MA / Natasha NawaTaNeko, Anna Lysee, Diana Vera and Yukiscream. This entry keeps current Prague / Subspace and touring-presenter links in the present era instead of collapsing them into the 2010s founding entry.

### Queerope

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/queerope/

Fact — Queerope, 2020s–, is documented as a German event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Karada House's queer rope conference for LGBTQIA+ people and womên, focused mostly on shibari, kinbaku and Japanese-inspired bondage. Queerope exists in both virtual and in-person Berlin formats: the February 11-16 2026 virtual edition published online workshops, performances, demonstrations and discussions, while Queerope Berlin 2026 is a five-day in-person conference at KARA / Karada House on September 2-6 with 37 facilitated spaces / workshops. Published 2026 line-ups include Caritia, Ceci Ferox, Mouse, Murasaki Haruan, Naoko, Renée de Sans and Sophia Rose among facilitators; the Berlin edition also records Juliette & Olive, Lief Bound, Icarus / Icky, Nana Quinn, Thumper and Bondagious. VoxBody's curriculum bio independently names Blue as having presented at Karada House's Queerope.

### QuIRKS

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/quirks/

Fact — QuIRKS, 2026, is a Canadian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2026

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — QuIRKS, the Queer International Rope & Kink Symposium, was a three-day Studio Allegory event in Vancouver on May 8-10, 2026. Official pages describe Studio Allegory's first QuIRKS as an in-person weekend for queer kinksters with 12 workshops / classes, 3 play parties, an opening orientation, local and international queer presenters, and a resource hub with presenter, timetable and class details. The published programme creates a visible connection between Studio Allegory's Vancouver scene and existing Berlin / London / Chicago rope educators such as Mamana, Renée de Sans, Sophia Rose, Barkas and Anoxia / Nox.

### Regina Nera

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/regina-nera/

Fact — Regina Nera, 2010s-, is an Italian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s-

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Italian recurring fetish / pleasure-art event shell active for more than a decade. Pre-2022 third-party traces place Regina Nera in Italian BDSM / fetish play-party and rope-adjacent programme contexts, such as Decadence collaborations with shibari / kinbaku programming and a TNG Italia play-party trace at Castello di Valbona. The public Instagram profile says Regina Nera has organized one of Italy's most exclusive events for more than a decade, and a September 2025 official Kappashibari feature preserves a concrete edition at Bolero Palace in Altedo, Bologna province while presenting Kappa as current official-rigger / resident context from 2024 onward.

### Reno Rope Bite

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/reno-rope-bite/

Fact — Reno Rope Bite, 2022–, is documented as an American event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2022–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Recurring Reno rope skillshare and practice gathering hosted via Entwine-NV since July 2022. Current programmes describe Reno Rope Bite as a safer practice space combining guided technique workshops with community learning, such as beginner-to-intermediate material, mobility-aware adaptation and risk checks.

### Rome Bondage Week

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/rome-bondage-week/

Fact — Rome Bondage Week, 2017–, is an Italian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2017–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Italian bondage festival / event series in Rome. Public 2017-2021 event pages describe Rome Bondage Week as a festival dedicated to the art of bondage, exploring Japanese tradition and contemporary Western experimentations via workshops, performances, peer-rope and cultural events, with production by Ritual The Club / Ritual LAB. Dated public traces place the 2017 edition at Lost and Found Studio / Qube, the 2018 edition at Largo Venue, and the 2019-2021 listings around Via del Mandrione / Snodo Mandrione. Red Lily's public events page still characterizes her as artistic director of Rome Bondage Week, giving a durable existing-entry connection into the event shell.

### Rope Camp France

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/rope-camp-france/

Fact — Rope Camp France, 2019–2025, is a French event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2019–2025

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — French shibari / rope camp organized via the ASKA orbit. Stella Spei's official bio records her as organizer of Rope Camp, described as an annual gathering for rope practitioners from France, Belgium, Italy and Spain. The official 2022 archive records an ASKA-run Saula-site camp with workshops, zones, volunteer teams and performance evenings, while the 2025 HelloAsso page frames Rope Camp as a weekend rope camp with mini-workshops, nightly shibari performances, community practice and camping at La Maison du Saula.

### Rope Spirit

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/rope-spirit/

Fact — Rope Spirit, 2010s-, is a Czech event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s-

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Prague / Czech shibari performance event produced in the Hell Events orbit. Rope Spirit frames nights of Japanese bondage / shibari performances with a strict no-photo policy and post-show tying floor, and Sanctuary's 2026 listing characterizes the twentieth Rope Spirit as having grown from a small first meeting roughly eleven years earlier into a larger but still intimate show format. Archived performer and live gallery traces preserve continuity from the 2015 first editions via Rope Spirit XX in 2026, such as Frater & Manako at Rope Spirit VI and Nawasabi with Schwarzes Biest at Rope Spirit IX.

### ROPECRAFT

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/ropecraft/

Fact — ROPECRAFT, 2016–, is documented as an American event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2016–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — US rope-focused kink convention with events in Austin, New Orleans, San Antonio, and Chicago. The official about page frames the event as Graydancer frames: ROPECRAFT and records the 2016 Austin / NOLA origins before later Chicago, San Antonio and online editions. Combines shibari, western bondage, power exchange, solo tying, wellness, play, presenters, performances, vendors, parties, and peer-learning formats such as Ropenspace.

### RopeDay Moscow

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/ropeday-moscow/

Fact — RopeDay Moscow, 2015–, is a Russian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2015–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Moscow rope-bondage / shibari event format in the ArtCo and Yugen orbit. RopeFest Peterburg's ArtCo explainer and Nawapedia's RopeDay documentation frame RopeDay Moscow as an open rope party for people practicing or interested in rope bondage, organized by ArtCo and supported by Shibari Club Yugen. Shibari.moscow photo traces connect RopeDay Moscow to Master Set and Boris Mosafir. Yakov KINKKNIGHT's RopeFest bio names him as a RopeDay resident, and his 2023 Telegraph article includes RopeDayMoscow action context.

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/ropeday-spb/

Fact — RopeDay SPb, 2014–2015, is a Russian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2014–2015

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Saint Petersburg one-day shibari / rope-bondage festival format in the ArtCo and Yugen orbit. Nawapedia's RopeDay page records the Saint Petersburg 2014-2015 one-day festival format with a later Moscow continuation, while Peterburg2 confirms the 13 June 2015 RopeDay SPb event at Phoenix Concert Hall. Shibari.Guru's performance-video list preserves RopeDay Peterburg 2014 performance traces by Boris Mosafir with Ishemia and Kristi Veres.

### RopeFest 2010s

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/ropefest-2010s/

Fact — RopeFest 2010s, 2012–2019, is a Russian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2012–2019

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — 2010s slice of Russia's first and largest shibari festival, first held in Saint Petersburg in 2012 and developed via the ArtCo / RopeSchool ecosystem before the pandemic-era break. ShibariNews characterizes Rope Fest as the first Russian Shibari Festival in St Petersburg, supported by Shibari Club Yugen, RopeSchool, RopeShop.Guru and KinkAfisha, with multi-day performances, seminars, peer rope, escape events and rope jams. The 2010s branch locations the early Saint Petersburg / Moscow / Baltia festival family and its international guest-master roster.

### RopeFest 2020s

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/ropefest-2020s/

Fact — RopeFest 2020s, 2020s–, is a Russian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — 2020s slice of the RopeFest event family, now covering current Moscow / RFMSK23 traces, a 2022 Saint Petersburg / Skorokhod photo-archive trace and the revived RopeFest Peterburg 2026 branch. The official Moscow site frames the 2023 edition as an international shibari festival and records current Moscow-scene participants and teachers such as Nick Freerider, Zevsa and Poryadnov, while Kommersant Photo preserves a May 2022 RopeFest show-program trace at Skorokhod and the official Peterburg site records XIII RopeFest on May 1-3 2026 in Saint Petersburg with a large main-stage, small-stage, workshop and fair program. This decade slice prevents contemporary Moscow, Saint Petersburg and regional Russian performers from visually collapsing into the 2010s founding festival entry.

### Ropegasms: Athens Shibari Festival

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/ropegasms-athens-shibari-festival/

Fact — Ropegasms: Athens Shibari Festival, 2012, is a Greek event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2012

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Athens rope / kinbaku performance-festival format established in 2012 by Rope Studies / Rope Shadow. Art&Life preserves Athens Shibari Festival Volume 1 as an October 11, 2012 Faust event and names Rope Shadow / Rope Studies plus Alexis-D on the performer bill, while VICE later characterizes Ropegasms / Athens Shibari Festival as the 2012 Rope Studies event format.

### ropeXchange

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/ropexchange/

Fact — RopeXchange, 2020s-, is documented as a German event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Berlin three-day shibari and rope-bondage community festival / workshop weekend. The event characterizes itself as centered around Shibari & Rope Bondage and other kinky topics, taking place four times a year in Berlin, with 30+ hours of workshops, skill labs, peer networking, play / rope jams and a queer-friendly, polynormal safer-space framework. The archived June 2025 "Summer in Jail" programme confirms the June 13-15 Berlin edition and records Dan, Frl. Frivol, Arto & Jessa and Tom Delirio spanning workshops, exchange formats, rope making and jam / show activity.

### Seattle Erotic Art Festival

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/seattle-erotic-art-festival/

Fact — Seattle Erotic Art Festival, 2003–, is documented as an American event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2003–

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — Long-running Seattle erotic-art and performance festival with a durable rope-relevant scene connection. The official history page says the first Seattle Erotic Art Festival was produced in 2003 by the Pan Eros Foundation, grew into a multi-format festival spanning visual art, performance, literature and film, and introduced an Aerial Stage for aerialists and rope performances in 2018. The official photo archive preserves dated 2011-2021 festival venue traces, while the current home page advertises the 2026 edition at Seattle Center Exhibition Hall.

### Shi Fest Ukraine

Fact — Shi Fest Ukraine, 2015, 2016, 2018, 2019, is a Ukrainian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2015, 2016, 2018, 2019

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Kyiv international shibari festival organized in the Ukrainian Soul Shibari / Tati Limati orbit. Soul Shibari records dated editions on November 14, 2015; August 26-27, 2016; August 26, 2018; and September 7-8, 2019, with the 2019 page framing that edition as the 4th international Shibari Festival and naming Tati Limati as the main organizer.

### ShibaRetreat

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/shibaretreat/

Fact — ShibaRetreat, 2020s-, is an Austrian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Annual three-day shibari retreat organized by Salzburg association Salzachknoten in the Attergau. The first-party page characterizes impulse workshops and thematic sessions, guided tying in nature, free tying by the campfire, community reflection, camping and full-board retreat infrastructure with two indoor workshop rooms. Testimonials from the previous edition thank the Salzachknoten and Boundless Studio Leipzig teams; the latter remains partner context pending separate research.

### Shibari Camp by kokxxx_rope

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/shibari-camp-kokxxx/

Fact — Shibari Camp by kokxxx_rope, 2023–, is a Croatian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2023–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Croatian shibari camp organized via Kokxxx rope. SM-ART Ljubljana's 2023 blog review characterizes the first edition as SHIBARI CAMP by kokxxx_rope & MyShibari, a three-day event near Zagreb. The 2026 CoreEvent listing characterizes the July 3-6 edition near Rivalno / Ludbreg as the fourth Shibari Camp by kokxxx_rope, combining workshops, performance, rope jams, music, community time, dungeon / play-party evenings, pool parties and all-inclusive camping. The public 2026 roster records Lexie Nawa as international guest, Ludvig on the advanced track, Angie Ziv for performance, and Teljes_Szakall & TarraXinha for the beginner track. Lexienawa, Ludvig and Angie Ziv are listed as map connections because they already have established map entries; Teljes_Szakall and TarraXinha remain roster-level until stronger independent sources or map connections appear.

### Shibari Camp Poland

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/shibari-camp-poland/

Fact — Shibari Camp Poland, 2024–, is a Polish event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2024–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Multi-day Shibari Camp in Poland organized around workshops, performances, rope jams, photo shoots and a Shibari Ball. A 2025 post-event report by Petite Pretzel records teaching and live-show participation with Ania Polsky at Castle Miedzylesie for Tati Limati's Shibari Camp. The 2026 edition is presented as an annual gathering at Zamek Miedzylesie; its team bio credits Tati Limati with running Shibari Camps in Poland and Ukraine and records current map-covered presenters / organizers such as Klaudyna, Astra, Vik & Gala, Marta Skinnysticks and Marta Pagan.

### Shibari Experience Brazil

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/shibari-experience-brazil/

Fact — Shibari Experience Brazil, 2025–, is a Brazilian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2025–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Curitiba / Brazil shibari performance, party and workshop event. Local 2025 coverage announced the first edition around Hajime Kinoko's Brazil visit, with October 3-4 staged programming at Ópera de Arame and October 5 workshops at SFCO179; the Friday stage coverage names Hajime Kinoko, Akira Nawa, Áspero, Phoenix and Rigger Maia, while Kinoko's public post-event traces preserve a Hajime Kinoko / Luiza Jesus do Prado performance context. The current official 2026 site frames a November 13-15 three-day edition organized around performance, electronic-music party with shibari performances, and workshop formats; later public Instagram / ticketing traces identify Marie Sauvage as the 2026 main attraction and Sunday workshop artist.

Fact — Shibari FEST Kazakhstan, 2024–, is a Kazakh event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2024–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — International Kazakhstan shibari festival in Almaty. Public Shibari FEST profile characterizes the event as a Kazakhstan shibari festival in Almaty, while 2024 posts say the festival was prepared by TEMA.Space and Studio Shi and list Enot as an Almaty / Moscow master. A July 2026 Telegram announcement confirms a May 1-9, 2027 edition with a shibari camp and performance-day format, with the detailed programme and participant list still pending. TEMA.Space and ShibariTutorial public posts also support Enot's organizer / performer role and the festival's function as a current Russian-language / Kazakhstan scene connection.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Recurring ASKA performance / exhibition evening around shibari, erotic art, live music, introductory practice and rope jams in the Toulouse / Albi orbit. Fact — Active: 2026

Fact — Era: 2020s

Safety / consent — Poland-centered Shibari Haven retreat scheduled for April 10-12, 2026, combining shibari workshops, mindfulness, guided meditation, safety / anatomy lectures, performance evening, rope jams and residential retreat structure. The official retreat page frames Saara Tsuru as the main shibari workshop teacher / performance artist and records Kasia as retreat organizer / teacher / presenter, with Carnivore, Jowita and Anastasia in supporting teacher / presenter roles.

### Shibari Valencia Fest

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/shibari-valencia-fest-2026/

Fact — Shibari Valencia Fest, 2026-, is a Spanish event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2026-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Valencia shibari festival organized via Reisei Studio. The first documented edition ran May 22-24, 2026 at Vaive Circ near Valencia, with simultaneous workshop tracks, performances and rope jams; the final public schedule gives the workshop grid and invited educator / performance-night pairs as Barkas & Addie, Supaku & Kel, and Soptik & Sansei. In June 2026, the official festival / Reisei channels announced a May 21-23, 2027 continuation and named Gorgone as the first confirmed presenter, with the model still to be confirmed.

### Shibaricamp

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/shibaricamp/

Fact — Shibaricamp, 2010s-, is a Swedish event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Recurring week-long summer camp on the west coast of Sweden focused on rope bondage, community, co-creation, classes, private tuition, rope play and small-group learning. Held in Bohuslan near Gothenburg / Smogen; the 2019 edition ran August 5-11 and listed a complete teacher team such as Sansblague & Bergborg, Lello Li & Alex Mino, Andrea Ropes & Chiara Fenice, MnR and Artifexual. The 2025 edition ran August 4-10 and listed Stuart, Luke & Kate, Rope and Yukiscream / Miya in its public teacher traces. The 2026 edition runs August 3-9 and the official teachers page names Annika, Isla & Luisa, Neya & Kameko, and Tiferet & Lily. Shibaricamp's own teacher archive also records a dense international 2010s / 2020s roster such as Anna Bones & Fred Hatt, Barkas & Addie, Sansblague & Bergborg, Ceci Ferox, Gestalta, Soptik, Tifereth, Joan von Brook, Petite Pretzel and Andrea Ropes.

### ShibariCon

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/shibaricon/

Fact — ShibariCon, 2004–2010s, is documented as an American event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2004–2010s

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — Historic Chicagoland rope-bondage conference focused on education and information exchange for lovers of Japanese rope bondage. The inaugural May 2004 edition was hosted in Rosemont, followed by documented Naperville and Lincolnwood editions in 2005 and 2006. ShibariCon became one of the key North American meeting points for international rope teachers and practitioners; the early official programmes connect Midori, Dov / Washi Nawashi, Bridgett / Lee Harrington, Lochai, Claire Adams, Graydancer, Mark of DV8House and other established educators to its formative years.

### Shibaripanda Minsk

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/shibaripanda-minsk/

Fact — Shibaripanda Minsk, 2020, is a Belarusian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Minsk shibari / rope-bondage festival co-created by Ryhor Shabeka and connected to the Belarusian Nawashibelarus community. Limits characterizes Ryhor as organizer of the Belarusian bondage-loving community Nawashibelarus and festival Shibaripanda 2020 in Minsk, while Litexpo names him as co-creator of Shibaripanda Minsk and Wisimi Studio records Shibaripanda and Nawapaty as Minsk festivals he established.

Fact — Active: 2026

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Moscow three-day Shibaru festival format scheduled for July 17-19, 2026. Public Telegram sources present SHIBARU FEST 2026 as a program of workshops, lectures, free tying, market, social space and performances. ### Space shibari-party

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/space-shibari-party/

Fact — Space shibari-party, 2026–, is a Russian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2026–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Saint Petersburg 18+ Vabi-Sabi meeting / party format hosted at PinkRabbit House. Public Vabi-Sabi and PinkRabbit House posts frame Space as a guide to emotions and reports via tactile practices such as shibari, waxplay, flagellation, sensory deprivation, meditation and massage, with a free shibari seminar, body practice or interactive, stage action, open social format and six suspension points. A PinkRabbit House February 2026 program names Experimentator, Stillness Tatiana, Renata Fushime and Arya Dark in the shibari / seminar program.

Fact — Active: 2022–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Swiss shibari festival launched in 2022 around the Bern / Kehrsatz scene. Kinbakuwiki characterizes Swissbound as a spring-and-autumn Swiss festival focused on workshops, performances and rope jams, and says the concept was developed and run by Atrim Namor and L|M. Harukumo's October 2022 post records the first Bern edition as a three-day Shibari fair with workshops and live performances. The current official Swissbound notice says the Autumn 2025 and Spring 2026 editions were cancelled because the planned venue was closing, while the team searches for a new location for future years.

### Tangled Rope Festival

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/tangled-rope-festival/

Fact — Tangled Rope Festival, 2023-, is a British event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2023-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — International rope festival centered in Derby, UK, focused on bringing rope enthusiasts from around the world together to learn, share and explore shibari. Tangled frames itself as inclusive, creative and safety-conscious, with workshops, demonstrations and social events covering traditional Japanese shibari and contemporary Western rope styles. The 2023 presenter roster included Tamandua, Ceci Ferox and Petite Pretzel.

### Thick Hitches Conference

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/thick-hitches-conference/

Fact — Thick Hitches Conference, 2025-, is documented as a German event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2025-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Rope conference hosted by Karada House and centered on fat-identifying people and bodies in rope. The 2025 Karada House page characterizes it as a three-day in-person Berlin rope conference with mostly Japanese-inspired rope bondage and facilitators Ceci Ferox, Ginger, Mouse and Queerbaku; the separate in-house programme records their workshop / session blocks spanning May 23-25. Karada's March 2026 update says Thick Hitches 2026 is going virtual on July 24-26, 2026. The current event page records Ceci Ferox, Ginger, Mouse, Queerbaku, Ms. Reemah and Hansu as facilitators and says the detailed programme is still to come.

### Toubaku

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/toubaku/

Fact — Toubaku, 2010–2012, is documented as a Japanese event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010–2012

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Tokyo international shibari festival organized by Hajime Kinoko and Ichinawakai. Expanded from the 2010 Winter Bondage event into an international festival in 2011, with performers such as Naka Akira and visiting Western practitioners such as Esinem. Nawapedia's Hebari chronology preserves a September 28, 2012 Petit in Toubaku performance trace at KunKun, documented as a bounded later occurrence rather than a full 2012 festival roster.

Fact — Tying in the Tropics, 2026, is an event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2026

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Seven-day shibari camp / kinky rope event at Hedonism II in Negril, Jamaica, May 9-16 2026, organized in the NYC Shibari orbit. The first-party page frames it as a week of sun, surf, shibari, play parties, beach rope and 36 hours of intensive education from international presenters such as Gorgone, Tifereth, RopuNawa, Sam Jay & Zero-Gi, Knot_head & Bodacious_Minx, plus event-specific model / partner participants.

### Unbound Tokyo

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/unbound-tokyo/

Fact — Unbound Tokyo, 2021–, is documented as a Japanese event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2021–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Recurring Tokyo shibari + music / performance-art event from the Hidden Layer orbit. Pablo Shibari's official bio says he and AIAIDA created Unbound with DJ SuperUser and Kojiro support after the launch party for Hidden Layer. PR Times frames the 2023 or MIYASHITA PARK edition as a Hidden Layer Studio / SuperUser event and says the first edition was held in 2021. Bonobo, Resident Advisor and WOMB listings show continued 2024-2025 editions combining shibari, philosophy, electronic music, visual art and performance, such as WOMB 2025 traces for Hajime Kinoko, Hidden Layer Show and LaoDai.

### Yugen Shibari Convention

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/yugen-shibari-convention/

Fact — Yugen Shibari Convention, 2018, 2024, 2026, is a Russian event in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2018, 2024, 2026

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Saint Petersburg shibari convention / seminar-event format in the Yugen and ArtCo ecosystem, currently based on confirmed 2018, 2024 and 2026 traces rather than as a continuously documented annual series. Nawapedia's Yugen Shibari Convention page characterizes the format as a Saint Petersburg exchange / peer-rope convention and names Shibari Club Yugen as organizer. RopeSchool's Karol profile records Yugen Shibari Convention among Karol Soulrope's active-scene performance contexts. A public TGStat mirror preserves a January 2024 Yugen Club announcement for a two-day convention with master classes, lectures, practical sessions and demonstrations. A 2026 Kinbaku Club / TGStat archive shows the format returning as a multi-day program with practical master classes, big peer rope and a closing shibari show, organized by Shibari Club Yugen.

Fact — Era: 2000s

### Bound

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/films/bound-2021-documentary/

Fact — Bound, 2021, is documented as a Japanese film in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2021

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — 2021 Japanese documentary directed by Jean-Armand Bougrelle and produced by Assemblage. IMDb records a September 25 2021 release and credits Murasaki Haruan, Harutokyo, Hinako, Daria Kira, Koyuki, Momoka, Iroha Shizuki, Yoi Yoshida, Aika Yoshioka and Yosuga as appearing as themselves; Festival du nouveau cinéma's 2021 listing frames it as an inside look at Japanese bondage and shibari. It records several Japanese rope-world figures via one bounded work; film connections here are documentary-subject credits, not lineage claims.

### I Am an S+M Writer

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/films/i-am-an-sm-writer-2000/

Fact — I Am an S+M Writer, 2000, is documented as a Japanese film in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2000

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — 2000 Japanese dramatic comedy directed by Hiroki Ryuichi, released under the original title Futei no Kisetsu / Season of Infidelity. Eiga.com names it as an 88-minute adaptation of Oniroku Dan's short story, while Nawapedia's Akira Naka profile records Naka as the film's bondage credit. It gives a compact connection between Oniroku Dan's fiction layer and Naka Akira's rope-for-camera work without pulling in broader cast or late-pink-film map clutter.

### Jyowa / Rope Stories

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/films/jyowa-rope-stories/

Fact — Jyowa / Rope Stories, 2011–2015, is a Portuguese film in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2011–2015

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Documentary / rope-film project by Pedro Cordas, also styled Jyowa "Rope Stories". Public event and media-source records describe it as starting in Tokyo in 2011 as a documentary film about rope communication, later becoming a five-chapter aesthetic film around Naka Akira, Yukimura Haruki, Nawashi Kanna with Kagura, Urado Hiroshi and Hajime Kinoko. It connects several senior Japanese figures via one bounded work and explains Pedro Cordas's filmmaker role without turning ordinary film credits into map hubs.

### Nawa Etsu

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/films/nawa-etsu/

Fact — Nawa Etsu, 2002–2008, is documented as a Japanese film in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2002–2008

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — Japanese rope / SM video series by Naka Akira with director Muryu ZOU, usually translated as Rope Joy. Kinbaku Books says Naka's 2017 photo-book and DVD set Shibari to Seme repackaged stills from sixteen Naka / Muryu ZOU videos made from 2002 to 2008, most from the original Nawa Etsu series and all earlier published by Art Video. Studio Yukari and Uplink both identify the Nawa Etsu / Muryu ZOU / Art Video collaboration as a major Naka production context.

### 性 ~SAGA~ / SEI ~SAGA~

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/films/sei-saga-2026/

Fact — 性 ~SAGA~ / SEI ~SAGA~, 2026, is documented as a Japanese film in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2026

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — 2026 Japanese kinbaku film programme by Ryuji Fukuyama, presented in Berlin by the Kinbaku Society of Berlin. IL KINO characterizes it as a 50-minute evening programme combining a roughly 15-minute narrative film featuring the late rope master Shigonawa Bingo and Aya with a 30-40-minute documentary co-directed by Ryuji Fukuyama and Mirei Nagahama, re-edited after Bingo's 2025 death as a tribute to his life and art. ISFF also records [性 ~SAGA~] in its 2026 Singular Perspectives selection.

### The Peony's Whisper

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/films/the-peonys-whisper/

Fact — The Peony's Whisper, 2016, is a French film in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2016

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Vincent Guilbert's 2016 self-produced documentary portrait of Naka Akira, released under the Japanese title Botan no sasayaki and the French title Le Murmure de la pivoine. Film-documentaire.fr characterizes it as a 65-minute portrait built around Akira Naka's memories, bodies, places and temporal fragments, while later public listings preserve festival selections such as Sitges and Festival du Film de Fesses in 2018. It creates a bounded portrait-film connection around Naka Akira with direct cast links to already documented figures such as Gorgone and Iroha Shizuki.

### The Pleasure of Rope

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/films/the-pleasure-of-rope/

Fact — The Pleasure of Rope, 2015, is a British film in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2015

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Feature documentary by Bob Bentley exploring kinbaku / shibari via footage gathered over four years in the UK and Japan. The official film and distributor pages describe multiple public versions - a 90-minute television feature, 120-minute theatrical feature, 3 x 52-minute series and DVD set - and frame the project around backstage access, workshops, performances and intimate testimony from rope practitioners. The documented cast provides a compact documentary connection spanning already documented Japanese and European figures without treating general documentary credits as a broad filmography layer.

### The Ties That Bind Us

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/films/the-ties-that-bind-us-shibari-malaysia/

Fact — The Ties That Bind Us, 2025, is a film in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2025

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — 2025 mini-documentary by Nicky Almasy about Lisa Anthony and Shibari Malaysia, also surfaced publicly as `The Ties That Bind Us - Shibari Malaysia`. Nicky Almasy's companion essay frames the work around the ancient art of shibari resurfacing in Malaysia, with Lisa Anthony guiding sessions via Shibari Malaysia and explaining rope as a medium for cultural, gender and identity exploration. YouTube listing information independently names the title and Nicky Almasy authorship.

### Fet-Erotica

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/magazines/fet-erotica/

Fact — Fet-Erotica, 2020s, is documented as an American magazine in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Online fetish / erotic art magazine and fine-art publication project associated with JC Stark. The official About page frames Fet-Erotica Fine Art as a publication for fetish and erotic concepts spanning fashion, artistic nude and wider fetish visual culture. The official Magazine page shows a long issue catalog visible via Issue No.91 and includes shibari / kinbaku entries such as Issue 57 - Shibari/Kinbaku and Issue No.67 Vol.2 INKED, which credits Kinbaku Boyscout with model Sandy. The official Instagram profile preserves the public @feterotica_magazine identity.

### Fuzoku Kitan

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/magazines/fuzoku-kitan/

Fact — Fuzoku Kitan, 1960-1974, is documented as a Japanese magazine in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 1960-1974

Fact — Era: 1970s

Fact — Japanese customs / SM magazine launched in 1960 by Bunken Shiryo Kankokai. Nawa-art notes that early issues published large quantities of kinbaku photographs, while J-DAC treats it as a broad monthly customs magazine spanning SM, gay, lesbian, cross-dressing and fetish content via the early 1970s.

### Fuzoku Soshi

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/magazines/fuzoku-soshi/

Fact — Fuzoku Soshi, 1953-1954, is documented as a Japanese magazine in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 1953-1954

Fact — Era: 1950s

Fact — Short-lived Japanese sexual-customs / SM magazine published by Nippon Tokushu Shuppansha from 1953 to 1954. J-DAC characterizes the title as centered on postwar sexual-customs culture such as sadism, masochism and kinbaku, while Nawa-art frames it as a powerful early rival to Kitan Club.

### Kinbaku Society of Berlin

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/magazines/kinbaku-society-berlin/

Fact — Kinbaku Society of Berlin, 2019–, is documented as a German magazine in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2019–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Berlin-centered kinbaku research forum and magazine project associated with Discover Kinbaku and Die Goldbomben. The official KSB site says the project began as a forum for studying kinbaku and the erotic / aesthetic ideas around it, then grew into a printed magazine with photography, poetry, fiction, essays, documentation, drawings and interviews. Discover Kinbaku characterizes the society as a forum with no formal membership, currently expressed via the KSB Magazine and Kinbaku Salon, and its magazine page frames KSB as a twice-yearly publication open to submissions and focused on Japanese underground culture, rope bondage, adult entertainment, aesthetics and Western reception. Nawapedia records the magazine as member-produced from December 2019 and rooted in a Studio 6x6 circle; Indiecon records it as an independent publishing project by international artists researching Japanese rope bondage and related subcultures. The KSB contributor page records Swen Brandy / Carnivore among contributors, and the official issue #4 page credits Carnivore for design. Die Goldbomben's current preorder page frames KSB Magazine N° I-26 as a 500-copy hand-numbered edition planned for the end of summer 2026, with original photography by Sugiura Norio.

### KK Tsushin

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/magazines/kk-tsushin/

Fact — KK Tsushin, 1952-1954, is documented as a Japanese magazine in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 1952-1954

Fact — Era: 1950s

Fact — Kitan Club-adjacent mail-order / subscriber publication issued in the early 1950s. Nawa-art characterizes KK Tsushin as a 20-yen mail-order publication running for roughly three years from 1952, while Kawahara names it as a club journal issued by mail to part of Kitan Club's subscriber base. J-DAC's Fuzoku Soshi commentary treats Kitan Club's KK Tsushin as a model for later subscription / reader-communication mechanisms in postwar SM publishing.

### Roca Magazine

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/magazines/roca-magazine/

Fact — Roca Magazine, 2021–, is documented as a Japanese magazine in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2021–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Tokyo-centered contemporary kinbaku print magazine edited and published by Kitaro Kasukabe via Studio Roca. Studio Roca's live shop says Kinbaku Magazine Roca began in 2021 and has expanded to five volumes with readers around the world; Kasukabe's 2024 Roca no Kai note frames the magazine as preserving and advancing kinbaku culture, supporting community growth and expanding from his photography into essays, columns, other artists' works and illustrations. The Roca #3 shop record characterizes the magazine as dedicated to disseminating kinbaku culture in Japan and worldwide, with contents such as Naka Akira / Shizuki Iroha / Sato Erisa photography and a Yugen Studio Magazine collaboration. The Roca #5 product page records a July 2025, 148-page bilingual issue with a special feature on Erisa Satoh, Yukiscream & Miya content and a Roca 2025 exhibition report.

### Schlagzeilen

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/magazines/schlagzeilen/

Fact — Schlagzeilen, 1988–, is documented as a German magazine in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 1988–

Fact — Era: 1980s

Fact — Long-running Hamburg BDSM print magazine and publishing line from Charon Verlag Grimme KG. The current official site frames Schlagzeilen as a magazine, publisher and shop rooted in Hamburg; current issue pages describe a recurring 100-page, full-color magazine with stories, photographs, classifieds, columns, comics, KunstWerk artist features, dates and groups; and the official jubilee page says `Vol. 1 von 1988 war unsere Geburtsstunde`. The official release notice dates SZ 220, themed `SM und Grenzen`, to July 8, 2026.

### Torsion Magazine

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/magazines/torsion-magazine/

Fact — Torsion Magazine, 2022–, is documented as an American magazine in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2022–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Current US-centered shibari / kinbaku art print magazine and community-facing submission project. The official home page frames Torsion Magazine as a publication celebrating the art of rope bondage, committed to artistry, craftsmanship, connection, community and consent, with editorial interest in history, techniques and artistic collaborations around Shibari & Kinbaku Art. Its official purchase page and MagCloud feed show a visible print run from Issue No.1 in January 2022 via Torsion Magazine Issue No.8 in December 2025, and the purchase page preserves sample editorial feature blocks such as Kinbaku Boyscout with model Sandy in Issue No.4. The official submissions page invites contributors to send image sets with release forms, bios and credits, while the Instagram profile characterizes it simply as `Shibari & Kinbaku Art Magazine` and names `@jcstarkphoto` as editor and staff photographer.

### Yugen Studio Magazine

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/magazines/yugen-studio-magazine/

Fact — Yugen Studio Magazine, 2022–, is an Italian magazine in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2022–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Milan-centered Yugen SM / Yugen Studio Magazine publishing line connected to Yugen Studio. The official Yugen Studio magazine category says the magazine features shootings made in Yugen Studio and cultural articles by Japanese and Western connoisseurs; current product pages show a recurring print format with shootings, backstage sections, interviews, viewpoints and original illustrations around kinbaku and its underground culture. SMpedia records Ema Kuroko as chief editor, Marta Tenshiko as editorial director and Federico Kirigami as shooting director. King Koala records Issue #0 in 2022, and Yugen Studio records product pages via at least #14; the official #14 product page and a 2026 Yugen Instagram post support the mid-2026 N14 continuation, with Ema Kuroko again credited as chief editor, Marta credited for editorial direction and Federico Kirigami / Rope Tales credited for art direction, shooting and cover-rope work. A separate Yugen product page records Tsubaki Kanda's concise text about Naka Akira, curated / edited by Yugen Studio.

### AMATSUNAWA

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/materials/amatsunawa/

Fact — AMATSUNAWA, 2014-, is documented as a German material in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2014-

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — German JBO-free natural jute Asanawa rope company for shibari / kinbaku. The first-party site frames AMATSUNAWA as jute Asanawa ropes developed for prolonged skin contact in Kinbaku / Shibari use and 100% mineral-oil-free, with mission / quality-control notes around selected jute yarn, labs, growers, mills and specialist rope makers. KOUMANAWA's 2025 report gives the structural connection: its managing-director history says Amatsunawa was established as a sole-trader project in 2014 after visits to Japanese rope manufacturers, included Asanawa analysis with Shigonawa Bingo at Bar UBU in 2015, moved to European manufacturing in 2017, and was later sold / left before the KOUMANAWA phase.

#### Reference links

- [website](https://www.amatsunawa.com/)
- [KOUMANAWA report 2025](https://koumanawa.com/en/report-november-2025/)

Fact — BondageRope.EU, 2023-, is documented as a German material in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2023-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — German rope-specific shop and material-information project launched in January 2023 from Shoga's previous shop. The official About page ties BondageRope.EU, Shoga Shibari and shibari.events to the same Holistio ecosystem, with a Schwülper legal address and Braunschweig-area scene context. Its catalogue and guide focus on rope for bondage / shibari / kinbaku, materials and knowledge; the public Instagram profile frames it as an EU-centered shop for bulk / treated rope, single ropes, sets and custom kits.

### Boutique du Shibari

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/materials/boutique-du-shibari/

Fact — Boutique du Shibari, 2020s-, is a French material in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — French shibari rope, accessories and education-adjacent material platform by Seb Kinbaku. The first-party site frames Boutique du Shibari as a shop specializing in Japanese shibari ropes in jute, hemp and rice straw, plus bondage accessories, safety-oriented guides, rope-care products, kits and bookable private classes / workshops. The project frames the shop as an extension of Seb Kinbaku's teaching practice, with Paris classes and resources for beginners via advanced practitioners.

### DeGiotto Rope

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/materials/degiotto-rope/

Fact — DeGiotto Rope, 2009-, is documented as an American material in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2009-

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — US handmade shibari-rope supplier and education-adjacent material infrastructure established by Giotto Shelton. DeGiotto characterizes itself as a Hockley, Texas home for luxury handmade shibari rope, home of whalebone jute rope and one of the highest-quality shibari rope makers in the USA. The site also hosts tutorial videos and private-lesson offerings by Giotto, making it relevant as material / education infrastructure rather than a lineage school.

### ESINEM Rope

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/materials/esinem-rope/

Fact — ESINEM Rope, 2010s-, is a French material in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s-

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Rope shop / material project tied to Bruce Esinem's shibari teaching and performance practice. The official About page explicitly connects the shop name to Esinem's performances and teaching, records his study with Japanese teachers, and frames the shop as a response to the difficulty of finding good shibari rope at sensible prices. The current shop frames itself as `Bondage and shibari supplies` for Japanese and western rope bondage, with jute, hemp, synthetic / cotton, semenawa rope, rope kits, shibari rings, treatment oils / wax and safety accessories. Instagram currently frames ESINEM Rope as a purveyor of fine natural-fibre rope.

### KoumanawaUK

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/materials/koumanawa-uk/

Fact — KoumanawaUK, 2020s-, is a British material in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — UK material-supply / distribution storefront for KOUMANAWA SHIZEN jute rope. The official SumUp store frames KoumanawaUK as the official UK distribution of SHIZEN jute rope and records treated / untreated 5 mm, 5.5 mm and 6 mm SHIZEN lengths, bundle programming includes and 2.5 kg untreated reels. Product pages describe SHIZEN as medium-loose-lay JBO-free rope for shibari / kinbaku use, made with premium 100% Tossa KI, while the delivery page says the rope is in the UK and dispatches in 2-3 working days.

### Langman POSH

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/materials/langman-posh/

Fact — Langman POSH, 2020s shibari-use trace, is a Dutch material in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s shibari-use trace

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Spun-polyester POSH rope by Langman Ropes, presented as a shibari material rather than as the whole Dutch rope factory. Langman's official POSH page frames it as a soft-grip, low-stretch, UV-resistant polyester rope originally oriented to nautical / decorative applications. Shibari Study's synthetic-rope guide names POSH as a Langman-developed rope adopted by the rope community and widely used for uplines because of its higher breaking strength and low stretch; Corneum and BondageRope.EU currently sell POSH for shibari use.

### Mayunawa (繭縄)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/materials/mayunawa/

Fact — Mayunawa (繭縄), 2020s-, is documented as a Japanese material in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Japanese handmade kinbaku rope brand and material supplier. The official BASE shop frames Mayunawa / 繭縄 as made-to-order rope for kinbaku, built from fine jute thread with a reverse S-twist structure, current 6mm / 7m and 6mm / 8m formats, and optional finishing. The official About page frames the project around safe, beautifully finished rope and also records the dedicated rope-cutting knife line 鉄鼠. Official Instagram, X and YouTube routes corroborate the live public identity, while Luna's 2025 Osaka event report independently places Mayunawa in a merchandise area as a handmade rope brand.

### Ogawa Ropes

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/materials/ogawa-ropes/

Fact — Ogawa Ropes, 1929-; shibari-supply trace 2020s-, is documented as a Japanese material in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 1929-; shibari-supply trace 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Tokyo rope shop / supplier alternate public name: 小川ロープ. Ogawa's official site frames it as a jute-rope and industrial-rope specialist established in 1929, not as a shibari-only brand. In shibari material infrastructure, Ogawa matters because current rope shops and education spaces sell, prepare, use or teach with Japan-sourced Ogawa jute for kinbaku / shibari use: NekoNeko sells untreated 6 mm Ogawa jute rope directly at its Belgrade studio, Shibari Study sells Berlin-prepared Ogawa Tossa jute via its shop extension, Anatomie / Shibari Store sells natural and dyed Ogawa variants as part of its studio feedback loop, and RopeMarks names Ogawa as one of the Japanese jute ropes he uses and stocks. It has direct studio / platform / practitioner stockist connections, not as a school or lineage entry.

### SAMMT

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/materials/sammt/

Fact — SAMMT, 1866-; shibari-use trace 2020s-, is documented as a German material in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 1866-; shibari-use trace 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — German rope maker Seilerei Sammt, active since 1866 and producing general rope goods such as hemp rope. BondageRope.EU and Shoga Shibari list SAMMT-branded jute / hemp rope products prepared or sold for shibari / bondage use. The page presents a weaker material entry than NAWAYA or ESINEM Rope because Sammt's own official site is generic rope manufacturing rather than shibari-specific; it is included only as a bounded material-infrastructure connection via current shibari stockists, not as a style, school or full catalogue expansion.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Polish shibari rope and accessory shop with a rope-art photography side. The first-party site frames ShibariArt.PL as a shibari store for ropes and accessories with handmade / custom jute-rope products, rope kits, rope jewelry, bags, cuffs, collars, gags, whips, floggers, posters and prints. The store page says its jute ropes are manufactured exclusively for the project according to its quality standards and inspected while orders are made; the public Instagram profile frames the shop as handmade jute ropes and accessories for rope art plus artist / photographer work capturing rope aesthetics.

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/materials/sirin-da-alkonost/

Fact — Sirin da Alkonost, 2020s–, is a Russian material in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Russian rope and wax-play material / publishing project associated with Dreadful Rabbit's birdsofjoyandsorrow channel. Public archives describe the channel as Sirin da Alkonost / Wax Play & Shibari and preserve articles on rope care, rope materials and wax-play materials; a public price-list post characterizes custom rope and candle making. The Knut i Pryanik brand page also records Sirin da Alkonost with wax-play candles and shibari ropes. The durable object is rope / wax supply and education around materials.

### Torinawa

Fact — Torinawa, 2020s–, is a Canadian material in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Toronto-centered rope producer / processor and supplier focused on JBO-free rope for shibari. Torinawa characterizes itself as partnering with manufacturers and hand-processing rope for shibari, carrying treated KOUMANAWA SHIZEN jute lengths / spools and Langman POSH. The project is an exclusive Canadian distributor of KOUMANAWA SHIZEN and a local Toronto rope-supply connection via Birdhaus availability.

### TwistedMonk

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/materials/twistedmonk/

Fact — TwistedMonk, 2004–, is documented as an American material in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2004–

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — Seattle-centered bondage rope maker and supplier active since 2004. Official TwistedMonk pages frame the project as a long-running source for hemp bondage rope, rope kits, bespoke silk / bamboo / wool fibers, steel accessories, gear bags and educational materials, with hand-finished production in Seattle. A 2010 Seattle feature places Monk's rope-making origin in the Center for Sex Positive Culture / Seattle craft-vendor scene and connects the initial hemp-rope practice to Midori's English-language bondage book.

### 17strockholmsyndrome

Fact — 17strockholmsyndrome is a Spanish rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · STUDIO CO-OWNER

Fact — Valencia-centered rope practitioner, performer and organizer using the public identity 17strockholmsyndrome. Their profile keeps the description simple: they do things with ropes, and they are listed as co-owner of Reisei Studio, one of Valencia's active shibari spaces. Reisei Studio has also credited 17strockholmsyndrome with organizing The Circle Vol. IV workshop with Floki. Other public traces place the handle around Spain and Prague workshop / performance contexts, such as Akai Ito Valencia, Rope Spirit XVIII, Madrid Ropes, Kinkoi Shibari and Flokiland.

### Aaron Blanchard

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/aaron-blanchard/

Transmission — Aaron Blanchard is documented as an American shibari teacher.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — SHIBARI TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Raleigh-Durham-area shibari teacher, performer and organizer behind Triangle Shibari. A 2025 VoyageRaleigh interview says Aaron Blanchard discovered shibari in fall 2022 and started Triangle Shibari as an educational initiative for accessible, safety- and consent-centered entry points into rope. The same interview characterizes private lessons and event performances, while the current Triangle Shibari link hub and Recess Yoga pages document beginner-to-intermediate lessons, private sessions, workshops and rope-jam programming in the Durham / RDU area.

### Addie

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/addie/

Fact — Addie is a Canadian model in the Osada / Western.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — MODEL · TEACHER

Fact — Vancouver-centered rope educator, switch, model / bottoming educator and self-suspension enthusiast. Works and teaches internationally with Barkas; together they established TheSpace2, Vancouver's first dedicated rope space, now continued as Studio Allegory, and teach a process-focused style centered on interaction, emotion and the person in ropes. Addie organizes workshops on dynamic self-suspension and versatile education for beginners and advanced students, and when co-teaching focuses on bottoming information and discourse. EURIX 2018 presenter.

Fact — School: Osada / Western

### Anastasia Afanaseva

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/afnseva/

Fact — Anastasia Afanaseva is a Russian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Russian shibari artist, rigger, teacher and performer using the public identity afnseva. Anastasia's current Instagram and Telegram surfaces place her work between Moscow and Saint Petersburg and advertise individual shibari sessions, photo shoots and one-on-one or couples shibari education. BRUXA's 2023 interview frames Anastasia Afanaseva as a shibari artist, discusses her practice via photography, trust, communication, improvisation, safety and aftercare, and credits her as the featured shibari artist. Independent photography portfolios by Nikolay Paustovskiy and Egor Kaneev credit @afnseva as rope artist / shibari master, while the Shibaru Fest 2026 schedule gives her a workshop and performance trace in Moscow.

### Ageha Asagi (浅葱アゲハ)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ageha-asagi/

Fact — Ageha Asagi (浅葱アゲハ) is documented as a Japanese model.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — MODEL · AERIAL PERFORMER

Fact — Japanese model, aerial performer, illustrator and writer active publicly from 2003. Nawapedia records her first show in 2003, a 2005 S&M Sniper / Yukimura-related magazine feature, a 2008 Vanilla Gallery solo exhibition, Fetish Japan illustration work depicting figures such as Akechi Denki, Nureki Chimuo and Yukimura Haruki, and later performance traces such as Sadistic Circus in 2019. Vanilla Gallery's 2008 archive characterizes her as a Tokyo-centered illustrator and aerial performer who entered the SM-show world at university age and created cover illustrations for Osada Steve's Fetish Japan magazine. Sadistic Circus preserves her later public aerial-performance profile, while TV Guide credits her in the 2010 documentary short Kinbaku / Kinbaku: The Art of Bondage alongside Osada Steve, Yukimura Haruki, Hajime Kinoko, Shuhei Kitagawa and others.

### Agustin Tentesion

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/agustin-tentesion/

Fact — Agustin Tentesion is a Spanish photographer.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — PHOTOGRAPHER · ARTIST

Fact — Barcelona-centered BDSM and shibari photographer / visual artist using the public identity Agustin Tentesion. Black Bible Books says he has worked in photography for about 25 years and places him early in the Barcelona BDSM scene via Club Rosas 5, Erotic Film Festival 2007, Fetish Weekend Barcelona 2011-2012 and the 2018 Invisibility exhibition. ShibariNews frames him as a widely known Barcelona-born shibari photographer, names Miradas, Shibari & Bondage and Tattooatados among his books, and explicitly says he collaborated with Jordi Lucena / Club Rosas Cinco to popularize the art while developing the Shibari Experience series. Codigo Nuevo independently characterizes him as a photographer with more than 13 years capturing moments between ropes and quotes Jordi Lucena in the same Barcelona rope-scene context.

### Aimi (あいみ)

Fact — Aimi (あいみ) is documented as a Japanese kinbaku model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — KINBAKU MODEL · PERFORMER · PRODUCER

Fact — Japanese model, performer and Aimist producer using the public identity Aimi / あいみ / @aimi_feti. Her public Linktree, Instagram and X profiles expose the current identity and contact trail around fitness, art and Japonisme. Piccadilly Osaka's July 2025 BENI x Aimi listing frames the pair as the performance unit 〼MASU and characterizes Aimi as founder of Aimist, handling SNS operation and event production while working as a gravure, kinbaku and underwater model. 2022-2023 public posts and catalog traces document the photo work KINBAKU-BI (Japanese SHIBARI) 365pics with rope by Marie Sauvage, Aimi as model and Enishi as photographer, plus a separate SHIBARI rope / photo collaboration with @Shibarijp.

### Air

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/air/

Fact — Air is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · EDUCATOR

Fact — US queer, disabled NB/trans rope educator and somatics-informed switch working as a self-tier, rigger and rope bottom. Air's own bio frames their practice via trauma-informed intimacy, intentional human connection, applied neurology and somatics, with about a decade of deep rope practice and national / international event experience. ROPECRAFT records Air as a Chicago 2023 / 2025 / 2026 presenter, and Air's own events page records Tying in the Tropics 2026 in Negril, Jamaica.

### Aisha Cruz

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/aisha-cruz/

Fact — Aisha Cruz is a Spanish rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Barcelona-centered shibari / autosuspension teacher, performer and artist from Andalusian theatre context. Tying With Friends 2026 frames Aisha Cruz as an Andalusian theatre actress, shibari performer and instructor who has practiced since 2018, blending theatre, dance, Japanese rope art and physical expression. Her public Patreon says she moved from Seville to Barcelona to develop her art and training, teaches workshops and private classes, directs shibari shows and organizes events. La Fundicion records the stage work Linea Roja by Manuela Gilabert and Aisha Cruz Ropes as butoh and shibari creation, and La Gabia TicketTailor records her 2023 self-tying / autosuspension workshop at La Gabia Studi. La Gabia's current atlas context records @aishacruz__ as a public contact lead; no founder, owner, lineage or certification claims are asserted.

### Akane (あかね)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/akane/

Fact — Akane (あかね) is documented as a Japanese model in the Yukimura-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — MODEL · PERFORMER

Fact — Japanese kinbaku model / ukete and performer. Studio Yukari characterizes her as working professionally as a kinbaku model in Japan since 2016 and collaborating with Yukimura Haruki, Naka Akira and Sugiura Norio. Shibari Lounge frames her as one of the best contemporary Japanese ukete, active as a model under Yukimura, recording kinbaku sessions with Naka and collaborating in kinbaku videos / photography with Kasumi Hourai. Rope Tales / Yugen Studio sources describe her as an important Japanese kinbaku model whose work attracts both semenawa and aibunawa audiences, with continuous Sugiura publication context and a 2023-2026 collaboration line via Rope Tales, Kirigami and Yugen Studio. Public European traces include Onawa Asobi Europe 2020 with BeShibari, Studio Yukari 2025 with Nuitdetokyo and Naoko, and a 2026 Kasumi Hourai / Akane weekend at Shibari Lounge. Door's model bio names Nomura Akane as a model influence.

Fact — School: Yukimura-ryū

### Akechi Kanna (明智神凪)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/akechi-kanna/

Fact — Akechi Kanna (明智神凪), born 1972, is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Kanna-ryū.

Chronology — Born: 1972

Fact — Era: 1990s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — Japanese nawashi born 1972 in Asakusa, Tokyo. Started BDSM activities as Mistress Kanna in 1998 with a focus on SM rather than bondage. First met Akechi Denki in 2000 and trained under him, debuting on stage by his side; characterizes Akechi Denki as their only master/shishō. Active as Nawashi Kanna from December 2010. Public Kanna / Kagura workshop biographies describe Akechi Kagura as Kanna's long-term partner, exclusive kinbaku model since 2006 and a crucial collaborator in stage performances, workshops, Kannawa Kinbaku salon and events. Established Kannawa-kai in October 2011 and presides over Kannawa-kai and Phantom-no-kai. Established the Kanna-ryū instructional lineage; first official instructor Raymond (February 2018), second Sparks (February 2020). On September 11, 2022, officially assumed the Akechi name as Akechi Kanna (明智神凪), with support of those connected to Akechi Kikaku and the Akechi Denki legacy.

Fact — School: Kanna-ryū

### Akira Nawa

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/akira-nawa-brazil/

Transmission — Akira Nawa is a Brazilian teacher.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · EDUCATOR · ARTIST

Fact — Brazilian shibari artist, educator and performer centered in São Paulo. Current official Akira Nawa and Escola de Shibari pages present her as a reference educator in Brazil, founder of Escola de Shibari / Espaço Shibari in São Paulo, and a full-time practitioner focused on performances, installations, photography and in-person teaching. An older official biography preserves the earlier Rio de Janeiro trajectory, saying she taught her first workshop in 2016 and opened Estúdio Karada in 2017 as the first shibari studio in Brazil; the current official services page places her private teaching studio in Liberdade, São Paulo.

### Akuaku

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/akuaku/

Transmission — Akuaku is documented as a Japanese teacher in the Ichinawa-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Tokyo / Nice rope teacher, performer and author also credited as Marc/Aku Aku. Anatomie Studio's 2022 workshop bio reports that he has lived in Japan since 2004, encountered shibari / kinbaku in 2006, trained via the Ichinawa rope group and graduated as a rope instructor in 2012. The current record clarifies that his primary class instruction in that period was with Otonawa and Shigure rather than Kinoko personally; Kinoko context is preserved as Ichinawakai teaching, installation and show collaboration via 2012-2023. His rope work is documented as sensual, connective and story/visual focused; he authored The Japanese Rope Kamasutra in 2020. EURIX 2019 presenter, 2022 beginner-course teacher and 2023 special guest.

Fact — School: Ichinawa-ryū

### Alberto Noshibari

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/alberto-noshibari/

Fact — Alberto Noshibari is a Spanish rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Barcelona-centered shibari teacher, rigger and artist, director of the Noshibari Art project. His work frames rope as contemporary research, teaching and artistic / psychological / bodily experimentation beyond purely canonical aesthetics. Noshibari Art characterizes a Barcelona collective project for contemporary shibari education, creative collaboration, sensory exploration and interdisciplinary work with performance, photography, dance and therapeutic contexts. Alberto's own bio notes years of classes with major Japanese and European teachers such as Yukimura Haruki, Naka Akira, Hajime Kinoko, Kazami Ranki, Osada Steve, Otonawa, Esinem, Nicolas Yoroi and Matthias Grimme, plus workshops / exhibitions spanning Europe; this remains inspiration / study context, not formal lineage. The 2026 Noshibari Art summer-workshop page names Alberto as the continuing instructor guiding open July workshops at Espai de Sants in Barcelona, while a March 2025 Amanita post records an Impromptu Ascension micromovement / creative-performance collaboration with Amanita. EURIX 2014 and 2015 presenter.

### Aleksandra Bor

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/aleksandra-bor/

Fact — Aleksandra Bor is a Russian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Moscow / Yerevan rigger, performer and teacher. RopeFest Peterburg 2026 says Aleksandra Bor was strongly marked by seeing Karol perform at RopeFest Moscow in 2018, and names Karol, Vlada Vedmovskaya and Salamandr as important guides for contact, sensitivity, technique, self-trust and attention to the model. The festival also records her in the master-class program.

### Aleksei Kalatsky

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/aleksei-kalatsky/

Fact — Aleksei Kalatsky is a Belarusian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Belarusian rope artist, performer, shibari teacher and event organizer based around Minsk. Nawapedia.ru characterizes Aleksei Kalatsky as a Belarusian shibari master who organizes events and master classes, makes ropes, develops shibari as a yoga practice and performs on stage. His own site frames him as a rope artist, performer, Shibari master and Nawashi; says he regularly organizes events, masterclasses and stage performances; and frames his public 'Getting to Know Shibari' lectures via yoga practice and safe, accessible entry into rope. The same site records Shibari events in Minsk, Brest, Vitebsk, Grodno and other Belarusian cities, and specifically says regular Introduction to Shibari lectures are held at RebootHome yoga studio in Minsk. In a current atlas context, Kalatsky names Ryhor Shabeka as his first shibari teacher.

### Alex Hitch
