Fact — Alex Hitch is a Russian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Moscow-centered Russian bondage artist and teacher. Nawapedia.ru names Alex Hitch as a Moscow bondage artist, and the Nawapedia.ru home page names Alex Hitch among contributors to the Russian shibari wiki. RopeFest Peterburg 2026 gives him a durable teacher trace via two current-scene participants: Shambler names Alex Hitch as his main mentor, while Norton Ognev records Hitch among the masters he studied with.

### Alex Kin

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/alex-kin/

Fact — Alex Kin is a Ukrainian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · FOUNDER

Transmission — Bali-facing shibari rigger, teacher and K-Shibari / Shibari.Bali founder using the public identity Alex.Kin / Alex Miklevych. The official K-Shibari site names Alex.Kin as Shibari Master / Rigger, names Alex Kin / Alex Miklevych in its public bio, links Telegram, Instagram, Facebook and YouTube routes, and says he has been immersed in shibari culture since 2015 and organizing international events and educational programs since 2016, such as forming the Shibari.Bali community and project in Bali.

### Alexander MA

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/alexander-ma/

Fact — Alexander MA is documented as a German rigger in the Naka-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Fact — Berlin-centered kinbaku rigger, researcher, educator and performer behind Discover Kinbaku with Natasha NawaTaNeko. Discover Kinbaku says Alexander and Natasha have tied together since becoming a couple in 2011, offer private classes / coaching in Berlin and international workshops, and established the Kinbaku Society and its Berlin magazine. Earlier public pages under the NawaRonin name place Alexander in the Warsaw / Poland teaching scene, where his official NawaRonin about page says he established Shibari Dojo Warsaw, operated it from 2015 to 2019 and resumed education work from 2023 after the Covid break. IKSK characterizes Alexander Madl as a researcher and kinbaku artist teaching traditional Japanese rope bondage in Berlin and spanning Europe. Alexander's official profile traces his rope turning point to New Year's Day 2008, then names a 2015 introduction to Naka-style semenawa via Scot Kinbaku and Namarié and intensive 2016-2021 study with Riccardo Wildties. Current Discover Kinbaku pages present his Elements System as a staged didactic framework for riggers, combining technique, philosophy, aesthetics, embodiment, movement, communication, limits and consent from first steps to advanced creative expression. Discover Kinbaku frames their work as rooted in semenawa by Akira Naka, with encounters with Naka Akira, Sugiura Norio and Miho Ikeda as key influences; Alexander's Bodies under Pressure concept is a semenawa / somatic-body approach formalized via workshops and a 2025 essay. Public Studio Kokoro listings for Discover Kinbaku use the name Alexander NawaRonin, which is treated here as an alias of the same Berlin / Discover Kinbaku figure rather than a separate person entry. Prague Shibari Festival Spring 2026 records Alexander MA and Natasha NawaTaNeko as presenters / performers.

Fact — School: Naka-ryū

### Alexey Poryadnov

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/alexey-poryadnov/

Transmission — Alexey Poryadnov is a Russian teacher.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — TEACHER · NAWASHI · ORGANISER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Moscow bondage artist, teacher, performer and Shibaru co-organizer. RopeFest Moscow 2023 records Poryadnov as a Moscow participant, Shibaru co-organizer and resident of Moscow sex-positive parties. A 2026 Por.Rope / RopeFest source gives the full name Alexey Poryadnov, says he came to rope via photography, received a base from Nikita RopeKit, continued developing via Russian and international influences, co-organizes Shibaru and teaches individually. The same 2026 channel trace also advertises a Shibaru meeting with open tying points, a performance, tea ceremony and social space. Kinbaku Yuga 2026 later records him for Embrace harness and Pole Acrobatic workshop blocks in Krasnoyarsk, and the festival afterparty recap names one of the afterparty actions as Poryadnov's.

### Alexielle

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/alexielle/

Fact — Alexielle is a British rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · MODEL · TEACHER · PHOTOGRAPHER

Fact — UK-scene rope switch, rigger / bottom, photographer and educator. Rope Podcast EP96 frames Alexielle via the Diverse Bodies in Rope class, focused on adapting rope for varied bodies, health conditions, disabilities, neurodiversity and communication. Anatomie Studio's 2022 advanced and 2024 beginners / intermediate Diverse Bodies in Rope listings name Alexielle as a facilitator with Little-Boy-Blue, and the 2024 listing also names Whiskey; together the listings frame their rope as moving from western-style rope toward traditional Japanese-style rope, transition-heavy suspensions and adaptive, sustainable tying.

### Alexis D

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/alexis-d/

Fact — Alexis D is a Greek rigger.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — RIGGER · PERFORMER

Fact — Athens-centered Greek rope / BDSM performer publicly documented as Alexis D. ShibariNews frames him as an early Greek public shibari figure whose Athens activity began after settling there in 2007. Booze Cooperativa's January 27, 2019 Athens Shibari event page repeats the stronger public-historical claim that Alexis D first presented shibari publicly in Greece in 2008 and gave the first performances and workshops for it there, while Art&Life and In2life independently preserve Alexis D within the Athens public rope scene spanning the 2012 festival and later House of Ropes / Athens Shibari context.

### Alfil Barcelona

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/alfil-barcelona/

Fact — Alfil Barcelona is a Spanish rigger.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Barcelona-centered bondage / shibari rigger, teacher and performer using the public identity Alfil / Alfil Barcelona. His official curriculum blog traces BDSM activity from 1989, serious bondage study from 1993 via Club Doma in The Hague, Madrid seminars with Dr Fatso and Mickael Kapoulas in 2002-2003, later Barcelona seminars with Matthias Grimme and Michael Will, Osada Steve private training in 2010-2012, and a 2012 Japan workshop period with Osada Steve and Yukimura Haruki. The same public CV says he taught monthly bondage workshops at Club Social Rosas5 from 2005 to 2007, while Cuadernos de BDSM #9 characterizes Kurt and Alfil as two major Barcelona rope specialists and places Alfil in Rosas5 seminar / dojo context. The curriculum blog also names collaborations with photographer Tentesion and a 2014 Barcelona Nawashi Kanna workshop organizer role.

Fact — RIGGER · PERFORMER

Fact — Lithuanian rope performer and rigger visible via the current LIMITS / LITEXPO public trail and Vilnius PAKILIMAS / Take-Off programming. LITEXPO's 2025 Limits Zone page frames Alfy as a shibari artist who entered rope over a decade earlier as a model and now works as a rigger, using traditional Japanese techniques and dynamic transitions for intimate performance; Resident Advisor also records ALFY in the PAKILIMAS 20.0% play-room performer lineup for a shibari suspension essentials demonstration.

### Alita Greenblack

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/alita-greenblack/

Fact — Alita Greenblack is a Chilean rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Chilean rigger, teacher, performer and organizer using the public identity Alita Greenblack, with public contact routed via the `@margaret.p.a` / `@alita.greenblack` / `@alitagreenblack` shell. Her public Linktree characterizes an autodidact dedicated to rope practice since 2017 and links classes / sessions plus Espacio de Ataduras and Tension Ataduras. Chilean public festival and media traces place her in Santiago rope / BDSM cultural work beyond social-only profile information: Convencion de Shibari 2026 frames her as a Chilean rigger active since 2017 who later refined her shibari technique with international teachers, Excéntrico coverage credits her as co-author of the 2023 BDSM short `A plena luz de dia`, and Canal Clandestino preserves a 2021 `Performance Shibari` screening trace. Public posts also place her as the lead recurring rope figure around Espacio de Ataduras in Providencia, while Shibari Study names her as a co-founder of Colectivo Tension.

### Alive Ropes

Fact — Alive Ropes is a Czech rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · PHOTOGRAPHER · TEACHER · ORGANISER

Fact — Prague-centered rigger, photographer, martial-arts / self-defence instructor and rope educator using the public identity Alive Ropes. His official Alive page names Alive Ropes as the founder and operator of Alive Kinbaku Dojo, an event organizer, the main teacher for the Bound Bodies Architect beginner course and a teacher for evening and weekend thematic workshops. The same public biography says shibari first caught his attention as a photographer in 2010 and that he began active tying about nine years later after workshops, then continued via private lessons and Czech / foreign workshop study. AKD and ShibariRigger sources place him as the organizer / realization-team lead of Alive Kinbaku Festival / Alive Onawa Matsuri, with workshop, photography and teacher context in the 2025-2026 editions. Instagram profile characterizes Alive Ropes as a kinbaku rigger, martial-arts instructor and photographer and links the separate Alive Photo route.

### Alma Ardor

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/alma-ardor/

Fact — Alma Ardor is documented as a German rope model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ROPE MODEL · WORKSHOP EDUCATOR

Fact — Berlin-centered art model, rope model and workshop educator using the public identity Alma Ardor / @almaardor. Her Instagram profile characterizes her as a Berlin art model whose work centers on smiles, ropes and bare skin, while Patreon frames her creator practice around photography, self-portraiture, shibari and life-drawing. Kinbaku Nexus's January 2026 Fly Strong and Dynamic Bodies pages list Gorgone & Alma as the workshop educators in Sabro / Aarhus and describe Alma as modeling in rope for over seven years, with dynamic and semenawa styles and a life-drawing background.

### Amanita

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/amanita/

Fact — Amanita is a Portuguese rope artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ROPE ARTIST · PERFORMER

Fact — Portugal-born, nomadic rope artist and performer using the public identity Amanita / @amanitaropes. She started rope in 2019 and has been centered in Barcelona since 2023. Her site frames the work as Rope Art and Ritual: contemporary shibari, ritualism, movement in restriction, contact improvisation, butoh, self-tying and self-suspension. Public performance and event traces place her work in Barcelona, Madrid, Berlin, Milan, Salzburg and Portugal contexts such as Waking Life, The Fool, Spazio Arteria, Sala de Luz, R.A.R.E Collective and Dr.Flow. Reference-supported collaborations and learning contexts include Alberto Noshibari / NoShibari, Stuart / @a.veces.cuerdas.3, Vik / @victor.shizen, Hajime Kinoko, VÆGABØUND, Tatiana Limati, Laodai, Riccardo Wildties, Lola Sapieha and Rüdger Schestag. Public Instagram names @amanitaropes as a rope ritual + performance artist, rope switch, self-suspension and movement practitioner. No certification, rank, deshi or formal-school claim is asserted.

### Amiya Bhanushali

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/amiya-bhanushali/

Fact — Amiya Bhanushali is a shibari artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — SHIBARI ARTIST · EDUCATOR

Fact — India-centered shibari artist, educator, performer and curator behind Silly Hands Shibari. Official Silly Hands Linktree characterizes the project as a professional rope studio practiced and conducted by @amiyabhanushali, with a studio in Bangalore and private-session / waitlist surfaces for multiple Indian cities. Homegrown's 2023 feature names Amiya as a Mumbai-centered shibari facilitator and frames Silly Hands as Amiya's independent project; The Established's 2025 article characterizes Amiya as a Mumbai / Goa-centered shibari practitioner and curator; The Nod's 2025 class feature characterizes Silly Hands as a professional studio dedicated to teaching shibari in India and says Amiya began teaching in 2023 after starting rope practice in 2021. Public media / culture sources also credit Amiya as the shibari artist for Karshni Nair's 2025 `Malapropism` music video and list Amiya among artists in `Spoken Bones Issue 02`.

Transmission — TEACHER · SELF-TIER

Transmission — Vancouver-centered rope teacher and self-tying / self-suspension practitioner using the public identity Amnesia Jane. Studio Allegory's organizer profile says they have worked with uplines since 2017, tie, are tied and self-tie, and now organize a recurring Self Tying with Amnesia Jane class at the Vancouver studio. ### Amrita (アムリタ)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/amrita/

Fact — Amrita (アムリタ) is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · MISTRESS · PERFORMER

Fact — Japanese professional mistress, kinbakushi / shibari artist and fetish performer active internationally from the early 2000s. Her official about page characterizes her as a Japanese rope-bondage / shibari expert born and raised in Tokyo, based between Europe and Tokyo, with world travel from 2002. Nawapedia says she studied kinbaku with Isao Take in 2000 and started international performance the same year; that study claim remains source record only because this review did not find independent public corroboration. LOFT/PLUS ONE's October 2001 schedule records Amrita in Takara Benio's Enchanting Rope Picture Scroll event, and Osada Steve's official chronology independently records a January 2005 joint performance with Amrita at Loft Plus One. Nawapedia's FICEB, Wasteland / TAIZO / COCO, SM Queen Road and AMRTA MAGENTA entries remain secondary selected-work / event background record.

### Amy May

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/amy-may/

Fact — Amy May is a Finnish rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Finnish rope artist, teacher, performer and organiser centered in Helsinki. Her own site programming includes shibari workshops, private tuition, private tying sessions and performance work from a private Helsinki studio, with an explicitly safer-space framing. Helsinki Shibari characterizes Amy May as a Finnish rope artist / teacher and one of the founding members of the Helsinki Shibari collective; City.fi's 2017 profile characterizes her teaching Japanese rope bondage professionally via Helsinki Shibari courses and frames connection and communication as central to her work. EURIX records AmyMay & Luisa from Helsinki as 2023 presenters and Julius Elo & Amy May from Helsinki as 2024 presenters.

### Anastasia Suazo

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/anastasia-suazo/

Fact — Anastasia Suazo is a Mexican artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ARTIST · EDUCATOR

Fact — Mexico-centered shibari artist, educator and performer originally from Russia, founder of Voudou Ropes. Her official bio says she began shibari in 2017 and has studied, performed and taught spanning Europe, Latin America and Japan, blending classical Japanese technique with a modern, intuitive and somatic approach. Via Voudou Ropes she programming includes online courses, group/private classes, photo sessions and retreats; the 2026 Shibari Getaway page credits her with almost eight years of shibari experience, three years teaching from zero, students worldwide, and 40k+ YouTube / 60k Instagram reach. Rope Podcast EP171 adds sold-out Mexico retreats and regular Playa del Carmen classes / events as current community infrastructure.

Fact — Andrea La quarta corda is an Italian rigger in the Naka-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · AUTHOR

Transmission — Italian Japanese-bondage rigger, teacher, performer and author known as La quarta corda. The La quarta corda site says Andrea studied with European and Japanese teachers, with Naka Akira and Yukimura Haruki in Tokyo as important influences for his move into traditional Japanese bondage and his personal kinbaku style. ### Andrea Rimon

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/andrea-rimon/

Fact — Andrea Rimon is documented as an American artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — ARTIST · EDUCATOR · ROPE TEACHER

Transmission — Chicago-centered artist, educator and rope / kinbaku teacher, formerly using the public identity ApikorosRope. Andrea's official site frames their tying and intimacy work via movement, culture, physical theatre, dance, intimacy work and gender / sexuality studies, while the Learn page programming includes rope-workshop style group classes, private lessons and tutorial structures. Andrea's official About page names Georg Barkas as friend, mentor and frequent collaborator who introduced Andrea to tying and provided much of the technical basis for Andrea's work. Karada House preserves a current organizer profile for Andrea and a 2026 `Kink is Political` discussion-facilitator trace with Barkas and Addie Tahl; Studio Allegory's QuIRKS 2026 programme records Andrea Rimon & Anoxia / Nox as Chicago presenters for Hammer Play and Character in Rope.

### Andrea Ropes

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/andrea-ropes/

Fact — Andrea Ropes is an Italian rigger in the Kanna-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Italian rigger, performer and international shibari teacher from Modena. His own bio says he began rope in 2006, first self-taught, then studying with teachers such as Nawashi Kanna, whom he names as his main teacher and inspiration, plus Yukimura Haruki, Hajime Kinoko, Bob RopeMarks, Otonawa and Shigonawa Bingo; Plume Noire additionally records Ren Yagami among his study influences. From 2015 he made rope a professional practice, teaching and performing spanning Italy and Europe; his style emphasizes dynamic movement, expressive performance, technical cleanliness, tension control and communication via rope. EURIX 2016 presenter, repeated BoundCon Munich performer in the late 2010s, Shibaricamp 2019 teacher, MoscowKnot performer, and BalkanBound 2025 presenter with AngieZiv; Plume Noire records Chiara Fenice as his long-term rope partner / workshop collaborator. A current atlas context from Andrea Ropes clarifies that he is not Andrea La quarta corda; the La quarta corda / Nawame project is covered separately.

Fact — School: Kanna-ryū

### Andreas Regner

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/andreas-regner/

Fact — Andreas Regner is documented as a German rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Strausberg / Ostbrandenburg rigger, teacher and Fesselkunstraum project lead. His official Fesselkunstraum profile characterizes him as an experienced rigger with passion for rope since 2010, a student of Seilartig, and inspired by Osada-Ryu and Felix Ruckert. Via Fesselkunstraum he helped organize Kinoko-related event / workshop contexts, such as the 2025 CANK Berlin Kinoko show featuring Marie Sauvage listed by Shibari.Events as organized by Maria Magdalena & @Fesselkunstraum. Via Fesselkunstraum he programming includes personalized shibari training, private sessions, performances and local workshops / learning formats; Strausberg-live listings independently preserve Yoga Lotos workshop traces in 2024 and 2026 with Andreas named as the public contact.

### Andrei Tanto

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/andrei-tanto/

Fact — Andrei Tanto is a Russian photographer.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — PHOTOGRAPHER · ROPE ARTIST

Fact — Russian photographer and rope artist also using the name Kinbaku Tanto / Nawashi Tanto. Public public-profile-centered bio and Nawapedia.ru both describe him as active in Japanese shibari / kinbaku aesthetics, rope practice and photography since 2010, first drawn to rope via the visual language of Japanese and European masters, with strong visual influence from the works of Naka Akira and Riccardo Wildties. He records RopeFest SPb 2015 and 2016, ShiFest Belarus 2017 in Vitebsk, RopeFest Moscow 2018, RopeFest SPb 2019 and the 1st Shibari Convention Chile in Santiago 2025 among his event traces. The same source trail says he was a regular participant of BondageCafe SPb from 2015 to 2019, organized private bondage apartment gatherings in Saint Petersburg, took part in thematic events spanning Russian cities such as Krasnodar, Rostov-on-Don, Omsk, Novosibirsk, Yekaterinburg, Kazan, Tyumen, Moscow and Saint Petersburg, and publishes short educational materials on rope practice and shibari aesthetics. Nawapedia frames his practice as Japanese-tradition-oriented, minimalist and focused on technique, safety, visual composition, emotional contact and atmosphere.

### Andy Buru

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/andy-buru/

Transmission — Andy Buru is a Swedish teacher.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · BODYWORKER

Transmission — Swedish, Stockholm-centered / nomadic rope teacher, bodyworker, writer and bondage artist. Combines Japanese and European rope bondage with theatre, medical massage, tea practice, bodywork and sadomasochism as lived research. Their own bio characterizes early rope learning in Tokyo in 2004-2005 with figures such as Yukinaga Max and young Yukimura, followed by the Berlin schwelle7 / Xplore scene. Established the neo-tantric rope approach in Scandinavia in 2010 via the School of Tantric Ropes. EURIX 2016 and 2019 presenter and recurring 2020s teacher.

Fact — Angela Nawa is a Spanish rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ARTIST

Fact — Barcelona-centered multidisciplinary artist, educator and shibari rigger. ShibariNews characterizes Angela Nawa's formation and classical training as taking place in Barcelona Shibari Dojo, with her teaching framed around both the visual / performative value of rope and the communication / emotion shared inside a scene. Beo Beyond's official site independently names Angela as the shibari artist behind the Blacklight Shibari collaboration, giving her a durable public performance-art connection beyond the directory-style profile.

### Anitta

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/anitta/

Fact — Anitta is a Danish rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Copenhagen-centered rope bondage teacher and facilitator working with Gabrielle in the House of Play / Copenhagen kink scene. Danish Shibari Festival's 2024 teacher bio says Anitta comes from yoga, meditation and Argentinian tango, has many years of experience teaching rope bondage to couples and larger groups, moved from Japanese bondage toward a freer creative tying style, teaches all levels and focuses on playful connection, movement, dance, depth and awkwardness. IKSK's 2025 Shame On You listing characterizes Anitta & Gabrielle as House of Play Copenhagen facilitators; House of Play's 2026 Shame On You page adds theatre, dance and therapist background. EURIX records Anitta and Gabrielle as 2024 presenters and 2025 special guests.

### Ann Antidote

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ann-antidote/

Transmission — Ann Antidote is documented as a German teacher.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Berlin-centered queer shibari teacher, performer and multidisciplinary artist using the public identity Ann Antidote. Daruma Berlin's official profile says Ann has taught and performed shibari from an unapologetic queer and diversity-centered perspective since 2010, develops rope-related work for film, theatre and photo projects, keeps classes trans-inclusive, body-positive and explicitly consent-centered, and names Dasniya Sommer, Arisue Go, Hourai Kasumi, Miumi-U and Felix Ruckert among selected teachers. Daruma's 2024-2025 workshop pages add current Berlin teaching context around suspension follow-up, moving larger bodies and chest-binding sessions, while Ann's public link hub and Bluesky profile provide the current independent identity and contact surface.

### Anna Bones

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/anna-bones/

Fact — Anna Bones is a British nawashi.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · MODEL · TEACHER

Fact — London-centered British shibari educator. Fell in love with shibari in 2013, met Fred Hatt the same year — tying partners since, married 2019. Co-established Anatomie Studio in June 2015 with Fred Hatt. A 'bottom who ties' and 'shy rigger' who prioritises teaching beginners and setting them off on a path to self-discovery. Teaches internationally and on the Shibari Study platform.

### Anna Kidd

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/anna-kidd/

Fact — Anna Kidd is an Australian model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — MODEL · ROPE PERFORMER

Fact — Australian model and rope performer using the public identity Anna Kidd / `@annakidd__`. Humanitix's 2024 Shibari Adelaide performance night bills `@Annakidd__` alongside `@Danydayze` as interstate performers for a night of shibari stage performances. Fox Lab Fine Art separately credits Anna as the model for a Dany Cox shibari print, placing her in Dany's collaborative fine-art shibari work. Public Instagram and Threads surfaces frame Anna as a model / bunny / spicy dancer, with the backup Instagram route preserved as contact continuity.

Fact — Anna Kii is a Russian rope artist.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — ROPE ARTIST · DESIGNER

Fact — Russian-born, London-centered designer and shibari / rope artist behind Figure of A, a wearable rope and bondage-fashion brand explicitly built around shibari-inspired design language. ### Anna Konoplova

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/anna-konoplova/

Fact — Anna Konoplova is a Ukrainian artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ARTIST · PERFORMER

Fact — Ukrainian shibari art performer living and creating in Vilnius with partner and model Tatsiana Ulasevich. In her 2024 ITSLIQUID interview, Anna Konoplova says she has studied shibari / kinbaku since 2016 with Ukrainian and European teachers, organized workshops, events, performances and a shibari camp in Ukraine from 2018 to 2022, and from 2022 onward has created performances in Vilnius, Kaunas and Klaipeda. The same interview ties her Lithuania scene visibility to Yaga, Pakilimas / Take Off Lithuania, Kinky.Pink and Karkle, while LITEXPO's 2025 Limits Zone page frames Anna-Valkirija as a Vilnius-centered fetish shibari artist and key figure in the local kink scene.

### Anna Lysee

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/anna-lysee/

Fact — Anna Lysee is documented as a German organiser.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — ORGANISER · RIGGER

Fact — Berlin-centered educator, organiser, rope practitioner, coach, rigger and model working at the intersection of embodiment, sexuality and community-centered education. Her own biography says she has been lead organiser of EURIX since 2016, took on xplore Berlin organisation in 2022, contributed to IKSK, and became involved in Death Festival Berlin in 2024. In shibari she has worked as both model and rigger since 2014, co-taught since 2015 and led her own classes since 2018, focusing on interpersonal, psychological and creative dimensions of rope and kink. IKSK records her as producer, rope performer and teacher coordinating EURIX and xplore Berlin and involved in IKSK organisation; EURIX records Anna Lysee as a 2023 and 2025 beginner-course teacher. Prague Shibari Festival Spring 2026 records Anna Lysee with Noemi as a presenter / performer.

### Anna Noctuelle

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/anna-noctuelle/

Fact — Anna Noctuelle is a British model.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — MODEL · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — London-centered shibari teacher, performer and practitioner with over twenty years of dedication to rope (discovered shibari over 15 years ago). Background in dance; Buddhist and meditation interests inform her aesthetic and philosophical approach. As a linguist, she explores non-verbal communication, especially in partnered work like tantra and shibari. A bottom-leaning rope switch who enjoys a wide range of tying — from floor-work to suspension, technical practice to playful and painful ties. Teaches at Anatomie Studio London since 2016; has performed and taught spanning Europe (Berlin, Prague, Athens, EURIX, etc.). Long-time collaborator of Saara Rei.

### Anoxia Rope

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/anoxia-rope/

Fact — Anoxia Rope is documented as an American rope artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ROPE ARTIST · EDUCATOR · ORGANIZER

Fact — Chicago-centered rope artist, performer and educator using the public identity Anoxia / Nox. Their official site says Nox teaches private sessions in a home studio, has taught extensively since 2019, and has presented in small community spaces around the United reports plus cons such as Westcoast Bound, Tethered Together, Kinkfest, Ropecraft and Desert Bound. The same site says Nox served on the NARIX steering committee from 2022 to 2026 and currently works at Shibari Study; Shibari Study's own 2024 team blog records Anoxia as Director of Marketing. ROPECRAFT's profile adds a West Coast rope start in 2015, multi-style study with educators in the US, Europe and Japan, and a class style focused on principles beyond pattern memorization.

### Antartica

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/antartica/

Fact — Antartica is a Dutch rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · PERFORMER

Fact — Amsterdam-centered show creator, pole dancer, shibari artist and private-session provider using the public identity Antartica. Her current House of Antartica site frames her as a pole dancer and performer centered in Amsterdam, advertises artistic performances centered on pole dance and shibari for private events, dinner shows, theater shows and festivals, and separately programming includes personalized shibari rope / BDSM sessions as Miss Antartica. Her public profile names her as founder of The Skin Deep Collective, whose official pages present an Amsterdam sensual performance-arts platform with shibari artists, workshops and shows; current performance traces include `Carnalia 2026 (with Luna)`, `Becoming all about love 2026 (with Qotton)`, `SDC House of Cards 2025`, `Supper on Tour 2025`, and `Diner Obscur 2025`.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — MODEL · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Brooklyn / New York-centered rope-bondage teacher, performer, model and rope bottom. Rope Podcast EP120 and Austin Rope Slingers frame Anya Demure as Marceline VQ's co-creator / model for inventive rope imagery. ### Aoki Junko (青木順子)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/aoki-junko/

Fact — Aoki Junko (青木順子) is documented as a Japanese performer.

Fact — Era: 1970s

Fact — PERFORMER · MODEL

Fact — Historical Japanese SM-stage performer and model active from the early 1960s into the 1970s. SMpedia frames Aoki Junko as Mukai Kazuya's long-term stage partner, the featured first model in Takashi Tsujimura's `SM Camera Hunt`, and a central receiver in early strip-theater SM productions that circulated via Kansai, Nagoya and Tokyo.

### Aram Oganezov

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/aram-oganezov/

Fact — Aram Oganezov is a Russian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · PERFORMER

Fact — Saint Petersburg rigger, performer and event host. RopeFest Peterburg 2026 says Aram Oganezov first encountered shibari as an event host, observing rope interaction before tying himself, and frames his practice via bodywork sensitivity, contact, partner response and rope as a way to preserve an embrace.

### Araneo Kinbaku

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/araneo-morphae/

Fact — Araneo Kinbaku is a French rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Lyon / Villeurbanne-centered shibari teacher, rigger and organizer. Araneo's official site says Araneo began rope in Paris in 2019, moved to Lyon in 2023, joined Coven Cordes first as a member, then as a free-practice facilitator and teacher, and has been co-president and active in the association's organization since 2024. The current course / session page records private lessons and sessions at Araneo's Villeurbanne rope room `La Toile`; Billetweb also records a July 3, 2026 Villeurbanne initiation by Araneo Kinbaku. Public Instagram uses the Neo / `@araneo_morphae` profile route and links back to The first-party site.

Transmission — Berlin-centered contemporary-rope teacher and rigger working publicly via Seilhafen. Seilhafen's current home and beginner-resource pages describe Arto as teaching contemporary rope bondage in Berlin, combining traditional shibari / kinbaku with queerfeminist ideals plus anatomy, psychology, mindfulness, communication and safety research. The same official profile says Arto rigs for performances, photo and video productions and gives workshops at kink and non-kink festivals, while the official ropeXchange June 13-15, 2025 programme records `Arto & Jessa` in the Berlin presenter roster.

### Asami Hime

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/asami-hime/

Fact — Asami Hime is documented as an American model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — MODEL · PHOTOGRAPHER · PERFORMER

Fact — Los Angeles-born, Barcelona / Tokyo / LA-centered half-Japanese model, photographer and shibari-model performer using the public identity Asami Hime / Asami / あさみ / @asami.hime. Her public Instagram and Linktree expose the `@asami.hime` modeling profile, `@himefotos` photography profile and stable public contact routes. KNOTY's 2025 Kasumi Hourai workshop and performance pages say Asami has performed as a shibari model for photoshoots, workshops and private sessions since 2016, began learning Japanese rope bondage and rope bottoming in San Francisco, and worked with Kasumi Hourai after meeting her at Kasumi's Nagoya studio. Later official listings place Asami as model for NuitdeTokyo's 2026 Yukimura-style workshop in Porto and for Kasumi Hourai's 2026 Madrid / Barcelona intensives and Shibari Soirée performances.

### Ash Snare

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ash-snare/

Fact — Ash Snare is an Australian nawashi in the Yagami-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — Australian nawashi centered in Melbourne. Began formal shibari training in Tokyo in 2015 under Osada Steve, and later studied with Hajime Kinoko, Akechi Kanna, S. Haruteru and, briefly, Federico Kirigami. Founder of Rope Dojo Melbourne. In 2023 became the only Australian accredited Yagami-ryu instructor after hosting Ren Yagami for a Melbourne performance; his formal current school context is Yagami-ryu.

### Asiana

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/asiana/

Fact — Asiana is a Dutch rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Dutch rope top, switch, teacher, performer and event organizer from Amsterdam. Asiana says she discovered shibari in 1996 via kikkou.com, began tying in 2005, was self-taught before taking lessons, initially tied more as a switch, and now mostly ties. Founder and principal teacher of RopeLounge Amsterdam, where she has run regular shibari classes and events since 2013; Prague Shibari Festival described her in 2019 as a well-known Dutch rigger who had been running RopeLounge for almost six years, and 2026 Hipsy course listings still place her as the RopeLounge teacher in Amsterdam. Her current atlas context records EURIX attendance / presenter / performer / beginners-course / masterclass roles spanning multiple editions, Ellipsis Rope Events performances in March and September 2016, Prague Shibari Festival 2019 presenter / performer, RopeLinked 2019 and 2024 presenter traces, and Onawa Asobi Antwerp 2020 performance context. In Amsterdam she hosted Otonawa & Tsubasa, Ren Yagami and Kasumi Hourai workshops / performances, and reports private lessons or classes with Arisue Go, Otonawa & Tsubasa, Akechi Kanna & Akechi Kagura, Naka Akira, Murasaki Haruan and Kasumi Hourai; these are listed as influence connections, not formal lineage. Collaborates with Door as the artistic couple Knot So / KnotSo and co-hosts the six-episode Rope Ramblings podcast; Door is described in the RopeLounge context as an occasional co-teacher / model, with Asiana working with a range of models as rope partners. A 2025 Volkskrant Magazine interview provides current Dutch press context for her public profile.

### Asimira

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/asimira/

Fact — Asimira is a Dutch rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · MODEL · TEACHER

Transmission — Dutch rope switch, rigger, model, teacher and photographer with current Berlin scene presence and earlier Rotterdam / Ellipsis context. Her earlier Asimira Iliac portfolio says she first encountered rope via a 2015 art-school graduation project, kept studying as both top and bottom, began modelling in 2016, and joined the Rotterdam Ellipsis organization as host, teacher and performer. Daruma's official profile frames her as a physiotherapist and visual artist whose rope practice centers playfulness, movement, touch, beauty and pleasure, shaped by her history as an illustrator, model and photographer. Current traces place her in Daruma Berlin guest-teacher context in 2024, among EURIX Spring 2025 presenters in Berlin, and as a Danish Shibari Festival 2026 presenter with Noxknot. She keeps separate public Instagram surfaces for rope work, photography and modelling.

### Astra

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/astra-ukraine/

Fact — Astra is a Ukrainian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Fact — Ukrainian rigger, educator and performer active under the name Astra / Astra Shibari. Shibari Camp Poland 2026 frames her as a Ukrainian rigger, educator and performer whose work is rooted in traditional Japanese aesthetics and whose path was shaped by seeing Naka Akira. Ukrainian event listings connect her to She.She.Shibari workshops in Lviv, Territory of Sensuality / Lion Kink events and Kyiv rope events; those listings describe 10+ years in shibari, Naka-style practice, 15+ years of teaching experience, and study with European teachers, plus a recent Naka Akira workshop. A 2020 Lviv article names Oksana, known as Astra, as a well-known Ukrainian rigger and co-founder attached to a sexual-health / body-practice studio context.

### Atrim Namor

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/atrim-namor/

Fact — Atrim Namor is a Swiss rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Swiss rope artist, rigger, teacher and performer behind Seilsport.ch. His current official site frames Atrim Namor as a Swiss school for shibari and kinbaku, offering classes, workshops, rope jams, private lessons, photoshoots and sessions; the current Atrim Namor Shibarischule page says his own `Atrim Namor Ryu` / `Der Weg von Resonanz und Harmonie` is presently taught in one-to-one form, with the Juku base in Oberbipp and an additional Biel option. The official about page says he had early rope experience before puberty, spent more than a decade in freestyle bondage before turning toward Japanese-inspired bondage, and studied via workshops with Umino, Seilschaften / Ibinuphis, Matthias J. Grimme, Barkas, Pedro Cordas, Gorgon, Michael RopeKnight, Kirigami & Marta Tenshiko, Ren Yagami and others. Public Swissbound sources place him among the concept developers / organizers of Swissbound and as a live performer in the first 2022 Bern edition, while current Doppelknoten pages identify him with Thomas Harukumo as co-host of the Swiss German-language shibari podcast `Doppelknoten`.

### Avalon

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/avalon/

Fact — Avalon is an Australian nawashi in the Osada / Western.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Australia-centered shibari / kinbaku teacher, performer and organizer publicly credited as Avalon / 123avalon. Humanitix and podcast profiles describe him as one of Australia's Japanese-rope pioneers, teaching since 2010 as a Sydney Rope Dojo co-founder, moving to Melbourne in 2015, opening his own dojo, founding Peer Rope Melbourne in 2017 and Peer Rope Byron Bay in 2020. The same public bios say he regularly studies in Japan, has been a student of Osada Steve since 2008, and received tutelage from Yukimura Haruki and Nawashi Kanna. Plume Noire's 2026 profile frames Avalon as an international teacher and connection between European rope practitioners and Japanese masters, with a fluid, sensory newaza / floorwork-centered style. Public event traces include Bound by Avalon in Melbourne 2018 and Studio K Shanghai / TIE UP! Guest-teacher context.

Fact — School: Osada / Western

### Barkas (春露)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/barkas/

Fact — Barkas (春露) is a Canadian nawashi in the Osada / Western.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · WRITER

Fact — Canadian kinbakushi, mathematical physicist, and bondage researcher centered in Vancouver. Became an Osada-ryu instructor in 2011, then was introduced to Yukimura Haruki in 2012 and studied directly with him until 2016. Yukimura gave him the Haru Tsuyu name and verbal encouragement to teach, but not a formal Yukimura-ryu instructor licence. Developed the "Interview Metaphor," "Ranboo," and "City of Kinbaku" conceptual frameworks. Runs Studio Allegory, formerly TheSpace2, in Vancouver with partners Addie and Emmy. Author of "Archaeology of Personalities — a Linguistic Approach to Erotic Rope Bondage."

Fact — School: Osada / Western

### Basanos

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/basanos/

Transmission — Basanos is an Australian teacher in the Kazami-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Australia-centered Kazami-Ryu teacher and performer in the Evil Girls HQ / Oceania line. Evil Girls HQ's official instructor list names Basanos as Shihan Dai, instructor and assistant to the Oceania head of school, and deshi of Miss Gen. The Evil Girls HQ Level 1 intensive page says Basanos began learning shibari in 2010, met Miss Gen in 2015, became a teacher in the Kazami-Ryu school in 2018, and took instruction from Nawashi Kanna, Hajime Kinoko, Hourai Kasumi and Kazami Ranki. Evil Girls HQ's 2025 cabaret page records Basanos & Angel as performers alongside Miss Gen and Kazami Ranki for the 10-year Oceania Kazami-Ryu milestone.

Fact — School: Kazami-ryū

### Bastet Yurei

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/bastet-yurei/

Fact — Bastet Yurei is an Italian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — RIGGER · ROPE BOTTOM · PERFORMER

Fact — Milan-centered Italian rope bottom, rigger and performer using the public identity Bastet Yurei and, in burlesque / stage contexts, Bettie Buio. A 2026 Tbilisi Collective interview names the same artist as Letizia, says she moved from southern Italy to Milan at eighteen, discovered shibari via the BDSM scene, has practiced rope with the same partner for about eight years, performs shibari publicly at times, and characterizes one of the styles she practices as part of the Naka Akira lineage.

### Bavragor

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/bavragor/

Fact — Bavragor is documented as a German rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Fact — Kiel-centered German shibari trainer, organizer and performer. AnuKan's seminar-leader profile says Bavragor teaches shibari workshops with Philia, shifted from earlier Western bondage toward shibari / kinbaku in 2015 via study at KlubNawa in Aarhus, has taught regularly in Kiel and Denmark since at least 2017, and has organized rope events from the Freiburg Tüdeltreff in 2013 and the Kiel Tüdeltreff in 2015. Tüdeltreff Kiel's official site names him on the current organizing / leading team, while LOFT Kiel and Dwarsdriewer pages place him in Kiel rope-performance work, such as the Flira und Bavragor project from 2019.

### BENI

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/beni-shibari/

Fact — BENI is documented as a Japanese shibari artist in the Ichinawa-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — SHIBARI ARTIST · PERFORMER · DIRECTOR

Fact — Tokyo-centered Japanese shibari artist, performer and director in Hajime Kinoko's Ichinawakai / ROPES orbit. Public Instagram confirms the BENI SHIBARI handle used for contact; Hajime Kinoko's official lessons page records Beni as a domestic Second-Class Instructor License holder, and the ROPES page records Beni among the group supporting Kinoko's artwork. A Piccadilly Osaka 2025 event bio says she began in 2019 as Hajime Kinoko's show model, later appeared in Art Basel Miami / Satellite Art Show context and performed in Mexico, Los Angeles, London, Melbourne and Singapore. KEYI Magazine characterizes her as a new-generation shibari artist centered in Tokyo, and BAKUKA / 縛華 sources identify her as the shibari / art-direction side of a shibari x ikebana project with Loyalty Flowers.

Fact — School: Ichinawa-ryū

Transmission — Hamburg-centered shibari teacher, organizer and photographer using the Benignus identity. Fact — Era: 1990s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · MISTRESS

Fact — Japanese kinbakushi, mistress and performer active in the early-2000s Tokyo SM stage layer. Nawapedia records regular SM shows at the Roppongi SM bar Mistress in 2001, an October 2001 LOFT/PLUS ONE performance under her own name, an International Award at the 2002 Pantheon of Leather Awards in Chicago, and repeated Europe performance traces in 2002.

### Benoit Descordes

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/benoit-descordes/

Fact — Benoit Descordes is a French rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — French / Paris-centered shibari teacher, rigger and model. A 2016 La Corderie Marseille workshop listing frames Marion and Benoit as guests from Place des Cordes Paris, with Shibartiste as Benoit's public route. Current course materials describe Benoit as practicing shibari since 2014 both as a rigger and as a model, and since 2024 making rope their main activity, teaching technique, risk, intention and the dynamics of rope sessions from a current Aubervilliers / Finistere course base. Earlier Port d'attaches event materials list Benoit among the four Port d'attaches teachers for suspension workshops. EURIX 2019 presenter with May Dosem.

### Bergborg

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/bergborg/

Fact — Bergborg is a Swedish rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Stockholm-centered rope teacher, researcher and writer working with Sansblague. Prague Shibari Festival described him as running the rope studio Kokoro in Stockholm with his partner Sansblague, teaching long-term study groups, thematic workshops, guided practice and private tuition, and giving historical Kinbaku Salon lectures. His practice is informed by contact improvisation and academic research in practical knowledge; via Kinbaku Books he shares historical kinbaku material and translations. EURIX 2014 presenter.

### BeShibari

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/beshibari/

Fact — BeShibari is a Belgian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Belgian shibari artist, performer, teacher and organizer, described by Shibari Lounge as one of the leading artists in the Belgian shibari scene and a pioneer of Japanese rope bondage in Belgium. Began exploring kink in 2000 after earlier inspiration from Japanese videos and SM magazines, organized RopeZensations peer meetings from 2006-2009, performed at the first London Festival of the Art of Japanese Bondage in 2009 and the second edition in 2010, hosted Go Arisue in Brussels in 2009, and established Shibari Lounge in Antwerp with Shibarista Jess. Performed at Onawa Asobi Tokyo in 2019 with Shibarista Jess and Akane, then organized Onawa Asobi Europe with Yoi Yoshida from 2020 onward.

### Bex Tatsunawa

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/bex-tatsunawa/

Fact — Bex Tatsunawa is a British rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Fact — London / Essex-centered shibari educator, rigger, rope model, performer and photographer using the public identity Bex Tatsunawa. Current official Shibari Lounge pages describe Bex as the creator and operator of an AuDHD-friendly rope studio and creative space in Leigh-on-Sea, Essex, offering workshops, rope jams, private sessions, photography, studio hire and community-led events near London. Her profile and booking pages frame the work around structured learning, communication, safety and intentional practice, alongside narrative-led performance, life modelling and kinbaku-focused photographic projects spanning the UK and internationally. EURIX XXIX records Bex among the Autumn 2026 newcomers in Berlin.

### Bimbo Daddy Lu

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/bimbo-daddy-lu/

Fact — Bimbo Daddy Lu is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Fact — Los Angeles-centered switchy rope person, educator and community member. Shibari Study's 2024 episode says Lu has been involved in the greater Los Angeles kink community since 2017, works spanning bottoming, topping, sex work and adult entertainment production, and found structured rope via Devil Mask Society. Threshold Education's Bound + Bondage listing says Lu started as a rope bottom in 2015, began learning to tie in 2018, completed Devil Mask Society's 9th Cell program, studied privately during lockdown, and teaches accessible rope education focused on all body types and fundamental principles rather than fixed patterns. MOCA's 2023 KINK OUT / EPHEMERA programme independently frames Lu as a Los Angeles shibari artist and educator in a sex-worker-community panel context.

### Blue

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/blue-voxbody/

Fact — Blue is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER · ORGANIZER

Fact — Bay Area rope educator, performer and founder of VoxBody Studio. VoxBody's core curriculum bio says Blue has been teaching and performing rope bondage since 2012 and opened VoxBody Studio in June 2017 as the Bay Area's first rope-dedicated venue. Shibari Study's Miss True Blue episode names her as a community leader, founding member of Bay Area Rope Exchange, rope switch and advocate for safer rope spaces and two-sided rope education. She has presented at ROPECRAFT, Bondage Expo Dallas, Desert Bound and Karada House's Queerope, and performed at events such as Folsom Street Fair, Twisted Windows and Vau de Vire Society productions.

### BondageOtter

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/bondageotter/

Fact — BondageOtter is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — San Francisco Bay Area queer rope switch, teacher and organizer focused on men-tying-men and masc-of-center rope. ShibariNews and Rope Podcast identify him as the founder of Rope Burn SF, a Bay Area men's rope jam / masc-of-center rope play party and skill share. VoxBody's 2025 event listing frames BondageOtter teaching Tying Men* with Purpose with Jay Campo, focused on tying the masculine form, larger or more muscular bodies, and connecting Japanese aesthetics with gay leather culture. Rope Podcast's 2025 interview adds that his practice draws especially on the styles of Naka Akira, Akechi Kanna and Nicolas Yoroi while remaining a community-organizing and teaching connection rather than a direct-apprenticeship claim.

### Bondagious

Transmission — Bondagious is a Canadian teacher.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Montreal-centered Canadian rope teacher and performer using the public identity Bondagious. Temple New York and East Bay Community Space event pages frame Bondagious with Thumper as a Montreal duo co-hosting a monthly GBTQ+ open rope practice for men who tie men, touring their `Exposure & Restraint` workshop spanning North American rope studios, and building a practice informed by Akechi Kanna-style rope bondage and performing-arts backgrounds. Queerope Berlin 2026 records Bondagious and Thumper among the facilitators.

### Boris Mosafir

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/boris-mosafir/

Fact — Boris Mosafir is a Russian nawashi in the Osada / Western.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Transmission — Russian nawashi, teacher, photographer and organizer; founder of Shibari Club Yugen and RopeSchool, creator of the ArtCo rope-event umbrella, and author of the Shibari.Guru online lesson / performance-archive project. Started tying in 2009, largely self-taught via workshops with Russian, European, and Japanese riggers. Received Osada-ryu certification in 2015. RopeSchool names him as founder and instructor, while the ArtCo / RopeFest Petersburg source says he created ArtCo to educate and support a careful approach to shibari via classes, festivals and rope evenings in Moscow and Saint Petersburg. Shibari.Guru's About page says its video lessons are based on the RopeSchool course and Boris Mosafir's additional author seminars, with performance videos from ArtCo-organized events and other shibari masters. Has organised seminars with Akira Naka, Hajime Kinoko, Riccardo Wildties, Bruce Esinem, and others. ShibariNews also credits him with organizing Russian events such as RopeFest, RopeDay, BondageCafe, NawaYoi, Fetish Faculty and RopeNight. Nawapedia.ru names Boris as chief editor of the Art Co-maintained Russian shibari wiki.

Fact — School: Osada / Western

### Braxas

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/braxas/

Fact — Braxas is a British rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Belfast-centered rigger, teacher and organizer publicly appearing as Braxas / Andrew Braxas. Belfast Kinbaku Dojo's official about page names him as a co-owner and says he first fell in love with kinbaku in 2016 at Kinbaku Dojo Berlin; it also reports that after traveling abroad to learn rope, he helped found Belfast Kinbaku Dojo in July 2019. Independent public event pages place him as organizer of a 2022 Belfast guest workshop with Anna Noctuelle and Nik YourKnots, as a 2022 Tying with Friends guest performer in Basel, and as a 2025 Daruma Berlin guest teacher with Neya. Daruma's own teacher bio says he began in Osada style and has been teaching bondage on a weekly basis for a number of years with an emphasis on technical understanding combined with emotional interaction.

### Briki

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/briki/

Fact — Briki is a Swedish model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — MODEL · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Stockholm-centered rope bottom, rope switch, teacher and performer publicly appearing as Briki. Official presenter bios describe Briki as a rope bottom and rope switch with deep knowledge of the body rooted in yoga and contemporary dance, working in recent years between performance art and the rope community. Her stated interests center on power exchange, bottomhood, objectification, suffering and the practical knowledge of caring for the body via rope over time. Public traces currently place her in a recurring workshop / presenter partnership with Tamandua spanning EURIX 2021, Zurich 2021-2023 and Prague Shibari Festival 2024, and Shibari Study's 2024 free-session roundup names Briki as Fred Hatt's model in the Predicament Session. Instagram profile confirms the live `@briki_666` public profile with a Stockholm location.

### Jake

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/browncoatjake/

Fact — Jake is an Australian rigger in the Ichinawa-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Fact — Brisbane-centered rope educator, performer and artist working with Laced as Nawa Musubi; Hajime Kinoko's official Ichinawakai roster records the same Brisbane / Nawa Musubi instructor as Jake Carl. Nawa Musubi's own site characterizes Jake and Laced as teaching Hajime Kinoko's Ichinawa-kai curriculum in Brisbane and offering workshops locally, interstate and overseas. Shibari Study's 2024 episode says Jake was officially certified by Hajime Kinoko as an instructor of his style in April 2018, and frames his teaching around model-aware communication, clean Kinoko-style line, adaptation and understanding why a tie works rather than only reproducing a form. Studio Kink records separate Kazami-Ryu study in Queensland and Jake's first-in-Oceania Level 4 Kazami-Ryu graduation; The current record treats that he studied Kazami-Ryu under Miss Gen and graduated via that curriculum, while framing it as an earlier stage before his current Ichinawakai affiliation with Kinoko as sensei.

Fact — School: Ichinawa-ryū

### ButterflyBondage

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/butterflybondage/

Transmission — ButterflyBondage is a Dutch teacher.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · COACH

Transmission — Dutch shibari teacher, coach and relationship therapist teaching as ButterflyBondage / Butterfly. Her official site says she has taught shibari workshops, masterclasses and private lessons since 2012, with workshops in Utrecht, private tuition in Amersfoort and teaching around Europe. Her approach focuses equally on technique and partner contact, with connection, intimacy and creativity as core values. The same site characterizes coaching work around relating, intimacy, sexuality, trauma, embodiment, conflict and emotions; her About page also records Japan study in 2012 with Osada Steve, Nawashi Kanna, Haruki Yukimura, Saki Kamijo and Otonawa, plus wider European study from 2011 to 2022. EURIX 2015, 2018 and 2022 presenter.

### Cait / ropespaced

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/cait-ropespaced/

Fact — Cait / ropespaced is documented as an American educator.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — EDUCATOR · STUDIO CO-OWNER

Fact — Southern Maine / Portland rope educator, performer and studio co-owner using the public identity Cait / ropespaced. Official Box Tie Studio pages identify Cait as one of the leaders of Box Tie Studio, say they have been active in the local Maine community since September 2014, presenting since 2016 and performing since 2017, and describe the Black Knot Rope Group period with Griffin from 2018-2023 before Box Tie Studio opened. Their official bio emphasizes rope education around health, safety, body positivity and bottoming, while the current Bluesky profile independently confirms the southern Maine rope-switch / educator identity and Box Tie Studio ownership context.

### Cannon

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/cannon/

Fact — Cannon is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — US rope-bondage teacher, performer and photographer based around the Ann Arbor / North American convention circuit. His official site says he has been fascinated by rope bondage for nearly two decades, has teaching and performance credits throughout North America, and takes primary inspiration from Japanese erotic rope bondage, kinbaku / shibari. As a teacher he emphasizes making rope bondage accessible, established and serves as chief instructor of Bondage Club Ann Arbor, and has taught large convention workshops since 2012 at events such as Shibaricon, Bound in Boston, Dark Odyssey Events and Bondage Expo Dallas.

### Caritia

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/caritia/

Transmission — Caritia is documented as a German teacher.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · ARTIST

Transmission — Berlin-centered kink and BDSM educator, artist, coach and rope/shibari teacher. Co-founder / CEO of Karada House and OhYesPlease, with more than two decades of personal and professional experience in kink, boundaries, empowerment and harm reduction. Programming includes Japanese-inspired rope workshops focused on foundational principles, adaptive practice, inclusion, movement, partial suspension, floorwork, health and collaboration. EURIX 2015, 2016, 2017 and 2018 presenter.

### Carnivore

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/carnivore/

Fact — Carnivore, born 1976, is documented as a German photographer.

Chronology — Born: 1976

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — PHOTOGRAPHER · FILMMAKER · KINK EDUCATOR

Fact — Berlin-centered kink photographer, visual artist, filmmaker and educator, alternate public name: Swen Brandy / CarnivorePic. FilmFreeway records him as a photographer / producer / artist born in 1976, with PURE PROJECT - Kink Documentary established in 2020 and My Element Water as a short documentary work; the KSB contributor page characterizes him as a Stuttgart / Berlin media artist, photographer, graphic designer, art director, art filmmaker, PURE Kink Documentary co-founder and kink educator focused on play-shooting and bodywork. In rope-facing sources, Carnivore appears via photography / film / education connections rather than lineage: KSB Magazine design / contributor work, workshops and screenings with Saara Rei at InFLUX and Kinbaku Lounge Copenhagen, a Danish Shibari Festival 2025 presenter trace, and a Shibari Haven / Shibari Retreat Poland 2026 teacher / presenter role around non-verbal communication, sensual play, documentary kink photography and body / mind challenges.

### Cat Mason

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/cat-mason/

Fact — Cat Mason is documented as a German rigger.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — RIGGER · PHOTOGRAPHER

Fact — Stuttgart-centered rigger, photographer, rope model and educator whose work combines shibari with art photography. Mason characterizes this approach as Romantic Bondage, centering the partner's comfort, emotion and sense of being held rather than conventional BDSM framing. Her official project guide frames more than twenty years of rope and photography experience and programming includes individual and group coaching, workshops, shows and commissioned shoots. Stuttgart-Schwarz separately records her long-running event photography and flyer work for the Our Darkness party series.

### Cath After Dark

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/cath-after-dark/

Fact — Cath After Dark is a Spanish model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — MODEL · PERFORMER

Fact — Barcelona / Spain rope bottom, model and performer publicly appearing as Cath After Dark. An official Köieparadiis event page for February 10, 2024 names `Cath After Dark (@cath_after_dark)` as Saara Kantanen's professional model, characterizes Cath as from Barcelona, and says she had taken part in many photoshoots and workshops before that first Estonia appearance. The official EURIX archive separately records `Shibari Sari & Cath After Dark` among Autumn 2023 presenters in Berlin, and Yugen Studio's public YSM N14 announcement credits `cath_after_dark` in the issue's shooting / model context. X profile further exposes the live `@Cathafterdarkk` account with the public bio line `Rope bottom centered in Spain, lover of semenawa`, while a public Telegram invite page confirms the current `Cath After Dark` channel.

### Ceci Ferox

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ceci-ferox/

Transmission — Ceci Ferox is a Finnish teacher.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · ARTIST

Transmission — Finnish-origin, Berlin-centered queer sexologist, kink activist, sex therapist and rope teacher. Organizes rope events and has taught shibari since 2014; teaches Japanese-inspired rope bondage, BDSM and sexuality at Karada House and internationally from top, bottom and switch perspectives. Their work centers safer spaces, empowerment, communication, body diversity and kink education. EURIX 2017 and 2019 presenter with floZif.

### Charles Ilcoatl

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/charles-ilcoatl/

Fact — Charles Ilcoatl is a Mexican rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ARTIST · PERFORMER

Fact — Mexico City shibari educator, rigger, performer and multidisciplinary artist. Official Arte Shibari sources present Charles as the operator of a local project offering private classes, SHIBARI LAB group pedagogy, personalized sessions, media output and performance work. His public profile combines martial-arts, somatic, movement and ritual approaches to rope, while the classes page places his in-person teaching base in Centro Historico, CDMX and the performance page frames him as a live shibari / aerial / theatrical artist.

Fact — Japanese kinbakushi from Kyoto Prefecture active in public rope / SM performance from the 1990s. ### Nureki Chimuo (濡木痴夢男)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/chimuo-nureki/

Transmission — Nureki Chimuo (濡木痴夢男), 1930–2013, is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Nureki lineage.

Chronology — Born: 1930

Chronology — Died: 2013

Fact — Era: 1970s

Transmission — NAWASHI · WRITER · TEACHER

Fact — Japanese kinbakushi, photographer, writer and editor born Iida Hoichi in 1930 in Asakusa, Tokyo. One of the first grandmasters of Japanese rope bondage. Drew from earlier masters such as Ito Seiu; rose to prominence via postwar SM publishing, such as Kitan Club, Uramado and Kubo Shoten editorial work, then via magazine bondage photography and video production from the 1970s onward. Established Kinbiken in 1985 as a regular meeting group focused on kinbaku practice, photography and instruction. Subsequent historical analysis treats his own recollections as essential but sometimes unreliable testimony, so broad historical claims around him stay source-critical. At his death in 2013 left only one authorised deshi, Naka Akira, to carry on his legacy.

Transmission — School: Nureki lineage

### Chris Be

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/chris-be/

Fact — Chris Be is documented as a German nawashi in the Yagami-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — German nawashi, teacher and performer centered in Munich. Ren Yagami's official instructor profile says Chris Be spent years studying with different teachers spanning Europe, started teaching and performing shibari in 2021, has since studied consistently under Ren Yagami, earned the Shoden certification in Yagami-ryu in 2025, and became an official instructor in 2026. That record says he teaches regularly at his shibari dojo in Munich, programming includes workshops and private tuition, and draws on a martial-arts background plus years running a ninjutsu dojo to shape a communication-led style built via subtle invitations rather than forced control. Shigetsu Shibari Dojo Munich's official about page independently places him in the current dojo team and Yagami-ryu teaching context.

Fact — School: Yagami-ryū

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Fact — Western Australia shibari educator, performer, rigger and self-tier using the public identity Cinnamon / Cinnamon_Living. Humanitix's Shibari 101 listing and host profile describe Cinnamon as a shibari student, educator and performer with years of experience as a rigger, bunny and self-tier, and frame the Morley class around rope safety, foundational knots, beginner harnesses, connection and play. Public Instagram frames `@cinnamon_living` with Australia / WA context and `Shibari | Connection | Art` wording, while the public Patreon route frames the project around shibari, inclusivity and self-love.

### Claire Adams

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/claire-adams/

Fact — Claire Adams is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — RIGGER · DIRECTOR · ROPE MAKER

Fact — US-centered Western bondage rigger, director, BDSM educator, rope maker and model in the Kink.com / Intersec adult-media orbit. Dark Odyssey characterizes her as a professional bondage rigger, model, director and rope maker active in professional and private BDSM community contexts since 2001. Vintage Rope names her as Damon Pierce's co-creator of the rope-making project, while AVN separately frames her Hogtied / Device Bondage director role around rope mechanics and BDSM education.

### Clover Brook

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/clover-brook/

Fact — Clover Brook, born 1986, is an Irish model.

Chronology — Born: 1986

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — MODEL · PHOTOGRAPHER

Fact — Ireland-born, England-centered kinbaku model, photographer, rope-bottom educator and long-term WykD Dave collaborator. Nawapedia characterizes her as Dave's partner, producing kinbaku photo shoots with him from 2010, publishing The Rope Bottom Guide in 2011, organizing LFRA in 2015, studying in Tokyo with Yukimura Haruki and Naka Akira in 2016, opening Studio Kokoro in Mansfield with Dave in 2019 and publishing TEN in 2020. Studio Kokoro's resources page keeps The Rope Bottom Guide visible as a free rope-bottom education resource translated into 14 languages.

### Coach (コーチ)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/coach/

Transmission — Coach (コーチ), born 1963, is documented as a Japanese kinbaku teacher.

Chronology — Born: 1963

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — KINBAKU TEACHER · PERFORMER · BAR OPERATOR

Transmission — Yokohama-born Japanese kinbaku teacher, performer and Ueno Fetish Bar Curiouss master using the public identity Coach / コーチ. Onawa Asobi Europe says he began learning bondage on his own around 2006, worked in charge of bondage at Yokohama Apollon and Zone from around 2007, later managed Ikebukuro SM bar Ecole and organized bondage classes, and performed in Onawa Asobi Osaka / Tokyo / Europe plus Thailand and Taiwan contexts. Current Curiouss pages and Bluesky profiles present him as the master of the Ueno bar, a semenawa-focused SMer / performer, and the teacher for monthly Coach-nawa classes at Curiouss.

### CoCo Katsura

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/coco-katsura/

Fact — CoCo Katsura is documented as a Japanese rope performer.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — ROPE PERFORMER · WORKSHOP TEACHER

Fact — Tokyo-born, Berlin-centered shibari and performance artist using the public identity CoCo Katsura. Her official site names her as a Japanese rope-bondage, fire and body-suspension performer, installation artist and shibari workshop teacher; it says she began body-modification performance in Tokyo's underground scene in 2000, moved to Berlin in 2011 and has performed on more than 100 stages internationally. ShibariNews independently profiles her as a choreographer, installation artist, workshop master and rope-bondage performer, while current host-side pages for Como Tattoo Convention and BMXnet name her in shibari performance contexts, such as the CoCo Katsura & Shimo `Scarecrow` shibari / butoh performance. Fresh RopeBerry sources identify CoCo as the shibari master and original co-creator of that Berlin butoh / shibari physical-theatre project, with host-side MaHalla and Expedition Metropolis pages documenting 2024-2025 RopeBerry performance editions.

### Conor Aphilia

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/conor-aphilia/

Fact — Conor Aphilia is a British rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — RIGGER · EDUCATOR · STUDIO OWNER

Fact — London-centered shibari practitioner, instructor and Aphilia Studio owner. Conor's official London Shibari site frames him as a London-centered shibari practitioner and instructor offering private sessions, tuition, workshops and portfolio work; the about page says rope entered his life in 2016 after he arrived in London in 2014. Aphilia Studio's private-class page names him as Studio Owner and says he has taught over 200 private classes in four years as a full-time educator, while Feeld's 2026 guide independently names him as a shibari practitioner and instructor of Aphilia Studio. Generic wording about studying Japanese masters is presented as biography context only; no lineage, school, deshi, certification or named study relationship is asserted.

### Cordafortis

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/cordafortis/

Fact — Cordafortis is a Swiss rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Swiss / Lausanne rigger, teacher and community organizer using the public name Cordafortis. Encordages Lémaniques' official team page says he began rope in 2015, trains with sweet_shiver, and regularly develops via workshops; the same page explicitly names Docvale and Kirigami in his study path and says those encounters opened Akira Naka's stripped-back style to him, while Osada Steve is cited as an admired model-connection reference point. A current atlas context names him as the Lausanne-centered rigger / teacher running Encordages Lémaniques with his partner sweet_shiver. The same context and official site context support Encordages as their 2017-established Suisse Romande rope community / dojo. A public BDSM.fr Munch de Genève listing separately names Cordafortis among the organizers, providing narrow external corroboration outside the Encordages site.

Fact — US-centered Western bondage rigger, rope artist, BDSM educator, rope maker and adult-media director / producer. ### Dan Carabas

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/dan-carabas/

Fact — Dan Carabas is documented as a German artist.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — ARTIST · TEACHER

Fact — Romanian-born, Berlin-centered multidisciplinary artist, photographer, shibari artist and instructor. Encountered shibari after relocating from Paris to Berlin and discovering Felix Ruckert's schwelle7; first teacher was Nicolas Yoroi, later drew influence from Felix Ruckert's contemporary shibari and studied with Hajime Kinoko. Established Shibari Studio Berlin in 2024, teaching an intentional, intimate approach to rope; EURIX 2026 presenter together with Dorcara.

### Dancehalldyke

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/dancehalldyke/

Fact — Dancehalldyke is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · MODEL · TEACHER

Transmission — San Francisco Bay Area rope artist, switch, teacher and performer alternate public name: Kit / Juno Sloane. Shibari Study frames Dancehalldyke and slab_me_harder as a San Francisco lesbian rope couple focused on risk, negotiation and debriefing. The Kink Store SF characterizes Juno Sloane / Dancehalldyke and Sluttyhands as Bay Area rope teachers whose rope is influenced by traditional Japanese rope, particularly Barkas and Addie. VoxBody's 2023 event records Kit as a San Francisco-centered switch performing with A-Nicolas, and Twisted Windows records Dancehalldyke among its troupe performers.

### Dany Cox

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/dany-cox/

Fact — Dany Cox is an Australian rope artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ROPE ARTIST · PERFORMER

Fact — Perth-centered Australian rope artist and performer using the public identity Dany Cox / `@danydayze`. X-Press Magazine names Cox as a Perth shibari artist returning to Fringe World in 2024 with `Tethered`, after earlier `Theatre of Bondage` and `Bound to You` performances, and its 2025 review names Miiko Rose as Dany's performance partner for a later `Tethered` staging. Humanitix's `Melt - A Shibari Ritual` host and event pages describe Dany as a Perth-centered rope artist blending performance, ritual and relational awareness and frame Dany / Miiko as a duo performing and creating together. The Bindery's 2024 Humanitix workshop listings separately bill Dany Dayze as demo top for Himboy's Rope Communication 101 / 102 workshops, while Shibari Adelaide's 2024 listing bills `@Danydayze` and `@Annakidd__` as interstate performers for a night of shibari stage performances. The current Linktree preserves 2026 travel / booking context for shoots, lessons, sessions, performances and workshops, routes upcoming workshops via On Sacral Ground, and records Perth rope resources such as Ignition Perth; a public Ignition recap separately names `@danydayze` among the people who lit up its Down the Rabbit Hole event. Fox Lab Fine Art independently frames Dany as a visual artist whose shibari work is available as fine-art prints.

### J / Darling Divine

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/darling-divine/

Fact — J / Darling Divine is an Australian organizer.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ORGANIZER · SOMATIC EDUCATOR

Fact — Perth / Western Australia organizer and somatic sex educator using the public identity J / Darling Divine. Public Instagram and Substack surfaces connect the `@darling_divine__` and `@somaticsexwithj` identities, while the Humanitix Darling Divine host profile frames the project as creating spaces and events for connection via the art of Kinbaku. The recurring Chaotic Erotic listings at Enclave bill Darling Divine as host and frame the series as Perth shibari / kink performance nights; Vol. 3 separately characterizes Chelsea and J as local artists making rope performance work together since a 2022 rope jam. Humanitix workshop listings support a broader consent-led somatic / body-education practice in Kardinya / WA, and Himboy's 2025 Perth trip listing names a private Darling Divine rope space in Kardinya among its rope-class venues.

### Dasniya Sommer

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/dasniya-sommer/

Transmission — Dasniya Sommer is documented as a German teacher.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — TEACHER · PERFORMER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Berlin dancer, choreographer and shibari teacher who co-established schwelle7 with Felix Ruckert. The venue's official 2007 programme records her weekly Bondage Jam and a Japanese Bondage for Beginners course from July 2007; a 2008 programme continues the resident Japanese-bondage teaching trace. She later studied with Norio Sugiura, Kasumi Hourai, Akira Naka, Chanta Rose, Kamijoo Saki, Tamandua, Gestalta and Arisue Go, and teaches at Haus Sommer and EURIX.
