### Davide La Greca

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/davide-la-greca/

Fact — Davide La Greca is an Italian kinbakushi in the Yukimura-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — KINBAKUSHI · TEACHER

Transmission — Italian kinbakushi, teacher, performer and author whose public alias is MaestroBD. His official bio says he first approached rope in 1990, was teaching shibari by 2004, studied from books and early online video material from 2001, travelled to London in 2010 to learn in person, and in 2015 found his main path in Tokyo as a Yukimura Haruki student before becoming a certified instructor in 2016. Shibari Dojo Italy says the first school began operating in Rome in 2012 and later grew into a network of dojos in Bologna, Milano, Pavia, Toscana, Veneto and Brescia, directly managed by Davide La Greca / MaestroBD with Lucio Pat Foster, with online and private lessons plus a structured five-level curriculum and safety-course / test pattern. Kinbaku Today's 2016 interview adds early Go Arisue / Midori / Nobuyoshi Araki visual and book influences, Nuit de Tokyo's mentor / Tokyo-scene introduction role, a 2014 post-Yukimura turning point, 2011 London encounters with Hajime Kinoko and Shigonawa Bingo, and a Tokyo Onawa Asobi stage trace. Public event traces also document EURIX II 2013, BoundCon Munich 2017, Onawa Asobi Europe 2020, Shanghai Ex Studio sessions, a BoFeWo Frankfurt/Main show and Obscene 2026 in Karlsruhe; BoFeWo's performer profile adds general Europe / Japan / United reports study and teaching context.

Fact — School: Yukimura-ryū

### Akechi Denki (明智伝鬼)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/denki-akechi/

Transmission — Akechi Denki (明智伝鬼), 1940–2005, is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Akechi lineage.

Chronology — Born: 1940

Chronology — Died: 2005

Fact — Era: 1980s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Transmission — Central figure of the late-Shōwa / early-Heisei kinbaku boom and founder of the Akechi-ryū lineage. In 1978 met Sakurada Denjirō and Shin Sakurada at GSG Planning; in 1985 took over Sakurada's stage shows. Opened Studio Phantom in Shibuya in 1987 for photo sessions and SM experimental kinbaku theatre. Established Bakuyukai with Takumi Miura in 1996. Public evidence supports Akechi Kanna as a direct student / Akechi-style successor context, while Saikatsu / Kazami accounts identify Raika, Enka and Kadera as Akechi's formal women students, while Saikatsu / Kazami accounts frame Saikatsu as assistant and friend rather than deshi. His work shaped later Akechi-style transmission via Kanna-linked teachers such as Ren Yagami and influenced Hajime Kinoko's early bar-period workshop exposure.

Transmission — School: Akechi lineage

### Dercein

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/dercein/

Fact — Dercein is a Chilean photographer.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — PHOTOGRAPHER · RIGGER · TEACHER

Fact — Chilean photographer, rigger and BDSM / shibari educator using the public identity Dercein / @dercein.art in Uruguay. His official bio names him as Adolfo Dercein, characterizes BDSM practice beginning around 2014, shibari arriving while he was living in Uruguay around 2019, a first 2020 workshop with the local Uruguay scene, and later real shibari sessions, photography and performance context. His official beginner-workshop page frames current basic shibari teaching with safety, consent, aftercare, materials, Uruguay community and international-reference sections, while El Pais Uruguay's 2024 article profiles Adolfo "Dercein" as a Chilean expert in BDSM and shibari centered in Uruguay and leading a public workshop. A public Shibari Uruguay post for Jam Agosto 24 credits @dercein.art in the rope / rigger slot, supporting a bounded 2024 jam appearance bridge only.

Transmission — Thailand-centered shibari artist, performer and teacher using the public identity Destroy Angel. The official DESTROYANGEL.COM home page says Destroy Angel has been exploring and mastering shibari for over six years, programming includes live shibari performances, private rope sessions and one-on-one classes, and runs a personal shibari studio and bar. ### Diana Vera

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/diana-vera/

Fact — Diana Vera is a Czech rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Prague-centered rigger and shibari teacher. Her official Czech bio says she began tying in 2014; her homepage and Prague Shibari Festival bio frame her current work around workshops, private instruction, conscious rope, dominance, women-only circles, bodywork / touch-training and rope sessions. Her own bio names Kristina Marlen, Soptik, Barkas and Pedro Cordas as especially important influences. Prague Shibari Festival Spring 2026 presented her Wheel of Consent in Shibari workshop.

### DirtyArchangel

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/dirty-archangel/

Fact — DirtyArchangel is documented as an American photographer.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — PHOTOGRAPHER · ARTIST

Fact — New York-centered rope / BDSM photographer and artist associated with the NYC Street Shibari team. The official NYC Shibari project page characterizes NYC Street Shibari as a collaboration between rope artists Sam Jay and Zero-Gi and photographer DirtyArchangel, with public / street shibari photo work in New York, magazine features and Jade Rope photo-competition recognition. ITSLIQUID's 2023 interview frames him as a published photographer specializing in erotic and BDSM photography who was then mainly focused on shibari / kinbaku performance work, while Bushwick Gallery's 2025 artist profile places his current gallery-visible work around power, vulnerability, transformation and masculinity within shibari.

### Dirty Kudo (ダーティ工藤)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/dirty-kudo/

Fact — Dirty Kudo (ダーティ工藤), born 1954, is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi.

Chronology — Born: 1954

Fact — Era: 1990s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · DIRECTOR

Fact — Japanese kinbakushi, director, producer and writer born in Hokkaido in 1954. Nawapedia says he discovered Kitan Club in 1969 and taught himself kinbaku, moved to Tokyo in 1973, wrote about SM-film history in S&M Sniper during the 1980s, directed his first title for Art Video in 1990, started the Dirty label in 1994 and DK Productions in 2000. Kinbaku Books' Ropemarks archive confirms his 1998 rope-mark photo-book and companion Jomonshiki DVD context.

### DirtyVonP

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/dirty-von-p/

Fact — DirtyVonP is a French rigger in the Naka-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER · AUTHOR

Transmission — French rigger, teacher, performer and author behind Ecole des Cordes. His official Ecole bio places his rope start in the early 2010s, says he trained with Akira Naka between 2013 and 2016, and credits later exchanges with Riccardo Wildties, Georg Barkas and Tesshin Doyama as shaping his semenawa / Naka Ryu-rooted practice. He took over Ecole des Cordes in 2016 and developed it as a French kinbaku teaching space focused on intention, listening, precision, safety and emotional connection. Co-author with Stephanie Doe of French rope manuals such as L'art du shibari Tome 1 and L'Art du Bondage, and later co-author with Lola Lalhow of 2026 Eveil Editions books on bedroom shibari, rope bottoming and self-tying. Performer at RopeFest and Onawa Asobi Europe 2025 with partner Lola Bunny 666.

Fact — School: Naka-ryū

### Docvale

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/docvale/

Fact — Docvale is a French nawashi in the Naka-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — French kinbakushi centered in Paris. Began rope practice in 2007; started teaching and performing internationally in 2012. In 2013 discovered Naka Akira's semenawa and devoted himself entirely to that style. Opened L'Atelier Simonet in Paris in 2018. In 2019, Naka Akira officially authorized him to teach his style — one of the first Europeans to receive this recognition. His practice centers on emotional perception and the psychological dimension of rope.

Fact — School: Naka-ryū

### Door

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/door/

Fact — Door is a Dutch model.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — MODEL · TEACHER

Fact — Dutch kinbaku model, performer, writer and educator behind SMothered. Began getting tied in summer 2017, soon met Asiana and became her frequent rope partner / artistic collaborator; The current record frames Door as an occasional RopeLounge co-teacher / model rather than RopeLounge's principal teacher. Together they perform and collaborate as Knot So / KnotSo, have taught and performed in the Netherlands and abroad at EURIX, Prague Shibari Festival, CAMERA JAPAN, Knot Here Amsterdam and other events, and co-host the six-episode Rope Ramblings podcast. The podcast records their rigger / model conversations around semenawa, trust-centered dynamics, Kanna-style tying, emotion and beauty in kinbaku. Also works often with Soptik. Door's model bio names Akira Naka, Riccardo Wildties, Barkas and Nicolas Yoroi as important rope teachers, and RedSabbath, Clover, NawaTaNeko, Nomura Akane and Addie as model influences. EURIX 2018 and 2019 presenter with Asiana.

### Dov

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/dov-washi-nawashi/

Fact — Dov is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 1990s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Fact — NYC-centered rope-bondage educator, artist, photographer and organizer using the public identity Dov / Washi Nawashi. His official bio says he has been in the New York scene since 1993, is one of the two founders of ShibariCon, and has taught Japanese rope bondage, rope bondage, canes and whips at TES, ShibariCon, Bound In Boston, RopeCamp, Xplore Vienna / Berlin 2014, Hong Kong Kink Con 2016 and other events. A 2017 Washi Nawashi post by Dov characterizes him as a Bound In Boston teacher for many years and self-reports being one of the two people who started ShibariCon in 2004. Fetish Fair Fleamarket #36 independently characterizes Dov as an NYC-centered artist and educator, a shibari and single-tail whip practitioner, and a presenter at TES, ShibariCon and Leather Leadership Conference.

### Dr Fatso

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/dr-fatso/

Fact — Dr Fatso is an Italian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Italian bondage teacher, rigger and author using the public identity Dr Fatso / DrFatso, also named as Angelo Tamburello. Alfil Barcelona's official curriculum places him among the early external teachers in Spain, stating that Alfil attended his Madrid bondage seminar in 2002 and repeated the seminar in 2003. Giornalettismo's 2011 shibari explainer characterizes Dr Fatso as a Turin-centered bondager teaching bondage courses to couples and quoting him on safety, while MySecretCase's 2015 course page frames him as the collaborator and instructor behind its Love Bondage workshop. A later public author bio for the 2020 book Nelle Corde characterizes him as a veteran of Italian bondage whose regular courses from 2003 helped spread bondage spanning Italy.

### Dragon

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/dragon-shibari-adelaide/

Fact — Dragon is an Australian rigger in the Kanna-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Fact — Adelaide-centered Australian rigger and Shibari Adelaide instructor using the public identity Dragon / @ll_dragon_l. Shibari Adelaide's official team page says Dragon rediscovered rope bondage in 2014 after earlier western-style rope teaching, attended workshops with major instructors, and works spanning semenawa, suspension and playful rope. The public Instagram profile names Dragon as a South Australian / Adelaide rigger and Kanna Ryu shibari instructor. Shibari Adelaide's June 2026 acknowledgement says @ll_dragon_l and @dragons_princess63 had been with the studio/community for 11 years, helped open Shibari Adelaide just over seven years earlier, were Sparks and Emerald's first instructors, and were stepping back from instructor and weekly-class assistant roles while remaining valued community members.

Fact — School: Kanna-ryū

### Osada Eikichi (長田英吉)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/eikichi-osada/

Fact — Osada Eikichi (長田英吉), 1925–2001, is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Osada / Western.

Chronology — Born: 1925

Chronology — Died: 2001

Fact — Era: 1970s

Fact — NAWASHI · PERFORMER

Fact — Pioneer of modern kinbaku as public performance. From the 1960s brought rope bondage from underground SM circles into live nude-theater shows, typically in 10-day engagements. At his peak in the early 1980s drew around 2,000 spectators per day and earned up to $3,000 per performance day. His public work opened the path for the next generation of nawashi. Teacher of Osada Steve. Collapsed on stage on January 20, 2001, and spent his final eight months in an elderly care facility before dying in September that year.

Fact — School: Osada / Western

- [website](https://osadasteve.com/osada-eikichi-senseis-profile_en.html)

### -EM-

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/em-memphis/

Fact — -EM- is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · WHIP EDUCATOR

Fact — Memphis-centered US rope artist, whip enthusiast and BDSM educator using the public handle -EM-. Indy Rope Expo characterizes him as a Memphis rope artist / whip enthusiast who joined the scene in 2010, studied with various instructors and teaches spanning North America and Europe, often presenting with NiraTheKitten. Rope in the Rockies similarly frames his practice around rope bondage, whips, high-impact teaching and technical precision. FIRE / FLARE 2024 scheduled EM and Nirathekitten for advanced rope / impact classes such as Suspension and the Single Tail, Agura Transitions and Heavy Play. An older Impact of Memphis Lewbari page also records EM as a contact for a Memphis three-day bondage weekend with Lew Rubens and Helixess.

- [Indy Rope Expo presenter](https://www.indyropeexpo.com/presentor)

Fact — EDITOR · PHOTOGRAPHER

A 2023 Bologna presentation of Yugen Studio Magazine says Federico Kirigami and Marta Tenshiko built Yugen Studio with valuable help from Ema Kuroko, and frames YSM as growing from Ema's original SM Showa editorial project combined with original material produced at Yugen Studio. A later Yugen SM N14 post again credits @emakuroko as chief editor.

### Emily Ann

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/emily-ann/

Fact — Emily Ann is documented as a German model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — MODEL

Fact — Berlin-centered kinbaku model using the public identity Emily Ann / @emilyann_kinbaku. Instagram profile confirms the handle but exposes the profile as private, so it is presented as a contact surface only. A public X-profile mirror characterizes her as a Berlin-centered kinbaku model, while Kinbaku Society of Berlin's 2025 contributor spotlight for its Female Figures / Modern European Kinbaku Photography issue credits Emily Ann as model in a Torienne photo set with Alexander MA as rigger, shot in Tenerife in 2023. The same public X trail supports a bounded Yugen Studio / Rope Tales / Kitaro Kasukabe workshop-model trace, documented as a Yugen Studio appearance rather than lineage or a person-to-person collaboration relationship.

### Ena Dahl

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ena-dahl/

Transmission — Ena Dahl is a Norwegian teacher.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — TEACHER · PERFORMER · ORGANIZER

Fact — Oslo-raised, Berlin-centered rope (s)witch, conscious-kink educator, author, bodyworker and SPNKD Berlin creative lead / GM. Her official site frames workshops, private sessions and writing around sensuality, conscious kink and rope; public IKSK, InFLUX and Danish Shibari Festival bios say she has been active in kink / BDSM since 2017 and with shibari since 2020. A 2026 SPNKD Salon host page names her as Creative Lead of SPNKD Berlin and characterizes her rope style as intimate and intuitive, prioritizing connection, communication and deep listening. After meeting Roxu at EURIX in spring 2024, she and Roxu have taught and performed together spanning Europe, with public traces at EURIX XXVI / Spring 2025, IKSK Berlin, InFLUX Bucharest, Danish Shibari Festival 2025, Daruma Berlin, LIMINAL Amsterdam and SPNKD Berlin.

### Enot

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/enot/

Fact — Enot is a Kazakh rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Almaty / Moscow shibari master, teacher, performer and organizer around the TEMA.Space / Shibari FEST Kazakhstan scene, with the public Instagram profile now placing him in Da Nang, Vietnam. Public TEMA.Space posts describe Enot as a master with 13+ years in shibari, one of the organizers of TEMA.Space and Shibari Fest, and a practitioner of sensual, painful and aesthetic tying. The same source announces a six-month Almaty course under Enot and frames his teaching around the classical style of Ren Yagami's shibari school. Shibari FEST public posts list Enot among the 2024 festival masters as Almaty / Moscow and identify TEMA.Space and Studio Shi as festival infrastructure, while the current Instagram shell narrows the present-facing public offer to performance, photoshoot and shibari-art work.

### Entangled Witch

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/entangled-witch/

Fact — Entangled Witch is a rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Tallinn-centered Estonian rope practitioner, teacher, performer and organizer publicly appearing as Entangled Witch. Kumu Art Museum's official September 24, 2025 workshop page names her as `Alina aka the Entangled Witch`, says she had practiced binding as creative self-expression for over seven years, and frames her as an experienced shibari instructor. Instagram profile confirms the live `@entangledwitch` profile with the public bio lines `Founder of Kultus Club. Rope classes. Rope performances.` A public Telemetr archive preserving reposts from Kultus Club Tallinn separately says the club was originally created by `Mistress Alina aka Entangled Witch`, bills `Entangled Witch and Elsa (EE)` in the club's Valentine's performance context, and advertises a later `Shibari school by EntangledWitch and Elsa` via the same Tallinn community shell.

### Erato Bänana

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/erato-banana/

Fact — Erato Bänana is a French nawashi in the Naka-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · MODEL · TEACHER

Fact — Paris-centered rope switch, shibari instructor and performer. Ten-year bondage practitioner who turned to shibari in 2017 and began following Docvale's teaching in November 2018. Co-managed Atelier Simonet in Paris with Docvale until summer 2023, then created La Cabäne in Paris during winter 2024 as a small shibari teaching space in the 19th arrondissement. Teaches both rigging and modeling, leveraging her prior background as actress and director for performances, photoshoots and video projects. Instructor on Shibari Study and guest teacher at Karada House.

Fact — School: Naka-ryū

### Eric Shore

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/eric-shore/

Fact — Eric Shore is a French rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Lyon-centered French shibari / kinbaku teacher, rigger and workshop organizer. Eric Shore's official bio says he discovered shibari proper in autumn 2017, met Riccardo Wildties in 2018, continued Kinbaku LuXuria study and obtained official certification in December 2022, while still studying in that direction. The same public site frames an ongoing Lyon teaching practice via private lessons, small-group classes and guest-teacher workshops. A separate essay on the site characterizes a broader rope chronology going back to 2011 and professional-level kinbaku context from 2013; presented as biography context rather than expanded into older scene connections from current evidence alone.

### Ero Ouji (エロ王子)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ero-ouji/

Fact — Ero Ouji (エロ王子) is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · PERFORMER

Fact — Japanese kinbakushi / bakushi and high-skill amateur performer in the Tokyo nawaki / Onawa Asobi orbit. Yoi Yoshida names Ero Ouji among the performers at the first Onawa Asobi in 2009, while Kinbaku Today's 2018 Onawa listing includes him in the Tokyo festival roster. The Toubaku 2011 report records him among the Ichinawakai-side Japanese performers at the international Tokyo event. Later Kinbaku Today essays frame him as an influential but non-professional public figure: an Evolving Era essay characterizes his rope at a 2010 Chika-shitsu meeting as a striking example of the high-skill amateur nawaki scene, and a Kazami Ranki interview says Ero Ouji never wanted to go professional but still left a strong impression. Sin's sources place Ero Ouji among the Japanese-scene contacts / influences behind Sin's return to BDSM and kinbaku. The `eroouji.com` lead did not resolve during the 2026-05-20 check, so the entry is sourced from the available event and article trail rather than the dead domain.

- [Toubaku 2011 report](https://bittersweet.asia/en/archives/2011/11/toubaku-report/)

### Eru

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/eru-berlin-ropes/

Transmission — Eru is documented as a German teacher.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · RIGGER

Fact — Berlin-centered rope-bondage / shibari personal trainer using the public identity Eru via Berlin Ropes. Her official site says she established Berlin Ropes in 2012 after first working with ropes at YourRope / Fesseltrieb and co-training beginner workshops from 2010, and that she programming includes individual and couple training in Berlin-Moabit. TipBerlin's 2024 Berlin shibari guide records Eru / Berlin Ropes as a rigger offering one-on-one instruction for beginners and advanced students; BoundCon XI's 2014 programme records Berlin Ropes with the workshop `Your personal Bondage Trainer`.

### Esinem

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/esinem/

Fact — Esinem is a British nawashi in the Osada / Western.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — British kinbaku practitioner and educator centered in London. One of the West's early adopters of Japanese rope bondage. Studied under Arisue Go, Osada Steve, Hajime Kinoko, Ranki Kazami, Akira Naka, and Yukimura Haruki. Co-organiser of BOUND (Europe's premier monthly shibari event, London) and the London Festival of the Art of Japanese Bondage. Represented the UK at Japan's first international kinbaku event, Toubaku. Collaborated with fashion designer Tom Ford (2013). Co-teaches ESINEM Shibari Classes with partner Nina Russ. Author of the first English-language kinbaku tutorial DVD series "Japanese Rope Bondage: Tying people, not parcels."

Fact — School: Osada / Western

### Etheros

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/etheros/

Fact — Etheros is a Belgian nawashi in the Yagami-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — Belgian nawashi and founder of Studio TIEY in Antwerp. Formerly a corporate trainer and NLP practitioner; left that career in 2021 to work full-time in shibari. Integrates budo, yoga, and Zen philosophy into his practice. Began tying in 2013 under Georg Barkas; studied with Docvale (Naka-ryu) and Michael RopeKnight (Yukimura-ryu). Became a private student of Ren Yagami in 2019 and a certified Yagami-ryu instructor in 2020.

Fact — School: Yagami-ryū

Fact — Japanese kinbakushi / mistress and Yukimura-ryu licensed instructor. Kinbakuwiki records Himuro Eve / Haruyoi as Yukimura-ryu license #13 in Tokyo, and Nawapedia says she learned bondage from Haruki Yukimura. Her public scene context includes Roppongi venues Sugar Heel, Kedama and Princess Rose, the latter two began operating in 2007 and 2011, plus Haruki Yukimura Live traces from 1996. A 2020 Shibari.PH report preserves a later public-performance trace alongside Yagami Ren and Hajime Kinoko.

### Eve X

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/eve-x/

Fact — Eve X is an Australian performer.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — PERFORMER · EDUCATOR

Fact — Australian performance artist, educator and curator using the public identity Eve X / @theevex. New Eden Projects says Sacred Primal Arts was first visioned by Eve X in 2019 and later evolved into the Australian School of Sexuality programme, while the public Bluesky profile frames Eve X as headmistress / festival curator at the Australian School of Sexuality and educator with Sacred Primal Arts. Eve X's own public chronology records recurring 2024 Infernal Desire Machine aerial-shibari performances with Sai Jaiden Lillith spanning Melbourne and Sydney, alongside Sacred Primal Arts workshops. Humanitix separately records a 2024 Sacred Primal Arts Sydney workshop billed with Eve X and Sai Jaiden Lillith.

### Eysar Vargas

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/eysar-vargas/

Fact — Eysar Vargas is a rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER · ARTIST

Fact — Valencia, Venezuela rigger, educator, visual artist and founder / director of Shibari Valencia Venezuela. The current community record says Eysar established the project in 2023 after several years of independent rope, drawing and artistic research, later received a Shibari Study training scholarship, and continued studying via in-person and online classes with teachers and communities from Japan, Italy, Mexico, Colombia and Uruguay. A current profile recheck adds virtual-learning context via Karada House, Shibari Study and Ren Yagami, and public reference profiles for teachers, communities and collaborators in Latin America, Japan and Europe; these are presented as training / reference context rather than visible map connections. Eysar's personal Beacons and Behance surfaces support the standalone public art / shibari-illustration identity. The public Instagram profiles are used as current identity / contact provenance; no formal lineage, rank, certification or school claim is asserted from The setting.

### Faviola Llervu

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/faviola-llervu/

Fact — Faviola Llervu is documented as an American researcher.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — RESEARCHER · PODCASTER

Fact — US-centered kinbaku researcher, translator, editor and podcast / site creator. Created The Kinbakunomicon, a podcast and online archive focused on kinbaku history, Japanese bondage and SM culture in Japan. Kinbakunomicon's Kinbaku Minus One page names her as current editor of Nawapedia, a translator for Japanese bondage artists, and the designer of the original cell structure that became the Devil Mask Society Cell program. Rope Podcast EP55 frames her work around Japanese SM source materials, translation, historical context and making Japanese kink perspectives more visible in the global rope community.

### Felix Ruckert

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/felix-ruckert/

Fact — Felix Ruckert, born 1959, is documented as a German choreographer in the Osada / Western.

Chronology — Born: 1959

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — CHOREOGRAPHER · ORGANIZER · TEACHER

Transmission — German choreographer, performer, teacher and organiser whose official biography says he discovered shibari / kinbaku on a 1999 trip to Japan. First teachers: Hagen, Matthias T. J. Grimme, Midori, Shadow and Osada Steve; later intensive study included Arisue Go, Akechi Kanna, Hajime Kinoko, Akira Naka, Otonaya Otonawa and Ren Yagami. In Berlin he ran schwelle7 from 2007 to 2016, initiated EURIX in 2012 and opened IKSK at Holzmarkt in 2020. His official site remains an active announcement hub for EURIX, xplore, Bondage and schwelle7 / IKSK-adjacent programming.

Fact — School: Osada / Western

Fact — FilmNetflick is a Thai shibari photographer.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — SHIBARI PHOTOGRAPHER

Fact — Bangkok-centered Thai shibari / fetish photographer using the public identity FilmNetflick. Zipevent's official I'MPULSE Shibari Art Exhibition listing names FilmNetflick among the guest photographers and names the photographer group as such as Nattapol Pattananithiboon. The 2023 Time Out Bangkok listing independently characterizes I'MPULSE as a multidisciplinary shibari exhibition with a photo-exhibition component by FilmNetflick and four other photographers. Kinky Bangkok then records a dedicated April 6, 2024 FilmNetflick Shibari Trip event presented by ThailandBDSM with UnnamedMinor. Public Instagram profile screenshots show the main `@filmnetflick_sssr` profile as FilmNetflick / Nattapol Pattananithiboon and a linked shibari-specific portfolio account `@shibari.fnf`.

### Flynn Racer

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/flynn-racer/

Fact — Flynn Racer is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · STUDIO OPERATOR

Fact — Detroit shibari educator, photographer-rooted rope practitioner and studio co-owner using the public identity Flynn / Flynn Racer. Oblige's official L.E.A.P. Biography characterizes more than fifteen years of shibari experience, a start via artistic photography, current co-owner / co-operator status and an engineering-informed approach to suspension, structure and mechanical bondage. The independent subMERGE interview names Flynn as Flynn_racer and corroborates the shared creation and ownership of Oblige with Paul / Serene_X.

### Fox Cordee

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/fox-cordee/

Fact — Fox Cordee, born 1981, is a French rigger.

Chronology — Born: 1981

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — RIGGER · PODCASTER · AUTHOR

Fact — France-born, Bangkok-centered rope rigger, author and co-host of Rope Podcast. House Cordee's official author bio says Fox Cordee has explored rope bondage since 2016 and has worked full time as a professional rigger at House Cordee since 2022, offering free rope-bondage sessions in Bangkok. The same official bio and the current Rope Podcast / Apple listings place Fox and Mya as long-running co-hosts of Rope Podcast, while Shibari Study's 2024 episode frames Fox as a rope top discussing the psychology, motivations and values behind their rope practice. House Cordee's book page frames `Together In The Dark` as Fox's first shibari art book, summarizing the first eight years of his rope practice and a slow, sensual, sustainable rope system.

### FranckStunn

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/franckstunn/

Fact — FranckStunn is a Belgian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Belgian shibari / semenawa teacher, rigger and rope artist behind Corneum in Lennik, near Brussels. Corneum's official info page names FranckStunn as its founder, characterizes him as active in shibari, semenawa and kinbaku for more than eight years, and frames his work around technical rope, creativity, photography, rope-top / rope-bottom focused teaching, safety, play, emotion, connection and communication. Current Fienta workshop listings for Corneum present him as teacher for 2026 hashira and fundamentals weekends, say he has been active in shibari for more than nine years and teaching intensive classes for more than five years, and list study / workshop influences such as Ropunawa, Naka, Ren Yagami, Nicolas Yoroi and Soptik. Shibari.Events also indexes an Intense Connection workshop at Corneum with FranckStunn and Candydandelion as presenters.

### Fred Hatt

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/fred-hatt/

Fact — Fred Hatt is a British nawashi.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — London-centered British nawashi and educator. Met Anna Bones in London in 2013 and they have been tying partners and lovers ever since (married 2019). Co-established Anatomie Studio in June 2015 — the UK's first and only shibari studio. Known for a technical approach with style leaning towards transitions and predicaments. Teaches internationally and on the Shibari Study platform; focuses on both the 'how' and 'why' of rope.

Fact — MODEL · PERFORMER

Fact — Swiss / Basel rope model, performer and workshop partner of RopuNawa. Prague Shibari Festival and Daruma Berlin bios state that Freya got into rope in 2015 and has travelled internationally as a model for workshops, shootings and performances since 2018. Her rope work is framed around bodily communication, intensity, personal expression, submission and working creatively with physical limitation. Tying in the Tropics 2026 presenter with RopuNawa.

Fact — Fuoco is documented as an American model.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — MODEL · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Fact — American shibari and rope-bottoming educator, aerialist and acrobat. Originally from Phoenix, Arizona, where she started shibari in the early 2010s via the local BDSM scene. Lived in London and Berlin; befriended by Shibari Study co-founder Gorgone in Berlin, where she began bottoming for rope workshops. Attended a professional full-time circus training program in Vermont (2016–2019), blending circus arts with shibari. 'Switchy rope person and educator.' Director of Education at Shibari Study — designs the platform's curricula and acts as community liaison. Her Shibari Study podcast episode frames her education work around bottoming responsibility, community accountability, body mechanics and tangible bottoming skills.

### Fuu (ふぅ)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/fuu/

Fact — Fuu (ふぅ) is documented as a Japanese kinbaku artist.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — KINBAKU ARTIST · BIWA PERFORMER · BAR STAFF

Fact — Tokyo kinbaku artist, biwa performer and Mitsu staff member using the public identity Fuu / ふぅ. Fuu's official profile says they moved from clinical engineering work at Tokyo Medical and Dental University into kinbaku, began studying kinbaku under Saikatsu and classical biwa in 2009, and developed a performance practice around the ritual beauty of tied bodies and biwa sound. That record places Fuu as staff at Shinjuku / Kabukicho SM bar Mitsu, while public Instagram and Bluesky profiles preserve the `ふぅ_密蜜-mitsu-` / `ふぅ緊縛琵琶 密蜜-mitsu-` identity, current `biwa.tokyo` route and note that Kabukicho Mitsu is on hiatus with weekend Akasaka travelling activity.

### Gabrielle

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/gabrielle/

Fact — Gabrielle is a Danish rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · FACILITATOR

Fact — Copenhagen-centered certified trauma-informed facilitator, educator, practitioner and rope performer, usually credited with Anitta in rope / conscious-kink teaching contexts. Danish Shibari Festival's 2024 bio characterizes Gabrielle as a familiar face in Copenhagen kink for seven years, involved in Ropetogether and The Conscious Kink Sessions, and focused on curiosity, authenticity, humour and sexuality as a path to deeper connection. IKSK's 2025 Shame On You listing says Gabrielle became part of House of Play's core facilitator group, teaching consent, confidence and authenticity in play and rope bondage, and had taught rope festivals with Anitta such as EURIX spring 2024 with a 2025 return. House of Play's 2026 page characterizes eight years of rope, play and conscious-kink experience and workshops / rituals / play-space facilitation.

### Gala Kinshinawa

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/gala-kinshinawa/

Fact — Gala Kinshinawa is a Spanish rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · MODEL · TEACHER

Transmission — Barcelona-centered shibari/kinbaku artist, rope switch, teacher and performer, working with partner Vik as Vik & Gala. Started from the bottom/model role before developing curiosity for tying others and self-tying. Yugen Studio's 2026 bottoming-workshop bio frames her as an international teacher and performer as bottom, rigger and self-suspender, with a dark, strong, delicate and erotic Semenawa-oriented style and an active, conscious bottoming focus. Together Vik and Gala teach internationally, perform live sessions, were invited to Milan to participate in the Kinbaku Lab research group, created Kinbaku Lab Barcelona with other riggers and bottoms, and run Shizen Studio Barcelona. EURIX 2023 / 2026 presenter, such as a separate 2026 Gala Kinshinawa newcomer trace.

### Gandalf

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/gandalf-shanghai/

Fact — Gandalf is a Chinese rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Fact — Shanghai-centered shibari artist and educator. Shibari Study and Espacio Nos present Gandalf as a former cognitive scientist, founder / organizer of Embodied Flow, conductor of a local Playback Theatre group and initiator of Kinbaku Theatre. Their rope teaching blends movement, theatre, dance, touch, consent, safety, tension and embodied thinking, with an explicit focus on queer and women's rope communities in China and international workshop contexts such as a 2023 Barcelona class at Espacio Nos.

### GanRaptor

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ganraptor/

Fact — GanRaptor is a Polish nawashi.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Fact — Polish shibari / bondage performer, educator and online-instruction author working with Slaanesh. GanRaptor's official bio frames him as a forerunner of shibari on the Polish stage, with shibari shows at major Polish fetish / BDSM events since 2009, a long-running collaboration with Slaanesh, and international workshop activity in Berlin, Rhein/Main, Copenhagen, Tallinn and Moscow. Nawado.pl characterizes the pair as authors of a Polish multimedia shibari / bondage manual, with their own photos, films, texts and workshop practice documenting years of rope work.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · ARTIST

Fact — Japanese rope / calligraphy artist using the public identity 雅蓮Garen / Garen. Hajime Kinoko's current lessons page records Garen among Second-Class Instructor License holders, and Garen's public X profile reports Ichinawakai / Hajime Kinoko teacher context while linking the Instagram `@garen.shodo`. Public WOMB / RA UNBOUND 2024 listings place GAREN in the Tokyo art-performance roster alongside Hajime Kinoko, ZIMA, Louis Kordexe and other rope artists, while Sansei Lounge Seoul preserves 2024-2026 guest / rope-calligraphy programming.

### Gestalta

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/gestalta/

Transmission — Gestalta is a British teacher.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — GB rope artist, teacher and performer first introduced to shibari in 2007. Published Shibari Suspensions in 2019, opened Daruma in Berlin in January 2024, and teaches there regularly. Recurring teacher or presenter at Karada House, Prague Shibari Festival, RopeFest and Shibaricamp, with a long performance history spanning Japan, Australia and Europe.

### Ginger

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ginger-rope/

Transmission — Ginger is a British teacher.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — TEACHER · SWITCH

Fact — Scotland-centered switchy rope practitioner and facilitator. Karada House's organizer profile characterizes Ginger as a switchy rope fiend whose kink practice is grounded in rope and whose teaching interest is creating spaces where people in fat bodies can learn together and share experience. Thick Hitches Conference records Ginger as a UK facilitator for the 2025 in-person Berlin edition and the 2026 virtual edition; the 2025 programme credits them with Embodied Tying, Fatness and minimizing of desire, and Guided Self Tying sessions.

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/giotto-shelton/

Fact — Giotto Shelton is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ROPE MAKER

Transmission — US shibari teacher, professional top and rope maker behind DeGiotto Rope. DeGiotto's own history says he began making ropes in Austin in 2009, later moved to San Francisco and worked with Two Knotty Boys while meeting many riggers in the community. The current private-lessons page characterizes him as Houston-centered, a full-time shibari teacher / professional top and a 20+ year teacher, offering Zoom, home-studio and local private lessons.

### Glü Wür

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/gluwur/

Fact — Glü Wür is a Chilean artist.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — ARTIST · PERFORMER

Fact — Chilean artist, performer and rope practitioner centered in Barcelona. Her own site characterizes a background in theatre, painting, sculpture, jewelry design, gemmology, aerial circus and shibari; she entered Barcelona's shibari community in 2013, first as a model, then via self-suspension and a circus / Japanese-bondage performance practice. Her rope-art archive records performances spanning Europe and the Americas, such as Big Bag at EURIX Berlin, Onawa Asobi Europe with Rouse Renoir, Rome Bondage Week, Shibari Freak Camp, BOUND London, Folsom Street Fair and Anatomie Studio. Sala de Luz's profile names her as Paola, says she has taught self-tying and shibari since 2016, organizes Hitchin Bitches BCN, and opened Sala de Luz in 2022 to connect arts and sexuality. EURIX XXVI spring 2025 records GLUWUR as a presenter.

### GNBY

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/gnby/

Fact — GNBY is a Czech rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Prague rigger, teacher and performer using the public identity GNBY / GNBY.cz; Czech press names him as Jakub Šesták and explains the GNBY alias as `gunboy`. His official site is a compact shibari portfolio, while LP-Life's 2019 interview says he professionally practices Japanese rope / shibari, teaches workshops and has performed at Tattoo Convention and an erotic fair. INKPPL's 2019 Tattoo Convention Prague page names GNBY as a Prague rigger presenting a dynamic shibari show and combining a personal approach to traditional Japanese rope with modern European influences; later Tattoo Convention Prague / Tetovani.cz coverage records recurring 2024-2025 shibari shows. EURIX records him as a 2020s presenter / beginner-course teacher.

### Arisue Go (有末剛)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/go-arisue/

Fact — Arisue Go (有末剛), born 1954, is documented as a Japanese nawashi.

Chronology — Born: 1954

Fact — Era: 1990s

Transmission — NAWASHI · PERFORMER · TEACHER

Transmission — Tokyo-centered kinbaku performer, teacher and author born in 1954. Nawapedia and Kinbaku Wiki place him as a senior connection figure whose early rope interest was shaped by Seiu Ito's paintings and Minomura Ko / Akira's writings, then by 1970s work for SM Select, SM Fan and SM-club shows while he was still a university student. Ohta's publisher profile also characterizes him as inheriting Ito / Minomura's conceptual line and working spanning 1970s-1980s SM magazines, Nikkatsu / Toei / V-cinema rope direction, photo books and overseas collaborations. Did the rope for the 2000s Flower and Snake remake series and the 1987 Angel Guts: Akai Nawa Hateru Made, appeared in Hiroki Ryuichi's Bakushi documentary, and became one of the early Japanese kinbakushi to teach and publish for international audiences. Selected authored works include Kinbakushi A's Ecstasy And Gloom, Arisue's Kinbaku Theory and Practices, and the 2009 Kinbaku Mind and Techniques manuals.

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/goku-enbushinbakukai/

Fact — Goku (悟空) is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · PHOTOGRAPHER · COMMUNITY REPRESENTATIVE

Transmission — Toyama-centered Japanese kinbaku artist, photographer, teacher and Enbushinbakukai / 艶舞心縛會 representative using the public identity Goku / 悟空. The current Bluesky profile says he represents Enbushinbakukai, now established for 17 years, and programming includes shows, events, private rope, lessons and kinbaku dojo activity in Toyama and Nagoya while also using the name a photographer. MotionGallery's Kanna / Kagura 10th-anniversary photo-book project independently characterizes Goku as a Hokuriku / Toyama-centered kinbakushi and photographer, operator of StudioG, Enbushinbakukai representative and regular organizer of kinbaku dojo, rope classes and Photo Nawa Club sessions.

### Goldie Sunshine

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/goldie-sunshine/

Fact — Goldie Sunshine is an Israeli rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · PERFORMER · TEACHER

Transmission — Tel Aviv-centered Israeli rope artist, performer and teacher. BalkanBound's public Fall 2025 archive says she discovered shibari in 2015 as a rope bottom and began tying in 2016, blending traditional Japanese shibari aesthetics with contemporary Western rope. The same source records Israeli scene appearances and international festival traces such as EURIX Berlin, Prague Shibari Festival, FIRE Orlando, Roma Bondage Week, RopeFest Russia, BoundCon Munich and BalkanBound; the EURIX archive independently records MyMay & Goldie Sunshine from Tel Aviv at EURIX XI / Autumn 2017. Her Vimeo On Demand archive preserves performance-video traces for RopeFest SPb 2016 / 2018, RopeFest Moscow 2019 and Shi Fest Ukraine 2018 / 2019.

Shibari Study's GoodMostTimes episode says Melissa started rope education as a bottom in early 2012, has been part of Devil Mask Society since its inception, teaches Cell and Cell X classes, helps drive local events, moderated rope-bottoming panels, helped produce Ropenspace LA, NARIX 2016 and Kinoko's Los Angeles Cyber Rope performance, and taught at Ropecraft NOLA and other North American intensives. Devil Mask Studio names Melissa / GoodMostTimes as a longtime educator, Devil Mask Society founding member and studio CFO.

Fact — Gordias is a Polish rigger.

Fact — RIGGER · PERFORMER

Fact — Polish shibari rigger and performer, often appearing with Anna Aurora / Kinky Aurora. Onawa Asobi Europe records Gordias & Anna Aurora as Polish performers and gives Gordias's self-bio: first encountered rope bondage in 2016 and, from 2020, dedicated more energy to kinbaku after being inspired by DocVale's tying and encouraged by his words. A Yugen SM N14 public post later credits Gordias in a shooting with Aurora for Kirigami.

### Gorgone

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/gorgone/

Fact — Gorgone is a French nawashi.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — NAWASHI · PERFORMER · CO-FOUNDER

Fact — French rope switch and one of the foundational voices of the European shibari scene. Real name Marika Leila Roux / Marika Leïla Roux. Her publicly documented chronology places her rope discovery around 2011, early Kinbaku LuXuria co-founding / co-running with Riccardo Wildties in 2011-2012, and early BOUND London co-organizing with Esinem from late 2012. Subsequent public traces place her in Naka Akira study / modeling / multi-year tour context, such as Nawana / Nawa Naka Kai model traces in 2014-2015 after 2014 Tokyo work with Naka and Sugiura Norio, as organizer and model for Ren Yagami's 2015 EU / US tour, in repeated Hajime Kinoko performance / modeling collaborations, and in early EURIX editions such as 2013, 2014 and 2016. Professional shibari model and rigger with 10+ years of intense practice, performing worldwide. Approach is deeply informed by yoga and other bodywork, and Nawapedia explicitly notes that she does not identify with one specific style. Co-established Shibari Study with Anton Martin in 2019, bringing the shibari know-how and contacts while Anton brought vision and operations. In 2016 initiated 'Study on Falling', an ongoing aesthetic and philosophical performance research presented on major stages such as the Opéra Garnier (Paris). Her public Gorgone Rope Salon channel records current private lessons, sessions, tour dates and a guesthouse-centered shibari experience in Ardèche, France.

### 御伽棋 王子 (御伽棋 王子)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/gozonji-ouji/

Fact — 御伽棋 王子 (御伽棋 王子) is documented as a Japanese nawashi.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — NAWASHI · PERFORMER

Fact — Osaka-centered Japanese nawashi and performer using the public identity 御伽棋 王子 / Gozonji Ouji. His current lit.link profile characterizes him as a nawashi, performer and ZOO staff member, and says he is a Shishinawakai-affiliated rope artist performing from Osaka. His Bluesky profile uses the same role wording, names FetishBarZoo staff context, and names Shishiwaka as a respected teacher figure; a public 2022 X post framed a Jyo-mon appearance via Shishiwaka and a Shishinawakai advanced-class learning context.

### Graydancer

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/graydancer/

Fact — Graydancer is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER · PODCASTER

Fact — US rope-bondage educator, performer, writer, organizer and podcaster alternate public name: Gray Miller; public event / book-context sources occasionally spell the handle Greydancer. MomentumCon's presenter bio roots him in Madison, WI and frames him as a rope-bondage podcaster, author and performer. ROPECRAFT's official bio frames him as a kink / BDSM teacher, performer and advocate whose work includes Ropecast, and says more than 50 GRUE open-space events occurred in five countries. His public Patreon profile characterizes kink / polyamory community work since 2003, rope-bondage podcasting from 2005, GRUE open-space organizing from 2007, ROPECRAFT creation in 2016 and Consent.Rocks incident-response work. Rope Podcast EP134-135 says he taught / performed / organized rope events until 2019 while traveling throughout North America and Europe.

### Griffin207

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — EDUCATOR · STUDIO CO-OWNER

Fact — Portland, Maine rope educator, performer and Box Tie Studio co-owner using the public identity Griffin / Griffin207. Official Box Tie Studio pages say he has more than eight years of tying, bottoming and self-tying experience, more than seven years teaching, performing and doing demonstrations, and co-led Black Knot Rope Group with Cait from 2018-2023 before they established Box Tie Studio. The same official bio records teaching / presenting engagements such as Kinky Con and Tethered Together, while the current Bluesky profile independently confirms the Griffin207 identity and Box Tie Studio co-owner role.

### Haag

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/haag/

Fact — Haag is a Dutch rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Rotterdam-centered professional rope bondage teacher, organizer and photographer. Event bio reports that Haag runs the Ellipsis Rope Events Foundation in Rotterdam and has hosted international workshops, performances, demonstrations and rope jams spanning twelve years. His tying is documented as safety-focused, knowledge / experience based, improvisational, dynamic and technical, complemented by a dance background and psychology studies. EURIX 2022 presenter, 2023 beginner-course teacher with Elisa T and 2024 presenter.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — TEACHER · NAWASHI

Transmission — Berlin teacher and Osada-ryu instructor connected to the Osada Kinbaku Dojo Berlin / Kinbaku Dojo e.V. Scene. Existing Berlin-scene sources frame the dojo as a Berlin shibari / kinbaku association teaching its interpretation of classical Osada Ryu via regular classes and workshops. Hagen is cited by Felix Ruckert as one of his first teachers when Felix discovered shibari in Japan in 1999, and EURIX archive material credits Hagen as a frequent beginner-course presenter with Arlis / Arliss.

### Hajime Kinoko (一鬼のこ)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/hajime-kinoko/

Fact — Hajime Kinoko (一鬼のこ), born 1977, is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Ichinawa-ryū.

Chronology — Born: 1977

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · ARTIST

Fact — One of the most internationally visible contemporary nawashi and a leading kinbaku artist. Born 22 October 1977 in Aichi Prefecture, Japan. Took up rope for the first time in 2001 via his girlfriend's interest; began self-teaching in 2003 with advice from Yumeko. While managing a fetish bar in Tokyo encountered Akechi Denki's workshops there and was profoundly shaped by him; also guided by Haruki Yukimura and Nawashi Kanna — most of Kinoko's teaching is based on Kanna's method, which he openly credits. Burst onto the mainstream stage with a performance at Fuji Rock Festival in 2009. Established his own school Ichinawakai where he trains a new generation of students (~40% women) and has lifted kinbaku into fine-art contexts worldwide.

Fact — School: Ichinawa-ryū

### Hangknot

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/hangknot/

Fact — Hangknot is a Canadian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PHOTOGRAPHER

Transmission — Montreal-centered Canadian rope rigger, teacher and photographer using the public identity Hangknot / Julien Lacoma. The public Instagram profile frames his practice as `Kinbaku and photography in Montreal`, advertises shibari private lessons, and reports `Naka Akira style only.` Montréal Campus independently names `Julien Lacoma, alias Hangknot` as a Montreal rigger and collaborator on bondage films and photo sessions, while the current Men* in Rope link hub places Julien Lacoma / `@hangknot` inside the same Montreal men-who-tie-men discovery surface already used for Bondagious, Thumper and Behemoth Chess Club.

#### Reference links

- [instagram](https://www.instagram.com/hangknot/)

### Isabelle Hanikamu (井佐鐘)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/hanikamu-isabelle/

Fact — Isabelle Hanikamu (井佐鐘), born 1961, is a Canadian kinbakushi in the Naka-ryū.

Chronology — Born: 1961

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — KINBAKUSHI · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Fact — Montreal-centered kinbakushi, performer and educator using the public identity Isabelle Hanikamu / 井佐鐘. Her official site characterizes her as a Japanese shibari and kinbaku performer and educator, born in 1961, who discovered Japanese bondage at 32, trained under Naka Akira and later created Nawakai 縄界 as a study and sharing group. The official learning page frames Hanikamu as a Sensual Semenawa Ryu teacher offering private and group shibari lessons in Montreal and globally via Nawakai, while the current Bluesky custom-domain profile gives the same 井佐鐘 / Hanikamu identity and public rope-artist / educator wording. The Naka relationship is documented only as study influence / teaching-permission context, not as formal deshi, rank or certification.

Fact — School: Naka-ryū

### Happa

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/happa/

Fact — Happa is documented as a German rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — RIGGER · MODEL · PERFORMER

Fact — German rope switch, rigger, model and performer publicly using the name Happa, with Happa Von Kruemel / HaPPa von Kruemel preserved as older social-media forms. The current record says Happa started in rope in 2014 via photo projects, helped at Stuttgart's Ropejam Kizuna around 2018-2020, worked as a workshop model, learned self-tying via Q-Shibari, tied with Umino / Dragonropes from 2020 to 2023 and turned toward rigging at the end of 2021 after RopeKnight workshops. Public corroboration now includes Happa's KunstWerk feature in Schlagzeilen SZ 198 (2023), a 2026 obscene stage slot with LucyLaCat, a Yugen SM N14 shooting credit and the Onawa Asobi Europe 2026 lineup. Kinbakuwiki's Harukumo entry records Happa von Kruemel among Harukumo's public learners since 2024.

### Shiratori Haruka (白鳥はるか)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/haruka-shiratori/

Fact — Shiratori Haruka (白鳥はるか) is a nawashi in the Ichinawa-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Taiwan-born, Tokyo-centered Ichinawakai rigger, performer and teacher. Hajime Kinoko's official lessons page records Tokyo Haruka as a domestic first-class official Ichinawakai instructor. A 2025 podcast interview characterizes her as a Taiwanese rigger living in Tokyo who entered the Taiwanese BDSM scene in 2022, went to Japan in 2023 to study with Hajime Kinoko / Ichinawakai, and became active from 2024 in performances such as Jyo-mon, Yamato Kitan, Onna Matsuri and Bara Yuri no Utage. The Jyo-mon in Taiwan 2024 performer page also characterizes Haruka as a Taiwan-born rigger living in Japan, a Hajime Kinoko student and Ichinawakai instructor, active as performer and teacher in Japan and Taiwan from 2024.

Fact — School: Ichinawa-ryū

### Yukimura Haruki (雪村春樹)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/haruki-yukimura/

Fact — Yukimura Haruki (雪村春樹), 1948–2016, is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Yukimura-ryū.

Chronology — Born: 1948

Chronology — Died: 2016

Fact — Era: 1980s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · PHOTOGRAPHER

Fact — One of the most active bakushi of his time and the founder of Yukimura-ryū. Held an interest in kinbaku from childhood. First entered rope as a photographer, editor and producer of more than 3000 videos, books and publications about shibari/kinbaku, then over time moved from behind the camera to performing rope in front of the lens. Father of the 'newaza' (floor-work) technique focused on the intense emotional relationship between rigger, model and rope. In his final decade taught his style to a small number of Western practitioners; conducted his first workshop outside Japan in Copenhagen in 2012, certifying instructors spanning Europe and beyond.

Fact — School: Yukimura-ryū

### Harukumo (春蜘)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/harukumo/

Fact — Harukumo (春蜘) is a Swiss nawashi in the Yukimura-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — Swiss nawashi and Yukimura-ryu instructor using the bakushi name Harukumo / Harukumo Katsumi. Harukumo-Juku's official team page traces a first long Tokyo stay in 2005-2006 and first encounter with Osada Steve at Studio SIX, followed by a 2011-2012 Tokyo stay in which Osada introduced him to Yukimura Haruki for intensive lessons. The same CV records Harukumo as co-instructor and interpreter at a 2012 Copenhagen Yukimura workshop, then records intensive Studio SIX instruction and assistance in early 2014, receiving the Harukumo name from Yukimura and an instructor licence on April 16, 2014. In June 2014 he established Harukumo-Juku in Zürich Oerlikon with Yoko, combining the teaching approach of Yukimura Haruki and Osada Steve with the Kitagawa Salon-inspired juku model; the Juku later operated in Neuenhof for the 2019 Osada Steve workshop series and announced a new home in Koblenz, Aargau in 2020. Later official entries document Osada-ryu kyū study / exams, first and second Osada Steve workshop series in Switzerland, a 2018 Helsinki Yukimura-ryu workshop and ongoing private lessons / workshops focused on Yukimura-ryu communication, Osada-ryu / Yukimura-ryu training structure and small-group supervision. A public 2021 Osada Steve online-class listing described Harukumo as a licensed Osada-ryu instructor and co-host / moderator; the current Osada-ryu directory instead records Tokyo, Vienna, Bremen and Secret56 as the active accredited-dojo network and notes that instructor licenses are renewed yearly, so Osada-ryu is treated here as historical study / license context rather than Harukumo's current primary school assignment.

Fact — School: Yukimura-ryū

### Haruteru (春輝)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/haruteru/

Fact — Haruteru (春輝) is an Australian nawashi in the Yukimura-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — Melbourne-centered Australian nawashi and certified Yukimura-ryu instructor identified as Haruteru (春輝), also credited publicly as Scott / Scott Haruteru. Haruteru is Yukimura-ryu license #6; his name Haruteru, translated as spring radiance, was given when he received his instructor licence. He started shibari in 2006, began regular Japan trips in 2009, had his first lesson with Yukimura Haruki in 2010, and continued yearly study until Yukimura's death in 2016, such as Yukimura's 2014 Melbourne trip. From 2011 to 2018 he ran Melbourne Rope Dojo, teaching Yukimura-ryu and Osada-ryu; a 2018 workshop archive also notes earlier study with Osada Steve. He currently teaches weekly Yukimura-ryu classes. Past students include Ash Snare and Von Riga.

Fact — School: Yukimura-ryū

### Harutokyou (春兜京)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/harutokyou/

Transmission — Harutokyou (春兜京), born 1966, is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi in the Akechi lineage.

Chronology — Born: 1966

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · RESEARCHER

Fact — Japanese kinbakushi and classical rope / hojojutsu researcher born in Osaka in 1966. Public references identify him as Harutokyou / 春兜京 / Haru-to-Kyou, an Akechi Denki and Bakuyukai-circle figure: he managed and later bought Ikebukuro fetish bar BUG from 2002, opened Haru BAR after closing BUG, and in 2006 was recorded in second-generation / second-delegate Bakuyukai context after Akechi's death. Kinbaku Today frames his work as nonprofessional, conjectural research into historical martial / penal rope practices, based on museums, old residences, archives, drawings and records, and records that he learned around Akechi by observation rather than formal teaching. Public surfaces link him to 春兜京STUDIO, Haru BAR and related project accounts. He also appears as self in the 2021 documentary Bound.

Transmission — School: Akechi lineage

### HaruTsubaki (春椿)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/harutsubaki/

Fact — HaruTsubaki (春椿) is an Argentine nawashi in the Yukimura-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · TRANSLATOR

Transmission — Argentine kinbakushi, teacher, performer and translator centered in Buenos Aires, alternate public name: Camelia. Her KinbakuMania chronology says she started learning shibari with Osada Steve in Barcelona in 2010 and 2011, assisted him as a Spanish translator, then continued a Japan / Europe study path via Yukimura Haruki, Yukinaga Max, Ranki Kazami, Nawashi Kanna and Takumi Miura. She received Osada-ryu 6th kyu and permission to teach basics in 2013, opened KinbakuMania Dojo in Buenos Aires on May 18 2014, was recognized by Yukimura as a student and given the name HaruTsubaki on August 31 2014, and received Osada-ryu 4th kyu in Tokyo in 2014. A 2023 Latin American shibari genealogy paper treats Kinbakumania Dojo's 2014 group classes with Haru Tsubaki / Camelia as a foundational Latin American shibari experience. Later public traces include Shibari Attack, Buenos Aires / La Plata performances, and mainstream film / TV shibari-art collaborations.

Fact — School: Yukimura-ryū

### Hasu

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/hasu/

Fact — Hasu is a Finnish rope artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ROPE ARTIST · FACILITATOR

Fact — Helsinki-centered queer rope artist and workshop facilitator using the public identity Hasu via @hasuropes and Kinkikynttilät. The official Kinkikynttilät site frames Hasu's wax-play, needle-play and shibari service shell, while Center for Everything's Temple of Godx Festival 2026 programme records Hasu as facilitator for Needle Play and Take My Breath Away and characterizes Hasu as the creator of the Finnish wax-play brand Kinkikynttilät.

### Hebari (春武)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/hebari/

Fact — Hebari (春武), 1975–2025, was an Australian nawashi in the Yukimura-ryū.

Chronology — Born: 1975

Chronology — Died: 2025

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · PHOTOGRAPHER

Transmission — Australian nawashi, teacher and photographer alternate public name: David Taylor / Haru Isamu. Nawapedia says he was born in Australia on November 1 1975, began practicing rope bondage in 1991, moved into Japanese-style rope around 2008 from books and the internet, attended an Arisue Go class in Sydney in 2010, established Naturally Twisted in 2010 and established Sydney Rope Dojo in 2011. Sydney Rope Dojo's instructor bio adds Japanese-style tying from around 2005, Satomi Zpira study in 2011, later Hajime Kinoko and Osada Steve study, meeting Yukimura Haruki in 2012, becoming an official Yukimura-ryu student / teacher in 2014 and later study with Nawashi Kanna. Kinbakuwiki and Nawapedia list him as Yukimura-ryu instructor No. 7, certified on March 13 2014 with the student name Haru Isamu. Also a certified advanced industrial rigger and fashion photographer; IRIS Magazine credits rope work for its Metamorphosis fashion editorial. Public event traces include Toubaku / Petit in Toubaku 2012, Japanese Rope Bondage Festival Australia 2013, Sydney Rope Festival 2014, ShibariCon 2015 and Bondage Expo Dallas 2016.

Fact — School: Yukimura-ryū

### Hedwig

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/hedwig/

Fact — Hedwig is a Swedish rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Fact — Swedish / Malmö-centered rope bondage educator, performer, writer and organizer behind Sense Shibari. Archived Sense Shibari bios describe Hedwig as a queer feminist switch femme from Sweden who first explored BDSM in London and Sweden in 2008, returned to Sweden in 2012, and taught / performed spanning Europe and North America from 2011 while running Rope Attic in Malmö. Her public teaching voice centers mutual experience, communication, consent culture, safer sex, gender and inclusive learning spaces. Earlier Nawa Ai pages document teaching / facilitation from 2010, dissertation presentations at Shibaricon, Shibaricamp, Stockholm Pride, LFRA and Rainbow Festival, former Peer Rope London crew work, Peer Rope Malmö context and founding Hitchin' Bitches in London in 2011. Official EURIX archive records Hedwig from Malmö at EURIX X / Spring 2017 and the EURIX XII / Spring 2018 beginner course.

### Saotome Hiromi (早乙女宏美)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/hiromi-saotome/

Fact — Saotome Hiromi (早乙女宏美), born 1963, is documented as a Japanese performer.

Chronology — Born: 1963

Fact — Era: 1980s

Fact — PERFORMER · WRITER

Fact — Japanese performer, writer and SM-film actress with durable Black Heart and late-Showa performance-culture relevance. Nawapedia places her at Shigeru Kayama's SAMM from 1980, in pink films from 1983, Nikkatsu work from 1984, the 1986 S&M Hunter breakthrough and Eikichi Osada live-model work in the same period. Her public note profile frames her as a subculture performer and author preserving Japanese sexual culture, and a 2023 note event post bills her directly as a performer for Black Heart's 25th anniversary while linking the official venue site.

### Hua Hua

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/hua-hua/

Fact — Hua Hua is a Chinese nawashi.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · ARTIST

Fact — Chinese shibari artist, dominatrix, and founder of Borderline Shibari. Began the first public bilingual shibari events in Shanghai in 2017, promoting a healthier approach to kink in China. Ran a Shanghai studio via the pandemic, then left China in 2022 to expand Borderline internationally; currently centered in London. Studied semenawa under the guidance of Naka Akira and publicly calls him her teacher; her Gote technique and semenawa approach are Naka-influenced. Her current teaching is framed as holistic and not tied to one particular style: slow, mindful pacing focused on intention, expression and the emotional process of rope. Uses film as a primary creative medium (genres from pornography to horror) and collaborates with artists in fashion, dance, and experimental theatre. Instructor on Shibari Study; runs Patreon community for Borderline.

### HwG-Paris

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/hwg-paris/

Fact — HwG-Paris is a French rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — French shibari artist, instructor and organizer using the HwG-Paris identity. His official biography characterizes him as founder of French Shibari Mafia, teacher of private classes and shibari stages in Paris and Marseille, organizer of Ateliers Shibari du Levant since 2021, and an online tutorial / contact presence via Instagram and YouTube. Naka / semenawa wording is presented as inspiration context rather than lineage.

### Ibarako Harumayu (荊子)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ibarako-harumayu/

Fact — Ibarako Harumayu (荊子) is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Yukimura-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Transmission — Tokyo-centered Japanese kinbakushi, teacher and official Yukimura Style instructor under the Harumayu name. Nawapedia records early bar-event activity around 1998, CineMagic package / VTR rope work in 1999, a 2000 S&M Sniper article, and her 2000 debut as Eizo Chiba's apprentice followed by roughly four years of apprenticeship. In a 2025 Nawa Jyou Wa interview she characterizes an art-school background in wrapping / binding, meeting Chiba via butoh / underground film connections, and being introduced to Yukimura Haruki at a CineMagic-related Meguro House shoot by producer Hayakawa. She characterizes learning from Yukimura by watching and assisting video production; separate instructor-list sources record her as the 14th licensed Yukimura Style instructor in 2016. Her practice frames rope as emotional expression and non-verbal communication rather than control alone. Nawapedia also records film / V-cinema / manga-support / adult-label contexts, individual teaching at Influence since 2016, Nawa Yuugi / Yukimura Style performance traces, and several instructional works.

Fact — School: Yukimura-ryū

### Ibinuphis

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ibinuphis/

Transmission — Ibinuphis is a Swiss teacher in the Osada / Western.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — TEACHER · RIGGER

Transmission — Swiss rope teacher, rigger and photographer; founding member and trainer of Secret56. Secret56's team page says he started via a 2003 workshop, co-established the Seilschaften in 2007 and Secret56 in 2009, has taught workshops in Switzerland and abroad since 2007, and focused deeply on Osada-ryu after a 2011 Osada Steve workshop in Hamburg and 2012 workshops / intensive training with Vinciens. Together with Jack the Rigger he received permission from Osada Steve to teach Osada-ryu officially from 2019 and was appointed Osada-ryu instructor on 2022-01-01.

### Icarus / Icky

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/icarus-icky/

Fact — Icarus / Icky is documented as an American model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — MODEL · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — US queer / non-binary rope bottom, switch, teacher and performance artist alternate public name: Icky. Rope Podcast EP127-128 frames Lief Bound and Icarus / Icky as a Los Angeles queer / trans rope-educator and performance-artist pair. House of Bound characterizes Icky as Lief's co-teacher / rope bottom for international performance work, while the Peace Temple 2025 retreat bio says Icky began rope bottoming in 2013, tying in 2020 and teaches consent negotiation via intersectional / trauma-survivor-informed kink experience. Queerope Berlin 2026 records Lief Bound & Icky as facilitators.

### Igor Ingvar

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/igor-ingvar/

Fact — Igor Ingvar is a Russian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · PERFORMER

Fact — Saint Petersburg rigger and performer who began in shibari as a model. RopeFest Peterburg 2026 says Igor Ingvar performed as a model in Saint Petersburg, Moscow and other Russian cities, was tied by Leonokra, later moved to the rigger side, and now works with contrasts between pain, tenderness, suspension positions and floor interaction. Dr.Moro's public bar archive preserves December 2023 Saint Petersburg shibari listings with Igor / Ingvarblog.

Fact — Inna Krauze is a Spanish artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ARTIST · PERFORMER

Fact — Visual artist and performer behind Trace of You, a rope-imprint / shibari-art-session project centered on body trace, human connection and personal art objects. Inklub / Resident Advisor list Inna among the artists for the April 2025 Atado a Madrid queer-art event, and her public January 2026 post records presenting Trace of You at BalkanBound in November 2025.

### Iroha Shizuki (紫月いろは)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/iroha-shizuki/

Fact — Iroha Shizuki (紫月いろは), born 1981, is documented as a Japanese model in the Naka-ryū.

Chronology — Born: 1981

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — MODEL · PERFORMER

Fact — Japanese kinbaku model and performer, one of the most visible contemporary rope models in the Naka Akira orbit. Nawapedia gives July 11, 1981 as her birth date, says she entered AV production in 2008, developed an interest in kinbaku around 2010, stopped AV production work in 2011 and debuted as a kinbaku model after contacting Naka Akira. In her 2014 Kinbaku Today interview, Iroha says she contacted Naka directly in December 2011, was tied by him for the first time at Nawa-Naka-Kai the following February, then became certain after a Norio Sugiura photo shoot that kinbaku modeling was the world she wanted to work in. She characterizes repeated professional work as a lesson model and live-show partner for Naka, photo-shoot / lesson-model work with Yukimura Haruki and Osada Steve, and self-tie / self-suspension practice. Later sources keep her visible as Naka's long-term model / partner in the international Naka Style circuit, with Kinbaku Lounge Copenhagen 2017, Studio TIEY 2025 and Roca #3 traces. Kinbaku Today's Murasaki / Iroha print article also frames her collaboration with Murasaki Haruan as part of keeping Yukimura-ryu visible after Yukimura's death.

Fact — School: Naka-ryū

### Ishara Gabri

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ishara-gabri/

Fact — Ishara Gabri is an Italian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ARTIST

Transmission — Italy-centered shibari rigger, teacher and artist working publicly in Trieste and Rome. Her official bio names her as a holistic practitioner, rebirther, rigger and artist, and says she opened Rope Essence in Trieste as a school around breath, connection and shibari. Public rope profile wording names her as `Shibari Rigger, Teacher & Artist - Trieste & Rome, Italy` and founder of IEStudio - Connection Hub. Official IEStudio / Ishara site pages route Rope Essence classes, monthly Rope Night / Peer Rope and Temple of Ropes programming via the Trieste studio context. A dedicated The Secret Rope profile adds five years of Shibari Dojo study with Davide La Greca plus workshops / individual lessons with Shigonawa Bingo, Tesshin / Tesshin Doyama, Gorgone and Hajime Kinoko; these are documented as non-deshi study influence only, not formal lineage, rank, certification or ryu affiliation.

### isith

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/isith/

Fact — Isith is a Swiss model.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Safety / consent — MODEL · SAFETY EDUCATOR

Safety / consent — Swiss / Basel-Vienna shibari model, safety educator and mentor in the Tying With Friends orbit. Her bio emphasizes rope, movement and inter-human connection, with international workshop study spanning Japanese, European and American teachers. She is especially known for medical and scientific rope-bondage safety expertise, such as nerve damage and psychological phenomena, and initiated ropehelp.ch. Names Nicolas Yoroi, Tifereth and Yagami Ren as current major influences. EURIX 2019 presenter.

Fact — RIGGER · PERFORMER · ORGANIZER

### Jack the Rigger

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/jack-the-rigger/

Transmission — Jack the Rigger is a Swiss teacher in the Osada / Western.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — TEACHER · RIGGER

Transmission — Swiss rope teacher, rigger, photographer and performer; founding trainer of Secret56 and Seilschaften leader. Secret56's team page says he discovered bondage in 2008, co-established Secret56 in 2009, became an important trainer in the Seilschaften / Secret56 team, and began intensive kyu-program work under Osada-ryu instructor Vinciens in 2015. Together with Ibinuphis he received permission from Osada Steve to teach Osada-ryu officially from 2019 and was appointed Osada-ryu instructor on 2022-01-01.

Fact — School: Osada / Western

#### Reference links

### Jake Wing

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/jake-wing/

Fact — Jake Wing is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Austin-centered US rope-bondage practitioner, teacher and organizer alternate public name: Jake_Wing. ROPECRAFT's official profile frames him as a long-running rope-bondage aficionado, early-internet bondage-site operator, active Austin community member and current Austin Rope Slingers leader. Rope Podcast EP50 adds the long-scene-history frame, Austin scene context, ROPECRAFT teaching trace and his blend of dark / Western / Japanese / Chinese single-strand rope influences. Austin Rope Slingers pages corroborate Jake teaching beginner and pandemic Zoom-lab classes inside the ARS peer-education community.

### JayKun_MA

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/jaykun-ma/

Fact — JayKun_MA is a Korean rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · PERFORMER · BAR OPERATOR

Fact — Seoul current-scene rigger, performer and venue operator using the public identity JayKun_MA / JayKun / `@jaykun_ma`, with X profile `@RatedJMA`. Public Instagram and Threads profile surfaces describe JayKun_MA as `SMBar M.A. Owner`, `SM Performance Group SimPL Leader` and `Shibari(Bondage) Player (not Artist)`. X and alternate Threads profile describe JayKun as operating Bar M.A., Project Sim:PL and Space Sim:PL, while X status profile information records M.A. Opening hours and bondage-salon notices. Public 2026 event traces list JayKun in shibari performance slots at Chunhee / Sabbaha's Blood & Lace, Chunhee's Fxxxtish Night and Tokyo's `LIBIDO@東京` show at Okubo MK Studio. The Yukimura-ryu wording in the X bio is retained as context only; no lineage, certification, rank or deshi relationship is documented.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — ROPE MODEL · TEACHER

Transmission — London-centered rope model, rigger and teacher. Anatomie Studio's current team page frames Jenni Noon as a volunteer teacher whose practice centers connection, model empowerment and creating spaces where people feel safe to explore and get creative, shaped by yoga, dance, movement research, Butoh and clown. Official Anatomie Studio and InFLUX pages place her in current teaching contexts, and InFLUX's Sunday School of Rope page characterizes her as a model and rigger who came to rope via bottoming in the early 2020s and now explores how emotions and movement influence tying styles.

Fact — RIGGER · DIRECTOR

Fact — US adult-media rope-bondage rigger, director and performer.
