### Maeda Juan (前田寿安)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/juan-maeda/

Fact — Maeda Juan (前田寿安), born 1939, is documented as a Japanese illustrator.

Chronology — Born: 1939

Fact — Era: 1970s

Fact — ILLUSTRATOR

Fact — Japanese SM illustrator whose rope and torment imagery became a recognizable part of 1970s-2000s Japanese fetish publishing. Nawapedia records his 1957 apprenticeship under Taiga Utagawa, 1970 debut in SM magazines via Oniroku Dan's Cellar Wife in the first issue of SM Select, a 1972-1973 Flower and Snake run in SM King, later illustration books, and a 2008 Akira Naka DVD-cover credit. WEB Sniper's 2010 Vanilla Gallery notice frames Dark Cherry / 闇桜 as Maeda's first solo exhibition and characterizes him as indispensable to the pages of SM magazines from the 1970s onward.

### Kai Navarro

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kai-navarro/

Fact — Kai Navarro is a rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Fact — Costa Rican rigger, rope educator, organizer and editor active in the EscenaCR orbit since the early 2010s. Public MsC Worldwide presenter bios identify `Master Kai` as active in community from 2012, co-director of EscenaCR and editor of its e-magazine from 2013, a shibari instructor in the rope community later known as `Sinistra`, creator of `Sinistra` as a `nawakai` in 2017, MAsT San José director from 2019 and organizer of monthly `Shibari Live Shows` from 2022; the 2026 presenter page separately records him as director at Sinistra (Nawakai). Additional public traces connect Kai Navarro / Rigger Kai to EscenaCR rope education via a 2022 Genially presentation on Japanese shibari aesthetics, older EscenaCR WordPress translation/editorial work around Yukimura material, the Rigger Kai Notion blog, and the current Aethy `Rigger Kai` profile. The Aethy profile frames him as an EscenaCR director, Latin American / MsC presenter and rope performer, while surfacing Spanish translation / lesson material, peer-rope, monthly Japanese-style shibari / kinbaku workshop and private-class context. These sources support a compact Costa Rica rope-organizer entry, but do not by themselves justify lineage, rank or teacher-study connections.

Her official Subspace Studio profile says she started as a model, now works on both sides of the rope, focuses strongly on self-tying and keeps developing rope-bottoming knowledge that she later shares in workshops. ### Ozuma Kaname (小妻要)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kaname-ozuma/

Fact — Ozuma Kaname (小妻要), 1939–2011, is documented as a Japanese artist.

Chronology — Born: 1939

Chronology — Died: 2011

Fact — Era: 1950s

Fact — ARTIST · ILLUSTRATOR

Fact — Japanese artist and illustrator known for both SM imagery and paintings of tattooed women. Nawapedia places Ozuma's Kitan Club and SM Select work in the 1960s-1970s and records later books that bridged shibari and irezumi visual culture. BME's 2010 Known Gallery coverage characterizes him as a traditionally trained Japanese portraiture artist whose tattoo-themed work shaped later audiences for Horiyoshi III and Japanese tattoo culture.

### Kano

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kano/

Transmission — Kano is a Russian teacher.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — TEACHER · NAWASHI · ORGANISER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Novosibirsk-centered Russian bondage artist, teacher, performer and local shibari-club organizer. Nawapedia.ru says Evgeniy Kano started as a master in 2014, developed a personal teaching methodology via practice and exchange with other bondage artists, helped organize theme parties, performed with shibari and took part in RopeFest in Saint Petersburg and Moscow plus Kinbaku Yuga in Krasnoyarsk. RopeFest Peterburg 2023 independently bills Kano as a Novosibirsk bondage artist who had already performed on the RopeFest stage, organizes a shibari club in Novosibirsk, teaches rope bondage by a personal method and was scheduled for the 6-8 May festival. RopeFest Moscow 2021 records Evgeniy Kano from Novosibirsk among main-stage performers, and Shibari.Guru preserves a RopeFest Peterburg 2020 performance trace.

### Kanso

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kanso/

Fact — Kanso is documented as an American kinbaku artist.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — KINBAKU ARTIST · TEACHER

Transmission — Bay Area kinbaku artist, performer, teacher and organizer using the public identity Kanso / Kanso Kinbaku. The Rope Circle instructor page characterizes him as a Bay Area-centered kinbaku artist, performer and sex-positive educator who encountered Japanese rope bondage during a 2006 performance in Japan and started The Rope Circle in 2017 to make shibari education more accessible. SF Station's 2019 Rope Circle class listing says he co-created Bondage Erotique in 2010, started Kiss of Rope in 2017, taught rope intensives around the United reports, performed in art / festival contexts and served as a regular SF Armory / Kink.com rope instructor for four years, teaching a year-long curriculum via suspension. Kinbaku Today's 2014 interview preserves his Bondage Erotique public identity, Blue collaboration context and interest in late-Showa / Nureki / Naka-inspired kinbaku.

### Roppongi Kaoru (六本木薫)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kaoru-roppongi/

Fact — Roppongi Kaoru (六本木薫) is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi.

Fact — Era: 1970s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · WRITER

Fact — Late-Showa Japanese kinbakushi active spanning 1970s-1980s SM magazines, manuals and photo books. Nawapedia records Roppongi entering kinbaku in 1975, serializing Camera Hunt in SM Select, publishing Shibari Scheme in Specially S&M, writing Sun&Moon memoir material about Naomi Tani ropework, and contributing how-to and Strange Fruit-era photo-book work.

### Kappashibari

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kappashibari/

Fact — Kappashibari is an Italian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Italian rigger, teacher and rope-community organizer using the public identity Kappashibari / Kappa. His public Instagram profile names him as official rigger of Regina Nera and co-founder of K Lab. Regina Nera's September 2025 official bio post gives the strongest public summary: Kappa became passionate about Japanese bondage as a young adult, has studied continuously for about twenty years, names Avalon, Aku Aku, Andrea Ropes, Vlada and Ren Yagami among the riggers he studied with, and says he created K Lab as a permanent space for regular courses, peer rope and workshops with international instructors. The same source places him in recurring Regina Nera context from 2024 onward.

### Karol Soulrope

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/karol-soulrope/

Transmission — Karol Soulrope is a Russian teacher in the Osada / Western.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · NAWASHI

Transmission — Saint Petersburg-centered rope artist, teacher and organizer. RopeFest / RopeSchool sources identify Karol Soulrope as an instructor at the Saint Petersburg branch of RopeSchool and as the organizer responsible for RopeFest Saint Petersburg and Bondage Cafe within the ArtCo ecosystem. RopeFest Moscow 2019 billed Karol as a Saint Petersburg presenter on the main stage and teacher of two shibari seminars; Karol's bio there says their rope practice began in 2011 and frames tying as a sensual, deep dialogue with a partner. RopeSchool's Karol profile says she completed basic Shibari training in the Osada Ryu school system and received a certificate in 2015; That record records Bondage Cafe and Yugen Shibari Convention among her active-scene performance contexts.

Fact — School: Osada / Western

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Auckland-centered shibari / kinbaku rigger, performer, host and teacher associated with Auckland Shibari Studio. ### Kasumi Hourai (蓬莱かすみ)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kasumi-hourai/

Fact — Kasumi Hourai (蓬莱かすみ), born 1980, is documented as a Japanese nawashi.

Chronology — Born: 1980

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — Japanese rigger born 1980, centered in Nagoya. Entered shibari in the late 1990s training under Akechi Denki, then refined her skills at Hajime Kinoko's Ichinawakai school. Studio Kink and Prague Shibari Festival bios identify the relevant Kitagawa as Salon Kitagawa's Kitagawa / 喜多川, where she received instruction in carabiner-less technique, locks, knots and bamboo suspension; Kinbaku Today's 2015 interview has Kasumi describe him as a respected Japanese-style rope influence. For a time she belonged to Naka-ryū and taught Naka Akira's style as his deshi; she is no longer his deshi and has established an independent Kasumi Hourai style. Since 2023 she has taught workshops spanning Europe, Oceania, Canada and Asia, with 2025 kinbaku workshops in Germany, Spain, Rome, Portugal, Australia, China, Taiwan and Thailand / Bangkok, and 2026 workshops planned for Belgium, France, Hong Kong, Australia, Spain and Bangkok. She also holds regular kinbaku events in Japan and currently focuses on training instructors in Kasumi-style kinbaku. In September 2024 opened her own Kinbaku Salon みやび処 (Miyabi-dokoro, 'elegant haven') in Nagoya for one-on-one lessons.

### Kata

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kata-littleredridingtree/

Fact — Kata is an Austrian rope switch.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ROPE SWITCH · BODYWORKER

Fact — Austria / Iceland-linked rope switch, bodyworker and community builder. Shibari Study's 2024 episode frames Kata as originally from Austria, then living in Iceland for eight years, where they discovered rope, connected with BDSM and spiritual communities, and created Spektrum Reykjavik and Reykjavik Ropes. Their work blends rope, touch, yoga, spirituality, sexological bodywork and community hosting; after moving back to southern Austria they continued rope classes, sessions and touch-centered collaborations.

Her official KIKO ROPE site and 2023 Shibari Study podcast place her in Los Angeles as a shibari instructor and Japanese dominatrix; in that interview she says Kazami Ranki taught her and names Koizumi as another teacher. Nawapedia records that in 2025 she became an official teacher of Ranki Kazami's style of kinbaku.

### Kina Neve

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kina-neve/

Transmission — Kina Neve is a British teacher.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · RIGGER

Fact — London-centered shibari educator, rigger, writer and multidisciplinary artist. Her official site says she has practiced shibari since 2016, has been part of the Anatomie Studio team since 2016, and teaches internationally. Kina characterizes an a-dogmatic approach grounded in education and psychology, with attention to individual learning styles, body and brain diversity, structure, physics, intimacy and shared emotional space. Her current shibari page records classes, co-teaching with Anna Bones, Fred Hatt, Aventurine, Kissiae and Mouse, and teaching highlights such as EURIX 2024 and Queer Knots 2025. She is also the author of Rope Compass for Bottoming, a 2026 community-informed guide to bottoming skills in shibari.

### Federico Kirigami

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kirigami/

Fact — Federico Kirigami is an Italian nawashi.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — Italian kinbakushi, educator and performer centered in Milan. Rope Tales says he has performed as a rigger since 2011, moved progressively toward Japanese-style bondage, established / teaches via School of Rope in Milan from 2016, and was a certified Kinbaku LuXuria instructor from 2018 to 2022 after an exam with Riccardo Wildties. With Marta Tenshiko he has worked since 2011, ran a smaller Milan venue from 2016 to 2020, and opened Yugen Studio in 2020. Current Rope Tales text frames Kirigami as working independently via Yugen Studio, editorial projects, performance and photography; his public framing is classical Japanese kinbaku / semenawa shaped by Sugiura Norio's photography and Naka Akira's bondage, with Kinbaku LuXuria presented as an earlier certification / training period rather than a present affiliation. Recent Yugen sources document collaboration with Sugiura Norio on workshops, online / print editorial projects and a 2025 Kinbaku Sajiki invitation. EURIX XII 2018 presenter with Tenshiko.

### Sahara Kise (佐原キセ)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kise-sahara/

Fact — Sahara Kise (佐原キセ), born 1893, is documented as a Japanese model in the Foundational.

Chronology — Born: 1893

Fact — Era: 1900s

Fact — MODEL · PERFORMER

Fact — Early Japanese model, actress and foundational visual partner in Seiu Ito's Taisho-period bondage photography. Nawapedia says she was introduced to Ito as a model in 1919, married him the same year and appeared as model for the snow-torment and early suspension photo sessions that became central to his historical torment imagery. Kinbaku Today independently frames her as one of the earliest SM models in Japan and as the willing partner in the 1921-1922 photographic works that helped establish Ito's notoriety.

Fact — School: Foundational

### KissMeDeadlyDoll

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kissmedeadlydoll/

Fact — KissMeDeadlyDoll is documented as an American self-suspender.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — SELF-SUSPENDER · TEACHER

Transmission — New York City-centered rope bondage artist, self-suspender, photographer, performer and teacher. Her own bio says she came to rope via demo modeling, then became primarily self-taught via trial and error, workshops and private lessons in the US and abroad. She teaches self-suspension and partnered tying via private lessons in NYC, creates rope performances and rope-focused photography, and works as a rope-bondage specialist for shoots, performances and content creation. Shibari Study's 2023 podcast bio frames KMDD as a switchy rope lover and ardent self-suspender who learned to tie herself after falling in love with bondage as a model, and the Shibari Study catalog includes her self-suspension tutorial / performance material. She contributed to Shay Tiziano's 2024 self-suspension anthology Soaring & Exploring.

### Kitagawa (喜多川)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kitagawa-salonk2/

Fact — Kitagawa (喜多川), born 1954, is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi.

Chronology — Born: 1954

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — KINBAKUSHI · TEACHER

Fact — Japanese kinbakushi and organizer of Salon Kitagawa / salonK2, distinct from Shuhei Kitagawa / 北川周平. Nawapedia names Kitagawa / 喜多川 as born in 1954, switching from cotton rope to asanawa in 1990 and organizing Kitagawa Salon in Tokyo from 2003 to 2009 before the group continued behind closed doors. The official salonK2 page says Salon Kitagawa started in 2003, Kitagawa partnered with kei in 2005 and salonK2 started in 2009; it frames the current Yokohama salon as a rope gathering and lesson space centered on semenawa / asanawa. Studio Kink and Prague Shibari Festival bios of Kasumi Hourai identify Salon Kitagawa as the source of her Kitagawa study, especially carabiner-less technique, locks, knots and bamboo suspension.

### Kitaro Kasukabe (カスカベキタロウ)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kitaro-kasukabe/

Fact — Kitaro Kasukabe (カスカベキタロウ) is documented as a Japanese rigger in the Naka-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · PHOTOGRAPHER · EDITOR

Fact — Tokyo-centered rigger, kinbaku photographer and editor / publisher of Kinbaku Magazine Roca. Studio Roca characterizes him as the operator of the Tokyo-centered studio dedicated to editing and publishing Roca. Discover Kinbaku's 2023 Japan report characterizes Kitaro as a young Japanese student of Naka Akira for rope and Erisa Sato for photography, making Roca via concept, photography, layout, print and fulfillment. KSB Magazine #15 featured him as a Japanese rigger / photographer and publisher of Roca; Yugen Studio / Rope Tales later presented him with Erisa Satoh in a 2025 online class on contemporary kinbaku photography culture in Japan.

Fact — School: Naka-ryū

### Kitty Rea

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kitty-rea/

Fact — Kitty Rea is a Romanian performer.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — PERFORMER · TEACHER

Safety / consent — Bucharest-centered artist, bondage performer, sex educator, consent / desire / kink coach and community organizer. InFLUX records Kitty Rea as part of the team managing the space, teaching consent, desire, kink, feminism, non-monogamy and ropes, with 7+ years of community organizing and 5+ years teaching sexuality, BDSM and non-monogamy. Event bios describe ten years of tying / being tied and international performance / teaching in London, Berlin, Lyon and Prague. EURIX 2017 beginner-course teacher, 2018 presenter and Danish Shibari Festival 2025 presenter with Rainbow.

### KJ

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kj-shibari/

Transmission — KJ is a Canadian teacher in the Kazami-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Toronto-centered Kazami-style shibari teacher and performer using the public identity KJ / @kj_shibari. The official Toronto Kinbaku Salon instructors page says KJ found his rope home at the salon, was recognized by Kazami Ranki as an instructor in his style in 2018, and has taught there since. The salon history page says KJ accepted the title of Shihan-Dai and Deshi to JD and AJ in November 2018. Prague Shibari Festival Autumn 2026 bills KJ and Lily as presenters, says KJ learned Kazami Ranki's style from Kazami Ranki and his deshi JD in Toronto, taught all levels weekly at Toronto Kinbaku Salon for seven years, teaches monthly at Birdhaus, and co-established the Suspended Harmony performance series with Tifereth.

Fact — School: Kazami-ryū

Fact — The Knaughty Otter is a British rigger.

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — London-centered queer rope artist and shibari teacher. The first-party site programming includes private shibari sessions, private lessons and group classes, while the OutSavvy organizer page records current beginner classes / GBTQ+ rope hangouts and a past Anatomie Studio London rope event.

### Knot_head

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/knot-head/

Fact — Knot_head is documented as an American rigger in the Osada / Western.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · PHOTOGRAPHER

Fact — US bondage rigger, photographer, educator and founder of Knot_head Nylon. His own bio and Rope Podcast profile describe roughly 25-30 years in bondage, starting from handcuffs, bathrobe belts and neckties, initially using Japanese-style ropework before gravitating to strict, clean, efficient Western bondage influenced by Jay Edwards, Matt Williams, Lew Rubens and Claire Adams. Teaches bondage and practical rope photography, with an emphasis on simple, strict positional bondage, predicaments and making bondage / photography approachable. Tying in the Tropics 2026 presenter with Bodacious_Minx.

Fact — School: Osada / Western

### Knot Posh

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/knot-posh/

Fact — Knot Posh is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PHOTOGRAPHER

Fact — Georgia-centered shibari rigger, instructor, photographer and Shibari ARTlanta founder. Shibari ARTlanta's private-lesson bio says he is centered in the Savannah area, began in 2019, developed his practice during the COVID period, teaches beginner foundations via suspension / performance / photography topics, and established Shibari ARTlanta as a classes-and-workshops community. Voudou Ropes' retreat page characterizes Knot Posh as an Atlanta-centered instructor and photographer tying in the same style as his teacher Voudou Ropes / Anastasia Suazo, and says he and partner Kat VanWylder established Shibari ARTlanta in March 2024.

Transmission — Boston-centered rope rigger, teacher and performer working as Knot Really Dead / Knotreallydead. Yugen Studio's public 2025 Performance Night listing says he began practicing bondage in 2018, has focused on semenawa since 2021, and performs with Specimen. Fact — RIGGER · PERFORMER

Fact — Ottawa-centered Canadian shibari artist, rigger and performer using the public identity Knotigan. Probe Ottawa's 2024 class and performance pages say he started artistic Japanese bondage about five years earlier, began performing in 2021 with appearances at NDC / La Nuit des Cordes and Tension Montreal's Soiree Shibari events, and creates suspension performances around art and engineering. MAX Ottawa's House of Kink listing bills Knotigan and Judy Joan as Ottawa-centered shibari artists and links his public Instagram profile.

### Knotty Devil

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/knotty-devil/

Fact — Knotty Devil is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · ARTIST · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — US shibari artist, teacher and performer with public Portland and Seattle event traces. Shibari Study Podcast EP 13 frames Knotty Devil's rope around flow, connected movement and person-aware tying; Rope Podcast EP165 adds a 2024 Portland / Oregon rope-switch framing and emphasizes connection, purpose, aesthetics and learning from multiple sources. Twisted Windows records Knotty Devil & Pixiebait as performers on the 2023 Seattle Erotic Art Festival Aerial & Bondage Stage. KinkFest 2026 records Knotty Devil and Pixie Bait for `Tethered Together`, a dual rope-suspension performance, and gives `@Knaughtydevil` as Knotty Devil's Instagram route. Creative Intimacy Program frames Knotty Devil as a shibari practitioner whose work emphasizes movement, connection, pauses and tension.

### Kokoro Kinbaku

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kokoro-kinbaku-atl/

Fact — Kokoro Kinbaku is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · PHOTOGRAPHER

Fact — Atlanta-centered photographer, artist and rope practitioner using the public identity Kokoro Kinbaku, the shibari alter ego of artist Beauregard Higgins. The official Guerrilla Hashira site frames Kokoro as a professional rigger / bakushi and frames the project around shibari suspension photography in hard-to-access, restricted or abandoned spaces, connecting rope imagery with architecture, decaying infrastructure and street-art aesthetics. The same site also routes inquiries for custom prints, sessions and collaborations. Lenscratch's 2024 exhibition post independently credits Beauregard Higgins / Kokoro Kinbaku on an Atlanta gallery image from the same project.

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Fact — Ottawa-centered Canadian rope top, instructor and performer using the public identity Kosmick / Kosmick Ropes. Their official site frames Kosmick Ropes as an Ottawa shibari rope-bondage practice for classes, private tuition, workshops, performances and artistic collaborations. Probe Ottawa class pages say Kosmick began studying rope bondage in 2011 and intensified that study after joining the Ottawa BDSM community in 2016; Probe also bills them as teaching Intro to Rope and Rope Foundations classes and as a regular rope instructor. The official performance page characterizes narrative and dynamic rope performances, self-suspension and partnered performance as top and bottom, with past-performance traces such as La Nuit des Cordes / NDC, Tension Montreal and Ottawa venues.

### Kristina Marlen

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kristina-marlen/

Transmission — Kristina Marlen is documented as a German teacher.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · PERFORMER

Fact — Berlin-centered sex worker, sex educator, bondage expert, performer and speaker for sex-positive culture. Has taught workshops and coaching for more than a decade, especially in German-speaking contexts, around sexual bodywork, touch, bondage and BDSM. In the Berlin rope map she connects the EURIX/IKSK performance scene with queer and FLINT*-oriented education, emphasizing embodiment, consent, improvisation and shame-free erotic self-knowledge.

### Krutý Sever

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/krutysever/

Fact — Krutý Sever is a Czech rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Fact — Czech rigger, shibari / BDSM educator, performer and bodywork practitioner using the public identity Krutý Sever / Ondřej Monhart. His official biography says he entered rope in May 2019, premiered his signature Vázání bez provazu workshop in 2022 after a joint workshop with his long-time shibari teacher Diana Vera, and now repeats workshops, private teaching, sessions and lectures in Czech cities and abroad. Refresher's 2025 interview frames him as one of the visible faces of the Czech BDSM scene and frames his public work around consent, communication, safety and the intersection of rope, bodywork, psychology and tantra; his own podcast archive and Lascivní.cz interview trail document broader public-education and Talkink lecture activity.

### Kuku

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kuku/

Fact — Kuku is a Chinese model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — MODEL · PERFORMER

Fact — China / Shanghai-associated shibari model and performer, often appearing in the same current-scene orbit as Davide LaoDai. EURIX records Laodai-Davide & Kuku from Shanghai as 2023 beginner-course teachers and Davide Laodai / Kuku as 2024 special guests. Shibari Lounge in Antwerp advertised a 2024 Shibari Night special performance by Laodai and Kuku, and a workshop day around Laodai's "Style of the Same Journey" with Kuku named in the visiting pair. Borderline Shibari's film "Kuku's Playdate" stars Kuku with Hua Hua, linking her to the Shanghai / Borderline Shibari film and education context.

### Andrea Kurogami

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kurogami/

Fact — Andrea Kurogami is an Italian rigger in the Naka-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · STUDIO FOUNDER

Transmission — Italian rigger, performer, teacher and co-founder of Peony Studio in Turin. With Shiawase, began studying kinbaku in September 2016 and attended their first Riccardo Wildties course in November 2016. Peony Studio says Andrea Kurogami became a certified Kinbaku LuXuria educator in February 2018; the studio's LuXuria page records him as certified to teach classes #0 and #1 in Milan / Turin. Kurogami and Shiawase later remained active as advanced-course students, performers and educators, and tied at the 25th Nawa Naka Kai in Tokyo in 2019 with Riccardo Wildties, Red Sabbath and Naka Akira.

Fact — School: Naka-ryū

### L-Corde

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/l-corde/

Fact — L-Corde is a rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · MODEL · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Luxembourg / Amsterdam-centered rope switch, rigger, model, self-suspender, teacher and performer using the public identity L-Corde. Their official site frames L-Corde as a queer, neurodivergent practitioner offering care via Shibari, yoga and sound, with rope teaching, private sessions, mentorship, workshops and rope / sound / movement formats. Visit Luxembourg City's 2025 and 2026 Intro to Shibari listings describe L-Corde as the Rainbow Center workshop lead now centered in Luxembourg, with workshops, private lessons and performances tailored to riggers, models and mixed-experience participants; Burlesque Luxembourg independently preserves a May 2025 decorative-rope workshop with L-Corde at Vedanza. A 2026 Naayya event page names the June 2026 Connective Shibari / Beginners Shibari Workshop teacher as Elsie C with the public `@l_corde_` Instagram handle, and the public Instagram profile characterizes L-Corde as based between Luxembourg and Amsterdam.

### La Virgen Blue

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/la-virgen-blue/

Transmission — La Virgen Blue is a Mexican teacher.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — TEACHER · EDUCATOR · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Mexico City-centered sex-positive educator, rope teacher and founder of Shibari Mexico. Official Shibari Mexico pages present La Virgen Blue / Blue as the founder of a local education-and-community hub built after she could not find a rope space in Mexico where she felt safe practicing. The same official bio characterizes five years of teaching and travel spanning Europe, Asia and Mexico, public performance and media work, podcast/talk participation, and kink-informed treatment background focused on safer, inclusive and non-normative sensual education.

### Laced

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/laced-nawa-musubi/

Fact — Laced is an Australian model in the Ichinawa-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — MODEL · TEACHER

Fact — Brisbane-centered rope model, aerialist, performer and educator working with Jake as Nawa Musubi. Shibari Study's 2024 episode frames Laced as central to Nawa Musubi's model-focused teaching method: she speaks from inside the rope, explains body feedback, rope feeling, strength, preparation and injury prevention, and helps make model education part of the class rather than a passive support role. Nawa Musubi programming includes group workshops and private lessons with Jake and Laced together when model input or advanced transition instruction is needed.

Fact — School: Ichinawa-ryū

### Lahtnor

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/lahtnor/

Fact — Lahtnor is a Swedish artist.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — ARTIST · TEACHER

Fact — Swedish rope artist and somatic guide whose work combines rope, movement and music into body-centered, emotionally resonant experiences. Lahtnor's official site frames the practice around performances, workshops, private lessons, rope sessions and handmade ropes, while Malmö Tantra Festival characterizes two decades of pedagogical experience, thirteen years of shibari practice, and workshops built around sensation, movement, stillness, intimacy and conscious touch. EURIX 2016 / 2018 presenter and Rope Spirit V performance collaborator with Saara Rei.

### LaMonstera

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/lamonstera/

Fact — LaMonstera is a Belgian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Brussels-centered rope artist, performer and teacher using the public identity LaMonstera / @_lamonstera. Instagram profile confirms the current profile and Brussels / Belgium location text. A Mia Sylvia performance page frames `Mia Sylvia x La Monstera` as a live collaborative rope / body / fantasy performance at Anatomie Studio in London, describing La Monstera's shibari approach as technically rigorous and theatrically sensitive. Bizouk's 2026 Velours Noir x LaMonstera listing in Martinique records a three-day shibari and erotic-arts program with talk / performance, objectification / constraint workshops, sensory table-work and an intermediate semi-suspension workshop around Mae and Agura shapes, and the Bizouk organizer profile makes Velours Noir a reusable Martinique host / community target. Linktree preserves public social surfaces such as TikTok and X / Twitter; FetLife is treated as private-platform context and is not published as a entry link.

### Davide LaoDai

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/laodai-davide/

Fact — Davide LaoDai is an Italian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — RIGGER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Italian professional rigger, shibari artist and teacher alternate public name: Laodai, a name he says the community gave him while he was living in China. Shibari Study's 2024 podcast bio credits him with founding Studio K, the first shibari studio in Shanghai, and creating Mainland China's first public rope party; it characterizes his current base as Thailand while traveling spanning Europe and Asia. His work centers on emotional connection, risk awareness, clean / minimal purpose-driven tying, and the idea that the untying process can be the climax of the connection. EURIX records Laodai / Davide as a 2023 beginner-course teacher, 2024 special guest, 2025 beginner-course teacher and 2026 master-class teacher.

### Lee Harrington

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/lee-harrington/

Transmission — Lee Harrington, born 1979, is documented as an American teacher.

Chronology — Born: 1979

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — TEACHER · WRITER

Transmission — US sexuality, spirituality and kink educator, author and artist whose rope-bondage work sits in the English-language Western education stream rather than a Japanese lineage. Passion & Soul characterizes Lee as a he/they educator, award-winning author/editor and teacher of 1100+ classes spanning North America, Europe, New Zealand and Australia, such as embodied rope bondage. IPG names Lee as the author of Shibari You Can Use and More Shibari You Can Use, both photographed by RiggerJay and published by Mystic Productions Press.

### Lew Rubens

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/lew-rubens/

Fact — Lew Rubens is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — RIGGER · SUSPENSION ARTIST · EDUCATOR

Fact — US Western-bondage rigger, suspension artist, educator and Lewbari creator. Seattle event copy says Lew Rubens started his bondage career in the Seattle area in 2001, set up BoundNDetermined.com in 2002, rigged for WaterBondage.com from 2006 to 2008 and later taught bondage classes around the world. Impact of Memphis' Lewbari weekend page frames him as an experienced rigger and suspension artist teaching a three-day `Bondage Basics via Suspension` curriculum; a 2015 Levitation listing in Quebec bills Lew Rubens and Drea Morgan for a full rope-suspension show and repeats the international workshop / demo context. The current Lewbari Store frames bondage tools, suspension rings and shibari rope sets created or approved by Lew Rubens.

### Lief Bound

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/lief-bound/

Fact — Lief Bound is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — US queer and trans rope artist, educator, performer and House of Bound creator. Shibari Study's 2023 episode frames Lief as a full-time rope artist and teacher in Los Angeles building House of Bound as a rope-education project. Rope Podcast EP127-128 frames Lief and Icarus / Icky as a Los Angeles queer / trans rope-educator and performance-artist pair focused on trauma-informed classes, top / bottom perspectives and rope-as-profession questions. House of Bound's bio says Lief began in the Boston rope scene, moved to Los Angeles in 2018, taught at TieHaus, Devil Mask Society and VoxBody, and later worked internationally with co-teacher / rope bottom Icky after their 2023 tour. The Subspace Exploration Project characterizes Lief and Icarus as an international rope-education pair now centered in the San Francisco area.

### Lilythothealley

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/lilythothealley/

Fact — Lilythothealley, born 2000, is a Thai nawashi.

Chronology — Born: 2000

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Bangkok-centered Thai shibari artist, rigger, performer and beginner-level teacher using the public identity Lilythothealley / Rata Shibari. Her official About page says she was born in 2000, began learning rope via online channels in 2020, started serious study under Unnamedminor in 2022, and later learned via Bangkok events and group workshops with visiting instructors such as CO, Laodai, Kasumi Hourai, Shiho Shinomiya, Murasaki and Seattle Shibari. The same site says she gave her first shibari performance in 2023, began teaching beginners in 2024, organized the late-2024 Nightmare Before Christmas: Shibari Workshop and Performance event, and had work featured in the 2025 For Your Pleasure shibari photo exhibition at Bangkok Art and Culture Centre.

### Lisa Anthony

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/lisa-anthony/

Fact — Lisa Anthony is a founder.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — FOUNDER · FACILITATOR

Fact — Malaysia-centered Shibari Malaysia founder, facilitator and embodied-rope writer. Cinema Oasis names Lisa Anthony as founder of Shibari Malaysia in the context of *The Ties That Bind Us*, while the official Shibari Malaysia Newsletter is authored by Lisa and frames the project as a philosophical understanding of life via ropes. Her official `How It All Started` essay characterizes beginning with self-tying, developing an embodied rope practice over several years, sharing it with hundreds of people and naming SOROPA as her method. Nicky Almasy's companion essay and YouTube title profile information support *The Ties That Bind Us* as a public mini-documentary about Lisa / Shibari Malaysia.

### Lochai

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/lochai/

Fact — Lochai is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — RIGGER · PHOTOGRAPHER · TEACHER

Transmission — US rope rigger, fetish / art photographer, teacher / organizer and adult-media bondage director using the public identity Lochai / NawaLochai. The Stranger's 2008 profile characterizes him as a rope-oriented bondage top / rigger and fetish / art photographer who moved from South Florida to the Bay Area to direct Hogtied.com, and says he had been one of the instructor / organizers at Shibaricon. DV8House's Mark De Viate education page independently places Lochai among the presenters at the first Austin Ropecraft Symposium in 2005. ROPECRAFT's 2017 Chicago and 2018 San Antonio pages preserve later presenter traces. The durable rope-specific role is historical education and event presence, not a general adult-studio catalogue.

Fact — MODEL · PERFORMER · AUTHOR

Her official Lalhow site frames current private shibari sessions and premium-content work under the Maîtresse Shibari branding. ### Louis Kordexe

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/louis-kordexe/

Fact — Louis Kordexe is a French shibari artist in the Ichinawa-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — SHIBARI ARTIST · TEACHER

Transmission — Tokyo-centered French shibari artist, photographer, teacher, performer and writer operating via Studio Y7. His official pages say he has lived in Tokyo for over twenty years, began rope in 2011, entered formal Ichinawakai training under Hajime Kinoko in 2019, later became an Ichinawa licensed instructor and ROPES member, and in 2021 opened Studio Y7 near Sasazuka / Shinjuku for private lessons, photoshoots and rope shows. His site also publishes articles and resources on jute-rope conditioning, technique versus connection, Tokyo kink shopping and shibari in Japanese nightlife, alongside a portfolio of Studio Y7 shibari photography. Public pages and event listings place him in Jyo-mon, 1OAK Tokyo and related Tokyo nightlife / performance contexts.

Fact — School: Ichinawa-ryū

### Ludvig Rigger

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ludvig-rigger/

Fact — Ludvig Rigger is a Bulgarian nawashi in the Yagami-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · PHOTOGRAPHER

Fact — Bulgarian nawashi centered in Sofia. Started tying in 2008, largely self-taught due to limited access to mentors in Bulgaria. From 2014 focused exclusively on shibari, quickly progressing to suspensions and public performances. In 2015 co-established Open Rope Space Sofia jam. In 2017 opened Sekibaku Shibari Dojo — the first Yagami-ryu dojo in Europe. Performs internationally spanning Romania, Greece, Germany, Belgium, and Spain. Highest-ranked Yagami-ryu instructor in Europe.

Fact — School: Yagami-ryū

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PHOTOGRAPHER

Fact — Brazilian shibarista, rigger educator and photographer using the public identity Luis Ferraz / Lord Bondage via Arte Shibari Brasil. Observatorio G's 2018 workshop article names him as a shibarista with ten years of practice at that time, teacher of workshops around Brazil, owner of Arte Shibari Brasil and participant in SPFW / ELLE fashion contexts. UOL Universa's 2024 shibari article credits him as a shibarista and creator of Arte Shibari Brasil, while public Instagram, Linktree and YouTube surfaces preserve the live @arteshibaribrasil / Luis Ferraz contact trail. A 2026 ShibariNews recheck surfaced Shibari Club Latin America as a direct regional community bridge, naming Luis Carlos Nunes Ferraz as one of its directors, founder of ArteShibari Brasil, rigger and shibari educator with more than 50 workshops.

### Luiza Jesus do Prado

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/luiza-prado/

Fact — Luiza Jesus do Prado, born 1988, is a Brazilian artist.

Chronology — Born: 1988

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ARTIST · SHIBARI MODEL · PERFORMER

Fact — Brazilian transdisciplinary artist, researcher and body-performance practitioner, publicly using Luiza Jesus do Prado / Luiza Prado and Hifa Cybe. Her official biography places her work between art, science, technology, memory, mental health, sexuality and body arts, while the dedicated shibari page names her as a shibari model and performer with self-tie, aerial, semenawa, stage and art-gallery experience. Public references connect her most clearly to the 2025 Shibari Experience Brazil performance with Hajime Kinoko at Ópera de Arame in Curitiba; a 2026 São Paulo Noite Das Cordas listing also frames Hifa Cybe's practice around body research using shibari, chains and BDSM aesthetics. Other names on her shibari page, such as Akira Nawa and Luminatus, are presented as bio/background context rather than documented relationships in this batch.

### Luke Ropes

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/luke-ropes/

Fact — Luke Ropes is a Czech rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · MODEL · TEACHER

Transmission — Prague-centered rigger, model, teacher and photographer using the public identity Luke Ropes / @luke.ropes. His official Subspace profile says he started rope as a rigger in 2020, began modeling in 2021, assisted Joan von Brook from early 2022 and joined Subspace's permanent independent-teacher team in March 2023. Prague Shibari Festival 2023 / 2024 bios and Shibaricamp archive sources place him as Joan von Brook's model / co-teacher, a Subspace Studio teacher and a Prague / Shibaricamp presenter; his current Instagram route frames him as a photographer and Subspace Studio team member.

### Randa Mai (乱田舞)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/mai-randa/

Fact — Randa Mai (乱田舞), 1959–2022, is documented as a Japanese nawashi.

Chronology — Born: 1959

Chronology — Died: 2022

Fact — Era: 1990s

Fact — NAWASHI · AV DIRECTOR

Fact — Japanese nawashi and adult-video director (25 March 1959 – 2022); self-described 'S&M entertainer' rather than nawashi. Alias loosely translates as 'wild dancer'. Versed in Shōrinji Kempō as a martial artist. Launched his career in the adult industry in the 1990s, collaborating with SM-themed magazines where his distinctive whip-and-discipline-driven style emerged. Created the influential 'Snake Bondage' (Jabaku / 蛇縛) series for Attackers and many instructional rope videos, and is often associated in subsequent historical writing with the iconic image of the rigger atop a gyaku-ebi tsuri suspension. In the early 2000s he became a dominant force in Tokyo's shibari scene, helping fill the void left by Akechi Denki (d. 2005). From 2016 he ran the SM bar Ranbukan, also known publicly as Bar Randa Mai, in Roppongi, and he performed and taught abroad in Europe and the United reports.

### Makoto Shibari

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/makoto-shibari/

Fact — Makoto Shibari is a Spanish rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · ARTIST

Fact — Barcelona-centered shibari / kinbaku artist using the public Makoto Shibari / `@makoto_shibari` identity. The first-party site frames the work as an art-led rope practice shaped by earlier dance, theatre and digital-art interests, records 2024 weekly private lessons with Guinju Gaijin and Espacio N'OS intro / intermediate / friction workshops, and records 2025 study contexts such as Aibunawa with Stuart and a Naoko workshop at Yugen Studio. The same site frames public offerings around performances, workshops, photography / video work, private sessions, event attendance and artistic projects. Linktree and Patreon corroborate the current Makoto Shibari identity and public creator / contact shell.

### Male Shibari

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/male-shibari/

Fact — Male Shibari is documented as a German rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · PHOTOGRAPHER · TEACHER

Transmission — Hamburg-centered rigger, photographer, teacher and visual artist using the public identity Male Shibari / Male_shibari. His official site frames a male-focused shibari / kinbaku photo practice built around private commissioned sessions, bodily communication, aesthetics and self-dyed ropes. Daruma Berlin's December 2024 Men in Rope weekend names him as Frank, a Hamburg-centered rigger and photographer, and frames the workshop around adapting Japanese-inspired bondage for male bodies; the following Daruma life-drawing listing names Male Shibari & Cons in the performance programme. Semjon Contemporary's 2025 Berlin exhibition page and Artsy / art press traces support the gallery-art layer without turning the exhibition into a separate lineage view hub.

### Mao Drakarys

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/mao-drakarys/

Fact — Mao Drakarys is an Italian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · SHIBARI ARTIST

Fact — Piacenza-centered Italian rigger and shibari artist using the public identity Mao Drakarys / Maodrakarys. Frammenti Rivista's 2021 shibari article frames Maodrakarys as the rigger in an interview with Rossane Graueaugen and La Femme Zulawskienne. Public Instagram, X, Telegram, Patreon and Facebook surfaces describe him as an Italian / Piacenza shibari artist, rope lover and kinbaku photo / video creator, with private-tuition and EMMEVI / SinSaturday context. In 2026, Cosa faccio a Piacenza's public event payload for CHEZ ART - Shibari Night named @maodrakarys2 and @artemisia_vv as one of the shibari performance pairs at ChezArt.

### Mao Escruceria

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/mao-escruceria/

Fact — Mao Escruceria is a Colombian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · PHOTOGRAPHER · EDUCATOR

Fact — Colombian rigger, photographer, educator and performer using the public identity Mao Escruceria / Sr. M / `@sr.m.106`. His current official ME-Photo site frames him as an erotic photographer and trainer in alternative sexualities, and explicitly says he is an active member of the BDSM scene in Colombia as a sensory dominant and kinbaku rigger. A public Magic.ly link hub ties the same Mao Escruceria identity directly to both `@maoe.fotografia` and `@sr.m.106` via a Bogotá-facing contact shell, while the public `@soy.yaritori` profile frames a Pereira rope collective and names `@sr.m.106` as gestor. An interview episode and the 2023 `Vergajo 4` rope-zine independently preserve the Mao Escruceria / Señor M alias pairing inside the Colombian rope scene.

### Marc GentLeeBrutal

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/marc-gentleebrutal/

Fact — Marc GentLeeBrutal is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Austin, Texas rope artist, teacher and performer also credited as GentLeeBrutal. Sexual Health Alliance's 2016 / 2018 Masquerade bios say GentLeeBrutal first picked up rope around 2012, was drawn to connective and sensual Japanese bondage, studied with Naka Akira, Pedro Cordas and Kanso from Bondage Erotique, and became known for sadistic and sensual sides. Bondage Expo Dallas 2023 records Gentleebrutal & PedalGirl as presenters who taught intensives, privates and performed together locally and spanning US venues. 1701 Studio frames Marc / GentLeeBrutal in its Japanese-influenced rope-bondage teaching context and Austin Rope Collective curriculum. EURIX records Marc / Jordyn as 2023 special guests, Marc as a 2024 beginner-course teacher and Marc GentLeeBrutal as a 2026 master-class teacher.

### Marceline VQ

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/marceline-vq/

Fact — Marceline VQ is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · MODEL · TEACHER · ARTIST

Fact — New York City-centered shibari artist, model and educator alternate public name: Marceline_VQ. Rope Podcast EP120 and Austin Rope Slingers describe her work as technically complex rope art shaped by human-sculpture, geometry, physics, analytic-philosophy and empathy themes. Her practice moves spanning self-tying, tying others and modeling for other riggers, and public references frame her as working with Anya Demure on inventive rope imagery. Austin Rope Slingers hosted her Breaking the Rules workshop / profile page, while a 2021 Daily Beast profile corroborates her public rope-artist / educator identity and her physics-of-rope teaching frame.

### Margherita Tisato

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/margherita-tisato/

Fact — Margherita Tisato is an Italian rope educator.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ROPE EDUCATOR · SOMATIC ARTIST

Fact — Italian / New York-centered embodiment, rope and movement educator, performer and dancer using the public identity Margherita Tisato / Mrghy. Her official site frames her work around embodied practices for liberation, dance, Butoh, trauma-informed yoga, somatic movement, body suspension, shibari and flesh-hook suspension. The site's rope-education page programming includes `Somatics for Rope` workshops around grounding, touch, hypermobility, sensation seeking, embodiment, trauma and resilience, while its classes page records shibari-retreat, `Roped In` and `Rope 0` offerings. Temple New York's official profile records Mrghy in the Temple team shell, and Bushwick Daily independently characterizes Margherita as a butoh, movement and trauma-informed educator and core practitioner at Temple. VoxBody and Peace Temple retreat pages further corroborate her public somatics-for-rope / shibari-retreat teaching context.

### Maria Morbid

Fact — Maria Morbid is documented as a German queer shibari artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — QUEER SHIBARI ARTIST · TEACHER

Transmission — Leipzig-centered queer shibari artist, rigger and teacher. Maria Morbid's public Wonderlink and Bluesky surfaces describe workshops, FLINTA rope meets, private tying sessions, private tuition, performances and photoshoots, with current Instagram, Telegram, email and Bluesky contact routes. Shibari.Events records Maria as presenter and organizer of a Leipzig beginner workshop, Fuck Yeah Sexshopkollektiv records 2026 Hamburg beginner workshops and a Queer Rope Jam with Maria, and QueeringDefaults' 2022 Leipzig programme points FLINTA rope-meet participants to Maria for shibari beginner workshops. RadioBlau's July 2025 Queer um Vier page independently records an interview with Maria Morbid about tying / shibari in a Leipzig queer-radio context.

### Marie Sauvage

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/marie-sauvage/

Fact — Marie Sauvage is a French nawashi in the Ichinawa-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — NAWASHI · PERFORMER · TEACHER

Fact — Paris-centered French rope artist and performer with New York City origins. Her official about page says she began rope practice in 2017 after meeting Hajime Kinoko at Burning Man, was invited to Tokyo to work at his studio, and later traveled with Kinoko and independently for performances and installations. Hajime Kinoko's public lessons page records Marie Sauvage among official Ichinawakai instructor-license holders, while her public Telegram channel characterizes later Yagami / Ren Yagami study in Tokyo as a separate influence.

Fact — School: Ichinawa-ryū

### Mark De Viate

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/mark-de-viate/

Fact — Mark De Viate is an Australian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Fact — Sydney-centered shibari educator, performer and community organizer, publicly documented via DV8House from 2001 and Studio Kink Sydney from its 2015 formation. Studio Kink frames Mark as a co-founder and instructor, a shibari educator / performer, a 2005 Austin Ropecraft Symposium presenter and later ShibariCon educator whose rope work was refined via Osada Steve mentorship in Japan and grounded in Akechi Denki tradition context. The ShibaRicon 2015 programme gives the stronger 2006 Osada influence wording, places his rope style in Akechi Denki lineage context, notes time tying with Hajime Kinoko, Akechi Kanna and Yukimura Haruki, and records an invited 2011 Toubaku performance trace. Studio Kink's online program includes Mark's Introduction to Shibari, Fundamentals and DV8 Hishi TK courses.

Fact — Mark Varley is a British rigger.

### Marta Skinnysticks

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/marta-skinnysticks/

Fact — Marta Skinnysticks is a Ukrainian artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — ARTIST · TEACHER

Fact — Lviv-centered Ukrainian artist, shibari practitioner, educator and founder of She.She.Shibari Studio. Gal-Info reported that she opened the She.She.Shibari school on December 27, 2021 at the Rema factory site in Lviv, after earlier rope-art exhibition and performance work. Espreso/Zahid's 2023 interview characterizes her as an architect by education, long-time design/art teacher, clothing-brand artist and founder of the Lviv shibari studio/school; she says she first encountered shibari via visual art references after time in China, began as a model, then studied with a Lviv practitioner and built her own rope practice. Shibari Camp Poland 2026 records her as a presenter and founder of She.She.Shibari Studio, with work spanning traditional Japanese rope technique, contemporary art, somatic / psychological practice, safety, boundaries and community education. EURIX XXVIII spring 2026 separately records her as Marta Skinnysticks from She.She.Shibari / Lviv.

### Martín Kinbaku

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/martin-kinbaku/

Fact — Martín Kinbaku is an Argentine rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Argentine shibari practitioner, teacher, performer, writer and analog photographer working as Martín Kinbaku / 4toscuro. Cuarto Oscuro's current official profile characterizes him as a disciple of Haru Tsubaki sensei, says he has studied Federico Kirigami's style since 2023, and places his present teaching activity spanning La Plata and Buenos Aires. The current Cuarto Oscuro teaching page frames his method around communication, expressiveness, technique and training via regular classes, private lessons and intensive workshops, while the official RopeTales 2026 event page shows him leading preparatory classes on Kirigami's style and technique for the Argentina stop of the Kirigami / Marta Tenshiko Latin American tour. A June 2026 Kinbaku Íntimo page also places Martín among the house performers for a six-year local performance-night line at Genya Kinbaku in Buenos Aires.

Martin Oskar Kramer's own photography site names him as a Berlin-centered non-commercial photographer open to collaboration and preserves rope-adjacent artist albums such as a Berlin bondage shoot and a creative bondage session photographed in 2016. Fact — ORGANIZER · ROPE PRACTITIONER

Fact — Italian-born, Málaga-centered rope practitioner and community organizer. Martina initiated and co-established Shibari Málaga, bringing shibari into a wider wellbeing and event-organizing practice. Since 2024 the community has held recurring workshops and social jams in Málaga, while Martina's own rope journal centers presence, consent and embodied connection.

### Masato Marai

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/masato-marai/

Transmission — Masato Marai, born 1957, is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Akechi lineage.

Chronology — Born: 1957

Fact — Era: 1980s

Transmission — NAWASHI · WRITER · TEACHER

Fact — Japanese kinbakushi born 1957. In the 1970s attended university and worked in architectural design while writing SM stories for SM Select and SM Fan magazines. Served as assistant to Chimuo Nureki and Norio Sugiura for SM Fan. Authored several instructional volumes: the 'Secrets of Shibari Tying for SM' series (books 1–4, Inter Labo, 2002) and 'Masato Marai's Art of Bondage for Advanced 2' (Sanwa Publishing, 2010). His techniques continue to influence contemporary practitioners.

Transmission — School: Akechi lineage

### Masha Mokosh

Fact — Masha Mokosh is a Serbian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Fact — Serbian shibari artist, performer and aerial instructor centered in Belgrade / New Belgrade, publicly documented as Masha Mokosh and Maša Štrbac. RTS Radio Beograd 2 characterizes her as a shibari artist and one of the founders of the Serbian shibari scene, and notes monthly Belgrade gatherings organized via her House of Mokosh brand. House of Mokosh's own class PDF frames Masha as founder of Serbia's first shibari studio, with more than half a decade of practice and classes / sessions ranging from floorwork to suspension. ### Master K (春虎)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/master-k/

Fact — Master K (春虎), 1950–2026, is documented as an American nawashi in the Yukimura-ryū.

Chronology — Born: 1950

Chronology — Died: 2026

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · WRITER

Transmission — American kinbakushi, teacher and historian centered in Los Angeles (18 April 1950 - 26 January 2026). Author of "Shibari: The Art of Japanese Bondage" (2004) and "The Beauty of Kinbaku" (2008), both important English-language works on Japanese rope history and practice. Certified Yukimura-ryu instructor (December 2013) after studying with Yukimura Haruki from 2010; Nawapedia records him founding LA Rope Dojo with Zetsu in 2013, and LA Rope's 2014 Naka Akira visit post names Master K and Zetsu as LA Rope Dojo proprietors. Also worked as a consultant and rigger for film/music-video productions and was named successor to Urado Hiroshi's school of kinbaku in 2013.

Fact — School: Yukimura-ryū

### Master Set

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/master-set/

Transmission — Master Set is a Russian teacher.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · NAWASHI · ORGANISER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Moscow bondage artist, teacher, performer and organizer in the RopeFest / ArtCo ecosystem. The ArtCo explainer names Master Set as the organizer responsible for RopeFest Moscow, while RopeFest Petersburg 2019 billed Master Set as a Moscow participant whose bondage practice began with self-study in the mid-2000s before turning toward dynamic suspensions. RopeFest Moscow 2023 channel archives describe Set / Master Set as a bondage artist, sadist and kinkster, co-organizer and regular host of RopeDay events in Moscow, practicing rope bondage since 2007, presenting stage shows, public actions, workshops and private teaching. Shibari.moscow photo traces also connect Master Set to RopeDay Moscow.

### Matija Vid Peček Black

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/matija-vid-pecek-black/

Fact — Matija Vid Peček Black is a Croatian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · ARTIST · PHOTOGRAPHER · TEACHER

Fact — Zagreb-centered contemporary rope artist, performer, photographer and self-tying educator alternate public name: Nikki / Nikky Lapore and MVPBlack. Their official site frames Matija as a published conceptual rope artist whose shibari practice combines rope bondage with darkness, introspection, emotional exploration, ritual / nature imagery, photography, installations, exhibitions and workshops. Rope Podcast EP218 frames their practice around self-expression, self-tying, nature, organic materials and Croatian rope culture. A 2026 public Patreon post records a self-tie workshop organized with Knot Noir Zagreb, and The first-party site notes that some of their photographs appeared in Shay Tiziano's 2019 self-suspension book Tying & Flying.

Transmission — Hamburg-centered kinbakushi, teacher, publisher and performer born in 1953. Started live rope shows and bondage teaching in the late 1990s / around 2000, wrote rope-bondage instructional material such as German-language bondage handbooks, and taught spanning Germany, Italy, Spain, France, Denmark, Norway and Russia in the early 2000s.

### May Dosem

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/may-dosem/

Fact — May Dosem is a French rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Paris-centered shibari artist, performer and educator. Started shibari in 2014 and became passionate about rigging after attending Paris rope jams; learned from teachers with varied styles and names Naka Akira's expressive and emotionally powerful style as a main influence. Erosphere 2019 program context records May among teachers who had led rope courses at Place Des Cordes and other French / European venues. Co-created Port d'attaches in 2019 and teaches regular floor-rope classes there. EURIX 2017 and 2019 presenter, such as with Benoit Descordes.

### Maya Vasilevskaya

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/maya-vasilevskaya/

Fact — Maya Vasilevskaya is a Russian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Moscow-centered Russian dominatrix, rope practitioner, teacher, performer and rope maker. Nawapedia.ru names Maya Vasilevskaya as a Moscow bondage artist. The Village's 2019 profile characterizes her as a professional dominatrix and one of the few women in Moscow who ties and suspends men in Japanese-style shibari, also teaching bondage and female domination workshops. Her Livemaster profile says she has tied since 2012, works privately and for photo / party contexts, processes and dyes jute ropes, and gives individual tying lessons. Master Set's RopeFest Peterburg 2018 report highlights her dynamic work in the festival show program.

### Metusa-nawa

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/metusa-nawa/

Fact — Metusa-nawa is documented as a German model.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — MODEL · PERFORMER

Fact — German current-scene rope model and performer publicly using the name Metusa-nawa, with `EVA - Metusa nawa` preserved from a 2023 Querverbindungen guest profile and `@metusa_nawa` confirmed as the public handle. Querverbindungen's official RopuNawa workshop page says she started rope in Basel in May 2017 via first rope experiences at Tying with Friends with Ropu Nawa and Isith, later broadened that experience with various riggers such as Tifereth, Ren Yagami, DeviFoxKitsune, Michael Ropeknight and Boris Mosafir, and centers her rope work on access to emotions and nonverbal communication with the tying partner. The official Onawa Asobi Europe homepage currently records `Happa Von Kruemel & Metusa-nawa (De)` in the first 2026 Antwerp lineup.

### Michael RopeKnight

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/michael-ropeknight/

Fact — Michael RopeKnight is documented as a German nawashi in the Yukimura-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Transmission — German nawashi, teacher and owner of Fourelements Kinbaku Lounge in the Cologne/Bonn region. His official Fourelements profile says he practiced bondage for three decades before focusing on shibari / kinbaku, made several Japan trips, became an official student of Osada Steve, and was introduced by Osada to Yukimura Haruki because of his communication-oriented approach. He studied extensively with both teachers, received a Yukimura-ryu instructor license in 2015 and an Osada Steve instructor appointment in 2017. Fourelements was established in 2014 as an Osada-ryu / Yukimura-ryu dojo and Kinbaku Lounge. His teaching emphasizes communication, emotional dialogue, intention, empathy and Ki; a 2024 Tying With Friends bio says he retired as an official Osada-ryu instructor that year because physical limitations no longer allowed him to teach high-level suspensions, and now focuses on Ropeknight's Yukimura-ryu. Teacher / influence context for Etheros (Belgium), and presenter at EURIX XI 2017 and Prague Shibari Festival 2019.

Fact — School: Yukimura-ryū

### Midori

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/midori/

Transmission — Midori is documented as an American teacher in the Osada / Western.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — TEACHER · WRITER

Safety / consent — Japan-born, San-Francisco-trained kink educator and pioneer of contemporary Western shibari pedagogy. Founder of Rope Dojo, ForteFemme and Consent Dojo. Author of 'The Seductive Art of Japanese Bondage' (one of the first English-language shibari books). Cited by Felix Ruckert as a first-generation teacher.

Fact — School: Osada / Western

### Mie Neko

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/mieneko/

Fact — Mie Neko is documented as a German switch.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — SWITCH · TEACHER

Transmission — Ruhrgebiet-centered rope switch, teacher, performer and organizer. Her own site says she began tying in 2017 and has been present as a teacher and performer at national and international events since 2020, while organizing her own events and workshops. Her teaching emphasizes smaller-rigger experience, rope-model education, safety in rope, body manipulation, power dynamics, semenawa and emotional connection via rope, with workshops in Essen, NRW, and private tuition in Dortmund. Her rigger profile frames her style as independent and shaped by many inspirations rather than one single school, with model experience as a major source for her rope practice. EURIX 2021 beginner-course teacher, 2022 / 2024 presenter and 2026 presenter with SickDucks.

### Mihail Balabanchik

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/mihail-balabanchik/

Fact — Mihail Balabanchik, 1975–2016, was a Belarusian rigger.

Chronology — Born: 1975

Chronology — Died: 2016

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · TRANSLATOR

Fact — Belarusian shibari master, organizer and translator from Minsk. Nawapedia.ru gives his dates as November 11, 1975 to February 6, 2016, characterizes him as one of the rope-bondage pioneers in Belarus, organizer of shibari community meetings in Minsk and beyond, and a major translator for the post-Soviet Russian-speaking kinbaku scene under the name Mihail_A. BondageFest 2007 and 2008 traces describe Mihail_A from Minsk demonstrating classical shibari in a master-class format and later presenting `Shibari: rare techniques`, pushing his public teaching trace back into the late-2000s Russian-language BDSM / rope scene. CityDog documented him leading a Minsk shibari master class in May 2015, such as safety warnings around nerve injury. Vogue UA later characterizes attending a Shibari Fest Ukraine / Shibari Projects Ukraine event that began with a lecture by Mihail Balabanchik. RopeSchool's theory PDF preserves one of his translations of Akechi Denki / Osada Steve material and cites the old shibari-by.com source.

### Miiko Rose

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/miiko-rose/

Fact — Miiko Rose is an Australian shibari performer.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — SHIBARI PERFORMER · FACILITATOR

Fact — Western Australia shibari performer and sensual / tantric arts facilitator using the public identity Miiko Rose / Shana. Humanitix's `Melt - A Shibari Ritual` listing names Shana AKA Miiko Rose as a shibari performer, facilitator and bodyworker, and frames the Dany Cox / Miiko Rose host profile as a duo performing and creating together. X-Press Magazine's 2025 `Tethered` review separately names Miiko Rose as Dany Cox's performance partner. TicketTailor corroborates the Shana / Miiko alias by saying Shana appears on stage for shibari performances under the Miiko Rose name, while the public Instagram route frames Miiko as a rope switch, performer and sensual-arts facilitator.

### Mikhail Karlson

Fact — Mikhail Karlson is a Belarusian bondage artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — BONDAGE ARTIST · PERFORMER

Fact — Belarusian / Minsk bondage artist and performer using the public identity Mikhail Karlson / Master Karlson. CityDog's 2020 report from a Minsk shibari festival names him as a Minsk bondage artist appearing on stage at the event, while the public Instagram profile confirms the current `@master_karlson` identity. Kinky Party Tbilisi later records Mikhail Karlson in its Secret Fantasy practice roster for impact play, electro play and wax play; The page presents treated as broader kink-performance context rather than shibari lineage or teaching authority.

### Milla Reika (ミラ嶺花)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/milla-reika/

Fact — Milla Reika (ミラ嶺花) is an Australian kinbakushi.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — KINBAKUSHI · TEACHER

Transmission — Australian-born, Japan-centered kinbaku artist, practitioner, performer and teacher. Kinbaku Today's 2019 interview characterizes her as leaving Australia in 2006, arriving in Japan in 2009, beginning kinbaku at the end of 2010, and becoming known in Japan for dynamic suspension transitions, creative erotic stage work and a separation between stage rope and private kinbaku practice. Her own official site frames Milla Reika as a kinbaku artist / practitioner / performer, with Senkaku Studio and the Kinbaku Diaries online video library. During the 2020 pandemic shift she developed Kinbaku Diaries as a learning-via-observation resource rather than a conventional pattern tutorial.

### Mino

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/mino-ropes/

Fact — Mino is a French performer.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — PERFORMER · TEACHER

Transmission — Marseille-centered French circus artist, rope performer and shibari teacher, active publicly as Mino / @mino_ropes and historically listed as Alex Mino. His current Linktree frames the practice around circus, shibari, downtempo music and immersive experiences, with live routes to Mino, Teppish People and Groovin Carpet projects. Shibaricamp's 2019 archive announced Alex Mino from Marseille with Lello Li from Berlin, describing a dance / play-informed shibari approach shaped after they met at EURIX. Rope Artists International and EURIX sources place Alex Mino / @mino_ropes in 2018-2019 Berlin performance contexts and the official EURIX archive records Misungui Bordelle & Alex Mino at EURIX XX / Spring 2022. Lyon Shibari's 2026 announcement keeps Alex Mino active as a floor-rope and dramaturgy workshop teacher.

Fact — STUDIO OWNER · PERFORMER

Fact — Hudson Valley dominatrix, performer and venue owner using the public identity Miss Adah Vonn. ### Miss Eris

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/miss-eris/

Fact — Miss Eris is a Brazilian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Fact — Brazilian shibari artist, performer and educator working from Barcelona and, in Prague Shibari's 2017 presenter wording, between Barcelona and Berlin. Prague Shibari says her rope journey started in 2012, grew via workshops, photoshoots and performance invitations, and later expanded into teaching from her own point of view; the same presenter page supports her 2017 Prague Shibari Festival appearance with Maya Homerton. ShibariNews frames her as a Barcelona-centered educator with strong self-suspension and workshop presence, while the existing By Night source places Miss Eris directly inside Gorgone's Study On Falling preview at Place des Cordes in Paris on March 11, 2017.

### Miss Fay

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/miss-fay/

Fact — Miss Fay is documented as an American somatic shibari artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — SOMATIC SHIBARI ARTIST · TEACHER

Transmission — Maine-rooted, Houston-centered somatic shibari artist, yoga teacher and performer using the public identity Miss Fay / Fay / Nadia. Holistic Kink Coaching characterizes her as the creator of Knotty Yoga, a yoga-and-shibari fusion where rope supports deeper stretches and body presence, and says seven years practicing Bondage Directed Somatic Medicine with Sir Pocketz shaped her sessions. The page also says she completed 200-hour yoga teacher training in 2022, co-facilitates Double Dom Immersion and Partnered Sensation Journey with Sir Pocketz, and performs spanning fire, Lyra and bellydance. Dark Tantra Temple's June 2026 event calendar records Fay-led Intro to Rope and Connection, Knotty Yoga, Rope Beyond Contact and Radiant Flames events in Maine. Current Instagram frames `@faytied` as a somatic shibari artist offering experiences, workshops, performances and retreats.

### Miss Gen

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/miss-gen/

Transmission — Miss Gen is an Australian teacher in the Kazami-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · PERFORMER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Australia-centered Kazami-Ryu teacher, performer, organizer and Japanese interpreter publicly appearing as Miss Gen / MissEvilGen, centered on Evil Girls HQ in South East Queensland. Evil Girls HQ reports that Miss Gen was appointed a qualified Kazami-Ryu instructor and deshi to Kazami Ranki in late 2015, and was announced in 2018 as Shihan / Head of School for the Oceania region; its 2025 cabaret page frames October 2015 as the point when she received deshi title and permission to teach Kazami's style in Australia, and says she built Kazami-Ryu into a large Australasia school with instructors in Brisbane, Perth, Auckland and Christchurch. Evil Girls HQ's 2020 intensive profile adds Australia / Japan performance context, Japanese-language interpreter work for Sydney Rope Festival 2013-2014, Kazami Ranki, Nawashi Kanna and Kasumi Hourai Australian tours, and Yoi Yoshida's O-Nawa 2014 in Japan. Studio Kink's 2019 Hourai Kasumi page uses the Miss Evil Gen spelling and characterizes her as an interpreter-grade Japanese translator and rope-aware educator. A 2025 podcast page gives current handles @MissEvilGen and @miss_gen_does_rope, and frames her as head of Evil Girls HQ with 25+ years rope experience.

Fact — School: Kazami-ryū

### Misungui Bordelle

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/misungui-bordelle/

Fact — Misungui Bordelle is a French performer.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — PERFORMER · EDUCATOR

Fact — French performer, professional domina specialized in shibari / kinbaku, sex educator and activist. Prague Shibari Festival characterizes her as a performer, X actress, domina and sex educator whose work grew from gender studies into images, videos, shows and workshops exploring nudity, sexuality, intimacy and perversity. Daruma Berlin characterizes her as a performer, sex worker and activist working spanning neo-burlesque, shibari, BDSM, feminist / queer post-porn, education, podcasting and writing. Her official site and current Kink Study profile keep shibari / BDSM, performance and sex-education teaching as active public contact context. Shibari Study places Le jardin perche in Bagnolet as her private shibari teaching / session / small-workshop space. EURIX 2022 presenter with Alex Mino and 2023 presenter with Tree Ropes.

### Miumi-U

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/miumi-u/

Fact — Miumi-U is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Ichinawa-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Transmission — Japanese mistress, nawashi and author. Trained at Ichinawa-kai under Shishi-waka and Kurage Umitsuki, then took advanced classes as a student of Hajime Kinoko for one year. Compiled the English-language manual "Miumi-U Teaches Japanese Shibari" with partner Kao, who had performed with her in Tokyo shibari clubs; the book was published by Kahboom in 2016 as a photo-rich practical introduction to Japanese rope bondage.

Fact — School: Ichinawa-ryū

Transmission — TEACHER · BODYWORKER

Transmission — Stockholm-centered shibari teacher, artist and somatic / embodiment practitioner using the public identity Molly Maria via Sensual Shibari. Her official site frames Sensual Shibari as rope education for intimacy, connection, creativity and sensual embodiment, with online courses, private tutoring / sessions and recurring Stockholm workshops. Molly's own story characterizes a 2016 entry into BDSM and a practice focused on presence, sensuality, tantra, dance / movement, massage, meditation and deep contact via Japanese rope bondage. ### Monko (縄師モンコ)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/monko/

Fact — Monko (縄師モンコ), died 2022, is documented as a Japanese nawashi.

Chronology — Died: 2022

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — NAWASHI · PHOTOGRAPHER · TEACHER

Transmission — Japanese nawashi, teacher and rope photographer publicly documented via Monko / Nawashi Monko until his death in 2022. His official Shibari & Lens blog characterizes the archive as Monko's world of rope and photography, with later public activity centered on Kamata Shibari Kobo / a Tokyo Kamata photo base, while a 2018 self-retrospective names Kinbaku no Yakata as his first official website and Kinbaku Gakuen as the second. SMpedia's Nawa-dokoro chronology places Monko no Mise in 2003 and says later regular activity continued as Zoku Monko no Mise / Kinbaku Gakuen. Nawapedia records the Monko website opening in 2004, and Osada Steve's official chronology records a March 2004 joint performance with Nawashi Monko at Loft Plus One. Barcelona sources document Monko's 2013 / 2015 La Orbita de IO workshop visits and frame him as a highly technical Japanese nawashi whose practice centers on precise rope and photography rather than stage-show style; the organizer account characterizes him as primarily self-taught via observation and reinterpretation.

### Mosaic Cat (Maomao)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/mosaic-cat-maomao/

Fact — Mosaic Cat (Maomao) is a Chinese shibari artist.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — SHIBARI ARTIST · TEACHER

Transmission — Shanghai-centered Chinese shibari artist, teacher, performer, photographer and organizer using the public identity Mosaic Cat (Maomao). Karada House's public organizer profile says Maomao began shibari in Taiwan in 2017, returned to Shanghai in 2019, and became active in the local rope community via workshops, event curation and practitioner-network building. That record says she has taught more than 500 students, performed in more than 70 shibari shows, and works spanning narrative, experimental performance, installation and photographic rope artworks exhibited in China, Germany and the UK. Public 2025-2026 event pages place her in That Rope Place's Singapore performance-night programming and Karada House's Virtual Queerope 2026 programme. Shibari Study names her as founder of Rope Links Shibari Salon, and a July 2026 public Instagram event post advertises a Rope Links gathering organized by `@mosaic_cat_maomao`.

### Mouse
