Fact — Mouse is a British bottom.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — BOTTOM · RIGGER · TEACHER

Fact — London-centered queer rope bottom, rigger and educator. Karada House's organizer profile says Mouse entered rope in early 2018, started as a bottom, later discovered tying, and had four years of teaching experience by the profile text. The profile frames their work around creativity, technical knowledge, adaptability, accessibility, body diversity and racial diversity in rope and kink scenes. Thick Hitches Conference records Mouse as a UK facilitator for the 2025 in-person Berlin edition and the 2026 virtual edition; the 2025 programme credits Mouse with Freeform Partials, Power Partnerships, and The Art of Slow Rope sessions.

### Mukai Kazuya (向井一也)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/mukai-kazuya/

Fact — Mukai Kazuya (向井一也) is documented as a Japanese performer.

Fact — Era: 1970s

Fact — PERFORMER · WRITER

Fact — Historical Japanese SM-stage performer and writer active from the early 1960s via the 1980s. SMpedia frames Mukai Kazuya as the leading partner of Aoki Junko on strip-theater and small-stage SM productions, founder of the troupe `Atarashii Nami` and later organizer of `Original no Kai`, with a role significant enough to the history of SM stage that both Eikichi Osada and Keiyu Tamai are named in his orbit.

### Murasaki Haruan (紫)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/murasaki-haruan/

Fact — Murasaki Haruan (紫) is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi in the Yukimura-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — KINBAKUSHI · TEACHER

Transmission — Tokyo-centered Japanese kinbakushi, professional mistress, teacher and photographer working as Murasaki / Haruan. Nawapedia records her professional-mistress debut in 2002, apprenticeship to Yukimura Haruki from 2006, and April 9 2014 certification as the 10th instructor for Yukimura Style Rope Play; the Yugen Studio workshop bio adds that she met Yukimura in 2003, assisted his video shoots, became one of his first formal students when classes started in 2006, and accompanied him as teaching assistant to Los Angeles in 2012 and Sydney / Melbourne in 2014. She was the first woman and first Japanese person certified by Yukimura, receiving the Haruan (春晏) name. After Yukimura's death in 2016 she began accepting a limited number of students, taught a 2017 LA workshop via Zetsu / LA Rope Dojo, later taught at Yugen Studio Milan, The Temple NYC, Studio K Shanghai and Karada House Berlin, and is presented by Karada House as an official successor of Yukimura-ryu kinbaku and founder of Grace Rope Lab. Kinbaku Today frames her later photography with Shizuki Iroha as part of keeping Yukimura-ryu alive.

Fact — School: Yukimura-ryū

### Muryu ZOU (夢流ZOU)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/muryu-zou/

Fact — Muryu ZOU (夢流ZOU), born 1955, is documented as a Japanese director.

Chronology — Born: 1955

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — DIRECTOR · NAWASHI

Transmission — Japanese director, performer and kinbaku figure also documented as Kuroda Toru. Important here as Naka Akira's director / production collaborator rather than as a lineage teacher: Kinbaku Books characterizes sixteen Naka Akira videos made with director Muryu ZOU from 2002 to 2008, mostly from the Nawa Etsu series and published by Art Video. Uplink's Naka bio and Studio Yukari's Naka bio both place Muryu ZOU / Art Video / Nawa Etsu among Naka's major production contexts. SMpedia records Muryu ZOU as an alias of Kuroda Toru and says he co-established Muruso Club / the Naka Akira Kinbaku Society with Naka in 2008.

### Mx Bliss

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/mx-bliss/

Fact — Mx Bliss is documented as an American rope switch.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ROPE SWITCH · SELF-SUSPENDER

Fact — Portland-centered rope switch, self-suspender, kink educator, performance artist and event curator. Shibari Study frames Mx Bliss as a switch discussing rope play, legal gray areas and diversity in kink. ROPECRAFT characterizes Mx Bliss as a rope switch / self-suspender, AlternaRope co-facilitator, Portland Consent Collective volunteer and diversity / negotiation educator. KinkFest 2026 records MxBliss as a presenter and characterizes her as an accomplished bondage artist who curates and produces kink shows and frames on western rope bondage. Her Bound Bliss site frames a current Portland event-production, entertainment and coaching project with MxBliss advice, video tutorials and session discounts.

### Mya

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/mya-rope-podcast/

Fact — Mya is a Thai rope bottom.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — ROPE BOTTOM · PODCASTER

Fact — Bangkok-centered rope bottom, author and co-host of Rope Podcast. The current House Cordee show page and Apple Podcasts listing describe Rope Podcast as a long-running show by rope partners Fox and Mya, while Shibari Study's 2024 episode names Mya as a rope bottom and places her and Fox as co-hosts exploring rope psychology and motivations since 2017. Rope Podcast EP181 records Mya running a local Rope Bottoming Fundamentals workshop, adding a bounded bottoming-education role without turning the podcast into a broad interview hub. House Cordee's book page credits Mya with `Sex Power and Control Series`, an erotic-short collection with bondage, power-exchange and trust themes.

### Myu

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/myu-blay/

Fact — Myu is a Czech model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — MODEL · TEACHER

Transmission — Prague-centered shibari model and AKD-associated teacher using the public identity Myu / `@myu_blay`. Alive Kinbaku Dojo's contact page records `myu_blay` among public contact surfaces, while the official 2026 Alive Kinbaku Festival page records `Alive Ropes & Myu` among expected teachers. ShibariRigger's report on the first Alive Onawa Matsuri / Alive Kinbaku Festival independently records Alive Ropes & Myu among the 2025 teachers and captions Myu as Alive Ropes's model in festival photography. The public Instagram profile frames Myu as a shibari model, Lady of AKD and rope partner of `@alive.ropes.2`.

### Nagaike Takeshi (長池士)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/nagaike-takeshi/

Transmission — Nagaike Takeshi (長池士), born 1945, is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Nureki lineage.

Chronology — Born: 1945

Fact — Era: 1990s

Fact — NAWASHI · WRITER

Fact — Japanese kinbakushi born 1945. Established SM Circle AMS in the 1970s. Part of the next-generation 'bakushi' community after the founding masters. Authored instructional works such as the video 'Rope of the Rising Sun' (1997) and the 2007 book / manual '完全総括SM手引き書'.

Transmission — School: Nureki lineage

### Nagare Aotsuki (蒼月流)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/nagare-aotsuki/

Fact — Nagare Aotsuki (蒼月流) is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · DOMINANT

Fact — Japanese kinbakushi / dominant and Kansai / Tokyo SM-scene organizer. The official ARCADIA Osaka site traces Nagare's venue network from Alcatraz SM Circle in 2000 to ARCADIA Osaka in 2003, ARCADIA Tokyo in 2009 and Titty Twister in 2016; Nawapedia also names him with MAJESTIC SM Bar in Shinjuku in 2014 and regular TOKYO ULTIMANIA SM-event organizing at ARCADIA Tokyo. ARCADIA Tokyo sources place Tesshin Doyama as the Tokyo venue's 2009 general manager and later owner, while Nagare remains the ARCADIA Group / Osaka origin location. Kinbaku Today's 2015 interview frames Nagare as an Osaka-centered kinbakushi dom, ARCADIA / MAJESTIC operator, Alcatraz Circle and Shibunawa-kai instigator, with a style fusing traditional rope and sadomasochism. He characterizes Akechi Denki's ropework as a partial influence and links Shibunawa-kai to close collaboration with Ranki Kazami and Nawanojoh in the Kansai kinbaku scene.

### Nahuel Knotctopus

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/nahuel-knotctopus/

Fact — Nahuel Knotctopus is a Chilean rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Chilean rigger, rope switch, teacher and performer using the public identity Nahuel Knotctopus / `@knotctopus` in Santiago de Chile. His public Instagram profile characterizes `Rope Art`, private sessions and rope classes, and uses he/they pronouns. Public Chile scene traces around Espacio de Ataduras and Convencion de Shibari place him in recurring workshop / performance activity beyond a profile shell: an indexed `Una cuerda peculiar` workshop post characterizes Knotctopus as a rigger and rope switch active since 2018 with a performing-arts background, Espacio de Ataduras announced a June 28 `Laboratorio de Ataduras: Futomomo` with Nahuel, and Convencion de Shibari 2026 announced the `Atando en movimiento` workshop with Rope Maki for the second convention in Santiago.

### Naka Akira (奈加あきら)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/naka-akira/

Fact — Naka Akira (奈加あきら), born 1959, is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Naka-ryū.

Chronology — Born: 1959

Fact — Era: 1990s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — Japanese nawashi born 1959, one of the most internationally recognized contemporary kinbakushi. At age 30 attended a study class by Nureki Chimuo and became his sole apprentice for over two decades. Became professional kinbakushi after Yokohata Kunihiko invited him to do bondage for a video production. Produced Amadas Studio, an SM production studio in Ikebukuro, Tokyo. From 2011 he organized Nawa Naka Kai, a Tokyo private rope-meeting and study format around Naka-style semenawa; in 2018 he established the Semenawa Kinbaku Research Group at Atelier Naka. Known for dynamic performance rope and extensive worldwide teaching. Public film credits also place him as bondage guidance on the 2013 R-18 Literary Prize adaptation Self-Bondage: All Tied Up With My Own Rope. Featured in the 2015 documentary 'Jyowa' on Japan's top rope artists. Founder of the Naka-ryū style.

Fact — School: Naka-ryū

### Namarié

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/namarie/

Fact — Namarié is a Danish performer in the Osada / Western.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — PERFORMER · TEACHER

Transmission — Copenhagen-centered performer, rope-model teacher and Kinbaku Lounge co-owner / community builder, also credited as Tanja. Kinbaku Lounge's staff page says Tanja has been a KL rope-model instructor since 2013 and began rope in 2008. Existing festival bios place her with Scot Kinbaku as Kinbaku Lounge Copenhagen co-runner; the pair present at EURIX and Onawa Asobi Europe.

Fact — School: Osada / Western

### Matsukawa Nami (松川ナミ)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/nami-matsukawa/

Fact — Matsukawa Nami (松川ナミ) is documented as a Japanese actress.

Fact — Era: 1980s

Fact — ACTRESS · MODEL

Fact — Japanese actress, model and performer active in the early-1980s Nikkatsu / Roman Porno SM-film layer. Official Nikkatsu pages identify Matsukawa as the newcomer lead in Slave Contract (1982), the star of SM Fairy Nami Matsukawa's Slave Candle Torment (1982) directed by Hiroshi Urado, and the lead of Ropes and Breasts (1983).

### Nana Quinn

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/nana-quinn/

Fact — Nana Quinn is a Finnish rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Fact — Helsinki-centered rigger, rope instructor and organizer using the public identity Nana Quinn. Karada House's organizer profile frames Nana as part of the Nana and roo teaching pair from Helsinki, says Nana is a member of the Helsinki Shibari organizing team, and characterizes long experience facilitating BDSM workshops from both top and bottom perspectives. Helsinki Shibari records Nana teaching a 2025 one-rope workshop focused on ichinawa / ippon nawa, nonverbal communication, movement, rhythm and intention. Queerope Berlin 2026 records Nana Quinn among the facilitators.

### Nagasawa Nao (なお ながさわ)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/nao-nagasawa/

Fact — Nagasawa Nao (なお ながさわ), born 1967, is documented as a Japanese model.

Chronology — Born: 1967

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — MODEL · ORGANIZER

Fact — Japanese kinbaku model, performer and event organizer. Nawapedia places her first professional SM-model and stage work around 2008-2010, then characterizes a focused 2011-2014 collaboration with Go Arisue that included planning the Bondage Drawing Board series, managing his website and blog, and producing the Erotica Japonesque events. A 2015 Sakura Publishing interview independently names Nao Nagasawa as a kinbaku model and Erotica Japonesque organizer, and attributes public rope-supervision and staging work on Flower and Snake ZERO, Tokubou and the Zero photo book to her Arisue partnership period.

### Naoko (奈緒子)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/naoko-subay/

Fact — Naoko (奈緒子) is a Hong Kong-centered nawashi in the Naka-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — Hong Kong-born, London-centered shibari artist, performer and educator formerly known as Subay / 緒本子. Established Kokoro Studio in Hong Kong (2015-2025) and Studio Yukari in London in 2022; Studio Yukari's physical London space ceased operation in November 2025 and continued separately as Studio Ma. After early study trips to Japan and a short 2014 encounter with Yukimura Haruki, she focused on Naka Akira's semenawa approach; current Nawa Jyou Wa, Studio Yukari and Yugen Studio sources state that in 2023 she was recognized as Naka Akira's third deshi and is part of Nawa Naka Kai. Has taught and performed internationally spanning Asia, Europe and North America.

Fact — School: Naka-ryū

### Sugishita Naomi (杉下なおみ)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/naomi-sugishita/

Transmission — Sugishita Naomi (杉下なおみ) is documented as a Japanese model in the Nureki lineage.

Fact — Era: 1980s

Fact — MODEL · ORGANIZER

Fact — Japanese SM model and early Kinbiken co-creator. Nawapedia names Sugishita as one of the three people who started Kinbiken with Akio Fuji and Chimuo Nureki, and records the first meetings under the name Naomi Sugishita's Club before the group became the Bondage Beauty Study Group. An academic article on Iida Toyokazu / Nureki independently corroborates the October 27, 1985 first meeting as 'Sugishita Naomi's Club' and the February 2, 1986 renaming to Kinbaku-bi Kenkyukai.

Transmission — School: Nureki lineage

### Tani Naomi (谷ナオミ)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/naomi-tani/

Fact — Tani Naomi (谷ナオミ), born 1948, is documented as a Japanese actress.

Chronology — Born: 1948

Fact — Era: 1970s

Fact — ACTRESS · MODEL

Fact — Japanese actress and model whose Nikkatsu Roman Porno work made her one of the defining rope / SM screen figures of the 1970s. The official Nikkatsu Flower and Snake page names Tani as the veteran lead in the 1974 adaptation that became the studio's first full-scale SM Roman Porno breakthrough. Eiga.com profiles her as a Fukuoka-born performer who met Oniroku Dan as a teenager, took the lead in Flower and Snake in 1974 and remained the era's `SM queen` until the 1979 retirement film Rope and Skin.

### Natalie Rose

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/natalie-rose/

Fact — Natalie Rose is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Fact — Portland-centered queer kink educator, lifestyle coach, rope top / bottom and performer. Shibari Study EP41 and Natalie Rose's official site present Nat as offering rope-bondage private tuition, tailored tying sessions and progressive learning at ROpenSpace. Current Bondage Soup pages identify Natalie Rose as founder of the Portland 12-month immersive program, whose curriculum has continued evolving since 2018 via volunteer instructors, graduate assistants and alumni. Rope in the Rockies 2025 records Natalie Rose & Etch as Portland presenters, with Natalie framed as a rope and power-exchange educator active since 2013 who has taught at 30+ North American events.

### Natasha NawaTaNeko

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/natasha-nawataneko/

Fact — Natasha NawaTaNeko is a Russian model in the Naka-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — MODEL · TEACHER

Fact — Russian-born, Berlin-centered kinbaku model, rope-bottom educator, performer and writer behind Discover Kinbaku with Alexander MA. Discover Kinbaku characterizes Natasha as a truth seeker and embodiment enthusiast whose kinbaku practice seeks true emotions, authenticity and transformational potential. Her official profile adds a 2015 Certified Sexological Bodyworker training background and frames her teaching via breath, movement, sound, posture, subtle attention and embodied agency. Current Discover pages present her individual coaching for rope bottoms, her keynote topics Surrender in Ropes, Safety in Ropes and Kinbaku as Ritual, and her book Somatics for Rope Bottoms, such as direct-order context and the September 2025 Italian edition. Dan Carabas' 2026 interview names her as author of Somatics for Rope Bottoms and frames her work around the inner experience of the person being tied. Discover Kinbaku and IKSK both place her in the Berlin teaching / performance context alongside Alexander. Prague Shibari Festival Spring 2026 records Natasha NawaTaNeko and Alexander MA as presenters / performers.

Fact — School: Naka-ryū

### Natsuki Aoyama (青山夏樹)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/natsuki-aoyama/

Fact — Natsuki Aoyama (青山夏樹), born 1970, is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi.

Chronology — Born: 1970

Fact — Era: 2000s

Safety / consent — KINBAKUSHI · AV DIRECTOR · SAFETY EDUCATOR

Safety / consent — Japanese kinbakushi / dominant, AV director and kinbaku-safety educator. Public profiles give 1970 Kyoto birth, professional SM-club work in Fukuoka from 1991, a 2001 move to Tokyo and a 2004 SM-specialist video label / director turn. Greenz and Nawapedia both say she studied under Randa Mai; sources also frame her as the second-generation Randa Mai / Mai Randa the Second before returning to the Natsuki Aoyama name. Her later work centers on modern kinbaku safety and education: Seijoukai / Seigaku, the BDSM Safety Support Association, Randa-ryu Kinbaku Juku, the manuals Gendai Kinbaku Nyumon and Kinbaku Jiko Yobo Guidebook, and the 2026 Kinbakugaku publishing project.

### Natsumi Scarlett

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/natsumi-scarlett/

Fact — Natsumi Scarlett is a Dutch shibari artist.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — SHIBARI ARTIST · RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Amsterdam-area shibari artist, rigger, teacher and performer using the public identity Natsumi Scarlett / Natsumi no Ito. Her official site frames Natsumi Scarlett as an Amsterdam-centered performer who has devoted time to shibari since 2016 and exposes public Instagram / YouTube routes. Current Hipsy organizer and workshop pages identify Natsumi no Ito as a shibari artist and rigger offering performances, workshops and one-to-one shibari sessions, with 2026 Amsterdam beginner-workshop listings at Kokopelli and bio wording around roughly twelve years of international shibari-artist / rigger work. The same public Hipsy profile says she followed lessons with Yagami Ren and Ludvig Rigger; these are documented as non-formal study influence only. Pride Amsterdam / Queer Kalender / qMeet list Natsumi Scarlett as one of three artists for the July 28, 2026 Shibari Bondage Demonstrations in the Fetish @ Pride Amsterdam programme, and Pride Amsterdam separately records her as the lead for the August 3, 2026 beginner Shibari Bondage Workshop at Hotel Arena.

### Nawa Aush (縄オシュ)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/nawa-aush/

Transmission — Nawa Aush (縄オシュ) is an Australian kinbaku teacher.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — KINBAKU TEACHER · ORGANIZER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Brisbane-centered kinbaku teacher, organizer and performer using the public identity Nawa Aush / Aush. The first-party site frames the practice as kinbaku since 2014, with education via Japanese, European and Australian communities, private and group tuition, a Fairfield home studio, Kenkyukai Brisbane facilitation, guest-educator hosting and performances. The same source says Aush is familiar with Akechi, Naka and Yukimura styles, while describing the personal practice as Naka aesthetics with Yukimuran and Shigonawa Bingo-style process; The page presents presented as style context rather than formal lineage. TicketTailor's 2026 Kenkyukai Resurrection listing independently names Aush as facilitator, says he established Kenkyukai in 2021, and names Kobakuraku as the school / event brand he operates.

### Nawa Kenshin

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/nawa-kenshin/

Fact — Nawa Kenshin is a rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PHOTOGRAPHER

Transmission — Singapore-centered shibari artist, teacher, performer and photographer using the public identity Nawa Kenshin / Sean / @nawakenshin. His official about page says he began shibari in 2010 after SM and gravure photography / videography work, received guidance from Chris Thomson / Calendar King, renewed his practice in 2019, and later took lessons from Osada Steve, Akechi Kanna and Kasumi Hourai while drawing on Yukimura, Yagami and Osada-ryu style influences. The same page frames his current approach as Nawakokoro / intentional tying with heart. Current official services pages offer Singapore shibari experiences, lessons and treated jute rope services, while official blog posts document regular introductory shibari classes at 66 Chambers, Q1 2025 performances at Costica Singapore and Manila / ShibariPH teaching plus rope-photography lighting classes. Shibari.PH independently frames him as a Singapore shibari artist and photographer teaching and performing in Manila in April 2026.

### Nawa Shishi

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/nawa-shishi/

Fact — Nawa Shishi is a Hong Kong-centered rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Fact — Hong Kong-centered rope practitioner, lesson / experience host, studio co-founder and performance artist using the public identity Nawa Shishi / Shishi. Public Instagram screenshots for `@shishi.nawa` and `@nawa.shishi.4th` both identify Shishi as co-founder of `@tsunagu.studio`; the newer `@shishi.nawa` profile also frames lesson / experience booking contact and links the public `NawaShishi` Patreon route. Tsunagu Studio's public X surface independently names `@nawa_shishi` as one of the operators of the Hong Kong rope studio. Art+ Magazine's 2024 Mondrian Hong Kong preview characterizes Nawa Shishi as one of the local artists in the MeART-curated Hajime Kinoko showcase and names Shishi's Love segment with calligrapher Merryislovely.

### Nawa Yumio (縄 弓雄)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/nawa-yumio/

Fact — Nawa Yumio (縄 弓雄), 1912–2006, is documented as a Japanese martial artist in the Foundational.

Chronology — Born: 1912

Chronology — Died: 2006

Fact — Era: 1950s

Fact — MARTIAL ARTIST · HISTORIAN

Fact — Japanese martial artist, historian, writer, and advisor to NHK; considered the leading authority on ninpō. Sōke (head and heir) of Masaki-ryū — also proficient in Masaki-ryū manrikigusari, kusarigama, Edo Machikata jutte, and Torinawa Atsukaiyō. Teaches at Masakikai in central Tokyo. Worked as a historical consultant on matters of law enforcement. A researcher of histories of martial art and torture, and master of hojōjutsu; his 1964 classic on Torinawa and other instructional books were a key step in preserving hojōjutsu as Japanese cultural heritage — the connection between historical rope restraint and modern kinbaku aesthetics.

Fact — School: Foundational

### Nawashi Murakawa

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/nawashi-murakawa/

Fact — Nawashi Murakawa is a British kinbakushi.

Fact — Era: 1990s

Transmission — KINBAKUSHI · TEACHER · AUTHOR

Transmission — London-centered kinbaku practitioner, teacher, photographer and editor using the public identity Nawashi Murakawa. His official site programming includes Authentic Japanese Kinbaku private tuition / workshops and records extensive 1990s-2000s UK / European film, video, TV, club and press work involving Japanese rope bondage. Kahboom and book-catalog records credit him with editing Kinbaku: The Art of Rope Bondage, and IMDb credits Jon Murakawa as a self-appearance in The Pleasure of Rope.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Buenos Aires-centered shibari teacher and founder of Muganawa Kinbaku Dojo. Her official bio says she began rope as a model, started weekly tying classes at Ritual Kinbaku in 2019, and established Muganawa Kinbaku Dojo in 2022 after leaving that first training context. Her public training list includes in-person study with Naka Akira / Iroha Shizuki, Federico Kirigami / Marta Tenshiko, RopuNawa, Riccardo Wildties / Red Sabbath, Harukumo, Tamandua and Seilartig, plus local study with Volcano / Mini Beast and Ritual Kinbaku and selected online classes via Yugen Studio, Kinbaku LuXuria, Karada House and Zetsu.

### Nevyn

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/nevyn/

Fact — Nevyn is an Italian performer.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — PERFORMER · TEACHER

Transmission — Italian rope performer and teacher using the public identity Nevyn / Max Nevyn and `@n_e_v_y_n`. A public Ravenna event listing names Nevyn and Eli of Shibari Dojo as the protagonists of `Shibari Night - Corde al Dante's`, while official MBDStudio / BDSM Shop pages preserve a current Emilia Romagna Shibari Dojo lesson shell at The Den in Ravenna. Davide La Greca / MaestroBD's June 25, 2026 Shibari Dojo Ravenna announcement names Max Nevyn as instructor for a July 5 Ravenna intensive and as the Ravenna Dojo referent. Current sources support a local Ravenna branch teacher / performer role, not a formal certification, direct lineage or wider school-authority claim.

### Nëya

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/neya/

Fact — Nëya is a Czech rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · MODEL · TEACHER

Transmission — Prague-centered rigger, model, teacher and performer publicly appearing as Nëya. Official Prague Shibari Festival and Subspace pages describe her as a rigger and model from the Czech Republic who has been part of Prague-centered Subspace studio's educators team since 2019, works spanning multiple technical and aesthetic styles, and especially enjoys improvisation and bamboo in her ties. The same public profile trail says she keeps studying shibari abroad, runs the waxplay brand Nëya Candles, and works to spread awareness and understanding of polyamory. Public festival pages also place her in a long-running teaching and tying partnership with Kameko from Prague, as a Prague Shibari Festival 2024 presenter, a Danish Shibari Festival 2025 presenter, and a Daruma Berlin guest teacher with Braxas in 2025.

### Nick Freerider

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/nick-freerider/

Transmission — Nick Freerider is a Russian teacher.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · NAWASHI · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Moscow-centered bondage artist, teacher, organizer and RopeSchool instructor; founder of the Shibaru community. RopeSchool records Nick Freerider as its Moscow instructor, while a MAXIM interview names him as Nikolay / Nick Freerider, an active Moscow bondage artist who develops shibari via artistic photo sessions, festivals, shows, RopeSchool teaching and his own training system. Kinky Practice characterizes him as a Moscow practitioner who has performed at major Russian festivals such as MoscowKnot and RopeFest, as well as Kinky Party, Kink Russia, Red Kinky, RopeDay, Shibari Convention SPb and Bondage Cafe SPb. RopeFest Moscow 2023 names him as founder of Shibaru, festival participant, party resident and RopeSchool instructor. His public Telegram archive adds current context: Nick runs his own multi-module Shibari Academy course, regular training sessions and jam formats in a Moscow studio, writes / lectures on kinbaku history and cultural framing, experiments with hojojutsu-derived tie adaptations, and records safety-oriented research around suspended-state risk.

### Nicolas Yoroi

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/nicolas-yoroi/

Fact — Nicolas Yoroi is a Belgian nawashi in the Osada / Western.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · PHOTOGRAPHER

Transmission — Belgian rope artist, educator, rigger and photographer with 20+ years in rope. Aikidoka since 1997 — martial-arts background became his main influence for life and kinbaku. Spent four years in Japan studying under Go Arisue, dedicating himself to Aikidō, Japanese culture and the art of rope. Has also studied with Tesshin Doyama, Hajime Kinoko, Ottonawa, Kazami Ranki, Felix Ruckert, Shadow, and Pedro Cordas. Began teaching rope bondage in 2011; upon returning to Europe developed his own tying style, 'Yoroi-ryu'. Opened a Brussels dojo in 2015; the current Nicolas's Dojo site records a Brussels / Anderlecht dojo address and frames the dojo around rope art, self-study, dance, massage, martial arts, performance, power exchange and safety. His work integrates Aikidō, Thai massage, contact-improvisation and the System A approach. Recurring EURIX teacher, Zurich Shibari Masterclasses guest and European workshop presenter; collaborator with Felix Ruckert.

Fact — School: Osada / Western

### Nikita Ropekit

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/nikita-ropekit/

Fact — Nikita Ropekit is a Russian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Moscow-linked Russian shibari rigger, teacher, performer and Shibaru organizer. Mamba's expert bio names Nikita Ropekit as a shibari master and one of the organizers of the Shibaru club for Japanese culture and rope bondage, says he had devoted about ten years to shibari, and names Ulyana LianaKnot as his spouse and long-term rope partner. Bookmate preserves a 2020 podcast episode with Nikita Ropekit as a bondage artist discussing shibari safety. A Section Moscow seminar archive records him as a beginner shibari teacher with 8+ years of rope-bondage practice. BalkanBound 2025 records RopeKit as a performer with LianaKnot, frames him as a precise and fluid rigger, and schedules him for Predicament and one-rope workshops in Belgrade. A 2026 Por.Rope / RopeFest source names Nikita RopeKit as the person who gave Alexey Poryadnov his base in rope.

### Nikoleta

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/nikoleta/

Fact — Nikoleta is a Czech rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · MODEL · TEACHER

Transmission — Prague-centered Czech rigger, model and teacher active in the Subspace studio orbit. Her official Subspace profile says she has practiced rope since 2018 on both sides of the rope, first as a model and later as a rigger, and frames her teaching around safety, trust and firm guidance. Prague Shibari Festival's 2024 Zach & Nikoleta presenter page characterizes her as working spanning several tying styles, especially darker Naka-ryu-influenced emotional work, movement in rope and Rough Body Play. Pinse Camp 2026 adds a second public teaching / performance trace and repeats the dual model / rigger framing, naming Soptik, Riccardo Wildties and A-Nicolas among her strongest influences.

### Nina Russ

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/nina-russ/

Fact — Nina Russ is a British nawashi.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — London-centered shibari performer, rope artist and educator. Became student of Esinem in 2011, then his full-time apprentice from February 2012 — has assisted his classes ever since and now co-runs the online ShibariClasses courses. Co-established BOUND (the UK's first regular shibari show night) in 2012 and ShibariClasses in 2015. Her official site names Sugiura Norio as a major photographic idol and frames videos of Yukimura sessions and Osada Steve stage shows as major influence context. Performs internationally — notably at the London Festival of the Art of Japanese Rope Bondage and RopeFest in St. Petersburg. Sees shibari as an art form for deep connection and personal development, with martial-arts-rooted discipline and growth.

### Noa Shibari

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/noa-shibari/

Fact — Noa Shibari is a Dutch rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Amsterdam / de Zaanstreek shibari rigger, teacher, performer and self-tying workshop organizer also credited as Miss Noa / Noana. Her official website frames her as Amsterdam-centered and offering private shibari sessions, couples classes, beginner self-tie workshops and intimate performances, with a consent-centered practice that blends meditative / ritualistic Japanese bondage with sensual body experience. Hipsy frames Noa Shibari as organizing meditative shibari events in Amsterdam, with a tantra / meditation background and Japan training with Japanese shibari masters; public Hipsy event pages document self-tie and couples workshops in Amsterdam from 2025. BNNVARA's bondage feature frames Noa as a rigger working with shibari in a trust / surrender context, and De Sloot's Skin Deep Cabaret archive records Noana in a shibari / sensual-circus performance program.

### Noemi Ferrari

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/noemi-ferrari/

Fact — Noemi Ferrari is documented as a German model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — MODEL · PERFORMER

Fact — Berlin-centered rope performer, model, workshop partner and actress / dancer using the public identity Noemi Ferrari. Prague Shibari Festival's 2026 Anna Lysee & Noemi page says she discovered rope in 2019, is drawn to the playful and creative physical challenge of shibari, and regularly performs within the IKSK Berlin rope community. The same source frames her workshop role via the active and creative side of modeling. Her Death Festival Berlin presenter page independently characterizes her as an actress, dancer and voice actress who builds workshops around emotional intensity, courageous feeling and boundary-pushing collective exploration.

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Taipei-centered rope artist, rigger, teacher, performer and organizer using the public identity Noir_fix / Nadja Charlie Lam. The official Cats Downstairs `Noir_fix & Ropes` page names her as in-house rigger, co-founder and current host of Cats Downstairs Rope Salon, and co-founder of The Suspended Canvas with Yisi_selfties in 2023. That record says she first explored non-traditional Japanese rope bondage and alternative erotic culture in Berlin, then established Cats Downstairs in 2018, and currently teaches private shibari classes, private sessions, small introductory workshops, all-women's rope salon, figure drawing with ropes, Dark Hours Rope Salon and performances.

### Norio Sugiura (杉浦則夫)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/norio-sugiura/

Fact — Norio Sugiura (杉浦則夫), born 1942, is documented as a Japanese photographer.

Chronology — Born: 1942

Fact — Era: 1980s

Fact — PHOTOGRAPHER · EDITOR

Fact — Japanese photographer born 1942 in Nagoya. Studied design but dropped out of college; worked as construction coordinator for a puppet troupe and joined Asakusa Tōyō Gekijō as a lighting technician. After Asakusa Tōyō Gekijō closed, was introduced to Oniroku Dan and joined Oni Pro (Oniroku Production), participating actively as editor of 'SM-King'. After 3–4 years in SM film production became a freelance photographer, working mainly with SM Select and SM Fan — the leading SM magazines of the time. In the 1980s most of his work was in collaboration with Chimuo Nureki (some with Shikō Shima). His 45-year journey in kinbaku photography has left a distinctive mark on the art. Current official surfaces include Kinbaku Sajiki, the `Products for Overseas` download catalogue for videos of Sugiura's kinbaku photography work in progress, and the Sugiura Norio Photo Office group site / social shells; the SNPG handles are treated as office/contact surfaces rather than a separate public profile.

### Norton Ognev

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/norton-ognev/

Fact — Norton Ognev is a Russian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · PERFORMER

Fact — Kazan rigger, performer and rope maker. RopeFest Peterburg 2026 says Norton Ognev studied with several masters such as Rino / RhinoRopes, Kotenok Gav, Alex Hitch and Freerider, and separately says he prefers Yagami Style as an authored style by Ren Yagami. Ren Yagami's official instructor list does not include Norton, so The page presents listed as a Ren Yagami style influence rather than official Yagami-ryu lineage. Norton also appears in the 2026 RopeFest fair as a rope maker.

### Nuit de Tokyo (春翔)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/nuitdetokyo/

Fact — Nuit de Tokyo (春翔) is a French nawashi in the Yukimura-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · INTERPRETER

Fact — French nawashi, interpreter / translator and certified Yukimura-ryu instructor using the Yukimura name Haru Kakeru. He first traveled to Japan in 1989 and began collecting kinbaku books in the early 1990s; Kinbaku Today dates his serious hands-on Tokyo-scene study to an extended stay around 2003. He names Osada Steve as his first real teacher; Osada introduced him to Akechi Kanna and Yukimura Haruki in 2005. After Kanna returned in 2010, Nuit de Tokyo completed the full Nawashi Kanna Rope Study Group course while also becoming a certified Yukimura-ryu instructor after long-term study with Yukimura. KNOTY and Rope Podcast both frame this as long-term immersion rather than workshop sampling. He has also taken lessons with Nureki Chimuo, Randa Mai, Miura Takumi, Arisue Go, Saikatsu, Hige, Masato Marai and others. Public traces include Onawa Asobi / Tokyo / Osaka / Sydney / Hong Kong performance contexts, interpreter or MC work around Naka Akira, Ranki Kazami, Ren Yagami and Tenma Haru, and English / French translation for Yagami-ryu study material. Teacher / mentor context for Steve Freeman and Davide La Greca.

Fact — School: Yukimura-ryū

### Oat Montien (โอ๊ต มณเฑียร)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/oat-montien/

Fact — Oat Montien (โอ๊ต มณเฑียร) is a Thai artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ARTIST

Fact — Bangkok-centered Thai erotic / queer visual artist whose recent public work uses shibari as installation, video and performance language. Schwules Museum's Young Birds exhibition site records Oat Montien's 2024 `American Dream Revisited` as a mixed-media shibari installation made from rope and clothes from the artist's late father, alongside the video `Untie (Death Valley)`. Berlin Art Link's 2026 studio visit characterizes Montien tying objects and clothing with shibari knots, such as `Pearl Boy` and father-memorial works, and frames the practice via sexuality, masculinity, grief and queer community-building.

### Odys

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/odys/

Fact — Odys, born 1981, is documented as an American nawashi in the Naka-ryū.

Chronology — Born: 1981

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — American kinbaku artist and educator centered in Virginia / the DMV. His KinbakuOdyssey resume characterizes him as a traditional Japanese kinbaku / shibari rigger and educator, endorsed by Naka Akira to teach NakaStyle basics in the USA, taught by Naka from 2017 to the present, and a Nawa Naka Kai member from 2018. He established KinbakuOdyssey Studio in Virginia in 2017 and later facilitated the DMV NakaStyle Study Group with Naka's permission. Rope Podcast EP93 frames his style as sensually erotic semenawa; Bound Lark's 2025 intensive bio independently characterizes him as creator of KinbakuOdyssey and a direct student of Naka Akira.

Fact — School: Naka-ryū

### Dan Oniroku (団鬼六)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/oniroku-dan/

Transmission — Dan Oniroku (団鬼六), 1931–2011, is documented as a Japanese writer in the Nureki lineage.

Chronology — Born: 1931

Chronology — Died: 2011

Fact — Era: 1970s

Fact — WRITER · PUBLISHER

Chronology — Born Yukihiko Kuroiwa on 16 April 1931 in Shiga Prefecture; died 6 May 2011 from oesophageal cancer at 80. Authored some 120 works of erotic SM fiction, about 50 of which were adapted into films. 1950s English-language TV translator (incl. Alfred Hitchcock frames); 1960s junior-high English teacher who wrote pink-film scripts under the pseudonym 'Matsugorō Kuroiwa'. His novel 'Flower and Snake' (Hana to Hebi) made him famous; works appeared in Kitan Club, Fuzoku Club, Uramado, Abu Hunter. Established the SM magazine 'SM King' (1972–74). Closed his desk in the early 1990s for a failed business venture, returned ~10 years later with the autobiography 'The Flower Must Be Crimson'. Long professional association with actress Naomi Tani; influenced later writers such as Murakami Ryū.

Transmission — School: Nureki lineage

### Osada Kazumi (長田一美)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/osada-kazumi/

Fact — Osada Kazumi (長田一美) is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Osada / Western.

Fact — Era: 1990s

Fact — NAWASHI · WRITER

Transmission — Japanese kinbakushi and writer who first met Eikichi Osada in 1986. Associated with the Osada lineage; participated in the memorial held for Eikichi Osada on 23 November 2001. (Less publicly documented than other Osada-line figures — research ongoing.)

Fact — School: Osada / Western

### Osada Steve (長田スティーブ)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/osada-steve/

Fact — Osada Steve (長田スティーブ), born 1952, is documented as a German nawashi in the Osada / Western.

Chronology — Born: 1952

Fact — Era: 1990s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — German nawashi, AV director and performer centered in Japan. Born 1952 in Berlin as Steve Hauser. Moved to Japan in the 1980s and worked as a fashion photographer; in the 1990s turned to fetish photography, documenting much of Tokyo's SM scene. First met his mentor Osada Eikichi in 1998 during a photo shoot, then helped during stage performances and studied his style of kinbaku until Eikichi's retirement in 2001. His official chronology also records study under Akechi Denki from 2002, Nawashi Kanna from 2005, Yukimura Haruki from 2007 and Yagami Ren from 2017. In 2000 established Studio SIX in Tokyo; in 2007 established Osada-Ryū as his official school. Yukimura-ryu sources identify him as Haruki Yukimura's first formal student from 2006, with the Harumine bakushi name and a Yukimura-ryu teaching license in 2012. Later projects include Kinbaku Academy, a structured online Osada-ryu / Yukimura-ryu teaching platform. Active internationally for 25+ years, one of the most influential figures in modern shibari.

Fact — School: Osada / Western

### Osaka Dan

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/osaka-dan/

Transmission — Osaka Dan is an Australian teacher in the Kazami-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Australian rope teacher and organizer active in Toronto in the 2010s. Established the first Toronto Kinbaku Salon space in 2013 and brought Kazami Ranki to Toronto for a five-day intensive in March 2014; Toronto Kinbaku Salon characterizes him as Kazami Ranki's only gaijin deshi at that time. Tifereth began exploring Kazami-ryu via Osaka Dan in 2013. He left Canada in 2015 and returned to Australia.

Fact — School: Kazami-ryū

### OtaKinbaku

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ota-kinbaku/

Fact — OtaKinbaku is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · PHOTOGRAPHER

Fact — Bay Area / Oakland-centered rope artist, nawashi, photographer, performer and educator working as OtaKinbaku / Ota. His Tumblr and Bluesky profiles frame him as a Bay Area / Oakland rope photographer, rigger and Oakland Rope Salon founder. VoxBody's Oakland Rope Salon listing places Ota as host and characterizes the salon as a small discussion-focused Japanese rope-bondage gathering, with Ota's 2015 Japan lessons and time around Naka Akira and other bakushi treated here as influence / study context rather than formal apprenticeship. Filthy Studios records Ota in its educator / production-support crew, and Twisted Windows records Ota among its San Francisco Bay Area rope troupe performers.

### Otonawa (音縄)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/otonawa/

Fact — Otonawa (音縄) is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi in the Ichinawa-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — KINBAKUSHI · TEACHER

Transmission — Tokyo-centered kinbakushi, teacher and performer in Hajime Kinoko's Ichinawakai / ROPES orbit. Kinoko's ROPES page says Otonawa debuted in January 2009 via a collaborative kinbaku show with Hajime Kinoko, studied under Kinoko for many years, teaches at Ichinawakai, and has toured shows and workshops with his wife Tsubasa spanning 14 countries / 25 cities. RopeMarks additionally frames his practice as having Kitagawa-ryu roots and characterizes him as a long-time member of Kinoko's Ichinawa-kai group. Nawapedia and the official Titty Twister site place him at Titty Twister, the Kabukicho / Shinjuku SM & fetish bar that began operating in the mid-2010s, and list early international teaching / performance traces such as Peer Rope Copenhagen, Australia, Canada and Los Angeles.

Fact — School: Ichinawa-ryū

Oblige's official About page says Paul established the project in 2020 and now operates it with Flynn. The independent subMERGE interview names Paul as Serene_X, characterizes Paul and Flynn as Oblige co-owners who created the Metro Detroit rope / kink studio together, and says shibari and rope bondage are primary practices for both. ### Pauline Massimo

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/pauline-massimo/

Fact — Pauline Massimo is a Brazilian artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ARTIST · EDUCATOR · PERFORMER

Fact — Brazilian interdisciplinary artist, shibari educator, performer and curator based between Spain and Brazil. Pauline's official bio characterizes 12+ years of art-field experience spanning painting, dance, performance, photography, shibari, event curation, community building and art direction, and names them as founder / director of Espacio Nos in Barcelona. Their public CV includes EURIX 2017 performances, the 2020 Karada House online Butoh + Shibari project, repeated Espacio Nos performance / curation work and the 2023 Shakira / Manuel Turizo "Copa Vacía" music-video rope work. ArtsHelp and Learn Shibari sources frame Pauline's current rope practice around gender-neutral, self-tying, safety-aware and interdisciplinary shibari education rather than formal lineage.

### Pedro Cordas

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/pedro-cordas/

Transmission — Pedro Cordas is a Portuguese teacher.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · NAWASHI

Fact — Lisbon-centered Portuguese rope artist, performer, video editor and educator. Began rope in 2003 while visiting Tokyo, studying first with Osada Steve and then, in 2004, with Akechi Denki; after Akechi's death he continued learning from Nawashi Kanna. Has taught from beginner to advanced levels spanning Portugal, Europe, the United reports and Australia, and performed at events such as the London Festival of the Art of Japanese Bondage and Bondage Expo Dallas. Filmmaker of the Jyowa "Rope Stories" documentary project.

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/petite-pretzel/

Transmission — Petite Pretzel is documented as an American teacher.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · PERFORMER

Fact — Shibari artist and rope switch from NYC, currently centered in London. Her official private-lessons page says she discovered Japanese rope bondage at Anatomie Studio in 2016; Anatomie and festival bios place her teaching at Anatomie Studio London since 2019. Performs at London nightlife events and British festivals; commercial credits include music videos for Charli XCX and Christine + the Queens. Her August 2025 post records teaching and performing with Ania Polsky at Shibari Camp Poland 2025. Prague Shibari Festival Spring 2026 records her with BoundBoar as a presenter / performer and says she plans to open E13 Playhouse, a new London rope venue, in July 2026.

### pikushii

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/pikushii/

Fact — Pikushii is a Swiss rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Swiss rigger, teacher, host and performer. Secret56's team page names pikushii / Barbara as a Secret56 co-trainer: first contact with bondage in 2010, first Secret56 workshops as an ukete in 2015, active tying from 2019, and regular support for private lessons, events and workshops from 2020 before joining the fixed co-trainer team in 2024. Nawaland's official pages say she co-established Nawaland with kamicane in 2025, teaches workshops, private lessons and trainings, hosts on-site and online events, and developed the independent pikushii ryu from Osada-ryu, Yukimura-style, Naka-inspired semenawa and other influences.

### Pilar LaOtra

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/pilar-laotra/

Fact — Pilar LaOtra is a Spanish rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — RIGGER · PERFORMER

Fact — Spanish filmmaker and rope performer also credited as Pilar Aldea. Started tying in 2011 and developed the Cuerdas Poeticas project with Ana Gutierrez between 2012 and 2015, bringing performances and workshops to rope festivals and short-film festivals in Barcelona, Madrid, Soria, Bilbao, Strasbourg, Moscow and Berlin. Studied with Osada Steve, Alberto Noshibari, Dasniya Sommer, Haruki Yukimura, Yukinaga Max, Shadow, Naka Akira, Nawashi Murakawa, Bergborg, Sansblague and Felix Ruckert. Regular EURIX participant and presenter.

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/poli-nasty/

Fact — Poli Nasty is a Colombian model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — MODEL · PERFORMER · ORGANIZER

Fact — Medellín, Colombia rope bottom / model, performer and Shibari Medellín community coordinator using the public identity Poli Nasty. Her public Instagram profile uses the `@xpoli.nasty` handle and names her inter-institutional-relations coordination role with `@shibari_medellin`; the 2023 `Vergajo 4` rope-bunny edition preserves the earlier `@poli.nasty` handle and a first-person profile placing her first rope encounter in late 2021 via Medellín's BDSM community, followed by a 2022 Festival de Cuerdas rope-bunny appearance and Colombian rigger performance context. NoShibari's official Taller Tríptico page also preserves a March 2024 Medellín performance image context with `@xpoli.nasty`.

### Polo Picart

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/polo-picart/

Fact — Polo Picart is a Swiss rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · PHOTOGRAPHER

Fact — Geneva-centered photographer and shibari performer, also billed as Polo Cosme / Polo Shibari in his Barcelona show materials. His official Shibari bio frames him as the rigger for the Erotic Museum of Barcelona, with 150+ shows, a path that began with photo shoots in Switzerland and France, earlier Hatha Yoga / dance teaching, and a self-taught rope practice built from close observation of Japanese masters and Western riggers. His public photography site and exhibition archive place his broader body / dance / nude-art photography work in Geneva and spanning Switzerland, Belgium, Germany, France and Spain, while Time Out, La Vanguardia and Fever document the Barcelona Live Shibari Show / exhibition context.

### Python / Pyth01

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/python-pyth01/

Fact — Python / Pyth01 is a rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Fact — Singapore-centered rope top, instructor and studio co-founder using the public identity Python / Pyth01. That Rope Place's official Meet the Team page says Rosa and Python established the studio in 2019 in their living room, names Python as Head of Rope Education and the studio's most experienced instructor, and gives over a decade of tying plus more than six years teaching rope. The same page characterizes his tying as connective, intentional and partner-experience focused, and says Rosa Canina and Python explored rope together from 2015 in London, were regulars at Anatomie Studio until 2019, and then moved to Singapore. Earlier Rope Podcast provenance under Pyth01 / Studio 1010 supports the historical Singapore studio-continuity and Anatomie study trace now merged into That Rope Place.

### Queerbaku

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/queerbaku/

Fact — Queerbaku is a French rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Fact — Lyon-centered rigger and rope instructor. Karada House's organizer profile characterizes Queerbaku's tying, movement and pedagogy as shaped by teaching queer individuals and living with chronic pain, with slow-paced sessions focused on intimacy and torment. Thick Hitches Conference records Queerbaku as a France-centered facilitator for the 2025 in-person Berlin edition and the 2026 virtual edition; the 2025 programme credits Queerbaku with Messy, ugly ropes, Breath play with ropes, and Mermaid Suspension sessions. Queerbaku's official site adds current Lyon private courses / sessions and a Coven Cordes pedagogical-lead context.

### Raika (蕾火)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/raika/

Transmission — Raika (蕾火) is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi in the Akechi lineage.

Fact — Era: 1990s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · MISTRESS

Transmission — Japanese mistress, kinbakushi and late-Akechi-lineage performer active from the mid-1990s to 2015. Nawapedia says she started as mistress Runa Tsuruki in 1994, met Denki Akechi in 1995 and became his student, succeeded to the Akechi name in 1996, published how-to work under Raika Akechi and returned the Akechi name after his 2005 death. Existing Akechi source notes already distinguish Raika from weaker late-lineage claims: public references frame her as one of Akechi's formal women students, making her materially stronger than ordinary stage-roster context.

Transmission — School: Akechi lineage

- [nawapedia](https://nawapedia.net/index.php?title=Raika)
- [Blog](http://ameblo.jp/seraika/)

### Akiyoshi Ran (秋吉巒)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ran-akiyoshi/

Fact — Akiyoshi Ran (秋吉巒), 1922–1981, is documented as a Japanese illustrator.

Chronology — Born: 1922

Chronology — Died: 1981

Fact — Era: 1950s

Fact — ILLUSTRATOR

Fact — Showa-era Japanese illustrator and painter active spanning postwar customs / SM magazine publishing. Nawapedia credits Akiyoshi with cover and illustration work from around 1950 onward, such as Decameron, Fuzoku Soshi, Kitan Club, Uramado, Kinbaku-Bi, SM Collector, SM Fan, SM Kitan and SM Play. Atelier Third's 2024 English-page reissue notes that Tatsuhiko Shibusawa praised his dreamlike paintings and that his first solo exhibition was held posthumously at Aoki Gallery.

### Kazami Ranki (風見蘭喜)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ranki-kazami/

Fact — Kazami Ranki (風見蘭喜), born 1962, is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Kazami-ryū.

Chronology — Born: 1962

Fact — Era: 1990s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — Kinbakushi centered in Kansai. Established Circle M's SM group in 1996. From 2000 serialized "M's Monthly Report" and began bondage training sessions; expanded teaching to Nawa-ya workshops from 2001. From 2011 gave workshops internationally in the UK, Australia, Canada, France, and the USA. In a Shibari Study podcast interview, Kazami characterizes learning by trial and error in an era without formal teaching, then teaching students to understand why a tie works rather than only memorizing order; the same interview characterizes an apprentice structure with two Toronto apprentices and one Brisbane apprentice. Evil Girls HQ public references identify Miss Gen as his qualified Kazami-Ryu instructor / deshi from late 2015 and Shihan / Head of School for Oceania from 2018.

Fact — School: Kazami-ryū

### Ray Duran

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ray-duran/

Fact — Ray Duran is documented as an American shibari instructor.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — SHIBARI INSTRUCTOR · EVENT PRODUCER

Fact — Spokane-centered shibari instructor, performer and event producer. The official Shibari is the Way about page names Ray Duran as founder of Shibari is the Way, teaching hands-on shibari classes in Spokane and producing live stage events that combine rope art, burlesque, drag and flow arts. Dream Studio's June 1, 2025 host-side listing independently names Ray Duran of Shibari is the Way as host of a rope-risk / rope-bottoming safety class.

### Red Sabbath

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/red-sabbath/

Fact — Red Sabbath is an Italian model.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — MODEL · TEACHER

Transmission — Italian rope model, performer and teacher. Life and work partner of Riccardo Wildties and later the main public model / teaching partner in Kinbaku LuXuria, a Naka-ryu-descended semenawa practice adapted for Western bodies and classrooms. A 2012 LuXuria Rome page records both RedSabbath and Gorgone as models for early private sessions; later public LuXuria / Peony Studio traces place Red Sabbath with Wildties in European teaching, performance and certified-educator context. Active with Wildties since the early 2010s; performed with him at Naka Nawakai in Tokyo in August 2014 and has taught spanning Europe. Current Peony Studio / TicketTailor workshop context exposes Alexandra Rouge / @alexandrarouge93 as a RedSabbath booking and contact alias. Former dancer and dance teacher whose rope work emphasizes body movement, surrender, and the darker emotional register of floorwork and partial suspensions.

Fact — ROPE COMMUNITY HOST

Fact — New York rope-community organizer using the public identity REDACT_NY / Redact. Knotty NY's official current schedule names `@redact_ny` as a host of its recurring NYC rope-bondage meetups, while the official news archive repeatedly credits REDACT_NY in host context from 2023. The public record is documented narrowly as community organization; no lineage, rank, certification or formal school claim is inferred.

### RedLily

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/redlily/

Fact — RedLily is an Italian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — RIGGER · ORGANIZER

Fact — Italian-born, Copenhagen-centered queer rope artist, educator, relational coach and sexologist alternate public name: Beatrice Gigliuto. Her own bio frames her work around intimacy, queerness, embodied connection, consent, emotional awareness and relational rope practice, and says she has taught and performed spanning Europe and beyond for more than a decade. RedLily is founder and artistic director of Copenhagen Queer Knots Festival; her current event pages also describe her as artistic director of Copenhagen Queer Knots Festival and Rome Bondage Week and co-organizer of Beyond the Norm. EURIX records RedLily among the 2024 presenters.

### Redolage

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/redolage/

Fact — Redolage is a French rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Fact — Paris-centered rigger, performer and shibari coach using the public identity Redolage / Redolage3.0. The official Cordiale S-Time / Beacons page names him as Cyril, a confirmed shibari practitioner, and characterizes a structured, safety-conscious teaching and experience model around confidence, limits, emotional exploration and occasional artistic performances. Current Instagram and Linktree surfaces corroborate the Paris-facing course / private-session identity.

### Reeli Hakman

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/reeli-hakman/

Transmission — Reeli Hakman is a teacher.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Fact — Tallinn-centered Estonian rope educator and organizer behind Köieparadiis. Her official Köieparadiis bio says she entered rope in autumn 2016 after earlier tantra and yoga study, first focused on bottoming, and later developed a broader tying / domination practice. The same page names workshop study with Andy Buru, Saara Rei, Ceci Ferox, Tifereth, Lahtnor, Boris Mosafir, Soptik, Gestalta and others. Current Köieparadiis pages present her with Marko Murulauk as one of the public teachers for courses, practice evenings, private trainings and private sessions in Tallinn.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Uruguay-centered rigger and teacher. Dercein's Latin American shibari resource says @rembrandt_shibari arrived in Uruguay several years earlier, began studying with Samael, and became a prominent Uruguayan / Latin American rigger with a movement- and eroticism-oriented style, private sessions and classes. Shibari Uruguay's current atlas context names Rembrandt as a former Shibari Uruguay member. Cuerdas Nómadas' 2026 announcement names Rembrandt with Kida for July 3-5, 2027 Medellín workshops covering Newaza, semisuspension, suspension and seme.

### Yagami Ren (鵺神蓮)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ren-yagami/

Fact — Yagami Ren (鵺神蓮), born 1984, is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Yagami-ryū.

Chronology — Born: 1984

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — Japanese nawashi born in Nagoya in 1984, creator of Yagami Style / sekibaku and founder of Yagami Juku. His official bio says he began self-tying at age 17, explored BDSM and SM clubs, then encountered Succubus in Tokyo, managed by Mistress Nawashi Kanna. Public Yagami chronology sources place that first Kanna-linked Succubus period around 2002-2003: he worked there as a staff member, became fascinated by Kanna's rope work, and then left the venue before his later formal apprenticeship. In 2011 he asked Kanna to take him as an apprentice and learned the basics of Akechi-style rope; from 2014 he worked independently on his own style and established Yagami Juku the same year. The official Yagami Style page frames sekibaku as a rope approach shaped by jujutsu, anatomy, psychology and everyday practice, focused on reducing body load for both model and rigger. The current curriculum is described via Shoden, Chuden, Joden and Kaiden levels, with Semenawa treated separately.

Fact — School: Yagami-ryū

### Renata Fushime

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/renata-fushime/

Fact — Renata Fushime is a Russian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Saint Petersburg shibari practitioner, teacher, performer and 18+ event organizer. RopeFest Peterburg 2026 frames her on the main stage, says she has worked with shibari for more than eight years as a conscious bodily and artistic practice, teaches shibari technique and sensual massage, and created the Vabi-Sabi and Space shibari-party projects plus the Vabi-Sabi event agency. Her public Telegram channel frames the work as shibari art, rope performances, body practices and Vabi-Sabi backstage context.

### René von Qibari

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/rene-von-qibari/

Transmission — René von Qibari, born 1963, is documented as a German teacher.

Chronology — Born: 1963

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — TEACHER · BODYWORKER

Transmission — Berlin-centered rope / bodywork teacher behind Qibari. Qibari's official about page frames René von Qibari / René Zechmeister as a bodywork practitioner born in 1963, active professionally in mobile Shiatsu since 2010, giving his first Rope-Shiatsu workshops from 2019, taking over the Lübbener Straße 26 rooms in 2023 to preserve them as a place for bodywork and shibari, and teaching within the Rope-Shiatsu training from 2024 onward. The current Qibari pages frame his work as a hybrid of classical Shiatsu, floorwork-oriented shibari, consent and body-centered partner practice, with active 2026 beginner, bodywork and training programming includes in Berlin-Kreuzberg.

### Renée de Sans

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/renee-de-sans/

Transmission — Renée de Sans is documented as a German teacher.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · ARTIST

Transmission — Berlin-centered trans visual artist, filmmaker, performer and shibari teacher with a visual-communication diploma from Bauhaus-University Weimar. Practicing rope since 2013, she co-established Karada House Berlin in 2019 with Caritia and Mamana and has taught / organized there since that period, framing it as a queer community space for rope bondage, performance and embodied practices. Teaches shibari internationally and at Karada House with emphasis on consent, safety and embodied practice, and uses rope, photography and film to explore bodies, identity, gender, sexuality and taboo. Her current official profile also frames her as co-director of the 2018 film The Artist and the Pervert. The same bio names Addie Tahl, Akechi Kanna & Kagura, Georg Barkas, Murasaki Haruan and Naoko among the practitioners in the personal-exchange context via which her rope practice developed, while keeping further Berlin-scene exchange with Caritia, Mamana, Ceci Ferox and Sophia Rose as collaboration context. In 2026, Studio Allegory's QuIRKS programme listed her as a Berlin presenter with classes such as Cocoon, Rope and Playfighting, and sessions with Mamana and Sophia Rose.

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PHOTOGRAPHER

Transmission — Hong Kong-centered shibari rigger, teacher and photographer using the public identity 小王子 Reve. Dreaming with Shibari's official Linktree names the Hong Kong studio as a project by Reve, while the studio's public profile names Reve as its manager and advertises recurring small-group rope gatherings, classes, private lessons and rope photography. Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Public event sources use Rino, Rhino and RhinoRopes for the same scene handle: RopeFest Peterburg 2023 records Rino & Kotenochkin as Kazan / Saint Petersburg participants, USelf Kazan records Rhino / RhinoRopes as a guest Strappado workshop teacher, and Saint Petersburg show rosters list Rhino / RhinoRopes in Kinbaku show and Shibari in the Bar contexts. ### Riccardo Wildties

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/riccardo-wildties/

Fact — Riccardo Wildties, born 1976, is an Italian nawashi in the Naka-ryū.

Chronology — Born: 1976

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — Italian nawashi, photographer and educator. Initially self-taught, then turned decisively toward Naka Akira's semenawa after encountering Naka's work in 2012 and meeting him in Moscow in 2013. Early public traces and current atlas context place the first Kinbaku LuXuria period in 2011-2012 as a project co-established / co-run with Gorgone, while later public LuXuria sources frame Wildties and Red Sabbath as the main teaching / performance pair. Officially became Naka Akira's deshi in 2015 and is commonly described as Naka's ichiban deshi. Kinbaku LuXuria is listed as a Naka-ryu-descended style that re-engineers aspects of the technique for Western students and models while preserving its emotional and aesthetic core.

Fact — School: Naka-ryū

### Rina Trevi

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/rina-trevi/

Fact — Rina Trevi is documented as an American shibari artist.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — SHIBARI ARTIST · AUTHOR · COACH

Fact — Austin-centered shibari artist, instructor, vulnerability / intimacy guide and author; the `us` country location reflects public scene-base / teaching context rather than a citizenship claim. Rina's official bio names her as Rina Trevi, formerly Ms. Renee Trevi, and frames Shibari as Ritual as both her practice and art-book project after years of Tantric practice, bodywork and rope as self-discovery. Intimately Tied frames her as an Austin shibari instructor, while Bound Studio's July 10, 2026 calendar records a Lancaster, Pennsylvania shibari performance featuring Rina Trevi and characterizes her rope style as creative, connective and storytelling-focused.

### Rocco Grigio

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/rocco-grigio/

Fact — Rocco Grigio is a British educator.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — EDUCATOR · RIGGER

Fact — London-associated shibari / BDSM educator and rigger using the public identity Rocco Grigio. Studio Ma's official team page records Rocco under events and educator roles, says he facilitates classes and formats, nurtures Studio Ma's Asia relations, and characterizes his work as strongly influenced by Naka lineage with a particular resonance with Mistress Chiaki. Rocco's own public site includes a shibari journal category and private-class / session enquiry page; the Chiaki context is listed only as a bounded style / method influence, not lineage, deshi, rank or certification.

### Roman Fordinál

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/roman-fordinal/

Fact — Roman Fordinál is a rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ROPE MAKER

Transmission — Slovak / Bratislava shibari rigger and teacher associated with Shibari Dojo Bratislava. The official July 2026 beginner-workshop page names Roman as the instructor, says he first encountered shibari in 2016, has practiced intensively since late 2018, prefers traditional style and bamboo suspension, and also makes jute ropes. A 2020 Refresher workshop report independently frames Roman as the instructor, says Roman and Sona with other organizers established Shibari DOJO Bratislava, and characterizes regular beginner workshops plus open bondage jam sessions. Ardent's 2025 Czech / Slovak BDSM community guide names Roman Fordinál as the person leading Shibari Dojo Bratislava, described there as probably the main shibari place in Slovakia.

### RomKnots

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/romknots/

Fact — RomKnots is an Israeli rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Tel Aviv-centered Israeli shibari rigger, teacher and performer also credited as Ded Mazai. BoHouse described him in 2023 as a well-known Israeli shibari rigger practicing and teaching in Israel and abroad for the previous four years. The current record frames his background as coming out of Morikai Studio rather than a direct Naka Akira line, with strong influence from Yugen Studio. BalkanBound 2025 frames his later work as evolving into a personal freestyle approach; his work with his wife and model NatNata is framed around intimate, expressive scenes, discipline and personal connection.

### Rope Phoenix

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/rope-phoenix/

Fact — Rope Phoenix is a Brazilian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — RIGGER · EDUCATOR · PERFORMER

Fact — Brazilian shibari artist, rigger, educator, performer and rope model using the public identity Rope Phoenix / Ana Paula Stock. Jornal JA names Rope Phoenix as Ana Paula Stock, founder of the Rio de Janeiro shibari art group Coletivo Wabi-sabi, first Brazilian participant at EURIX in 2022, returning Brazilian representative in 2023, and an IKSK Berlin residency artist and workshop teacher. A 2026 Lovenox event page names Phoenix as founder of Studio Phoenix RJ, a shibari space in central Rio de Janeiro, and characterizes her Brazil / international event history spanning Shibari Experience, Shibari in Concert, Mr Fetiche Brasil, Sexibility BR, Orgastica, EURIX and IKSK.

### Rope Shadow

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/rope-shadow/

Fact — Rope Shadow is a Greek rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PHOTOGRAPHER

Transmission — Athens-centered Greek photographer, filmmaker, performer, teacher and shibari artist using the public identity Rope Shadow, Chris Shaghal and Christos Tsakalos. ShibariNews frames him as the local Athens Rope Dojo teacher from 2012 and ties him to Rope Studies, his shibari performance team. Independent Greek sources make the project relationship strong enough to model directly: Athens Voice says Rope Studies was created in late 2011 by Tsakalos and had already logged dozens of Athens performances by 2014, VICE characterizes Rope Studies as combining photography, live performance and seminars with Alice V. As the other named Rope Studies participant, and Booze Cooperativa's Rope Incident pages place Rope Studies and Athens Rope Dojo together in recurring Athens workshop / performance programming. Public references also place Rope Studies in a 2013 Gorgone collaboration and a 2014 EURIX teacher / seminar context.

### RopeCat

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ropecat/

Fact — RopeCat is documented as a German model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — MODEL · TEACHER

Fact — German bondage model, performer and co-trainer known as Nicole / RopeCat, long-term rope and performance partner of Matthias T. J. Grimme. German sources describe her as Grimme's co-presenter and co-trainer; Institut ATMA says she has performed with Matthias for 20 years and been his co-trainer for almost 18 years. EURIX XXVI 2025 records Matthias T. J. Grimme & Nicole for the beginner course. BoundCon 2026 records Matthias Grimme and Rope Cat together for a BDSM workshop and BoundCon's archive includes Matthias Grimme & RopeCat video traces.

### RopeMarks

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ropemarks/

Fact — RopeMarks is a Dutch nawashi in the Naka-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Transmission — Dutch nawashi and teacher Bob Roos, centered in Amsterdam and operating as RopeMarks. His own RopeMarks Ryū page says he started studying and professionally specializing in Japanese rope bondage in 1999, studied in Japan under the lineages of Denki Akechi and Naka Akira, and teaches worldwide spanning Europe, the USA, Russia and Japan. RopeMarks is known for dynamic suspensions, energetic performances, a distinct personal style built from traditional Japanese bondage techniques, and hands-on RopeMarks Ryū courses / masterclasses. The site records a long event trail via RopeFest, Moscow Knot, EURIX, Prague Shibari Festival, Shibari Lounge, Kinbaku Studio Vienna, Pinse Camp, Onawa Asobi Europe and BalkanBound.

Fact — School: Naka-ryū

### RopEmotion

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ropemotion/

Fact — RopEmotion is documented as a German nawashi in the Yagami-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — German nawashi / bondage artist centered in the Ruhr Valley, with The first-party site imprint placing RopEmotion in Gelsenkirchen. Official Yagami Ryu instructor. The official profile says he has studied shibari intensively since 2010 with national and international riggers such as Ren Yagami, Osada Steve, DocVale, RopeKnight, Wildties and Mosafir, and names Ren Yagami as the strongest continuing influence. His work combines psychology, eroticism, communication, emotion and safety, and he programming includes workshops, private sessions, individual coaching, homestudio / home-visit tuition, photoshoots and rope sales. The first-party site records current German workshop locations such as Furth / Nuremberg, Gelsenkirchen, Gera, Hamburg, Hildesheim, Leipzig, Munich and online, plus event traces such as BoundCon, WGT Leipzig, M'era Luna Festival Hildesheim, Ropefest, Moscow Knots and Flowers and Bees.

Fact — School: Yagami-ryū

### RopePuppet

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ropepuppet/

Fact — RopePuppet is an Australian rope performer.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ROPE PERFORMER · MODEL

Fact — Adelaide-centered rope performer and model using the public identity RopePuppet / @ropepuppet. The public Instagram profile frames the account as a shibari journey from Adelaide and links to the 2026 Adelaide Fringe show B.D.S.M. Adelaide Fringe's official listing frames B.D.S.M. As an R18+ circus / physical-theatre show by Devious Adelaide with shibari in its performance frame. Upside Adelaide's March 2026 review separately names Rope Puppet and Will Bound in the show's opening live shibari demonstration, describing Rope Puppet as the person being tied, rigged and suspended.

### RopuNawa

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ropunawa/

Transmission — RopuNawa is a Swiss teacher in the Naka-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · NAWASHI

Transmission — Basel-centered rope artist. Started in 2008 after seeing Arisue Go perform in Australia; began with Osada Steve's style via local Swiss teachers. Eclectic lineage: early study and ongoing aesthetic influence from Akira Naka; further shaped by Nicolas Yoroi (early influence), Yagami Ren ('less is more' direction), and Tifereth (mentor from ~2019). Runs his own dojo in Basel and teaches internationally.

Fact — School: Naka-ryū

### Rory Ropes

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/rory-ropes/

Fact — Rory Ropes is a British educator.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — EDUCATOR · PERFORMER

Fact — London-centered shibari educator, performer and rigger. Devil Mask Studio's host-side workshop bio and Anatomie Studio's current team page identify Rory as part of Anatomie Studio's teaching team, describe a shibari practice started in 2020, and frame his rope as playful, dynamic and attentive to accessibility, body manipulation, nerve risk and model wellbeing. Rough + Ready's current RoryRopes collection and booking page additionally route private lessons and rope-session experiences via his home studio near Aylesbury, with Anatomie Studio in Peckham offered subject to studio availability.

### Rosa Canina

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/rosa-canina/

Fact — Rosa Canina is a rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Singapore-centered rope bottom, rigger, teacher, organizer and rope-community writer using the public identity Rosa Canina / @rosadoesrope. The public Instagram profile frames Rosa as a kinky bi rope practitioner sharing rope journey, perspectives and opinions and links directly to That Rope Place. That Rope Place's official Meet the Team page names Rosa as owner, characterizes her as a bottom who ties, and says she began tying in 2019 to help more female riggers develop in the community. The same page says Rosa Canina and Python are a married couple who explored rope together from 2015 in London, were regulars at Anatomie Studio until 2019, then moved to Singapore. That Rope Place's official private-class page names Rosa as one of the studio's current instructors, Shibari Study's April 3, 2024 community article credits Rosa as co-owner of the Singapore-centered studio, and an owned That Rope Place blog post names Rosa Canina as author of studio-facing rope-jam etiquette guidance.
