### Roxu

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/roxu/

Fact — Roxu is a Spanish rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Murcia-origin rigger, teacher and performer, now based between Berlin and Murcia. Public IKSK, InFLUX and Danish Shibari Festival bios say he discovered shibari in 2016, traveled widely to study, co-established Espacio Takibi in Murcia after finding few local rope opportunities, and organized jams, workshops and study groups there. His current teaching with Ena Dahl focuses on erotic tension, teasing, floorwork, partials and predicaments; the same bios describe his personal style as influenced by Aibunawa and Semenawa. Public Instagram and Meetup traces also frame him as a rigger / educator connected to SPNKD Berlin. EURIX XXVI / Spring 2025 records Ena Dahl & Roxu as Berlin / Murcia presenters.

### Roy Burn

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/roy-burn/

Fact — Roy Burn is a rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · DOJO OPERATOR

Transmission — Lima, Peru shibari teacher, rigger and dojo lead. The 2022 `Vergajo 3` Festival de Cuerdas publication profiles Roy Burn as a Peru-centered rigger with more than twenty-five years in BDSM, more than ten years focused on shibari, intensive study with Osada Steve in Tokyo, regular students in Lima and operation of `Deviant: Shibari Dojo`. Independent public Lima sources also name Roy Burn as a shibarista in a cultural conversation, quote him as a professional shibarista in the `Nawashi Love` photo project, and preserve older Lima Shibari Crew context.

### Rumi Kasuga (春日ルミ)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/rumi-kasuga/

Fact — Rumi Kasuga (春日ルミ), born 1930, is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi.

Chronology — Born: 1930

Fact — Era: 1950s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · MISTRESS · MODEL

Fact — Showa-era Japanese kinbakushi, mistress and model born in Osaka. Nawapedia says she first appeared in Kitan Club in 1954, contacted the magazine and was hired on a monthly contract, wrote about herself in the 1950s and returned with scene reports in the 1970s. Her bondage was credited as Design by Rumi Kasuga, and Suruga-ya issue listings independently show 1955 Kitan Club items under her name plus repeated Kasuga / Ibuki pair-photo credits.

### Ryhor Shabeka

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ryhor-shabeka/

Fact — Ryhor Shabeka is a Belarusian nawashi.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · PERFORMER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Belarusian rope artist, teacher, performer and community organizer centered in Poland. Limits names Ryhor as a Belarusian rigger centered in Poland who started his rope journey in 2012 as both model and rigger, organizes the Belarusian bondage-loving community Nawashibelarus and the Shibaripanda 2020 festival in Minsk, and performs expressive shibari on European stages. Litexpo's 2025 Limits Zone page similarly bills him as a Poland-centered rope artist, creator of the modern shibari community Nawashibelarus and co-creator of Shibaripanda Minsk. Wisimi Studio's 2025 workshop listing adds that he has organized exchanges and trainings since 2016, established Nawashi Belarus and the Shibaripanda / Nawapaty festivals in Minsk, and works in a chaotic style aimed at speed, minimalism and drawing emotion from the model. Gdansk event pages in July 2024 give an additional Polish performance trace under the @dzikalak identity. In a current atlas context, Ryhor confirms that Mihail Balabanchik taught him.

### Ryuan (流庵殿)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ryuan/

Fact — Ryuan (流庵殿) is a Spanish nawashi in the Yagami-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — Spanish nawashi and instructor centered in Gijón, Asturias. Active in the Spanish shibari scene since approximately 2004–2005, making him one of the most senior Western practitioners. Leads Nawakai Gijón / Escuela de Shibari, a Yagami-ryu dojo for in-person teaching in Asturias, and Shibari Dojo as an online Spanish-language school. Developed the #MSAFE teaching methodology for accessible rope education. A 2017 interview characterizes Osada Steve's 2010 Barcelona workshop as a positive influence on his perspective and preserves his Gijón organizing context around a Hajime Kinoko tour stop. Ren Yagami's official profile says Ryuan began studying under Yagami Ren in 2016 and became a certified instructor in 2018; a current atlas context gives the Japanese certification wording as 流庵殿 認定教師 鵺神流初傳.

Fact — School: Yagami-ryū

### Ryuzaki Asuka (龍崎飛鳥)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ryuzaki-asuka/

Fact — Ryuzaki Asuka (龍崎飛鳥) is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi.

Fact — Era: 1990s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · DOMINATRIX

Transmission — Kyoto-centered dominatrix, kinbaku practitioner, teacher and salon owner active in the Kansai SM / fetish scene since 1990. Kinbaku Today's 2020 interview says she joined Kyoto's Rosencreutz SM club in 1990, studied rope via videos of Nureki Chimuo and Akechi Denki at a time when there were no professional kinbakushi in Kansai, managed Barajujikan / Rosencreutz in 1993-1994, and built the VELVET / BAR-BARA salon lineage from 1995 onward. The official BAR-BARA Kyoto page frames Asuka Ryuzaki as a renowned SM / fetish mistress welcoming visitors to a salon-style bar around fetishism, SM, bondage, shibari and kinbaku.

### s'Witch Salt

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/s-witch-salt/

Fact — S'Witch Salt is an Israeli rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Fact — Tel Aviv-centered rigger, rope instructor, Domme and Morikai Studio founder / co-founder using the public identities s'Witch Salt, Witch Salt and S'witch Salynea. A public Instagram screenshot names @witch_salt as s'Witch and says she is one of Morikai Studio's founders; Instagram profile confirms the current handle and display name. Public X profile for @Witchsalt1 characterizes Witch_salt as a Tel Aviv-centered rigger / rope instructor / Domme and Morikai Studio co-founder. Morikai's Tazman booking pages list Witch Salt as a teacher for current Morikai classes and specials, and EventBuzz records S'witch Salynea / Witch Salynea leading a 2020 History of Kinbaku lecture at Morikai Studio.

### Saara Rei

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/saara-rei/

Fact — Saara Rei is documented as a German rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · ROPE BOTTOM · PERFORMER · TEACHER

Fact — Berlin-centered multidisciplinary performance artist, kinbaku practitioner and workshop facilitator with a professional background in music, dance, education and public speaking, as well as a PhD in mathematics. Active in Japanese-inspired rope bondage since 2014, she works as both a rigger and an experienced rope bottom. Her surreal, provocative and emotionally raw performance and teaching practice explores vulnerability, power, intensity, sadomasochism and emotional transformation via embodied exchange. She is a co-founder of the rope-bottom education project Embodied in Rope with Anna Noctuelle and Natasha NawaTaNeko, and co-hosts Model Behaviour with them. Saara names Felix Ruckert and the Schwelle7 community as formative teachers and influences.

### Saara Tsuru

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/saara-tsuru/

Fact — Saara Tsuru is a Finnish rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Finnish shibari / kinbaku artist, performer and teacher, also credited as Saara Kantanen / Saara-Tsuru / Shibaritsuru. Kakumei Kan names Saara Kantanen (Saara-Tsuru) and Jarno Kantanen as instructors and co-owners of the Helsinki kinbaku studio. BookRetreats and Shibari Haven describe Saara Tsuru as a Finland-centered artist, performer, practitioner and teacher who works professionally with shibari, teaches workshops, programming includes private sessions and performs in Finland and abroad. Her teaching prioritizes emotional connection over complex patterns, with Naka-influenced semenawa, dynamic play, ritual elements, fundamentals, safe suspension technique and model agency.

### SadiKarla

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/sadikarla/

Fact — SadiKarla is a Chilean rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · ROPE BOTTOM

Fact — Santiago / Chile rigger and rope-bottom using the public identity SadiKarla / K de Kuerda via the @k.dekuerda profile. The public profile names her as a rigger and ropebottom, BDSM switch and Gashira Nawa-linked figure, with @sadi.karla listed as the responsible / backup account. Convencion de Shibari's public flash-session programme characterizes SadiKarla / @k.dekuerda as a rigger whose approach combines shibari aesthetics and semenawa. Separate Chilean Excéntrico festival coverage credits K de Kuerda with Elmundonomeadivine and Alita Greenblack on the 2023 BDSM short `A plena luz de dia`, giving a non-social public media trail into the Santiago rope / erotic-art scene.

### Saikatsu (斎勝)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/saikatsu/

Transmission — Saikatsu (斎勝), 1940–2024, is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Akechi lineage.

Chronology — Born: 1940

Chronology — Died: 2024

Fact — Era: 1990s

Fact — NAWASHI

Fact — Japanese nawashi (1940 - 1 July 2024), remembered as a close confidant and long-time assistant to Akechi Denki. Born in Kanazawa and drawn to rope via pink films and SM magazines in the 1960s, he began tying later after helping Akechi. Taught at Black Heart in Ginza, published how-to articles in SM Mania Club, and remained active at Bakuyukai, the rope space established by Akechi Denki and later carried on by Miura Takumi. Valued in the Japanese scene for patient teaching and deep historical memory.

Transmission — School: Akechi lineage

### Saki Kamijoo (上条早樹)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/saki-kamijoo/

Fact — Saki Kamijoo (上条早樹) is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · PERFORMER

Fact — Kansai-centered kinbakushi, mistress and performer, described by Nawapedia as the owner / manageress of SM Salon PISS in Osaka, began operating in November 2004. Known for fast, fluid stage rope, for the 'Queen of Dokutsuya-kai' performance context, and also as lead singer / guitarist of the band Kalra. Toured the United reports in 2013 and performed at BoundCon San Francisco on September 18-21, 2014 with Norio Sugiura, Nagaike-shi and Ranki Kazami.

### Sakurada Denjiro (桜田伝次郎)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/sakurada-denjiro/

Transmission — Sakurada Denjiro (桜田伝次郎), born 1952, is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Akechi lineage.

Chronology — Born: 1952

Fact — Era: 1980s

Fact — NAWASHI · PERFORMER

Fact — Japanese nawashi and underground-theatre figure. Led the underground troupe Sekai Gekijō (Theater of the World) and established GSG Planning to organise SM shows. In the 1970s formed relationships with Shigeru Kayama, Eikichi Osada, Denki Akechi and Keiyu Tamai. In 1978 met Denki Akechi (with brother Shin Sakurada) at GSG Planning. Opened the small hall Theatre Poo in Shinjuku in 1983. Retired from performing in 1985, after which Akechi Denki took over his shows. The 'Den' in 'Denki' was borrowed from his given name Denjirō.

Transmission — School: Akechi lineage

- [nawapedia](https://nawapedia.net/index.php?title=Denjiro_Sakurada)

### Sakuya (さくや)

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ROPE PERFORMER · BAR OWNER

Fact — Tokyo / Ikebukuro rope performer, cosplayer and Mistress Bar Guernica operator using the public identity Sakuya / さくや. Guernica's official staff page records Sakuya on the cast, the linked Note profile says Sakuya runs Mistress Bar Guernica and the nearby GuernicaStudio while performing domestic and international cosplay shows under the SABAKU / 鎖縛 label, and Tokyo Love District characterizes Guernica's nightly rope performance show as led by owner and rope performer Sakuya. The public Bluesky profile preserves the same Guernica, cast-from-2022 and owner-from-2024 context.

### Salamandr

Fact — Salamandr is a Russian nawashi.

Fact — NAWASHI · PERFORMER

Fact — Moscow-centered Russian bondage artist and RopeFest performer. Nawapedia.ru records Salamandr as a Moscow bondage artist. RopeFest Moscow 2021 names Salamandr from Moscow among the main-stage performers, while RopeFest St. Petersburg 2023 records Salamandr & Vasilisa Lesnaya in the participant announcement. ### Sam Jay

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/sam-jay/

Fact — Sam Jay is documented as an American performer.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — PERFORMER · TEACHER

Fact — Co-founder of NYC Shibari and NYC Street Shibari with Zero-Gi. Studying rope since 2012; teaming up with Zero-Gi in 2018. Known for technically innovative suspensions (Thighbinder, hashira TwistyPops). Prague Shibari Festival's Autumn 2025 presenter bio says Sam Jay picked up strong fundamentals from 2011 to 2017 in the Osada-ryu-centered Melbourne Rope Dojo as one of the first five students, and names Sam Jay & Zero-Gi as international teachers / performers known for NYC Street Shibari, high-altitude urban suspensions and dynamic stage work. EURIX 2022 special guest; performer at Onawa Asobi Europe 2026.

### Sansblague

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/sansblague/

Fact — Sansblague is a Swedish switch.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — SWITCH · TEACHER

Transmission — Stockholm-centered rope switch, teacher, writer and organizer working with Bergborg. They have tied together since 2011 and teach creativity, movement and dynamic interaction as central elements of kinbaku. In addition to regular teaching at their rope studio Kokoro in Stockholm, Sansblague and Bergborg have taught at EURIX, LFRA, Kinbaku Weekend Helsinki and IRW Utrecht. Sansblague has organized rope events specifically for women and trans people since 2013, with a practice influenced by bodywork, dance and stage performance. EURIX 2014 presenter.

- [Shibaricamp bio](https://shibaricamp.se/teachers/sansblague-and-bergborg)

### Sansei

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/sansei/

Fact — Sansei is a Czech rigger in the Naka-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · MODEL · TEACHER

Transmission — Czech / Prague-scene rigger, model, teacher and performer using the public identity Sansei / Tereza Sansei, usually with Soptik / Peter Soptik and via Subspace Prague's Kinbaku workshop programme. Prague Shibari Festival's 2024 and 2026 presenter pages, the Soptik & Sansei official site and Subspace's Sansei profile describe a practice centered on traditional kinbaku, Kinbaku LuXuria, deep surrender and darker semenawa emotions. The same sources state that Sansei has been doing rope since 2017, studying with Soptik since 2019 and studying Kinbaku LuXuria with Riccardo Wildties and Red Sabbath since 2022; the Prague / Subspace sources say Soptik and Tereza Sansei are certified in that style up to level 1. Knot Here Amsterdam and Shibari Valencia Fest provide additional 2025-2026 public performance / educator traces.

Fact — School: Naka-ryū

### Sarah Calanthe

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/sarah-calanthe/

Fact — Sarah Calanthe is a Dutch rope bottom.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ROPE BOTTOM · DISCUSSION LEAD

Fact — Amsterdam-area rope bottom / discussion lead using the public identity Sarah Calanthe. Knot Here's first Rope Jam page names Tasteofdinner and Sarah Calanthe as announcing the first monthly Knot Here Rope Jam and as the people who controlled suspension-point permission. Knot Here's Bottoms Up page and the Hipsy mirror name Sarah Calanthe and Tasteofdinner as discussion leads for a rope-bottom knowledge-exchange event, describing both as rope bottoms with more than four years of experience with different riggers and styles. The official Sarah Calanthe domain, exact Bluesky custom-domain profile and linked Instagram route provide public identity / contact continuity only. The available public record does not support lineage, teaching certification, a person-to-person collaboration claim or adult-platform context.

### Sarca Martello

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/sarca-martello/

Fact — Sarca Martello is an Italian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Italian rigger, teacher, artist, photographer and holistic operator credited by BoundCon as Sarca and by other event sources as Sarca Luca Martello. His Iyasu Ropu history page says he discovered ropes around 2015 and moved into full-time study; the same site frames him as creator, teacher and operator of the 'Rope that heals' method, with shibari work built around art, care, photography, meditation and psychophysical well-being. BoundCon records him for repeated Sarca & Winter performances, 2026 custom-shoot, escape-challenge and Ropes and breath control workshop contexts. Sakura Rope records Iyasu Ropu as a method by Sarca Martello and became an official instructor of the method in 09/2023. Japanese Shibari Week records Sarca Luca Martello as a shibari photo exhibitor; EURIX XXVII records Sarca & Kat Schrodinger as 2025 presenters.

### Seb Kinbaku

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — Paris-centered shibari / kinbaku teacher, artist and material-platform founder. Boutique du Shibari frames Seb Kinbaku as a teacher and artist with more than fifteen years of practice, combining technical rope work, safety, connection and PNL / hypnotherapy-informed pedagogy. The official shop frames Boutique du Shibari as the direct extension of his teaching and rope-selection work, with Japanese shibari ropes, accessories, guides and private or group classes in Paris; Billetweb and Weezevent preserve current Paris workshop / initiation formats under the Seb Kinbaku name.

### Ueda Seishiro (上田青柿郎)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/seishiro-ueda/

Fact — Ueda Seishiro (上田青柿郎) is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi in the Foundational.

Fact — Era: 1950s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · EDITOR

Transmission — Early postwar Japanese kinbakushi, editor and writer. Nawapedia names Ueda as editor of Yomikiri Romance and notes Seiu Ito as a major influence. Yomikiri Romance's own chronology records Ueda as editor during the magazine's early-1950s shift into one of Japan's earliest recurring bondage-photo and SM publications. Kinbaku Books' 2016 Kinbaku Photo Senshu post further characterizes him as a younger friend of Itoh Seiu, credits him with the rope work for Yomikiri Romance's 1952 special bondage issue, and records the early-1970s Kinbaku Photo Senshu anthology series.

Fact — School: Foundational

### Ito Seiu (伊藤晴雨)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/seiu-ito/

Fact — Ito Seiu (伊藤晴雨), 1882–1961, is documented as a Japanese painter in the Foundational.

Chronology — Born: 1882

Chronology — Died: 1961

Fact — Era: 1900s

Fact — PAINTER · WRITER

Fact — Father of modern kinbaku. Born Hajime Ito (伊藤一) on 3 March 1882 in Asakusa, Tokyo; son of a metalworker, trained in ivory carving and sculpture before turning to painting. Adopted the alias 'Seiu' (clear-rain) at age 13. By the 1910s a newspaper illustrator and theatre critic; later head of the Performing Arts section and chief illustrator at Yomiuri News. Drawn to scenes of torture in theatre and stories, produced a large body of paintings, drawings and photographs documenting kinbaku — such as the 1919 suspension photo published in Hentai Shiryo (1926). Hired model Kise Sahara in 1919, who became his second wife. Censored from 1930, lost much of his work in the Great Tokyo Air Raid. Received an award from the Japan Artists Association in 1960; died 28 January 1961.

Fact — School: Foundational

The official Stoa team page says he first explored shibari via books, joined Lyon Shibari in 2016, studied first with Dragon, built his first Lyon Shibari training cycles in 2018 and now teaches again via Stoa Shibari. Named workshop teachers on the same page remain study background record and are not expanded into lineage connections.

### Sergej Pejić

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/sergej-pejic/

Fact — Sergej Pejić is a Serbian shibari master.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — SHIBARI MASTER · PERFORMER

Fact — Serbian shibari practitioner publicly credited as Master Sergej Pejić. A 2015 Sajsi MC / Dragan Vurdelja project credits him for shibari, and VICE characterizes him as the rope specialist brought into the shoot via the Belgrade Akademija lepih boli scene. VICE Serbia's 2016 documentary on Belgrade BDSM / fetish culture later records Sergej Pejic as the shibari master. Nova.rs in 2022 frames him as a key Serbian shibari master, quoting him on growing local interest, domestic and international festival performances, and his role in the development and commercialization of shibari in Serbia.

### Sergey A.

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/sergey-a-bern/

Transmission — Sergey A. Is a Russian teacher in the Osada / Western.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · RIGGER

Transmission — Russian-born rope teacher and rigger behind Kinbaku Studio Bern. His official about page says he first encountered rope around 1999, later resumed a continuous shibari practice in Bern around 2014-2015 with his wife, met Osada Steve during the latter's 2018 Switzerland visit, studied Osada-style tying intensively at an affiliated Swiss studio from 2019 to 2024 and completed Kyu 5 certification, then opened Kinbaku Studio Bern in 2021.

Fact — School: Osada / Western

### The Shady Lady

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/shady-lady/

Fact — The Shady Lady is a Dutch model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — MODEL · PERFORMER

Fact — Rope model / bottom, performer and workshop collaborator in the RopeMarks orbit. Prague Shibari Festival 2023 frames the Shady Lady with RopeMarks as a presenter duo and characterizes her as tying regularly with him for the preceding 1.5 years, with a practice centered on semenawa, Japanese aesthetics, authentic rope dynamics, surrender, suffering and erotic self-exploration. RopeMarks' own site names her as part-time secretary and rope model for RopeMarks, and later event reports show her co-teaching / performing with him at Prague Shibari Festival 2023, Kinbaku Studio Vienna 2024, Pinse Camp 2024 and Onawa Asobi Europe 2024.

### Shambler

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/shambler/

Fact — Shambler is a Russian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Moscow / Almaty rigger, teacher and performer; creator of the ShibariTutorial Telegram education platform. In a current atlas context, Shambler says his map relevance is primarily the creation of @ShibariTutorial rather than a RopeFest appearance, and clarifies that his RopeFest Peterburg 2026 performance was on the main stage rather than the small stage. RopeFest Peterburg 2026 says he has practiced shibari for more than ten years, was inspired by attending RopeFest as a viewer about eight years earlier, names Alex Hitch as his main mentor, and is listed in the master-class program.

### Shiawase

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/shiawase/

Fact — Shiawase is an Italian rigger in the Naka-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · STUDIO FOUNDER

Transmission — Italian rigger, performer, teacher and co-founder of Peony Studio in Turin. With Andrea Kurogami, began studying kinbaku in September 2016 and attended their first Riccardo Wildties course in November 2016. Peony Studio characterizes the pair as continuing via advanced Riccardo courses while taking part in kinbaku events in Italy and other European cities as jam participants, performers and educators. In 2019 Kurogami and Shiawase tied at the 25th Nawa Naka Kai in Tokyo with Riccardo Wildties, Red Sabbath and Naka Akira.

Fact — School: Naka-ryū

### Shibari Mari

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/shibari-mari/

Fact — Shibari Mari is documented as an American shibari artist.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — SHIBARI ARTIST · ORGANIZER · CONSULTANT · DESIGNER

Fact — Chicago-centered shibari artist, sensual / kink consultant, Pro-Domme, designer and organizer using the public identity The Tantric Mystic / Shibari Mari. Her Substack and current Instagram profile identify her as The Tantric Mystic / Shibari Mari and frame a shibari practice of roughly eight to nine years; Eventbrite characterizes The Tantric Mystic as specializing in shibari rope play with a facilitation style around emergent-strategy practice, imagination, resilience and social-emotional care. Black & Kinky Academy Weekend's official 2026 page names The Tantric Mystic / Shibari Mari among the Chicago-centered kinksters, educators and organizers who created and lead the Black-led BIPOC kink / BDSM conference. Current Haus of ZOA sources also place her in wearable-rope / shibari-fashion design, describing Haus of ZOA as a Shibari Fashion House created by The Tantric Mystic.

### Shibari Sari

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/shibari-sari/

Fact — Shibari Sari is documented as a German rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · COACH

Fact — Halle-centered German rope practitioner, rigger / model and coach for bodywork and sexuality. German podcast bios describe her workshops for adults around self-love, BDSM and bondage, her work with rope, bondage shootings and safety work behind the camera on the Amazon series Gefesselt. Köiejutud's 2024 shibari podcast characterizes her as an emerging international rope-scene talent with years of model experience, a style marked by direct aesthetics, intense emotion, D/s dynamics, anatomical precision and bodywork informed by dance and yoga. EURIX 2022 presenter with Fynn, 2023 beginner-course teacher and 2023 presenter with Cath After Dark; IKSK Berlin also hosted her March 2024 two-day guest weekend and performance afternoon.

### Shibari Shawn

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/shibari-shawn/

Fact — Shibari Shawn is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Fact — Seattle-centered gay kinbaku artist, rope bondage educator and organizer using the public identity Shibari Shawn. His official House of Knotty biography says he started rope bondage in Cedar Rapids in 2016, taught via Homer Shibari Dojo from 2019, established House of Knotty in 2023, and co-organizes RopeLab Seattle for intermediate and advanced men ropers tying on the masculine form. Ropecraft and Rope In Seattle profiles independently frame him as House of Knotty's founder / lead instructor and list wider teaching traces such as Pan Eros Foundation, Rope Burn SF, Men in Rope / MenRopingMen, Austin Kink Weekend, WestCoast Bound and Ropecraft.

### Shibarista Jess

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/shibarista-jess/

Fact — Shibarista Jess is a Belgian model.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — MODEL · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Belgian / Antwerp shibari model, photographer, rigger, teacher and organizer. Met Marc BeShibari in 2007 and began modelling for his Japanese-style rope work, then performed and gave workshops with him throughout Europe. Started photographing his rope sessions in 2014, inspired by Nobuyoshi Araki and Norio Sugiura, and co-established Shibari Lounge in Antwerp in 2016. Since 2018 she has also tied and taught there. Organizes Onawa Asobi Europe with Yoi Yoshida and BeShibari, and performed at the 2020 edition and in the announced 2026 lineup.

### Kayama Shigeru (賀山茂)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/shigeru-kayama/

Fact — Kayama Shigeru (賀山茂), 1926–2014, is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi.

Chronology — Born: 1926

Chronology — Died: 2014

Fact — Era: 1950s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · PHOTOGRAPHER

Fact — Showa-era Japanese kinbakushi, photographer and publishing / salon organizer. Nawapedia says Kayama studied rope work under Seishiro Ueda in 1954, became acquainted with Takashi Tsujimura in 1958 and deepened exchanges with Oniroku Dan in 1959, later assisting bondage for Nikkatsu films and opening the Roppongi SM salon SAMM around 1980. Kinbaku Books' Beautifully Bound archive names him as editor of Minomura Kou's 1953 photo album Beautifully Bound, one of the earliest artistic kinbaku photo books.

### Shigonawa Bingo (紫護縄びんご)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/shigonawa-bingo/

Fact — Shigonawa Bingo (紫護縄びんご), 1963–2025, is documented as a Japanese nawashi.

Chronology — Born: 1963

Chronology — Died: 2025

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — Tokyo-centered kinbakushi (9 May 1963 - 24 November 2025) who refined his craft via private practice and observation; self-taught, and resistant to school labels or dogma. Came into the public scene in his late 40s after years of private tying. Co-ran Bar UBU and Mitsu in Shinjuku with Yoi Yoshida, taught internationally, and co-authored the 2016 manual "You Can Do It Now! How to Tie" with Ranki Kazami. Remembered for fluid, natural rope, signature purple ropes, kotobazeme, dry humor, and a philosophy that treated rope as a language of connection rather than a rigid system.

### Shima Shikou (志摩紫光)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/shikou-shima/

Fact — Shima Shikou (志摩紫光) is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi.

Fact — Era: 1980s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · DIRECTOR

Fact — Tokyo-born Japanese kinbakushi, AV director and prolific SM-media maker whose public trace spans late-Showa print culture, the cassette-video boom and a still-active self-hosted current phase. Nawapedia says Shikou Shima worked under Musha, then for U Production in the 1980s, appeared in Nikkatsu films at the dawn of the home-video era, later self-produced a long run of SM videotapes and manuals under the Shima Planning label, described himself as "The King of SM," and sustained a more than 30-year career spanning SM publishing and film. The same source notes his harder, unorthodox rope style, Norio Sugiura photo documentation and early needle-play notoriety. His current official Kyukyokuai site frames him as the long-running host of the trust-centered `Ai Off` / `Kyukyokuai Off` gatherings and says he has advocated and practiced that BD-centered philosophy for more than 40 years.

### Shin Nawakiri

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/shin-nawakiri/

Fact — Shin Nawakiri is a kinbakushi in the Kanna-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — KINBAKUSHI · TEACHER · AUTHOR

Transmission — Taiwan-centered kinbakushi, performer, teacher, author and organizer using the public identity Shin Nawakiri / Shin Nawakari. Mystic Productions Press's public author profile says he began studying kinbaku under Nawashi Kanna in Tokyo in 2003, co-established BDSM Company Taiwan in 2004 as a public BDSM-rights support group, participated in the local Dancing Rope Maiden performances from 2006 to 2010, and debuted internationally as a kinbaku performance artist in 2011. The Google Books record for Essence of Shibari adds that he publicly advocates for BDSM rights in Taiwan and has taught in Macao, Taipei, Moscow and London.

Fact — School: Kanna-ryū

### Shinobu Fuwa (芙羽忍)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/shinobu-fuwa/

Fact — Shinobu Fuwa (芙羽忍) is documented as a Japanese nawashi.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Japanese nawashi / kinbaku teacher using the public identity Shinobu Fuwa / 芙羽忍. Fuwa's official site frames the practice around rope as communication, beauty and performance rather than generic SM play, says Fuwa is mainly Tokyo-centered, holds regular kinbaku classes and private tuition, and links public Facebook, X and Instagram surfaces. The same official site and the public Lotus Bluesky profile identify Fuwa as owner of the Lotus project, kept here as project/contact context rather than as a separate entry. A 2018 Tokyo Shibari Aid report names Shinobu Fuwa among the established masters performing at Hajime Kinoko / Ichinawakai's Jyo-mon night alongside Nawashi Kanna, Shishi-waka and Maruku.

### Shirin

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/shirin-shibari/

Fact — Shirin is documented as a German rope switch.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — ROPE SWITCH · TEACHER

Transmission — Berlin-centered rope switch and shibari teacher using the public identity Shirin / @shirin.shibari. Daruma Berlin's official profile says she started shibari a few years ago, first as a bottom and quickly also as a rigger, and frames her practice around connection, intention, trust, communication, body awareness and both sides of the rope. Daruma's 2024-2025 pages place her in current teaching context: leading the November-December 2024 beginner course, co-teaching the May 2024 Introduction to Hashira with Ztella and Aja, returning for Hashira Level 2 as model / co-teacher, and co-leading the breast-emphasising rope class with Ztella during the Big FLINTA Weekend.

### Shishiwaka (獅子若)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/shishiwaka/

Fact — Shishiwaka (獅子若), born 1976, is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi in the Ichinawa-ryū.

Chronology — Born: 1976

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — KINBAKUSHI · TEACHER

Fact — Kansai / Osaka-centered Japanese kinbakushi and nawashi. Nawapedia names Shishiwaka as active in Kansai, born in 1976, starting kinbaku in 2008, running the Wakakaze Bondage Group from 2012 and owning Black Pearl in Osaka; Shishiwaka's 2013 event blog also records the Wakakaze Kinbaku Salon as a Ranki Kazami and Shishiwaka salon at Jyohya Club in Osaka. A Toubaku 2011 report records him among Hajime Kinoko's Ichinawakai members, so the map treats Kinoko / Ichinawakai as historical influence rather than current membership. MotionGallery frames him as an Osaka / Tokyo underground-scene performer, Bar BlackPearl master and Shishinawakai organizer; a 2026 Jack Rose listing names him as Osaka FetishBar ZOO owner and Shishinawakai organizer, while Zoo's public Bluesky and lit.link surfaces describe the venue as produced by Shishiwaka.

Fact — School: Ichinawa-ryū

### Shoga

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/shoga/

Fact — Shoga is documented as a German rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ROPE SUPPLIER

Transmission — Braunschweig-area German shibari / bondage rigger, teacher and rope-material shop operator using the public identity Shoga / Shoga Shibari. The official Shoga site says he began bondage in 2020 outside a classical school path, now programming includes group workshops and private instruction in the Braunschweig / OWL region, and anchors regular Wednesday shibari sessions at OCTAGON Braunschweig from 2024. BondageRope.EU's About page names Shoga as the owner / operator behind BondageRope.EU, says the rope-specific shop grew from Shoga's earlier shop in January 2023, and ties BondageRope.EU, Shoga Shibari and shibari.events to the same Holistio ecosystem. His public Instagram profile names the rope account as Shoga / Robert and routes visitors toward the official OCTAGON and BondageRope.EU accounts. Kunst der Unvernunft's 2022 episode places him in Braunschweig-area rope teaching / location-building and rope-preparation context. No formal lineage, certification or school assignment is asserted.

### Oki Shoji (沖渉二)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/shoji-oki/

Fact — Oki Shoji (沖渉二), born 1922, is documented as a Japanese artist.

Chronology — Born: 1922

Fact — Era: 1950s

Fact — ARTIST · ILLUSTRATOR

Fact — Showa-era Japanese illustrator in postwar SM publishing. Nawapedia supports Oki as a 1922-born illustrator published in Kitan Club, Fuzoku Kitan and related magazines. Kawade Shobo's 2023 publisher page for Kinbaku: Golden Age of Japanese Restrained & Torture credits Oki as one of the named authors/artists alongside Ozuma Yoko and Akiyoshi Ran, and frames the volume as a survey of Japanese kinbaku / seme-e artists.

### Shuhei Kitagawa (北川周平)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/shuhei-kitagawa/

Fact — Shuhei Kitagawa (北川周平) is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · PERFORMER

Fact — Japanese kinbakushi distinct from Salon Kitagawa's Kitagawa / 喜多川. Nawapedia names Shuhei Kitagawa / 北川周平 as born circa 1978, working at Roppongi Mistress from 2005 and mode et Baroque from 2008. His own FC2 official site characterizes him as a rope artist / kinbakushi active via show appearances and a Roppongi rope salon, with profile notes for Roppongi Mistress, mode et Baroque, Dokuenkai and a weekly Salon Kinbaku Kitagawakai at Roppongi INDIGO. Onawa Asobi performer records list him in the 2011 and 2012 Tokyo rosters, and TV Guide credits him as appearing in Jouni Hokkanen's 2010 documentary short Kinbaku alongside Osada Steve, Haruki Yukimura, Hajime Kinoko and others. No source in this review supports making Shuhei the Kitagawa teacher / influence named in Kasumi Hourai's bio.

### SickDucks

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/sickducks/

Fact — SickDucks is a Dutch model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — MODEL · TEACHER

Transmission — Dutch rope model, teacher and performer from Arnhem using the public identity SickDucks / sick.ducks. Official Prague Shibari Festival and current Mie Neko co-teacher pages say Fem came to rope via photography modeling, shifted from aesthetic image-making toward connection-focused rope, and is known for assertive bottoming, strong non-verbal communication and bottom-focused technical / anatomical teaching input. Current Mie Neko workshop pages bill MieNeko & sick.ducks as co-teachers for the 2026 Exploring Suspension series in Essen, while the official EURIX XXIX roster records MieNeko & SickDucks as Autumn 2026 presenters.

### Sin

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/sin/

Fact — Sin, born 1962, is documented as a German kinbakushi.

Chronology — Born: 1962

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · WRITER

Fact — Munich-centered professional kinbakushi, writer and performer associated with Kannōnawa, a sensual / carnal rope approach. Nawapedia names him as a Munich-centered professional kinbakushi and Kinbaku Today contributor born in 1962, and frames his Japanese-scene return via Nagare Aotsuki, Ero Ouji, Shigonawa Bingo and Haruki Yukimura after an earlier BDSM / rope period. Kinbaku Today's Onawa Asobi 2018 listing names Sin among the performers and characterizes him as resident in Germany, author of Year of The Bakushi and The Psychology of BDSM & Rope Bondage, and CEO of KOUMANAWA. A 2025 Kinbaku Today interview expands the source trail: first rope-bondage experience in 1984, regular underground performances since 2011, Japanese-scene friendships / lessons such as Bingo and Yukimura contexts, Amatsunawa history, and later KOUMANAWA rope-development work.

### Sir Pocketz

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/sir-pocketz/

Fact — Sir Pocketz is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — RIGGER · SOMATIC PRACTITIONER

Fact — Houston-centered rope / somatic practitioner and aerial shibari performer using the public identity Sir Pocketz. The Holistic Kink Coaching host page frames him as founder and lead practitioner, creator of Bondage Directed Somatic Medicine, and a practitioner whose rope / sensation / consent work has run spanning Houston, Austin, Dallas, NYC, Maine and Hawaii. The same page frames his practice around Muganawa / empty-mind rope tradition language and says he is also a fire performer with Miss Fay, his wife and creative partner, and an aerial shibari performer. The Japanese lineage wording on that page is retained as self-framing context only; no Osada Steve, Akechi Denki or certification relationship is documented from it.

### Skinny Redhead

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/skinnyredhead/

Fact — Skinny Redhead is a Polish artist.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — ARTIST · PERFORMER · TEACHER

Transmission — Polish-born, Edinburgh-centered shibari / self-suspension artist, performer, teacher and organizer using the public identity Skinny Redhead / Skinny-Art / Kasia Zawadzka. Her official Skinny Art biography says she first entered rope as a model in 2011, began tying and experimenting with self-suspension in 2012, and completed a Diploma in Physical Theatre Practice in Edinburgh in 2016 before continuing to tour internationally. Public surfaces frame her as founder / host of the Shibari Haven retreat, while Shibari Retreat Poland frames Kasia as retreat organiser / teacher / presenter and the Shibari Haven code of conduct ties the retreat identity to Kasia Zawadzka aka Skinny-Art. Earlier festival coverage and current presenter listings place her as a long-running EURIX participant bridging performance, retreat and education contexts.

### Slaanesh

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/slaanesh-pl/

Fact — Slaanesh is a Polish performer.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — PERFORMER · TEACHER

Fact — Polish shibari / bondage performer, educator and co-author of Nawado.pl, working in a long-running duo with GanRaptor. GanRaptor's official bio frames Slaanesh and GanRaptor as partners whose Polish BDSM-scene presence includes stage shows, one of the largest Polish-language shibari / bondage photo resources and the continuously developed Nawado.pl site. Nawado.pl reports that both Slaanesh and GanRaptor maintain the site's substantive content, built from their own rope experience, workshops, photos, films and texts.

### slab_me_harder

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/slab-me-harder/

Fact — Slab_me_harder is documented as an American model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — MODEL · PERFORMER · TEACHER

Transmission — San Francisco Bay Area rope performer, model and teacher alternate public name: Juliette. Shibari Study frames Dancehalldyke and slab_me_harder as a San Francisco lesbian rope couple focused on risk, negotiation and debriefing. Twisted Windows records Juliette / slab_me_harder in its troupe as a flyer who explores emotions, capabilities and vulnerabilities via rope, subverts traditional rope norms, builds space for queer and trans community in rope, performs at local, national and international events, and programming includes private lessons.

### SluttyHands

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/sluttyhands/

Fact — SluttyHands is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Fact — San Francisco Bay Area rope artist, performer, educator and photographer using the public identity SluttyHands. The Kink Store SF, VoxBody and Dark Odyssey presenter pages repeatedly list SluttyHands teaching with Dancehalldyke, describe the pair as Bay Area rope artists working in traditional Japanese-influenced styles, and identify Barkas / Addie influence in their shared teaching context. Seattle Erotic Art Festival's 2024 schedule separately records SluttyHands in an Aerial & Bondage performance. The current Bluesky profile supplies the public contact route and Linktree shell; adult-platform and private-session details are not expanded.

Safety / consent — Sofie / Sorisk is a Danish model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — MODEL · TEACHER

Safety / consent — Danish rope model, teacher and Kinbaku Nexus co-runner using the public Sorisk / `@sorisk_` identity. The official Kinbaku Nexus team page profiles Sofie / Sorisk under the founders section and emphasizes collaboration in rope, the model's active role, adaptation around limited mobility, transitions and takedowns, breath, calm under pressure and creating meaningful rope experiences. Her public Instagram profile separately characterizes Sorisk as a kinbaku enthusiast from Denmark and says she is co-running `@kinbakunexus` in Aarhus, Denmark.

### Soft Armor

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/soft-armor/

Fact — Soft Armor is documented as an American rope model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ROPE MODEL · SELF-TIER · FACILITATOR

Fact — NYC-centered US rope bottom, rope model, self-tier and workshop facilitator publicly appearing as Soft Armor / @soft_armor_. Karada House and Temple event bios say Soft Armor / Fern found rope in 2022 and felt at home in the intimacy, aesthetics and body-trust explorations possible in rope; they describe Soft Armor as NY-centered and traveling / tying in LA, Paris, Berlin and beyond. Studio Friction's 2026 pages describe Soft Armor as a NYC-centered rope bottom and self-tier working with themes of body-trust, art-making, interpersonal connection, pain processing and ableism in rope. Public Temple, Atelier Simonet / Billetweb, Karada House and Studio Friction pages repeatedly bill Soft Armor together with Bänana for classes, sessions and workshops.

### Solina

Fact — Solina is a Finnish model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — MODEL · PERFORMER

Fact — Finnish shibari artist, model, performer and workshop guide centered in Oulu. Ropevision's official `Meistä` page characterizes Solina as a shibari artist and model focused on conveying story and emotion to the audience, says she aims to bring rope art to wider audiences, and notes that she is actively developing her own tying practice. Ropevision's official workgroup and events pages place her as one of the two founders of the Oulu project with Theokino and explicitly name her as guide / teacher in current studio workshops, such as the spring 2026 `Kukkia ja sidontaa` self-tying workshop at the Ropevision studio in Hiukkavaara.

### Sophia Rose

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/sophia-rose/

Transmission — Sophia Rose is a British teacher.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — TEACHER · DOMINANT

Fact — London-centered BDSM professional, rope educator and performer. Official Venus Hour and Karada House organizer pages present Sophia Rose as offering private rope bondage sessions, shibari education and kink / BDSM experiences in London, practicing rope since 2013, and teaching via practical hands-on skills, power-dynamics awareness and communication. Her public Instagram / Venus Hour link hub frames the work as `sensory experiences & tactile learning` and exposes individual classes, couples exploration, workshops, writings and London FLINTA jam context. Karada House's Queerope Berlin 2026 page records Sophia among the facilitators, and Renée de Sans' official bio characterizes co-teaching with Sophia Rose at Queerope / Karada House from 2022 onward.

### Soptik

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/soptik/

Fact — Soptik is a Czech nawashi.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · ORGANISER

Transmission — Prague-centered bondage teacher, performer and event organiser; co-founder of the Czech rope scene. Created his first educational rope website in 2000. After ten years of self-education met Hajime Kinoko, who became his first teacher of Japanese bondage; later studied with Osada Steve, Akechi Kanna, Nicolas Yoroi (who taught him movement, intimacy and simplicity) and Naka Akira. Met Riccardo Wildties who introduced him to Naka-ryū, and has studied Kinbaku LuXuria with Riccardo Wildties and Red Sabbath since 2013. Together with his partner Sansei he teaches traditional kinbaku / semenawa material; their official site says both are certified in Kinbaku LuXuria up to level 1. Regular presenter and organiser at the Prague Shibari Festival.

### Ohshita Souichiro (大下壮一郎)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/souichiro-ohshita/

Fact — Ohshita Souichiro (大下壮一郎) is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi.

Fact — Era: 1950s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · WRITER

Fact — Enigmatic early-postwar Japanese kinbakushi, writer and photography-oriented bondage stylist. Nawapedia says Ohshita started shortly after World War II, worked for Fuzoku Soshi after newspaper reporting in Tokyo, later appeared in Fuzoku Kitan and SM Kitan, and became known for close-up rope photographs built around neck ropes, desks and acrobatic poses.

### Sparks

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/sparks/

Fact — Sparks is an Australian nawashi in the Kanna-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — Australian nawashi centered in Adelaide. Began tying in early 2015 together with his now-wife after attending a rope performance at a BDSM party — they decided to pursue rope as a couple, eventually married and had a child. Took on an educational role in 2016, then teaching and performing interstate. Studied Akechi style first, then specialized in Kanna-ryū: travelled to Sydney annually for four years to take private lessons with Akechi Kanna. In February 2020 made an intensive 'pilgrimage' to Japan (six hours of lessons for six days in a row) and was accepted as deshi of Nawashi Akechi Kanna, receiving his teaching certificate — the second official Kanna-ryū instructor. Established Shibari Adelaide where he teaches and grows the local community.

Fact — School: Kanna-ryū

### Specimen

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/specimen/

Transmission — Specimen is documented as an American teacher.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Boston-centered rope practitioner, teacher and performer working with Knot Really Dead. Yugen Studio's public 2025 Performance Night listing says Specimen began a kinbaku path almost ten years earlier, centers connection and intimacy in rope, and has explored semenawa with Knot Really Dead for four years. The pair regularly teaches in Boston, with international teaching and performance context at AthenShibari, Shizen Studio Barcelona and Yugen Studio.

### Stella Spei

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/stella-spei/

Fact — Stella Spei is a French rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — RIGGER · PERFORMER

Transmission — French shibari / kinbaku rigger, performer, model, teacher and organizer behind Stell'Art Ropes. Her official bio frames her work around semenawa, emotional intensity, self-bondage, rigger performance, rope fashion / structures and nude-art photography. She has taught initiations and beginner / intermediate shibari since 2018, became president of ASKA in 2019 and organizes Rope Camp, described in her bio as an annual rope gathering for practitioners from France, Belgium, Italy and Spain.

### Stephanie Doe

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/stephanie-doe/

Transmission — Stephanie Doe is a French teacher.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · AUTHOR

Transmission — French shibari teacher, author, therapist and trainer. Her own bio says she has taught shibari since 2016 and developed a pedagogy beyond technique, centered on consciousness and the body / psyche relationship. Nuit Demonia characterizes her as teaching kinbaku since 2015 in a traditional style and as author of three shibari books. Co-author with Alex DirtyVonP of L'art du shibari Tome 1 and L'Art du Bondage.

### Steve Freeman

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/steve-freeman/

Fact — Steve Freeman is a Hong Kong-centered nawashi in the Yagami-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · PHOTOGRAPHER

Fact — Hong Kong-centered nawashi, shibari / kinbaku tutor and photographer, and the only official Yagami-ryu licensed instructor in Asia outside Japan. Began teaching shibari in 2015. Haru Kakeru / Nuit de Tokyo was his first sensei and direct apprenticeship; with Osada Steve, his training since 2016 has been a long-term apprenticeship via documentation work, class shadowing, direct mentorship and explicit teaching endorsement, apart from COVID-19 travel restrictions. His official site frames his current teaching as spanning Yagami-style and Yukimura-style material, and characterizes him as a full-time photographer who published the photo album "We Love Kabukicho" with Kano Hana and Yagami Ren. Known for an expressive, painterly style exploring intimacy and the human form. Performed alongside Hajime Kinoko at "The Shibari Connection: Love, Lust, and Beauty" showcase (Mondrian Hong Kong, 2024).

Fact — School: Yagami-ryū

### Stuart

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/stuart-a-veces-cuerdas/

Fact — Stuart is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — American, Barcelona-centered rigger / teacher / performer using the public identity Stuart / @a.veces.cuerdas.3 and Both Hands Kinbaku. Instagram profile characterizes him as an American living in Barcelona and fascinated by the seductive power of aibunawa. Stuart's official about page says he began rope in 2014, dove deep into Naka-style semenawa in 2018 with leading European practitioners and Naka-sensei, later moved toward a stronger aibunawa focus after learning from several of Yukimura-sensei's direct students, and turned that focus toward public teaching after K.A.R.E. In southern Spain in spring 2023. His current teaching pages frame both hands kinbaku as exploring sensuality and eroticism in the caressing-rope style of Yukimura Haruki, say his workshop approach is built via more than 100 hours of study with recognized Yukimura-ryu instructors, and present workshops plus private lessons. A 2024 shibari.events listing names him as presenter / organizer for Aibunawa Fundamentals at Espai Kink in Barcelona, while a 2025 Knot Here Amsterdam page characterizes his use of ryote / both hands for an approach combining aibunawa and Naka-style semenawa with floor-centered sensual / erotic technique. Hipsy 2026 bio text adds non-verbal-conversation language around rope, presence, intention and deep emotion. Amanita's February 2026 post records a Barcelona 2025 magazine-shoot / rope-journey collaboration with Stuart for @guinju.gaijin Magazine. No direct lineage, rank, certification, deshi or formal-school claim is asserted.

### Sudo

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/sudo/

Fact — Sudo is a rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PHOTOGRAPHER · ROPE SUPPLIER

Fact — Cape Town kinbaku practitioner, educator, photographer and rope-supply project operator publicly documented as @sudo_kinbaku_. Embodiment Rope Space's about page characterizes Sudo as a strategist, educator and researcher who helps with teaching, curriculum R&D, sessions and physical-space development, and its Quicket listings preserve Sudo / Fire Lily teaching and performance context at the Salt River space. Those same public references say Sudo runs the private Cape Kinbaku Salon with Fire Lily, has studied spanning Copenhagen, Berlin, Kanna and Naka contexts, and practices mainly semenawa. SudoJute's official site links the same public contact route to private shibari lessons and a Cape Town / South Africa rope-supply project.

### Sunga Konji

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/sunga-konji/

Fact — Sunga Konji is a rope artist.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — ROPE ARTIST · INTIMACY PRACTITIONER · PERFORMER · TEACHER

Fact — Cape Town rope and intimacy practitioner / artist active professionally since around 2012. Sunga's official site frames the work around rope, intimacy and pleasure, while the about page says the practice has shifted away from a rope-bondage identity toward using rope as a tool for desire, pleasure, sensuality, Black joy and Afro-descendant empowerment. Official CV traces list Casa Kink performances / workshops in 2024 and 2025 plus Embodiment Rope Space workshops / performances in 2023 and 2024. Public Quicket and Daruma Berlin listings independently corroborate recent performance / workshop biography context. Included without lineage, school, deshi or generic rigger framing.

Transmission — Swiss / Lausanne rope practitioner, model and teacher using the public name sweet_shiver. Encordages Lémaniques' official team page says Cordafortis / Markus introduced her to rope in January 2017, that the pair now train together weekly, and that she accompanied him to workshops with Docvale, Seilartig, Ligatio, Etheros, Pedro and Kirigami while both also intensively study each summer at RopeRetreat. A current atlas context names sweet_shiver as a Swiss rigger, bunny and teacher linked to Encordages Lémaniques, the Suisse Romande rope community / dojo she runs with Cordafortis.

### SweetZephyr

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/sweetzephyr/

Fact — SweetZephyr is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — US kinbaku teacher / organizer and founder of Heartland Kinbaku in Chicago. Heartland's official team page names SweetZephyr as organizational director, founder and lead instructor; it also notes earlier Oakland Rope Collective founding context and NARIX steering-committee service from 2018 to 2022. Their public bio says they came to rope after martial arts, circus and climbing, have tied for about eight years, and since 2018 have traveled repeatedly to Europe and Japan for study. Their practice and teaching are described as most influenced by Nawashi Kanna, Riccardo Wildties and Naka Akira, without claiming authorization in any one style.

### Sydona

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/sydona/

Fact — Sydona is documented as an American rope artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — ROPE ARTIST · TEACHER

Fact — Brooklyn / New York City-centered rope artist, movement practitioner, photographer, performer and educator. Her official bio characterizes professional dance training, Brooklyn / NYC nightlife and immersive-performance work, resident rope educator / facilitator roles at Temple and Hit Me Up, management of the private Studio Rogue rope / photography space, and 2020s travel for rope performance, education and study. Public references place her in Hajime Kinoko's ROPES orbit and in repeated Kinoko Shibari Web assistance, but her current profile is multi-teacher and independent rather than a formal Kinoko-lineage claim. EURIX XXIX records Sydona & faeriebruises as 2026 special guests; her own schedule also records work with Faerie Bruises at Red Pavilion and FLARE 2026.

### Syuusaku Kamiya (香宮 周策)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/syuusaku-kamiya/

Fact — Syuusaku Kamiya (香宮 周策) is documented as a Japanese sm performer.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — SM PERFORMER · SALON OPERATOR

Fact — Osaka-centered SM performer and kink-salon operator using the public identity 香宮 周策 / Syuusaku Kamiya. The official Salon Garuda site says the salon is hosted by 周策, an S / dominant man with more than twenty years in the scene, and frames Garuda as an exchange salon for people with minority sexual interests. Syuusaku's Lit.link and public Bluesky profile route the same identity to Salon Garuda, while the Dalia host-side profile independently characterizes about twenty years of SM experience, many SM show appearances in Osaka, Tokyo and Belgium, and rope / spanking by request.

His public event traces include ARCADIA Osaka, Ranki Kazami's DX Kabukicho SM Convention context and the 2024 National Kinbaku Expo organizer group.

Transmission — Tokyo-centered kinbakushi, teacher, organizer and writer operating in Shinjuku-Kabukicho via the Shibaraku project. Official pages frame him as the organizer of the Shibaraku rope project, the Shibaraku Salon exchange space and the communication-focused Hug-Bondage lesson program `Shibaraku-shiki`. ### Takashi Tsujimura (辻村隆)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/takashi-tsujimura/

Transmission — Takashi Tsujimura (辻村隆), 1921–1987, is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Nureki lineage.

Chronology — Born: 1921

Chronology — Died: 1987

Fact — Era: 1970s

Fact — NAWASHI · WRITER

Fact — Credited with establishing modern Kinbaku Art. Active as a kinbakushi for virtually the entire run of Kitan Club magazine; contributed historical dramas in its pulp era plus countless articles, stories and reports. Took over as Kitan Club's main kinbakushi after Toshiyuki Suma left in 1953. Created series like 'The True Face of Bondage Models' (1955) and 'SM Camera Hunt' (1964–73), establishing 'SM kinbaku as a practice'. In 1968 he crossed into commercial film work as the bondage adviser for Teruo Ishii's History of Tokugawa Female Punishment, then wrote about the experience in Kitan Club. Together with Oniroku Dan, a driving force in the late-1960s/early-1970s SM boom. The term 'Nawashi' is believed to have been first used by him in Kitan Club in the 1950s; he and author Minomura Kou coined the term 'Kinbaku' itself in the May–June 1952 issue of Kitan Club.

Transmission — School: Nureki lineage

### Take Kenkou (武健虹)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/take-kenkou/

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — KINBAKU TEACHER · SALON OPERATOR

Transmission — Kansai kinbaku teacher, performer and salon operator using the public identity 武健虹 / Take Kenkou. The official Nawadokoro Maitake site says the Takatsuki salon / classroom is run by Take Kenkou with partner Mai, characterizes Take as a rope artist with more than ten years of rope experience, and records him as the person providing instruction and rope-receiving experiences. Maitake's people page names Take Kenkou as the organizer of Nawadokoro Maitake. Independent event pages from Grand Saloon JUSO / Jail Osaka and Bar Paraphilia credit 武健虹 with Mai / Nawadokoro Maitake in public show / birthday-event performer contexts, and the current personal Bluesky profile says Take teaches and performs rope in Kansai and runs Nawadokoro Maitake.

### Tako

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/tako/

Fact — Tako is a Swiss rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — RIGGER · PHOTOGRAPHER

Fact — Geneva-centered Swiss shibari artist, photographer, rigger and performer also credited as Tak-ko / Geoffroy Baud. His own site frames shibari and photography as intertwined tools for exploring Eros, intimacy and the expression of bodies, and records performances such as Anasobi Antwerp 2019, EURIX Berlin 2021 and Mos Espa Genève 2022. Tak-ko news pages document shibari courses / initiations in the Geneva / Haute-Savoie orbit and announce EURIX Spring 2023 with The Sand; the EURIX archive also records Tako in 2021 and 2025.

### Miura Takumi (神浦匠)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/takumi-miura/

Transmission — Miura Takumi (神浦匠), 1964–2021, is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Akechi lineage.

Chronology — Born: 1964

Chronology — Died: 2021

Fact — Era: 1990s

Fact — NAWASHI · PHOTOGRAPHER

Fact — Nawashi, editor and photographer (1964-2021). Known for documenting kinbaku and contributing to its visual aesthetic tradition. Entered the SM world in the 1980s, studied Akechi-style foundations via the Akechi circle, and co-established Bakuyukai with Denki Akechi in 1996, where Kinbaku Today characterizes him as head instructor and later founder / continuing teacher at the dojo.

Transmission — School: Akechi lineage

### Tamandua

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/tamandua/

Fact — Tamandua is a Swedish nawashi.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — Queer Swedish rope artist and kinbaku / shibari instructor centered in Berlin, working with rope professionally since 2016. Teaches private classes in Berlin, group workshops abroad and online mentoring, performs, programming includes intimate kinbaku sessions and appears as an instructor on Shibari Study. His official schedule records a 2019-2026 international teaching and performance trace via venues and events such as Spektrum Reykjavik, EURIX, Tangled Rope Festival, Anatomie Studio, Prague Shibari Festival, Espacio Nos and Iridescent Studio Amsterdam. His practice foregrounds aesthetic expression, emotional / sensual intention, sadism, technical clarity and student individuality.

### Tanako Kawabata (川端多奈子)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/tanako-kawabata/

Fact — Tanako Kawabata (川端多奈子) is documented as a Japanese model.

Fact — Era: 1950s

Fact — MODEL · WRITER

Fact — Early postwar Japanese Kitan Club model and contributor. Nawapedia names Tanako Kawabata / 川端多奈子 as a Matsuyama-born model appearing in Kitan Club from 1952, with selected 1953 Utsukushiki Imashime credits. Nawapedia's Utsukushiki Imashime page records her as the sole model for No.1 and among the No.2 models; Kinbaku Today frames her as an early model and voice of postwar kinbaku culture via Kitan Club correspondence, essays and model work. Her Kitan Club and Utsukushiki Imashime role is structurally useful; no kinbakushi, teacher or lineage claim is asserted.

### Taste of Dinner

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/taste-of-dinner/

Fact — Taste of Dinner is a Portuguese model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — MODEL · CO-TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Portuguese rope bottom / model living in the Netherlands, Mie Neko co-teacher / shibari model partner, and Knot Here Amsterdam organizer. The public Mie Neko rope-partners page records Tasteofdinner among Mie Neko's co-teacher shibari models, says Taste of Dinner started rope in 2017, attended workshops and events spanning Europe, and now organizes rope jams and workshops at Knot Here Amsterdam. Knot Here's first Rope Jam page names Tasteofdinner and Sarah Calanthe as announcing the first monthly jam and controlling suspension-point permission.

### Tati Limati

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/tati-limati/

Fact — Tati Limati is a Ukrainian artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — ARTIST · TEACHER

Fact — Ukrainian shibari artist, instructor, performer and organizer also credited as Tatiana Tereshenko. Event bios describe study in Europe and Japan with Naka Akira, Ren Yagami, Hajime Kinoko and Nicolas Yoroi / Arnoys, plus Yukimura style study, and a background in aikido, tango, contact improvisation, tantra and psychology. Her own projects include Soul Shibari and the “4 Elements” approach to sensuality and improvisation; Soul Shibari names her as main organizer of Shi Fest Ukraine 2019, and Shibari Camp Poland records her as teaching worldwide, curating Shibari Theater and running Shibari Camps in Poland and Ukraine. EURIX 2022 / 2024 beginner-course teacher and 2026 presenter.

### Tatiana Moressoni

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/tatiana-moressoni/

Fact — Tatiana Moressoni is an Italian model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — MODEL · PERFORMER · ORGANIZER

Fact — London-centered multidisciplinary artist, professional art model, shibari model / switch and Skirt Club event manager using the public identity Tatiana Moressoni / @thelilacaroundtheworld. Fox&Badge's Noisily 2024 facilitator bio characterizes her as a Shibari switch / self-suspender, trained dancer, certified yoga teacher and regular Fox&Badge / London kink-art scene performer and model. Skirt Club public event pages preserve her rope-programming role from Paris shibari sessions in 2023 via Anna Kii / Figure of A performance contexts in 2025 and resident rope-artist / workshop-facilitator listings in London 2026. The Anatomie Studio Friday Rope Night listing independently names Anna Kii and Tatiana as a performing duo.

### Tatiana Stillness

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/tatiana-stillness/

Fact — Tatiana Stillness is a Russian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — RIGGER · PERFORMER

Fact — Saint Petersburg rigger, performer and session practitioner. RopeFest Baltia's 2018 archive says Tatiana became interested in shibari in 2010, began as a model, joined many masterclasses by foreign and local bondage masters, and started practicing as a rigger in 2013. RopeFest Peterburg 2026 says she conducts individual shibari sessions and has appeared on Russian and European stages such as RopeFest and BoundCon's small stage.

Fact — MODEL · PERFORMER

Fact — French rope model, tying partner and performer using the public identity TatiAna / Tatiana Tallhyar. Onawa Asobi Europe profile text characterizes more than ten years as a tying partner and photo model, with stage work in Japan, Belgium, France and Ukraine alongside Shigonawa Bingo, Kasumi Hourai, Kazami Ranki and others. Onawa's current and archive pages place her in multiple European-edition pairings, such as Shigonawa Bingo in 2020, Hazel Kinbaku in 2025 and Yoi Yoshida in the 2026 Antwerp lineup. No public lineage, teaching, rank or certification claim is inferred from the checked sources.

### Tenga Jin (天我迅)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/tenga-jin/

Fact — Tenga Jin (天我迅) is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · PERFORMER

Fact — Tokyo-centered kinbaku show performer using the public identity 天我迅 / Tenga Jin. The first-party site and link hub present `Tenga Style` as inspired by Yagami Style / 鵺神流, list X, Instagram, Bluesky and lit.link contact routes, and preserve a substantial 2023-2026 appearance archive. Nawapedia corroborates the Tenga Jin / 天我迅 identity, the October 2023 performance start and the January 2025 name change from BEST to Tenga Jin. Tokyo Love District's Kokonawa 4 page records `Tenga Jin x Misa` in the June 29, 2025 Tokyo shibari / kinbaku show roster.

### Tenma Haru (天馬ハル)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/tenma-haru/

Fact — Tenma Haru (天馬ハル), born 1975, is documented as a Japanese nawashi.

Chronology — Born: 1975

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — Japanese master nawashi centered in Tokyo / Yokohama with his own school: the 'Tenma style' of kinbaku. Characterised by sensual, intense exchange that supersedes verbal communication — his signature gote feels not painful but like being hugged, despite making breathing difficult. Apprentices begin with two classes per week for three months, then private classes once a week, with full apprenticeship taking about a year. The official Gensounawa site names Tenma as the presiding kinbakushi of Yokohama Gensounawa, a kinbaku salon / class space for Tenma-style practice, salons, private lessons and events. Calls his cohort the 'blank generation' — was called a 'newbie' for ten years because the next generation didn't appear. Believes Japanese kinbaku should also be taught to Japanese, not only exported overseas. His apprentice Sora is a young kinbaku prodigy.

Fact — London-centered model and artist of British, Asian and Jamaican heritage whose public work combines fetishism, shibari, BDSM, leather, chains and surrealist / psychoanalytic imagery. ### Tesshin Doyama (堂山鉄心)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/tesshin-doyama/

Fact — Tesshin Doyama (堂山鉄心), born 1966, is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi.

Chronology — Born: 1966

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · PERFORMER · OWNER

Transmission — Japanese kinbakushi, performer and ARCADIA Tokyo owner / organizer. Nawapedia gives his birth date as April 6, 1966 in Osaka, characterizes him as self-taught, influenced by Nagare Aotsuki and Ero Ouji, and places him in the ARCADIA Osaka orbit before becoming general manager of Nagare's ARCADIA Tokyo in 2009 and owner in 2014. The official Doyama Tessin profile says his first kinbaku show was in 2005, that he has continued many shows and AV kinbakushi credits, and names him as owner of Kabukicho SM bar ARCADIA TOKYO and organizer of the rope group Bakkushinkai. ARCADIA Tokyo's official site gives the Kabukicho address, records the monthly Bakkushinkai event, and directs studio-rental inquiries to Doyama. Kinbaku Today's 2018 Arcadia Tokyo review also characterizes Doyama as the Tokyo location's general manager, moving from Osaka when Arcadia expanded to Tokyo in 2009.

### Tsukamoto Tetsuzo (塚本鉄三)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/tetsuzo-tsukamoto/

Fact — Tsukamoto Tetsuzo (塚本鉄三) is documented as a Japanese kinbakushi.

Fact — Era: 1950s

Fact — KINBAKUSHI · PHOTOGRAPHER

Fact — Japanese kinbakushi, photographer and writer active spanning the long Kitan Club era. Nawapedia says Tsukamoto began bondage photography around 1950, photographed both 1953 Utsukushiki Imashime / Beautifully Bound volumes, and remained a prolific Kitan Club contributor via the magazine's final 1975 issue, such as serials such as The Reality of Bondage Photography and Camera Report.

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Fact — Singapore-centered rope artist, instructor, photographer and performer. TheRopeholic's official Linktree characterizes him as a Singapore-centered rope artist and official Kinoko-style shibari instructor. KinkyMinky says he travelled to Japan to study under Hajime Kinoko, was mentored by Kinoko, earned licensed-instructor accreditation in Kinoko's shibari style, and conducts classes, workshops and performances locally and internationally. Hajime Kinoko's official lessons page records TheRopeholic among Second-Class Instructor License holders in the Ichinawakai instructor-license system. The 2026 Amazing Tied-Land Ticketmelon page and poster place TheRopeholic / Ropeholics in the Singapore performance slot in Bangkok.

### The Silence

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/the-silence/

Fact — The Silence is documented as an American rope photographer.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — ROPE PHOTOGRAPHER · RIGGER

Fact — Oakland / San Francisco Bay Area rope, kink and boudoir photographer using the public identity The Silence. Shibari Study frames The Silence as a professional photographer centered on rope, kink and boudoir, with a transcript describing a rope-photography practice that began around the early 2010s and combines rope bondage, high-fashion and portrait-lighting references. VoxBody's Kinky Photo Jam listing says the Bay Area kink and rope scene has had The Silence documenting events for years and frames a VoxBody / The Silence photo-jam series around basic and kinky photography guidance. A separate VoxBody Vignette listing bills The Silence tying with BeautifulyAbused for a rope and life-drawing event, characterizes his style as based on traditional kinbaku art, fine art and high fashion, and says he has taught photography at Bondage Expo Dallas, F.I.R.E. And VoxBody. Dark Odyssey's public photo-gallery index gives recurring The Silence event-gallery credits from 2021 via 2025.

### Theokino

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/theokino-ropevision/

Fact — Theokino is a Finnish rope artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — ROPE ARTIST · TEACHER

Transmission — Oulu-centered Finnish rope artist, teacher, session rigger and photographer in the Ropevision workgroup. Ropevision's official `Meistä` page characterizes Theokino as an Oulu rope artist and influencer whose technical approach made rope a major part of his life and leisure, and says he programming includes rope teaching, session tying and photography. Ropevision's workgroup page says Theokino and Solina established Ropevision to increase shibari visibility and provide rope-related services and education, and the current events page plus Circus & Dance Finland list Theokino and Solina together as workshop teachers.

### TheRawKitty

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/therawkitty/

Fact — TheRawKitty is documented as an American rope educator.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ROPE EDUCATOR · PERFORMER

Fact — Seattle-centered rope educator, performer and facilitator using the public identity TheRawKitty / J therawkitty. Their Linktree and Bluesky profile frame the identity as a Seattle rope creature, performer and educator, with inquiry, resource and local-event routes. A public inquiry form characterizes J as a switchy transmasculine rope educator, performer and facilitator who entered the Seattle rope scene around 2014, programming includes private and group lessons / rope sessions, and uses Seattle studio, event and public-park settings. Seattle Erotic Art Festival's 2026 performer page independently records L & J therawkitty as a long-established tying partnership, says they teach intermediate rope classes locally and internationally and curate rope spaces, and the SEAF 2026 schedule records a bounded ambient performance slot for L&J therawkitty.

### Thure Nyyrikki

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/thure-nyyrikki/

Fact — Thure Nyyrikki is a Czech rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Fact — Prague-centered Czech kinbaku educator, rigger and performer active on the Czech scene since the mid-2010s. Prague Shibari Festival's 2016 archive frames him as a fast-rising Czech rigger strongly inspired by Naka-ryu and explicitly names Riccardo Wildties and Soptik as teachers. A 2025 Bad Reputation workshop bio characterizes his work as combining Naka Ryu and Nureki Chimuo elements, says he has practiced shibari for about nine years, names Naka Akira, Kasumi Hourai, Norio Sugiura and Riccardo Sergnese among his direct teachers, and places his Prague teaching activity at Alive Kinbaku Dojo. Public profile text frames his work around private sessions and private lessons.

### Tifereth

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/tifereth/

Fact — Tifereth is a Canadian nawashi.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Toronto-centered rope educator and performer. Began rope in 2011 as a model and self-suspension practitioner, first tying in an American convention-influenced style. In 2013 she began early Kazami-ryu study via Osaka Dan and later Kazami Ranki, then a 2015 EURIX trip shifted her away from Kazami maximalism toward inspiration from Pedro Cordas, Vlada Vedmovskaya, and the growing European Naka-ryu scene. Her mature work emphasizes minimalist harness construction, body mechanics, movement arts, sadism, performance, and model-first teaching. Co-owner of Birdhaus in Toronto, where she teaches and performs locally; international credits include Prague Shibari Festival, Bondage Expo Dallas, EURIX, Helsinki Shibari, Shibaricon, and workshops worldwide. Mentor to RopuNawa from ~2019.

### TOHJIRO

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/tohjiro/

Fact — TOHJIRO, born 1956, is documented as a Japanese director.

Chronology — Born: 1956

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — DIRECTOR · PRODUCER

Fact — Japanese AV director and producer born in Tokyo in 1956. Nawapedia records that he established Dogma under SOD in 2001, made it independent in 2002, and established the Dogma CORE label with Naka Akira in 2006. Important here as a production-context connection rather than a bakushi lineage entry: TOHJIRO was a Kinbiken member, produced works by Nureki Chimuo, and collaborated with kinbakushi such as Naka Akira and Ren Yagami. Studio Yukari's Naka bio says Naka met TOHJIRO while working at Cinemagic, and that TOHJIRO later created Dogma and immediately employed Naka.

Transmission — Austin-centered US rope top, rigger, photographer and teacher using the public identity Tomporarily.
