### Topologist

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/topologist/

Fact — Topologist is documented as an American rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · PLATFORM CREATOR

Fact — US / North American rope-bondage educator and creator of Crash Restraint, also publicly identified as Chris. Crash Restraint's bio says he has studied rope bondage since 2007 and taught throughout North America since 2009, with a non-dogmatic approach combining Eastern and Western bondage, safety, reliable ties and adaptability to different bodies and play styles. His public technical impact includes the Somerville Bowline family, chest-loading takate-kote work, suspension-lift mechanics and safety / negotiation education. Current and archival sources frame him as an online-course creator and conference / workshop educator rather than a formal Japanese lineage figure.

### Toronawa (トロ)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/toronawa/

Fact — Toronawa (トロ) is documented as a Japanese artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ARTIST · RIGGER · INSTRUCTOR · ORGANIZER

Fact — Tokyo-centered underground artist, rigger, instructor, photographer and organizer using the public identity Toronawa / トロ. His official site frames rope, photography and painting as the core of a Tokyo underground / neon subculture practice, says he began Japanese rope / kinbaku in 2019, studied for three years at MilkyWay Tokyo under Waka-sensei and in 2023 became MilkyWay's first non-Japanese rope instructor. His weekly Toro Rope Salon at MilkyWay combines English-language rope class, show and salon / free-practice time, while his performance page also records current Cafe & Bar ecole performance and Tokyo La Nuit exhibition context.

### Tsukioka Yoshitoshi (月岡芳年)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/tsukioka-yoshitoshi/

Fact — Tsukioka Yoshitoshi (月岡芳年), 1839–1892, is documented as a Japanese ukiyo-e artist in the Foundational.

Chronology — Born: 1839

Chronology — Died: 1892

Fact — Era: 1800s

Fact — UKIYO-E ARTIST

Fact — Last great master of the ukiyo-e genre of woodblock printing. Born Owariya Yonejirō on 30 April 1839 in the Shimbashi district of Edo. Apprenticed to Kuniyoshi in 1850, receiving the name 'Yoshitoshi' in the Utagawa school lineage. His 'bloody period' (late 1860s) produced images of graphic violence and brutality — a key source for kinbaku visual culture, such as the muzan-e (cruel-images) genre. Suffered a nervous breakdown in 1872 and stopped working; resumed in 1873 under the new name 'Taiso' ('Great Resurrection'). His most celebrated late series — One Hundred Aspects of the Moon (1885–92) and New Forms of Thirty-Six Ghosts (1889–92) — coincide with an 1885 Tokyo Hayari Hosomiki ranking that placed him as Japan's number-one ukiyo-e artist. Died of cerebral haemorrhage on 9 June 1892, aged 53.

Fact — School: Foundational

Transmission — Russian bondage / shibari practitioner and teacher known as Uncle Shu / Dyadyushka Shu. ### UnkownRigger

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/unkownrigger/

Fact — UnkownRigger is documented as a German rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Berlin-centered rope teacher and performer also identified as Alex. Morphee Kinbaku's 2023 workshop bio says he had been practicing rope for almost 14 years, started active tying in Dusseldorf, learned Osada-ryu basics, moved to Berlin and explored Naka Akira, Ren Yagami, Yukimura and Boris Mosafir styles. Since 2019 he has taught his non-dogmatic tying approach at his own school in Berlin-Lichtenberg and internationally. BoundCon 2026 records UnkownRigger & Zazoo for a connection and body movement workshop.

### Unnamedminor

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/unnamedminor/

Fact — Unnamedminor is a Thai nawashi.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Bangkok-centered Thai shibari artist, performer, rigger and teacher using the public identity Unnamedminor / Minor. Khaosod's 2018 Bangkok scene report names Phetcharada "Minor" Pacharee as one of the few Thai suspension-capable shibari practitioners and says she helped bring aerial suspension into public performance in Bangkok. Wonderfruit's 2020 interview names her as Phetcharada Pacharee / Unnamedminor, characterizes a live Wonderfruit 2019 appearance, and says she started studying and tying herself around 2016 before studying with foreign teachers in Asian and European styles. GroundControl, Time Out / Zipevent and Kinky Bangkok document later art, exhibition, teaching and workshop contexts, while public social surfaces frame the work around shibari art, photoshoots, private sessions and shibari + music performance in Bangkok. A 2026 public Instagram profile screenshot links the Mino Shibari / @mino.shibari profile back to the same @Unnamedminorxxx X route and records @mino_r.shibari as backup, so the Mino handles are listed as current Unnamedminor contact / alias context rather than a separate person.

### Usagi Shibari (うさぎ縛り)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/usagi-shibari/

Fact — Usagi Shibari (うさぎ縛り) is a Thai rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · ROPE ARTIST · STUDIO OPERATOR

Fact — Bangkok / Thailand current-scene rigger and rope artist using the public identity Usagi Shibari / Bunny Usagi. Public Instagram and Threads routes identify the `@bunny.usagiii` profile as `[ Usagi.Shibari うさぎ縛り ]`, while the `@usagi.shibari` route and public posts preserve the dedicated rope-art identity. Public indexed captions in 2025-2026 repeatedly credit rope to `@bunny.usagiii`, such as the FOR YOUR PLEASURE / BACC rope-work trace with Kathalyn / FilmNetflick context and other FilmNetflick-adjacent rope-photo snippets. A FilmNetflick-adjacent rope-art caption also preserves `inspired by Yagmi REN` wording, documented only as a weak style-inspiration trace from Ren Yagami rather than study, lineage or certification. The public profile view links Bunny Playroom Studio as her studio; the Bunnyroom_Studio X profile independently characterizes Bunny Play Room as a private dungeons and bar in Bangkok.

### VÆGABØUND

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/vaegabound/

Fact — VÆGABØUND is documented as a German rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · KINK EDUCATOR · BODYWORKER

Fact — German shibari artist, rigger, kink educator, bodyworker, systemic coach and photographer using the public identity Sascha Achner / VÆGABØUND / @vaegabound. The official VÆGABØUND profile characterizes Sascha as an artist and coach whose rope work links shibari with bodywork, dance, yoga, meditation, photography, personality-development and systemic coaching; the service page records private sessions, coaching, workshops / retreats / rope jams, collaborations, shows / performances and a shibari shop around shibari and kink. Public Instagram profile evidence names the account as verified, Education category, Kink-Educator / Bodyworker / Shibari-Artist, with Halle, Dresden and Berlin location text plus public service, linkhub, media-kit and Facebook links. Atelier ÆØS names Sascha / VÆGABØUND with Lola Sapieha as the team behind a Dresden kink-education / shibari / bodywork atelier. Shibari Studio Berlin's 2026 interview frames Sascha aka VÆGABØUND as a kink educator, shibari artist, photographer and entrepreneur and records Naka Ryu / Yukimura Ryu as style / study background record. A May 2026 Amanita post records Connection with Sasha in Dresden with @vaegabound and new @atelier_aeos context. No lineage, rank, certification, deshi or formal-school claim is asserted.

Fact — Valentine Amartey is a British rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — RIGGER · DESIGNER · PERFORMER

Fact — British-born, Brooklyn-centered designer, performer, rope artist and educator. Interview Magazine profiled Valentine Amartey in 2013 as a New York-centered designer whose couture rope / knitwear work overlaps with performance and who taught rope-bondage classes at SHAG in Williamsburg. ShibariNews preserves the Brooklyn educator / performer framing, while Moxy Williamsburg's February 14, 2024 calendar independently bills a `Shibari Bondage Performance by Valentine Amartey` for SHAG's `A Night of Eros`.

### Van Darkholme

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/van-darkholme/

Fact — Van Darkholme is documented as an American rope-bondage artist.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — ROPE-BONDAGE ARTIST · PHOTOGRAPHER

Fact — Vietnam-born, US-centered rope-bondage artist, photographer, author and adult-media director using the public identity Van Darkholme / VansamaOfficial. San Francisco Bay Guardian's 2008 Armory feature names him as a Shibari rope-bondage expert, porn performer and proprietor of Muscle Bound Productions, and records his move from Los Angeles to San Francisco to helm Kink's Bound Gods launch. The Stranger independently characterizes him as a shibari expert and author / photographer of the 2006 photo book Male Bondage: Art Deserves a Witness, while his official site preserves the 2008-2017 Kink.com director period and later KinkMen return. His rope-specific relevance is the shibari / bondage-craft role, photography and selected book rather than a full adult-media catalogue.

### Vasilisa Lesnaya

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/vasilisa-lesnaya/

Fact — Vasilisa Lesnaya is a Russian nawashi.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Rostov-on-Don-centered Russian bondage artist, teacher and RopeFest performer. Nawapedia.ru names Vasilisa Lesnaya as a bondage artist from Rostov-on-Don. RopeFest Moscow 2021 records Vasilisa Lesnaya from Rostov-on-Don among the main-stage performers, while RopeFest St. Petersburg 2023 records both Vasilisa Lesnaya and a Salamandr & Vasilisa Lesnaya participant slot. Shibari.Guru's performance-video archive preserves multiple Vasilisa Lesnaya RopeFest traces, such as 2019 and 2022 items. Public Instagram and Telegram surfaces tie `@vasiliisalesnaya`, `@vasrope` and `@Basilica0206` to Vasilisa Lesnaya, frame her as a shibari / kinbaku master and body-oriented practitioner, and describe teaching, private sessions and practice since 2011.

### Velvetine

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/velvetine/

Fact — Velvetine is documented as a German model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — MODEL · WRITER

Fact — Berlin-centered shibari model, rope bottom, kink / erotica writer and movement practitioner. Podcast bios describe Velvetine as coming to shibari from BDSM, treating getting tied as an erotic and kinky practice rather than only an artistic one. In her 2024 Medium essay she writes that she became hyper-focused on shibari during the Berlin lockdowns in 2020, later presenting at EURIX, meeting Naka Akira in Prague as a model via A.Veces.Cuerdas, and visiting Riccardo Wildties for a photoshoot session; those traces are biographical background rather than lineage claims. Shibari Study published her 2025 rope-bottom education article on nerve injuries and emotional recovery. EURIX records Kata LaLaLaa & Velvetine as 2023 presenters and Saara Rei & Velvetine as 2024 beginner-course teachers.

### Vik

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/vik/

Fact — Vik is a Spanish rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Barcelona-centered shibari/kinbaku rigger, teacher and performer, working with partner Gala as Vik & Gala and publicly reachable as @victor.shizen. Began exploring both roles before preferring the rigger side. Together they developed a non-dogmatic, emotionally intense semenawa-oriented approach, perform internationally, were invited to Milan to participate in the Kinbaku Lab research group, created Kinbaku Lab Barcelona with other riggers and bottoms, and run Shizen Studio Barcelona. A May 2026 Amanita post records a March 2026 Madrid performance / teaching weekend with Amanita during Progression In Semenawa II at Madrid Ropes. EURIX 2026 presenter.

### Vlada Vedmovskaya

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/vlada-vedmovskaya/

Fact — Vlada Vedmovskaya is a Russian nawashi.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Fact — Kinbakushi and organizer associated with the Russian and later Belgrade rope scenes. Presented at EURIX VII in autumn 2015 with Alex Falco and later appeared at EURIX XXII in 2023 as Vlada Vedmovskaya. BalkanBound characterizes her as a kinbakushi who shaped the Russian bondage community, curated MoscowBound from 2013, organized MoscowKnot International and later established BalkanBound in Belgrade; RopeFest's RFMSK23 archive also names her as organizer of the Moscow Bound festival. Public Telegram mirrors connect her @AkiraWL / Chaos Essence activity to Belgrade shibari education and KnotCoven schedule / registration traces. Named by Subspace Studio as one of the 2015 EURIX influences that helped shift Tifereth's style.

### Steph / Void

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/voiddd/

Fact — Steph / Void is documented as an American rope bottom.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ROPE BOTTOM · PERFORMER

Fact — New York-centered rope bottom, performer and Rope Chubbies project contact using the public identity Steph / Void / S. / `@voiddd__`. SimpleTix's Coil & Cord Heavy Lifting class page names Liv / Soft Daddy and Steph / Void as New York-centered rope partners, characterizes Void as a bottom and performer, and frames their practice around fat bodies in rope bondage and adapting shibari motifs for larger bodies. Rope Chubbies' public profile / profilecard links the project to S / `@voiddd__`, while a December 2025 reintroduction post tags `@voiddd__` in a by-and-for fat people in rope community framing.

### Von Riga

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/von-riga/

Fact — Von Riga is an Australian nawashi.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Naarm / Melbourne-centered shibari / kinbaku artist, performer, teacher and curator. Von Riga began shibari / kinbaku under Scott Haruteru in 2014, later studying with a wider range of local and international teachers while developing an independent style around passion, provocation, poetry, presence and partner connection. Eventbrite characterizes Von Riga as a founding curator of Melbourne's Shibari Lounge performance night and as a live performer from underground clubs to festival stages. ASSFest and Giddy Garden sources identify Von Riga with Soul Rope Studios, established in 2022, with weekly classes in Naarm / Melbourne.

### Wakabayashi Miho (若林美保)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/wakabayashi-miho/

Fact — Wakabayashi Miho (若林美保) is documented as a Japanese performer.

Fact — Era: 1990s

Fact — PERFORMER · ACTRESS

Fact — Japanese actress, model and multi-performer active publicly from the late 1990s. Her official Hotoke no Za blog frames her directly as an actress, model and multi-performer. SMpedia preserves the bounded map-relevant chronology: a 1999 Toyo Show stage debut, 2005 Go Arisue partner context at `SM DX WORLD`, a 2010 Theatre Poo appearance as Otonawa's partner, and 2011 participation in the London Festival of the Art of Japanese Rope Bondage.

### Wataru Shindo (進藤渉)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/wataru-shindo/

Fact — Wataru Shindo (進藤渉) is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Ichinawa-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · PERFORMER

Fact — Japanese shibari / rope artist, performer and musician. Wataru Shindo's official portfolio says he began studying under Hajime Kinoko in 2021 and works as a bondage artist around shoots, shows and rope performances that combine VJing, while also creating rope accessories, venue decoration and costumes. Hajime Kinoko's official lessons page records Wataru Shindo among Second-Class Instructor License holders in the Ichinawakai instructor-license system. The 2026 Amazing Tied-Land Ticketmelon page records Wataru Shindo as a Japan performance-slot participant in Bangkok.

Fact — School: Ichinawa-ryū

### William

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/william-tiemetightly/

Fact — William is documented as an American artist.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — ARTIST · ORGANIZER

Fact — Brooklyn / New York City-centered queer artist, designer, builder, educator and organizer using the public identity William / TieMeTightly. His official Temple profile says he has studied shibari since meeting Midori in 2003, learned most notably with Chiaki in New York and Tokyo plus KissMeDeadlyDoll in New York, and focuses primarily on Naka style and semenawa. That record names William as the founder of Temple New York and says that since Temple's 2019 founding he has worked as a community organizer, educator and professional Dominant, with production context such as Dances of Vice, Torture Garden and Hajime Kinoko installation performances in New York. EURIX XXVIII records William & Anna Noctuelle as Spring 2026 special guests in Berlin.

### Winter

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/winter/

Fact — Winter is an Italian model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — MODEL · PERFORMER

Fact — Italian model, singer and shibari performer working with Sarca Martello. BoundCon's artist page says she studied opera singing from age 15, trained in fashion and costume in Florence, worked as a photo model for 10 years, met Sarca, and has since toured with him as a model / shibari performer. BoundCon frames their shared opera&ropes project and credits Winter in Sarca & Winter 2026 stage and workshop contexts.

### WisDomme

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/wisdomme/

Fact — WisDomme is a Greek rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — RIGGER · EDUCATOR · PERFORMER

Fact — Athens-centered rigger, educator and performer. BalkanBound says her rope journey began in 2012 via local communities and self-exploration, with a full dedication to shibari by 2017. In 2018 she co-established AthenShibari with three other rope enthusiasts, later teaching weekly classes and performing or teaching in Greece, Cyprus and internationally. The same source names Felix Ruckert, Sophia Rose, Boris Mosafir, Jonathan Ryan, Pauline Massimo, AkuAku, Gestalta, Green Gorilla, Tamandua, La QuartaCorda and Tako among her study / workshop influences and bills her with NyxRope for BalkanBound 2025.

### WolfRope

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/wolfrope/

Fact — WolfRope is documented as a German rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — German shibari / kinbaku teacher, artist and dojo lead using the public identity WolfRope. The official WolfRope site names Thorsten Mann in the imprint and frames WolfRope as a Karlsruhe-centered rope teacher offering beginner modules, regular trainings, individual rope sessions, workshops, performances and rope-preparation work via WolfRope Juku. The official About page says his style is strongly shaped by Yukimura-ryu and Osada-ryu, naming Michael Ropeknight and Osada Steve as the teachers who significantly influenced his rope, while a later paragraph says he has pursued deeper study in Osada-ryu and Naka-style contexts via regular trips to Japan.

### Wredna Wiedźma

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/wredna-wiedzma/

Fact — Wredna Wiedźma is a Polish performer.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — PERFORMER · MODEL · TEACHER

Transmission — Polish model, performer, rope bunny and shibari teacher using the public identity Wredna Wiedźma. Her official profile says she has documented since 2007, worked as a performer / rope bunny since 2017, performed at events spanning Poland and abroad, participated in many shibari workshops and shows, and teaches safety plus psychological and anatomical aspects for both sides. That record frames her as the female half of Krimson Kinbaku, a project for kinbaku shows, `Heavenly locations` photography and beginner shibari workshops. Municipal Tychy and Warsaw Tattoo Convention programme sources independently preserve Krimson Kinbaku stage traces; Nawado's 2019 Krakow workshop post names Wredna Wiedźma as the model in its workshop documentation.

### WykD Dave

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/wykd-dave/

Fact — WykD Dave, born 1968, is a British kinbakushi.

Chronology — Born: 1968

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — KINBAKUSHI · TEACHER

Transmission — British kinbakushi, teacher, performer and photographer alternate public name: Dave Rickman. His official site frames WykD Method as a personal style honoring the aesthetics of Nureki Chimuo and Naka Akira while incorporating the technical / structural integrity he associates with Akechi Denki and the photographic aesthetics of Sugiura Norio. The WykD about page characterizes him as UK-centered, teaching and performing internationally with partner Clover; the cited event-brochure bio says he was self-taught for 17 years until a lesson with Osada Steve changed his way of tying. Nawapedia records additional Yukimura Haruki and Naka Akira study, LFRA organization with Clover, film / music-video rope work, and the opening of Studio Kokoro in Mansfield, UK. Promonews credits Dave WyKd as rope specialist for FKA twigs' 2015 `Pendulum` music video.

### Xa Na

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/xa-na/

Fact — Xa Na is an Italian bodyworker.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — BODYWORKER · TEACHER

Transmission — Italian / Berlin-Rome bodyworker, BDSM practitioner, teacher and kinbaku performer. Melania Mieli's 2019 interview characterizes Xa Na as a Shiatsu expert since 2000, researching the fusion of bodywork and BDSM, and presenting workshops at Xplore Rome on Shiatsu, shibari, Hara/bodywork and creative action. The interview also notes a Moscow Knot bondage-festival performance. EURIX 2019 presenter and recurring 2020s beginner-course teacher.

### Yada Kinbaku

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/yada-kinbaku/

Fact — Yada Kinbaku is a Thai nawashi.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Transmission — Thai shibari artist, educator and facilitator centered in Bangkok, and founder of Kotori Studio / Kotori People Studio. Student of Yoi Yoshida; began exploring Japanese rope bondage in 2015, started teaching in 2018, and has hosted workshops under the KOTORI name since 2019. Current Kotori profile frames her practice at the intersection of rope, embodiment and social inquiry, adapting rope to participants rather than teaching one fixed style, with consent, communication, vulnerability, agency and political / storytelling expression central to the work. Helsinki Shibari characterizes her approach as non-verbal communication and bodily attention rather than merely tying someone up. The official Onawa Asobi Europe performer page also places her in the 2024 Antwerp lineup with Miss Bop and characterizes her as active with classes and performances spanning Thailand, Japan, Taiwan and Europe.

### Yakov KINKKNIGHT

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/yakov-kinkknight/

Fact — Yakov KINKKNIGHT is a Russian rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — RIGGER · ORGANIZER

Transmission — Moscow rigger, teacher, performer, writer and Shibaru organizer. RopeFest Peterburg 2026 says Yakov began his broader kink path in 2007, entered shibari in 2018, worked at Kinky Party, Kinky Soft, NZM, RedClub, WPP and other events, became a RopeDay / SON resident, performs shibari numbers and art performances, teaches master classes, and joined Shibaru as an organizer in summer 2023. A separate RopeFest 2026 listing gives him a lecture / partial-demonstration master class on functional one- and two-rope bondage. His 2023 Telegraph article under the KINKKNIGHT name was published as part of the Shibaru project and includes RopeDayMoscow action context, while a Wonderful People post places him in a Moscow rope-practice lineup with Nikita Ropekit, Nick Freerider, Zevsa and Alex Hitch.

### Yoi Yoshida (吉田よい)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/yoi-yoshida/

Fact — Yoi Yoshida (吉田よい) is documented as a Japanese nawashi.

Fact — Era: 1990s

Fact — NAWASHI · PRODUCER

Transmission — Tokyo-centered independent kinbakushi, teacher, producer and performer. Opened Good-Studio / Enma in Kabukicho in 2007, launched Onawa Asobi in 2009 as a platform for both highly skilled amateur and professional bakushi, and later closed Good-Studio in 2012 before co-opening Bar UBU and then Mitsu in Shinjuku with Shigonawa Bingo. Nawapedia also names her as the producer of Omuchi Asobi. Current official Onawa Asobi Europe pages still frame Yoi as the initiator and organizer of the Tokyo festival and place her in the 2026 Antwerp lineup with TatiAna. Teacher / mentor context is also public via Yada Kinbaku's Bangkok profile.

### Muku Yoji (椋陽児)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/yoji-muku/

Fact — Muku Yoji (椋陽児), 1928–2001, is documented as a Japanese illustrator.

Chronology — Born: 1928

Chronology — Died: 2001

Fact — Era: 1950s

Fact — ILLUSTRATOR · WRITER · PHOTOGRAPHER

Fact — Japanese SM illustrator, writer, editor and photo-book producer whose work helped define the visual face of postwar kinbaku publishing. Nawapedia says Muku joined Uramado's editorial department in 1963 while Chimuo Nureki was editor-in-chief, drew illustrations and comics for Jitsuwa Zasshi, and later wrote stories for SM Select under the related Ryuji Ochiai identity. Kinbaku Today characterizes him as one of the key figures in kinbaku history, notes his editorial role at Uramado, and says his work often drew directly from photographs of Nureki's rope scenes. Nawa-art's Ochiai photo-book page and Kosho's book listing resolve *Ketteiban Kinbaku no Hada* as a 1971 Ryuji Ochiai / Muku Yoji photo-book compilation.

### Yossy Balpoa (よっしー ばるぽあ)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/yossy-balpoa/

Fact — Yossy Balpoa (よっしー ばるぽあ) is documented as a Japanese rigger.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER · STUDIO OPERATOR

Fact — Tokyo / Ikebukuro kinbaku salon operator, rigger, instructor and performer using the public identity Yossy Balpoa / よっしー ばるぽあ. His public Bluesky profile names him as CSO / representative cleaner at Kinbaku Salon Yoridokoro and says he regularly hosts salon activity and kinbaku lessons, naming Coach as teacher and Airi as partner. Yoridokoro's official site characterizes the salon as a members' space began operating in Ikebukuro in 2023 for kinbaku practice, experience, viewing and exchange, with Yossy Salon and other beginner-friendly events. Fetish Bar Curiouss records Yossy as staff, a Coach-nawa graduate, Yoridokoro cleaner, and performer in rope shows.

### Yukinaga Max (雪長)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/yukinaga-max/

Fact — Yukinaga Max (雪長) is a Danish nawashi in the Osada / Western.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Fact — Danish nawashi and certified Yukimura-ryu instructor (2013). Also holds Osada-ryu Shodan and instructor license (2012). Has practiced rope bondage since 1995. The name "Yukinaga" was given jointly by both Yukimura Haruki and Osada Steve in 2012. Established Copenhagen Shibari Dojo in 2010. His public Vimeo archive preserves 2017 technical rope videos spanning takatekote / gote, hip harness, ebi shibari, kannuki, nijubishi and Yukimura-style ties.

Fact — School: Osada / Western

### Yulia Vasileva

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/yulia-vasileva/

Transmission — Yulia Vasileva is a Ukrainian teacher.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · ORGANIZER

Fact — Ukrainian shibari instructor and fetish/BDSM event organizer known as Psiheya. One of the first thematic organizers in Ukraine, creating parties, meetings, training spaces and practice exchanges. From Kharkiv, where she ran Bondage Studio with regular parties and shibari lessons; later centered in Kyiv/Berlin and teaching in other Ukrainian regions. Teaches private lessons and seminars on tying technique, suspension and model work, and organizes shibari photo sessions. Bondage Studio reports her path in shibari began 14 years before the site bio; UNIAN described her in 2018 as an organizer of fetish/BDSM events in Ukraine and a shibari instructor with eleven years of practice. Zaborona's 2023 profile characterizes her Kyiv studio on Kyrylivska Street, notes that images of people tortured with tied limbs at the beginning of the full-scale Russian invasion made her project horror onto shibari, and reports that after several months abroad she returned to Kyiv and found the studio still active with returning and new students. Has attended seminars with international teachers such as Riccardo Wildties, PhilipAnn Debeaumond, WykD Dave, Boris Mosafir, Akechi Kanna, Naka Akira, Mai Randa, Nawasabi, Ranki Kazami and Shigonawa Bingo. EURIX 2026 presenter.

### Sunohara Yuri (春原悠理)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/yuri-sunohara/

Transmission — Sunohara Yuri (春原悠理) is documented as a Japanese model in the Nureki lineage.

Fact — Era: 1980s

Fact — MODEL · ORGANIZER

Fact — Japanese SM model, writer and later public Kinbiken revival organizer. Nawapedia preserves her longer Kinbiken model / staff context, while a public Blogmura profile for SELFISH names her directly as `緊縛美研究会主宰` and an archived post recounts proposing the Kinbiken restart with Nureki Chimuo. The Kinbiken page also records Yuri Sunohara as organizer of the October 16, 2016 first meeting of Naka Akira's Bondage-Beauty Study Group.

Transmission — School: Nureki lineage

### Yusyou (熊掌)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/yusyou/

Fact — Yusyou (熊掌) is documented as a Japanese rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — RIGGER · PHOTOGRAPHER · WRITER

Fact — Okinawa-centered Japanese rigger, photographer and writer using the public identity 熊掌 / Yusyou. The Blue Wind site records self-produced kinbaku image and video work from 2014 onward, such as 2017 work and a post-hiatus return in 2023–2025. In a public essay, Yusyou characterizes tying and photographing models via a practice focused on the person being tied, atmosphere and understated Japanese aesthetics. Public profiles continue to place this rope practice in Okinawa.

### Zero-Gi

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/zero-gi/

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — PERFORMER · TEACHER

Fact — Co-founder of NYC Shibari and NYC Street Shibari with Sam Jay. Studying rope since 2017; teaming up with Sam Jay in 2018. Edgy suspension specialist. Prague Shibari Festival's Autumn 2025 presenter bio names Sam Jay & Zero-Gi as international teachers / performers known for NYC Street Shibari, high-altitude urban suspensions and dynamic stage work, and characterizes Zero-Gi's 2017 rope start plus solo-study / self-suspension development context. EURIX 2022 special guest; performer at Onawa Asobi Europe 2026.

### Zetsu (春豊)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/zetsu/

Fact — Zetsu (春豊) is documented as an American nawashi in the Yukimura-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2000s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER · WRITER

Fact — American kinbakushi and writer centered in Los Angeles, named Haru Yutaka in Yukimura-ryu. Began training at Osada Steve's Tokyo dojo in 2006, was accepted into Yukimura Haruki's school in 2010, and received a menkyo / Instructor First Class certificate in 2013. Head instructor and owner of LA Rope Dojo, with co-founder / proprietor context alongside Master K in Nawapedia and LA Rope's 2014 Naka Akira visit post. Editor of Kinbaku Today magazine and author of "Discovering Kinbaku."

Fact — School: Yukimura-ryū

### Zevsa

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/zevsa/

Transmission — Zevsa is a Russian teacher.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — TEACHER · NAWASHI · ORGANISER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Moscow bondage artist, performer and organizer connected to the Shibaru community. Zevsa is Nick Freerider's wife and is listed here as his student / influence connection in the Moscow scene. RopeFest Moscow 2023 names Zevsa as a Shibaru organizer and Moscow bondage artist working with Kinky Party, Kinky Soft, NZM and WPP. KinkAfisha's 2021 regular Shibaru club meeting records Freerider & Zevsa in the show program. Art Maison's "SHIBARU. Rope and Shadow" exhibition page characterizes Nick Freerider and Zevsa, as members of the creative association Jute, as well-known Moscow bondage artists with years of shibari practice, hundreds of creative photo sessions, film / music-video consultation work and large-scale rope performances.

### ZIMA

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/zima/

Fact — ZIMA is documented as a Japanese nawashi in the Ichinawa-ryū.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — NAWASHI · TEACHER

Transmission — Japanese kinbakushi, teacher and show performer in Hajime Kinoko's Ichinawakai / ROPES orbit. Kinoko's ROPES page records ZIMA as ROPES leader, and the Ichinawakai lessons page names ZIMA as vice-president / Tokyo co-organizer for Ichinawa group classes and as a domestic first-class official instructor. ZIMA's current lit.link and Bluesky profiles describe Hajime Kinoko as his teacher / master and frame ZIMA as an Ichinawakai / Ichinawa-class co-director and show performer; the lit.link bio says he began show performances in 2018 and has appeared in Japan as well as Taiwan, Shanghai, Thailand and Korea. Tokyo Love District's 2025 Jyo-mon listing names ZIMA & Mafuyu in the performance roster, and 2022 public coverage quotes ZIMA describing his helper role for Kinoko's Harajuku installation.

Fact — School: Ichinawa-ryū

### Zlatan Sky

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/zlatan-sky/

Fact — Zlatan Sky is a Russian nawashi.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — NAWASHI · PERFORMER · ORGANISER

Fact — Rostov-on-Don-centered Russian bondage artist, performer and event organizer. Nawapedia.ru names Zlatan Sky as a bondage artist from Rostov-on-Don. RopeFest Moscow 2018's official participant page names Zlatan Sky from Rostov-on-Don, says he had organized themed events there since 2012 and began studying rope bondage in 2012. ### Zoe Spiro

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/zoe-misfit/

Fact — Zoe Spiro is documented as an American rope facilitator.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — ROPE FACILITATOR

Fact — Seattle / White Center rope facilitator associated with Misfit Strength and Circus [after dark]. Shibari Study's Misfit profile says Zoe began practicing bondage in 2013, works with non-traditional bondage styles, acrobatics and dance, and brings rehab-medicine and bodywork study into an anatomical understanding of bondage as a physical and social medium. That record places Zoe as a facilitator for Misfit's recurring after-hours rope programme. Misfit Strength & Circus separately records Zoe Spiro as a strength and handstand coach with Cyr Wheel and performance background, and public Instagram traces connect the same Zoe Spiro / `@octopokalypse` identity.

### Zor

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/zor/

Fact — Zor is a Spanish rigger.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — RIGGER · TEACHER

Transmission — Spanish / Madrid shibari rigger, teacher and performer, also publicly documented as Carlos NawaTanuki / Carlos Nawatanuki / @carlos.nawatanuki; co-owner of Madrid Shibari Dojo with Margout Darko. Madrid Shibari Dojo's current instructor page characterizes him as studying rope since 2005, teaching 300+ shibari / kinbaku workshops in Europe, presenting at EURIX, Prague Shibari Festival, Kink&Ropes, ShibariCamp, Xplore, Zensex and MSDA Berlin, organizing Ropescape, co-organizing MSDA Berlin, and being part of Kinbaku Society of Berlin. His style is often described as Kuzushi Shibari, oriented toward creativity, improvisation and asymmetry, with study/influence from Haruki Yukimura, Norio Sugiura, Naka Akira, Ranki Kazami, Kasumi Hourai, Osada Steve, Ren Yagami and Riccardo Wildties.

### Ztella

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/ztella/

Transmission — Ztella is documented as a German teacher.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — TEACHER · PERFORMER

Transmission — Berlin-centered shibari teacher and performer using the public identity Ztella. Her official Daruma profile says she loves rope for the intense, touching and erotic connection it can create, is obsessed with shibari as a craft, programming includes regular study groups and weekend intensives at Daruma Berlin, and also teaches internationally at festivals, retreats and conferences. The profile frames her teaching around adaptability, safety, structured classes and foregrounding the specific skill sets of both riggers and models, while her own site gives the current independent public identity hub. EURIX XXVIII records Ztella & Taan among the Spring 2026 presenters in Berlin.

### Zu

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/zu-pendragon/

Fact — Zu is a Dutch rope model.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — ROPE MODEL · CO-TEACHER

Transmission — Netherlands-centered rope model, rope bottom, self-tier and co-teacher using the public identity Zu / Pendragon in the Haag and Zu teaching context. The Bi+ organizer bio says Pendragon/Zu became fascinated by rope in 2019, practices regularly as a rope bottom and self-tier, is gradually also tying as a rigger, and focuses on rope-bottoming education, informed consent, neurodivergence and mental-health-aware teaching. Public event traces list Haag & Pendragon on the Prague Shibari Festival autumn 2024 programme and in the EURIX XXV / Autumn 2024 archive, Haag / Zu as Pinse Camp Denmark 2024 co-teachers, and Haag / Pendragon as 2025 Amsterdam Mini Queer Shibari Festival workshop facilitators.

### Zuberi

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/zuberi/

Transmission — Zuberi is a British teacher.

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — TEACHER · RIGGER

Transmission — London-centered rope teacher and rigger. Anatomie Studio frames Zuberi as a core-team teacher whose practice since 2014 blends martial arts, tantra and shiatsu, moving between Naka Akira-associated traditional rope and more modern approaches such as Fred Hatt's. Public 2022-2025 class pages place him leading rope-bondage fundamentals and POC-focused learning spaces in London, such as Connection in Rope at Anatomie and Noire London's POC Foundations events at the studio. Spanning those public bios he is framed as committed to sex-positive, body-positive spaces, especially within the POC community.

### BAKUKA / 縛華

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/performances/bakuka-tokyo/

Fact — BAKUKA / 縛華, 2025–, is documented as a Japanese performance in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2025–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Tokyo shibari x ikebana live-performance and art-production project around BENI and Loyalty Flowers / Koichi Hashiguchi. Public Instagram confirms the 縛華 BAKUKA / @bakuka.tokyo profile used for current contact. HVEN and Resident Advisor sources document BENI x Loyalty Flowers' 縛華礼讃 exhibition / closing-party performance at HVEN in Tokyo, framed around SHIBARI and IKEBANA. THE MAGAZINE's 2026 BAKUKA release article names 縛華 as a project born from BENI and Loyalty Flowers and records the DJ BAKU music-release connection; ABEMA Times separately frames BAKUKA as a SHIBARI x IKEBANA art project in Shibuya night-culture context.

### Guerrilla Hashira

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/performances/guerrilla-hashira/

Fact — Guerrilla Hashira, 2024–, is documented as an American performance in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2024–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Atlanta shibari suspension-photography project by Kokoro Kinbaku / Beauregard Higgins. The first-party site frames Guerrilla Hashira as an ongoing series using hashira-style suspension imagery in hard-to-access, high-traffic, restricted or abandoned spaces, connecting rope with architecture, decay and graffiti / street-art aesthetics. Lenscratch and VoyageATL preserve public gallery and exhibition context around the same project.

### Kazak Shibari Theater

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/performances/kazak-shibari-theater/

Fact — Kazak Shibari Theater, 2023–, is a Russian performance in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2023–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Saint Petersburg shibari performance-theater project organized by Aleksandr Kazak in 2023. Kazak's official site characterizes Shibari-Teatr as a creative space uniting masters interested in stage expression and the performative language of shibari, while the official theater page frames the recurring shows as three-hour 18+ Saint Petersburg performances where rope and emotion create a wordless scenario. Public channel posts preserve credited rope performers, such as Aram Oganezov in November 2025 and the July 2026 `Obryady` programme with Aram Oganezov, Ivan Baronardi, Karol Soulrope with Kwistis, and Aleksandr Kazak.

### Knot So

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/performances/knot-so/

Fact — Knot So, 2023–, is a Dutch performance in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2023–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Amsterdam-linked rope-bondage art project by Asiana and Door, also styled KnotSo in its public contact handle. The current record names KnotSo as their artistic-couple collaboration, while a public Dutch business-registry mirror records KnotSo / Knot So among Asianawa trade names alongside RopeLounge. The first-party site frames shibari as performance art, sculpture, fashion and dance, with work spanning performance, installation, photography, video, film and TV. De Parade 2023 listed their 30-minute show Fragile with Asiana as rope top and Door as rope model, and Theaterkrant's Queer Arts Festival 2024 review characterizes KnotSo as a performance duo making performances and workshops around shibari, with an endurance-performance / intimate theatre framing around power exchange and mutual trust.

### Krimson Kinbaku

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/performances/krimson-kinbaku/

Fact — Krimson Kinbaku, 2017–, is a Polish performance in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2017–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Polish kinbaku performance / photography / beginner-workshop project by Wredna Wiedźma and Sir Grant. Wredna's official profile frames Krimson Kinbaku as the project via which she continues kinbaku shows, the `Heavenly locations` photography exhibition and beginner shibari workshops. A Tychy municipal event listing characterizes the project as multidisciplinary and created by Marta / Wredna Wiedźma and Sir Grant around rope art, sensuality and brutality, while Warsaw Tattoo Convention 2025 records a Krimson Kinbaku shibari show.

### Live Shibari Show at Erotic Museum

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/performances/live-shibari-show-barcelona/

Fact — Live Shibari Show at Erotic Museum, 2023–, is a Spanish performance in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2023–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Recurring public shibari performance / museum experience at Barcelona Erotic Museum, associated with Polo Picart / Polo Cosme and collaborators such as Maïna and Jolie Poupée in current and archival sources. Fever frames it as a 60-minute live shibari show with post-show discussion and museum visit; Polo Shibari says five shows per month have been presented for more than two years. Time Out and La Vanguardia document the 2023-2024 public-exhibition / live-show context at the museum.

### Rope Studies

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/performances/rope-studies/

Fact — Rope Studies, 2011-2017, is a Greek performance in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2011-2017

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Athens shibari performance and education project created in late 2011 by Christos Tsakalos / Chris Shaghal, using the public identity Rope Shadow. Athens Voice characterizes Rope Studies as a group with dozens of Athens performances by 2014, such as the `Cellobaku` collaboration with Stavros Parginos at Faust, while ShibariNews frames it as Rope Shadow's shibari performance team. VICE names Alice V. As the other public Rope Studies participant and places the project spanning photography / documentation, live performance and seminars. VICE also records a 2013 Gorgone collaboration and 2014 EURIX teacher / seminar context, while Booze Cooperativa sources place Rope Studies and Athens Rope Dojo together in mid-2010s Athens programming.

### Shibari Theater

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/performances/shibari-theater/

Fact — Shibari Theater, 2017–, is a Ukrainian performance in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2017–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Performance project by Tati Limati / Soul Shibari, currently surfaced publicly as Shibari Theater by TL. Soul Shibari's Path of The Master chronology dates the first Shibari Theater to Bali in August-October 2017, while the public Instagram profile frames the project as a cultural event showing life stories via rope since 2017 and presenting performances around the world. The Soul Shibari offerings page characterizes the travelling Shibari Theater programme as a three-hour format with master introduction, matcha tea ceremony, narrative rope performance, a brief open master class and live music. Later public traces place the project in New York, Tel Aviv and Tbilisi; documented as Tati / Soul Shibari performance infrastructure, not a formal school or lineage authority.

### SIBUI

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/performances/sibui/

Fact — SIBUI, 2023–, is a Russian performance in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2023–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Saint Petersburg creative project and recurring Kinbaku show format focused on public shibari actions, art installations and close-audience performance. The public channel archive frames SIBUI as a closed-list 18+ project with regular Kinbaku show meetings, a May 2026 SIBUI x RopeSchool weekend, and organizer credit for XIII RopeFest Peterburg 2026. It now creates multiple strong connections among existing Russian-scene people and infrastructure rather than only a one-person source trace.

### Study On Falling

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/performances/study-on-falling/

Fact — Study On Falling, 2016–, is a French performance in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2016–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Named shibari / performance-research work by Marika Leila Roux / Gorgone, developed around falling, gravity, suspension and the body. Public references place Study On Falling at Place des Cordes in March 2017 with Gorgone, Miss Eris and Marlene Shroers as a preview before opening Asaf Avidan's French opera shows, and at Palais Garnier on March 17, 2017 before Asaf Avidan's concert. Pauline Massimo's official works page names Pauline as interpreter of the second study, created by Marika Leila Roux for the 2017 Asaf Avidan tour, with further presentation context at Salle Pleyel, Opera de Rouen, Auditorium Maurice-Ravel and EURIX. It recurs spanning venues and sources rather than being a one-off event card.

### Crash Restraint

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/platforms/crash-restraint/

Fact — Crash Restraint, 2010–, is documented as an American platform in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Free online rope-bondage education platform created by Topologist / Chris. The homepage frames Crash Restraint as an extensive free course in rope bondage, shibari and kinbaku, focused on accurate, up-to-date, step-by-step video and written instruction, with categories spanning safety, negotiation and consent, rope preparation, floorwork, decorative tying, suspension basics and technical knots. The site is treated here as 2010s English-language online education infrastructure, not as a lineage school or a hub for every tutorial tie. Rachel Ki appears in many videos and homepage examples, but remains background source record until a stronger standalone map connection appears.

The official About page frames Douglas Kent Rope as a hand-processed jute-rope shop and says Kent started the Complete Shibari series in 2006 to make in-depth shibari information more accessible and approachable. Official product pages list Complete Shibari Volume 1: Land, Complete Shibari Volume 2: Sky, and Rogue Hojojutsu as practical rope / suspension / hojojutsu books; Open Library corroborates the 2010 Complete Shibari Land / Sky publication profile information.

### Embodied in Rope

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/platforms/embodied-in-rope/

Fact — Embodied in Rope, 2025–, is documented as a German platform in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2025–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Rope-bottom education project and curriculum co-established by Saara Rei, Anna Noctuelle and Natasha NawaTaNeko. Its published Embodied Semenawa format centers the bottom's active physical and emotional experience of intensity, combining somatic bodywork, theory and partnered rope exercises while remaining useful to both sides of the rope. The project developed alongside the Model Behaviour podcast as a separate educational practice.

The first-party site translates Iyasu Ropu as 'Rope that heals' and characterizes it as a discipline for breaking down barriers and mental schemes connected to emotional imbalance. Fact — Independent online journal established in 2014 to promote kinbaku to the Western-speaking world. The site covers event and book announcements, interviews with Japanese bakushi and models, tutorials, galleries, reviews, translations and kinbaku history.

Fact — Active: 2015–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Podcast and online archive created by Faviola Llervu for the history, art and practice of kinbaku. Apple Podcasts characterizes The Kinbakunomicon as an audio guide to kinbaku covering Japanese bondage, SM in Japan, fiction, film, theatre, sociology and related historical / cultural subjects, active from 2015 to 2026. The project site frames podcast, article, gallery, resource and blog sections and is treated here as a high-signal historical / media source platform rather than a lineage school.

### La quarta corda

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/platforms/la-quarta-corda/

Fact — La quarta corda, 2010s–, is an Italian platform in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Italian Japanese-bondage / shibari education and performance project by Andrea La quarta corda, documented separately from Andrea Ropes after a separate public-identity cleanup. La quarta corda frames Andrea as a rope artist whose practice centers communication via rope, strong control of the bottom, and a personal style where tradition and innovation meet. The project includes courses, workshops, performances, the free webbook Nawame launched in 2017 with tutorials, videos, glossary content, safety guidance and articles on kinbaku / Japanese bondage, and the separate Safety Guidelines for Bondage booklet.

### Model Behaviour

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/platforms/model-behaviour/

Fact — Model Behaviour, 2025–, is documented as a German platform in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2025–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Current rope / shibari podcast co-created by Anna Noctuelle, Natasha NawaTaNeko and Saara Rei. The official Discover Kinbaku page frames it as a podcast about rope bondage in general and shibari in particular, created by three rope models at heart, friends and educators with a combined 40+ years of experience, and centered on embodiment, consent, intimacy, relationships and the inner life of the tied body. Podbean, Apple and the official RSS feed confirm a live 2025-2026 show with 29 public episodes at the latest check, such as bounded interviews with already documented current-scene entries such as Gestalta and Freya Hellesdim.

### Nawado.pl

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/platforms/nawado-pl/

Fact — Nawado.pl, 2010s–, is a Polish platform in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Polish-language shibari / bondage education and documentation site created and maintained by Slaanesh and GanRaptor. Nawado.pl characterizes itself as a multimedia manual of bondage and shibari techniques, built from the authors' own rope experience, photos, films, texts and workshops. GanRaptor's official bio calls Nawado.pl one of the largest bondage / shibari technique manuals available online, with galleries, tutorial material, rope supplies and workshops, while the current GanRaptor site frames the project family as online courses, prepared shibari ropes, rope-care materials and accessories.

### Nawapedia

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/platforms/nawapedia/

Fact — Nawapedia, 2010s–, is a platform in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — English-language online encyclopedia of Japanese bondage and related kink activities. The current main page says Nawapedia aims to make Japanese SM information accessible to a global audience, focusing on Japanese-language information so English-speaking readers can build a solid knowledge base. In atlas terms it is both a heavily reused secondary source family and a bounded source-infrastructure platform in its own right, especially for global readers tracing Japanese kinbaku history.

### Noshibari Art

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/platforms/noshibari-art/

Fact — Noshibari Art, 2008-, is a Spanish platform in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2008-

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Barcelona contemporary shibari project directed by Alberto Noshibari. The first-party site characterizes NoShibari Art as a collective project for experimentation and teaching around contemporary shibari, organized around creation, teaching and interdisciplinary mixing with art, sexuality, therapy, movement, photography and dance. Its current education framing centers sensation, rope technique and external gaze, with private classes, micro-workshops, thematic modules and collaborative workshops. Instagram profile evidence characterizes @noshibariart as a collaborative experimentation / teaching project for contemporary shibari and links The first-party site, Telegram channel, Threads profile, the A, Tríptico del Amor performance page and summer workshops. Current pages place summer workshops at Espai de Sants in Barcelona, guided by Alberto Noshibari, and frame A, Tríptico del Amor as an extended work-in-progress where shibari intersects music, dance, visual art and affect. A March 2025 Noshibari Art Instagram post also records a Shibari for Lovers workshop at Amantis Barcelona with @nonoshibari and @amanitaropes.

Fact — Active: 2010s–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — New York shibari / rope education, performance, photography and production platform associated with Sam Jay and Zero-Gi, such as NYC Street Shibari and Tying in the Tropics. The current official site frames NYC Shibari around private classes, workshops, live shibari performance, stunt rigging, fashion / film / music-video rope work and booking professional rope artists. Event bios describe Sam Jay and Zero-Gi as joining forces in 2018 and becoming known for high-altitude urban suspensions, street shibari, performances, inclusive adaptations and new ties / transition sequences.

### Rope Connections

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/platforms/rope-connections/

Fact — Rope Connections, 2015–, is a New Zealand platform in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2015–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Safety / consent — Online rope-bondage and BDSM education site by Pete Riggs, active from at least 2015. The official start page organizes beginner rope-bondage tutorials, rope-kit advice, safety / negotiation material, scene-planning ideas, reviews and FAQ-style learning paths, while the About page says Riggs uses the site to organize and share what he learns and characterizes a shibari-influenced but non-dogmatic rope style. The teaching page records occasional in-person lessons in Christchurch, New Zealand, covering negotiation, safety, rope types, ties, connection / intimacy, scene mood and rope finishing, with a current note that in-person teaching is on hiatus. The site also sells Riggs's beginner-focused `Rope Bondage The Smart Way` e-book and broader BDSM scene-planning e-book.

Fact — Active: 2023–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Seattle-area rope-bondage and shibari guide, event calendar and directory platform. The first-party site frames Rope In Seattle as a curated guide to the regional Seattle rope bondage scene, with instructor and venue directories, first-time and online resources, gear resources, a curation policy, calendar subscription routes, and event / venue submission workflows. Its About page frames the project as a curated list of rope bondage events in Seattle and regional areas, while the public Substack archive shows weekly Seattle rope roundups distributed by web, email, RSS and Google Calendar from at least April 2023.

### Rope Study

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/platforms/rope-study/

Fact — Rope Study, 2015–, is documented as an American platform in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2015–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Richmond, Virginia rope-bondage education project established in fall 2015 by Alex / Ma'iitsoh Yazhi and Bound Light. The official About page says it previously provided in-person instruction and online support materials for risk-aware rope bondage education for tops and bottoms, with regular classes, workshops, private instruction and self-study materials intended to bridge peer-share / online basics and intermediate-to-advanced conference preparation. As of the 2026 check, Rope Study no longer provides in-person instruction but maintains the website as a practitioner resource, with core values around scholarship, citizenship, stewardship and humility.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Online rope-bondage / shibari education platform authored by Ebi McKnotty. Rope365 frames a free 365-concept curriculum for learning, practicing and exploring rope, with sections for getting started, foundations, body positions, techniques, mastery, explorations and external resources. Its own pages emphasize consent, communication, safety, rope shopping, vocabulary, body mechanics, rope care, history and community learning, and invite local communities to adapt the curriculum with attribution rather than copy the site content.

### Shibari.Guru

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/platforms/shibari-guru/

Fact — Shibari.Guru, 2020–, is a Russian platform in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Online shibari education and performance-video archive by Boris Mosafir. The site programming includes paid video lessons covering basics, patterns, training and suspension, and its About page says the course content is based on the RopeSchool course and Boris Mosafir's additional author seminars. The same page frames video lessons as supplementary study content to live training and says another major part of the project is performance-video documentation from ArtCo-organized events and recordings sent by other shibari masters. Its Shibari show section preserves 120+ full performance videos from RopeFest, RopeDay, Bondage Cafe SPb, Moscow Knot, Rome Bondage Week and other event traces.

### Academy for Shibari in Israel

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/platforms/shibari-israel-academy/

Fact — Academy for Shibari in Israel, 2026–, is an Israeli platform in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2026–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Hebrew-language online shibari education platform. Its official site frames the project as the Academy for Shibari in Israel, offering online lessons, live shows, a community forum and shop. The lesson library includes beginner safety, partner negotiation, rope types, knots, harnesses, flexibility and communication modules, with Ragnar presented as a recurring instructor and as the voice explaining the idea and vision behind the academy. The public disclaimer frames the content as adult, fully clothed, consent-centered education focused on technique, communication, connection and movement.

The public YouTube channel preserves additional Shibariland course-video lessons.

### Shibari Study

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/platforms/shibaristudy/

Fact — Shibari Study, 2019–, is documented as a German platform in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2019–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Online education platform for shibari, established in 2019 by Gorgone (Marika Leila Roux) — who brought the shibari know-how and instructor contacts — together with Anton Martin, who brought the vision and operations. Berlin-centered GmbH. Programming includes 800+ tutorials by world-class instructors at multiple skill levels, live classes, a public community page and a member-only Discord community. Director of Education: Fuoco. Web and mobile apps. The same project also operates Shibari Study Shop, selling Berlin hand-treated Japanese Ogawa / JBO-free jute rope, safety gear and beginner kits with a digital learning path; The page presents tracked as the platform's material-supply extension rather than a separate public profile. Its Shibari Study Podcast, hosted by Wicked Wren, provides interview / media-source record for selected instructors and scene figures.

### SPNKD

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/platforms/spnkd/

Fact — SPNKD, 2021–, is a Belgian platform in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2021–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Antwerp-rooted hybrid BDSM / intimacy platform combining education, events, loft stays, coaching and retail since 2021. Official pages describe SPNKD as building sensual spaces, curated content and exclusive events, while the live event program shows active Antwerp and Berlin strands and explicitly frames the offering as spanning beginner BDSM via starter shibari skills and more advanced rigging. The linked Teachable course also confirms a durable online education lane under founder Paul Meyers and MissV.

Transmission — San Francisco-centered Western rope-bondage instruction project by Dan and JD, presented on their official site as professional riggers, workshop teachers, performers and photo-shoot riggers. Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — Tokyo / Ikebukuro SM production studio associated with Naka Akira's late-2000s production work. Nawapedia records that Naka started Studio Amadas in 2009 with Mr. T; existing Naka biography notes Amadas as an SM production studio he produced before the later Atelier Naka / Semenawa Kinbaku Research Group phase.

### Cinemagic

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/productions/cinemagic/

Fact — Cinemagic, 1983–, is documented as a Japanese production in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 1983–

Fact — Era: 1980s

Fact — Japanese SM / rope video production company established in 1983 by Yoshimura Shoichi. Kinbaku Today characterizes Cinemagic as one of the largest and most important rope and SM studios of the 1980s, and later sources frame it as central to the 1990s kinbaku VHS era. Important here not as a filmography hub, but as historical media infrastructure that connected Yoshimura with Nureki Chimuo, Haruki Yukimura, Akechi Denki and Naka Akira, giving several bakushi production context and visibility.

Fact — Dogma CORE, 2006–, is documented as a Japanese production in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2006–

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — Narrow Japanese SM / AV production label launched in 2006 from the Dogma / TOHJIRO context and co-established with Naka Akira. Loft Plus One's March 2006 launch event frames Dogma CORE as TOHJIRO's SM label and records a special live rope / SM segment by Naka Akira and TOHJIRO; Nawapedia records both Naka and TOHJIRO as co-founders of the label.

### 1701 Studio

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/1701-studio/

Fact — 1701 Studio, 2017-, is documented as an American studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2017-

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — Austin / Texas Japanese-influenced rope-bondage and shibari teaching project associated with Marc GentLeeBrutal. Current official pages present 1701 Studio via the Austin Rope Collective curriculum, with four modules spanning foundations, intermediate partial suspensions, introductory static suspension and advanced suspension, regular Tuesday classes and Thursday practice / jam nights, plus private lessons, photoshoots and video sessions such as Tethered Together 2023 with Marc / GentLeeBrutal and Jordyn / PedalGirl. The current ARC page also frames the curriculum as a collaboration with Heartland Kinbaku, but public evidence keeps the strongest current map connection centered on Marc's existing studio-teaching role.

### Aēr Place of Light Tbilisi

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/aer-placeoflight-tbilisi/

Fact — Aēr Place of Light Tbilisi, 2020s-, is a studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Tbilisi art / community studio and rentable venue space publicly operating as Aēr Place of Light / `@aer_placeoflight`. The Dikidi booking profile supports a current booking and hall-rental surface, while the public Telegram channel frames Aēr as a space for public-event announcements, free-slot schedules and organizational information. Public Telegram shibari / bondage search results preserve repeated rope-facing programming at Aēr: Elena Sulima beginner bondage, Tati Limati's `4 Elements` and beginner shibari course context, a Shibari Theater in Tbilisi immersive evening, butoh / shibari performance-class context and a Mikhail Karlson safety-seminar trace.

Fact — Bogotá, Colombia shibari / rope-art project using the public handle @akira.dojo. The public Instagram profile names Constanza Nieves and Christbari in the bio and frames the project around Shibari / Kinbaku / Rope Art in Bogotá. Repeated 2025-2026 public Colombia workshop flyers independently place Akira Dojo in local support / organizing context around a Bogotá Sekibaku intensive with Ryuan / Sr. Interior of Escuela de Shibari and a Medellín seminar with Mango. ### Anatomie Studio

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/anatomie-studio/

Fact — Anatomie Studio, 2015–, is a British studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2015–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — London shibari studio and community space in Peckham, established in June 2015 by Anna Bones and Fred Hatt. Current official pages frame it as the UK's first full-time shibari educational space, with weekly beginner sessions, multi-level classes, rope jams, social events, life drawing and private hire. Its Shibari Store / Anatomie Shop extension has prepared and sold rope since the studio opened and launched online in 2016, with hand-treated ropes refined via feedback from Anatomie classes, workshops and rope jams.

### Aphilia Studio

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/aphilia-studio/

Fact — Aphilia Studio, 2020s-, is a British studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — East London Japanese rope / shibari studio at Cable Street Studios. Official Aphilia pages present the space as an intimate studio for body-centric Japanese rope practice, emotional exchange and physical dialogue, with an ethos of radical tenderness, fierce compassion, vulnerability, empathy and safety. Public programming includes private classes with studio owner Conor Aphilia, multiple-date beginner classes, a weekly Wednesday Rope Jam, Aibunawa study sessions and 2026 salon / performance listings via TicketTailor. Aibunawa / Yukimura wording and guest-event names are treated as programme context, not as lineage or broad collaborator relationships.

Fact — Active: 2024-

Fact — Dresden kink-education, shibari and bodywork atelier operated publicly as Atelier ÆØS / Atelier AEOS. The first-party site says Sascha / VÆGABØUND and Lola Sapieha stand behind the atelier as teachers, body therapists and artists, with professional work since 2021 and a dedicated Dresden studio opened for their own work. Official offer, rental and calendar pages present shibari / kink education, coaching, bodywork / sessions, studio rental, photoshoots, smaller events, bamboo / ring hanging points, mats, current ARISE / AWAKENING programming and a public address at Rankestrasse 56.

### Atelier Mae

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/atelier-mae/

Fact — Atelier Mae, 2020s–, is a Belgian studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Namur / Erpent shibari, movement and art studio run as Atelier Mae ASBL. Its official site characterizes workshops, classes, jams, private tuition and rental in a protected community-learning space. The 'Who are we?' section names Eric and Laurianne as the organizers and says they began their rope apprenticeship at Yoroi-Dojo in Brussels, run by A-Nicolas / Nicolas Yoroi, and fell in love with his rope style. The current 2026 agenda also shows recurring Perf & Jam evenings, Graines de Cordes III programming in Namur, and the International Shibari Film Festival opening-night screening in Brussels, such as the June 6, 2026 Lexienawa & SilentLynx guest-performance evening.

### Auckland Shibari Studio

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/auckland-shibari-studio/

Fact — Auckland Shibari Studio, 2024–, is a New Zealand studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2024–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Auckland / K Road shibari studio and weekly class community. Button Factory's class page says Auckland Shibari Studio has been building a beginner-to-intermediate rope community for over a year, with weekly Tuesday classes at 2 Abbey St covering safe rope handling, body awareness, connection, creative expression, beginner classes and intermediate partnered work. Humanitix and festival listings connect the studio to performance nights, rope facilitation and Kaspian's beginner workshops.

### Bondage Studio Kyiv

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/bondage-studio-kyiv/

Fact — Bondage Studio Kyiv, 2010s-, is a Ukrainian studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s-

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Kyiv shibari / bondage studio associated with Yulia Vasileva (Psiheya). Bondage Studio records Yulia as its shibari master and characterizes private lessons, seminars on tying technique, suspension and model work, plus shibari photo sessions. Zaborona reported that after earlier Kharkiv studio activity, Vasileva found a spacious hall on Kyrylivska Street in Kyiv for lectures and practical lessons; its 2023 profile also says that after several months abroad following the full-scale Russian invasion, she returned to Kyiv and found the studio still active with returning and new students.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — DC metro / Maryland rope studio and community space co-established by Veers and Violet. The official About page characterizes Bound Lark Rope Studio as a dedicated rope studio for the DC Metro area, a local community space and a private space for learning and interpersonal development around rope bondage. The current programming pages document recurring Rope Lab and Share, Rope Jam, Midweek Meetup, BIPOC Rope Jam, Cinema Circle, Work of Art, rotating classes and a KinbakuOdyssey collaboration with Odys and Paige. The studio appears in the map via bounded existing-person teaching traces, such as Odys / Paige, Wildties / RedSabbath and Erato-Banana.

Fact — Active: 2023-

Fact — Portland, Maine shibari education studio began operating in 2023 after the Black Knot Rope Group period. Official Box Tie pages describe it as Maine's first full-time shibari education studio, queer-owned and run by Cait / ropespaced, Griffin / Griffin207, Beck and Grace, with multiple monthly rope-focused events, all-level classes, private and small-group lessons, sessions, rope jams / socials, play parties, rope sales and consent / negotiation education.

### Cats Downstairs Rope Salon

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/cats-downstairs/

Fact — Cats Downstairs Rope Salon, 2018–, is a studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2018–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Taipei rope salon and studio established in 2018. The official about page characterizes Cats Downstairs as a long-running shibari practice space that moved within Taipei in 2021 and, from 2023 onward, expanded into a more active rope-culture host with salons, figure-drawing collaborations, markets, guest artists and international workshops. Since 2024 it has operated a new shared studio near Yuanshan Station together with The Suspended Canvas, framed as a multicultural, gender-friendly rope base with multiple suspension points.

### Corneum

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/corneum/

Fact — Corneum, 2019-, is a Belgian studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2019-

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Belgian shibari studio / lounge / rope space established by FranckStunn / Frank Stunn. Corneum's official pages describe a Lennik / Brussels-area venue for shibari workshops, private sessions, rope jams, semenawa and hashira practice; the info page says the project has existed since 2019, first for private workshops and jams, and expanded after a 2022 makeover with multiple suspension / bamboo / hashira setups. The founder bio records international workshop study and style influences such as Yukimura Ryu, Ropunawa, Naka, Ren Yagami, Nicolas Yoroi, Soptik, Docvale, Ropeknight, RopeMarks, Felix Ruckert, Asiana, Sin and others; The page presents mostly presented as context rather than formal lineage. Public traces also show Corneum hosting Kirigami & Tenshiko and selling Ogawa jute rope via its shop.

### Cuarto Oscuro

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/cuarto-oscuro-argentina/

Fact — Cuarto Oscuro, 2020s–, is an Argentine studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Argentine rope-teaching, performance, photography and publishing project active in the 2020s. Official pages present Cuarto Oscuro as Martín Kinbaku's teaching and analog-photography project, offering regular classes, private lessons and intensive workshops framed around communication, expressiveness, technique and training. The site also preserves events, blog, video and contact infrastructure: its RopeTales 2026 event page frames the project as the Argentina host context for Federico Kirigami and Marta Tenshiko workshops with preparatory classes led by Martín, while the Kinbaku Íntimo #16 page records a six-year local performance-night line at Genya Kinbaku in Buenos Aires.

### Darkpeach (暗桃桃 / 桃源舍)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/darkpeach-guangzhou/

Fact — Darkpeach (暗桃桃 / 桃源舍), 2019–, is a Chinese studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2019–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Guangzhou BDSM and rope cultural-creative studio established in 2019, with the offline rope space Taoyuan She began operating in Yuexiu District in 2022. Public project pages describe regular salon parties, performances, teaching, workshops, private rope lessons, member activity and an online community presence spanning WeChat, Instagram, X, Xiaohongshu and Matters. The Shibari Study profile adds current English-facing community-studio context, such as beginner-friendly classes, jams, performances, play parties and a team led by Hermann, with Eme, Li Mao and Jinyi presented as background context rather than separate atlas entries.

Fact — Active: 2024-

Fact — Dedicated Berlin shibari studio opened by Gestalta in January 2024 and owned / run by Gestalta. Daruma Berlin operates as a Neukolln shibari space for classes, curriculum events, private lessons, community events, performances and guest-presenter workshops; it is documented as compact studio infrastructure rather than a lineage school.

### Devil Mask Studio

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/devil-mask-studio/

Fact — Devil Mask Studio, 2024–, is documented as an American studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2024–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Queer-owned and operated shibari studio in the Historic Core of downtown Los Angeles, launched in January 2024 by Devil Mask Society members Ordinals, Ojipan and GoodMostTimes. Current official pages describe Devil Mask Studio as a consent-forward kink, pleasure and somatic-education space with membership, a full in-house shibari curriculum, classes and events, private instruction, rope sessions, consulting and rentals. The staff page names Nora / Ordinals as CEO, Andrew / Ojipan as COO and tying instructor, and Melissa / GoodMostTimes as CFO and longtime educator / Devil Mask Society founding member. Its documented 2025 program includes Shay Tiziano's Tying & Flying self-suspension workshop and performance.

### Discover Kinbaku

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/discover-kinbaku/

Fact — Discover Kinbaku, 2010s–, is documented as a German studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Berlin-centered kinbaku education, performance, photography and research project by Alexander MA and Natasha NawaTaNeko. Current Discover Kinbaku pages frame the project as a Berlin kinbaku studio and rope-bondage education system that teaches Japanese-inspired rope as an embodied craft for riggers, rope bottoms and couples, combining technique, non-technique, body, mind, communication, consent and shared scene design. Alexander's Elements System gives the rigger side a staged didactic framework, while Natasha's rope-bottom coaching and keynotes extend the project into somatic bottoming education. Discover Kinbaku also programming includes private coaching, international workshops for host venues, live performances, Studio 6x6 work in Berlin-Moabit, the Kinbaku Salon and the Kinbaku Society of Berlin / KSB magazine project. Its own materials frame the work as rooted in semenawa by Akira Naka, with key influence from encounters with Naka Akira, Sugiura Norio and Miho Ikeda. Alexander's Bodies under Pressure concept is taught as a Discover Kinbaku workshop and articulated in his 2025 essay as a somatic / pressure-centered semenawa approach. EURIX records DiscoverKinbaku for beginner-course teaching in 2021 and Alexander / Natasha in later 2020s editions.
