### Espacio de Ataduras

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/espacio-de-ataduras/

Fact — Espacio de Ataduras, 2023-, is a Chilean studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2023-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Providencia / Santiago rope-practice and workshop space closely tied to Alita Greenblack's public activity. Public Instagram programming characterizes Espacio de Ataduras as a room specialized for rope practice, designed to provide a safe and comfortable environment, and repeatedly places recurring jams, laboratories, introductory classes and drawing-with-shibari sessions at Galvarino Gallardo 1638 in Providencia. The same public shell shows the venue hosting Chilean local rope programming around Alita Greenblack and current international guest teaching such as the scheduled October 24-25, 2026 Vik & Gala semenawa workshop from Barcelona.

### Espacio Nos

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/espacio-nos/

Fact — Espacio Nos, 2020–, is a Spanish studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Barcelona interdisciplinary shibari / movement / art / performance space created by Pauline Massimo and began operating in 2020. The official about page frames Espacio Nos as a full-time physical space focused on LGBTQI+ / femininities / marginalized communities and as a Spanish pioneer in educational and entertainment shibari events with explicit anti-abuse policies. It is structurally useful for the map because its own archive and current program connect Barcelona to international shibari teachers and performers already in the map, such as Kasumi Hourai, Akechi Kanna & Kagura, Barkas & Addie, Gandalf, Tamandua, Hua Hua, Gestalta and Ceci Ferox.

Fact — Estudio Jorogumo Shibari, 2020s–, is a Mexican studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Mexico City rope studio and collective active in the 2020s. Official Jorogumo pages describe it as a trans-disident space dedicated to learning, experimentation and creation with rope and diverse bodies, treating shibari as a channel of connection and political pleasure. The current official workshops page says the project builds connections between the shibari community in Mexico and the wider world via collaborative sessions with international artists and educators, such as the public Hua Hua / Borderline Shibari guest-workshop trail.

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/flower-and-snake-seattle/

Fact — Flower and Snake Seattle, 2020s-, is documented as an American studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Seattle Capitol Hill rope-arts lounge operating publicly as Flower and Snake / Flower & Snake. The official home page characterizes classes, workshops, tying space, rope-lounge hours, ongoing rope-skills development, intensive workshops, photography and professional rigging, with six tying spaces appointed with bamboo and columns for hashira shibari. The About page frames the lounge as a custom-built rope environment opened by Jacques LeBouc and Aven the Maiden, while the membership page confirms rope-lounge events, educational events, limited weekend intensives and member-only rope events.

Fact — Hidden Layer Studio, 2023–, is documented as a Japanese studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2023–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Tokyo / Omotesando studio and artist-unit infrastructure around Pablo Shibari and AIAIDA. Pablo's official bio says he and AIAIDA established a company called Hidden Layer after performing rope, installation and new-media work in Tokyo's underground art / techno scene, then opened an Omotesando studio at the beginning of 2023. PR Times names Hidden Layer Studio with SuperUser as the organizer of a 2023 Unbound event, while Bonobo uses the public `@hiddenlayer.unbound` identity for Hidden Layer's Unbound sessions.

Fact — House Cordee, 2020s-, is a Thai studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Bangkok rope house / shibari studio operating publicly as House Cordee near BTS Asok. The first-party site frames it as an open, free-of-charge rope house for models of all genders, orientations and experience levels, while the About page says the project is centered in Bangkok, exists to promote and spread shibari, and is not a business. The studio page records foam mats, multiple suspension hardpoints, photographic equipment, shower / towels and safety tools.

### House of Knotty

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/house-of-knotty/

Fact — House of Knotty, 2023-, is documented as an American studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2023-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Seattle gay / queer rope studio established by Shibari Shawn in 2023 and operating from 3100 Airport Way S. The first-party site frames House of Knotty as Seattle's Gay Rope Studio with beginner and intermediate rope classes, suspension cohorts, rope jam, Tea Dance, play-party and Impact206 programming. Its Rope Jam page characterizes a monthly practice / play space with bamboo and ring hard points, while the Tea Dance page frames the project as LGBTQ+ community infrastructure with inclusive rope / brunch programming. Rope In Seattle's current venue page independently records House of Knotty as a Seattle gay shibari studio and venue.

### IKSK Berlin

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/iksk-berlin/

Fact — IKSK Berlin, 2020–, is documented as a German studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Institut für Körperforschung und sexuelle Kultur — Felix Ruckert's new venue at Holzmarkt 25, Berlin, opened October 2020. Successor to schwelle7 (2007–2016) as Berlin's flagship shibari and sex-positive practice space. IKSK's own community page frames it as a project of Compagnie Felix Ruckert / Berlin e.V. And explicitly compares its accessibility model to schwelle7; Felix's 2020 IKSK text says EURIX continues twice yearly at Holzmarkt as a rope-bondage exchange / research festival.

Fact — Kakumei Kan, 2020s–, is a Finnish studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Helsinki kinbaku studio co-owned by Saara-Tsuru and Jarno-Karasu. Kakumei Kan characterizes itself as a kinbaku studio offering shibari sessions, workshops, performances, mentoring, coaching, ropes, weekly classes, weekend workshops, international workshops, twice-monthly jam evenings and studio rental. The site records Saara Kantanen (Saara-Tsuru) and Jarno Kantanen (Jarno-Karasu) as instructors. Kakumei Kan announced a move to a new Helsinki location from the beginning of August 2026, with workshops, coaching and jam evenings continuing there.

### Karada House

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/karada-house/

Fact — Karada House, 2019–, is documented as a German studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2019–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Berlin-centered queer rope hub, body-positive art space and teaching collective co-established by Caritia, Renée de Sans and Mamana. Hosts Queerope, in-house and online shibari / BDSM / sexuality workshops, private lessons and performance formats. Karada House organizer, homepage and Rope School pages identify Caritia, Ceci Ferox, Mamana and Renée de Sans as current teachers, while its event archive records Dasniya Sommer and Gestalta classes / performances. Central 2020s diffusion point for queer, body-diverse and safer-space kinbaku education in Berlin.

### Kinbaku Lounge Copenhagen

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/kinbaku-lounge-copenhagen/

Fact — Kinbaku Lounge Copenhagen, 2013-, is a Danish studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2013-

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Copenhagen / Valby kinbaku venue, teaching space and shop established by Scot Kinbaku, with community activity since 2013 and the venue open since 2015. The first-party site records regular rope nights, classes, beginner/newbie onboarding, international workshops and KinbakuShop; the staff page names Scot and Tanja / Namarié as co-owners. Discover Kinbaku characterizes it as an influential European rope hub that hosted Naka Akira, Iroha Shizuki, Norio Sugiura and Miho in the late 2010s; current official listings include Norio Sugiura's 2026 photography masterclass, an Alexander MA / Natasha NawaTaNeko Hashira intensive, Ropu Nawa & Freya Hellesdim's August 2026 Rope Fusion weekend and a still-too-thin Hajime Kinoko 2027 teaser.

### Kinbaku Nexus

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/kinbaku-nexus/

Fact — Kinbaku Nexus, 2024-, is a Danish studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2024-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Sabro / Aarhus-area kinbaku studio and membership space dedicated to Japanese rope bondage. The official about page says the idea began in spring 2024 and the doors began operating in January 2025, with fixed teaching, workshops and other activities. The project is run by a four-person steering group from the Aarhus rope scene and is framed as a place for Jutland rope-interested people to learn, network, socialize and play.

Fact — Swiss shibari / kinbaku studio began operating in 2021 by Sergey A. ### Kinbaku Studio Munich

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/kinbaku-studio-munich/

Fact — Kinbaku Studio Munich, 2021-, is documented as a German studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2021-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Munich kinbaku / shibari studio shell currently surfaced publicly via the Instagram display name `Kinbaku Studio Munchen`. The visible profile wording frames it around kinbaku, shibari, semenawa and hojojutsu, with workshops, events, training and photo shootings. Independent Tamandua schedule pages from September and December 2021 also preserve `Shibari Kinbaku Studio Munich` as a named Munich workshop host, with organizer / host contact routed via Mcarthur. `_diabolicart` and Mcarthur stay source record only in this review because a stronger standalone public identity trail has not yet been established for either name.

### Knot Here Amsterdam

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/knot-here-amsterdam/

Fact — Knot Here Amsterdam, 2024-, is a Dutch studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2024-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Amsterdam-area rope space and event organizer. Knot Here's official site frames it as a community rope space organizing rope jams in Amsterdam and aiming to create a welcoming place for the community to gather and share rope. Its public event archive records a move from referral-centered rope jams into public rope jams, Kinbaku Night performances, monthly 2026 rope jams, and intensive workshops with Asiana / Door, Kurogami / Shiawase, Soptik / Sansei, Stuart / PuurKris and other guests. Event pages describe a multi-floor rope venue near Weesp station with twelve bamboo suspension points, social space and floorwork space; Hipsy independently corroborates the Amsterdam shibari-event organizer profile.

### Knot Lounge Lisbon

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/knot-lounge-lisbon/

Fact — Knot Lounge Lisbon, 2017–, is a Portuguese studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2017–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Lisbon rope studio / venue and practice project. The 2017 'Knot Lounge - The Beginning' listing characterizes a monthly rope-jam project at Anjos 70 open to all experience levels, with suspension and floorwork practice areas, basic tying support and a goal of developing rope practice in Portugal. Later listings preserve Knot Lounge workshops, photography-focused shibari events and public contact via knotlounge@gmail.com. The Joy Spot names Monad and Morgana as part of the Knot Lounge project, giving workshops and performances. A 2026 Instagram recheck frames Knot Lounge as a Lisbon venue for learning and practicing shibari, kinbaku and rope bondage. First- and second-half 2026 schedule posts show continuing programming: beginner rope jams, rope-talk / jam sessions, basics and suspension workshops, Between the Lines with Rope Garden, performance programming, a May 2026 Natasha NawaTaNeko talk and Alexander MA / Natasha NawaTaNeko workshop, a Stuart & Cath intensive and a ninth-anniversary open day.

Fact — Croatian shibari community / space dedicated to research, practice and education in Japanese rope bondage. ### KNOTY

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/knoty-porto/

Fact — KNOTY, 2021–, is a Portuguese studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2021–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Queer-owned rope and BDSM education space in Porto, established by Ana Peres in January 2021 and operated publicly as KNOTY / The KNOTY (W)Hole. Its Portuguese- and English-language programme includes introductory and advanced shibari classes, intensives, rope jams, private and virtual lessons, play nights, talks and visiting-teacher workshops. The space emphasizes self-tying access, rope-bottom education, knowledge sharing and sliding-scale options where possible.

Fact — Koden Studio Dojo, 2025–, is a Colombian studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2025–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Bogotá, Colombia shibari studio/project using the public handle @koden.studiodojo. The public Instagram profile names @sr.isoffler and @cuervovivian, gives a Bogotá street address, and frames the project around performance, workshops, courses, sessions and products with an emphasis on tradition and safety. Repeated late-2025 public course posts place Koden Studio Dojo in local workshop-organizing context around Ryuan / Sr. Interior of Escuela de Shibari and in direct collaboration with Akira Dojo. The public evidence does not yet expose a standalone teaching site, full organizer biographies or a formal authorization structure for Koden itself.

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Castel San Giovanni / Piacenza shibari studio publicly presented as Koguma Dojo inside Spazio Musubi and the MBDSTUDIO Shibari Dojo ecosystem. Official Davide La Greca pages list Spazio Musubi at Strada del Merlino 8, Castel San Giovanni, while shop pages offer Koguma Dojo as a Japanese-style private shibari lesson space and as an equipped shibari-photography rental studio with ropes, traditional props, lighting, makeup / shower facilities and studio-rental options.

Fact — Kokoro Studio Hong Kong, 2015–2025, is a Hong Kong-centered studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2015–2025

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Hong Kong shibari studio established by Naoko / Subay in 2015. Subay's official site frames Kokoro Studio as her Hong Kong rope base; later Studio Yukari / Yugen Studio sources describe Kokoro Studio as her Hong Kong studio before her London move. The public archive preserves rope lessons, rope salons, Queerope 2020 participation and a 2020 virtual sharing session with Ibarako.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Bangkok / Old Town private shibari and kinbaku studio space established by Yada Kinbaku. The official Kotori site frames Kotori People Bangkok as a conscious collective of shibari practitioners with a private studio dedicated to body-centered practice, offering workshops and performances by somatic professionals, body workers, local and guest shibari artists, plus rope jams, mini classes and social practice. Yada's Kotori profile says she has hosted workshops under the KOTORI name since 2019, while the February 2026 Bangkok Shibari Showcase page records Kotori Studio as a two-day local / international performance showcase at the Phra Nakhon address.

### LIMINAL Amsterdam

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/liminal-amsterdam/

Fact — LIMINAL Amsterdam, 2020s-, is a Dutch studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Amsterdam sensual-arts studio and rope community space. The current Hipsy organizer profile characterizes LIMINAL as a place for embodied sensual self-expression, accessible gatherings, playful co-creation, inclusion, personal growth and sobriety around intoxicating substances. Current 2026 listings show recurring Wednesday peer-rope evenings, casual Sunday rope jams, Tea & Shibari performances, Kinbaku Cafe programming, embodied crossover formats and guest workshops. The May 2026 Tease & Torment listing brings the existing Roxu / Ena Dahl teaching pair to LIMINAL, and the August 2026 Aibunawa workshop adds a second current existing-entry connection via Stuart. The public Instagram profile characterizes the project as `LIMINAL || Sensual arts studio`.

Fact — Takatsuki / Osaka kinbaku salon and lesson space operated by Take Kenkou / 武健虹 with partner Mai / まい. The first-party site characterizes Nawadokoro Maitake as a kinbaku salon / classroom for beginners and experienced visitors, with rope lessons, private lessons outside ordinary hours, rope-receiving experiences, optional model arrangements by request and pair practice. ### Mie Neko Studio

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/mie-neko-studio/

Fact — Mie Neko Studio, 2020s–, is documented as a German studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Ruhrgebiet / Essen studio and teaching organization around the existing Mie Neko person record. Shibari Study characterizes Mie Neko as a feminist safer-space organization for FLINTA*, LGBTQIA+, fat, BIPoC, disabled and other discrimination-affected participants, while still welcoming others who respect that space. The studio programming includes skill-level workshops, private tuition, monthly training between workshops and rope jams, and occasional performance nights; existing official Mie Neko pages support Essen / NRW teaching and the 2026 workshop-series activity.

### Morikai Studio

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/morikai-studio/

Fact — Morikai Studio, 2018–, is an Israeli studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2018–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Shibari studio in the Montefiore neighbourhood of Tel Aviv, Israel (Soncino 17). Established in 2018 by a group of friends from the local BDSM and kink community to create a welcoming space for amateur riggers and models to learn and practice — open to all lineages, with focus on the passionate Kinbaku side of shibari. Operates four days weekly (Tue–Fri); programming includes fundamentals via advanced classes plus Wild Thursdays, Open Jams, Kinbaku Show Nights, and Public Sessions. Has hosted international workshops such as Naka-ryū intensives with Riccardo Wildties (Kinbaku Luxuria) and a Riccardo Wildties / Red Sabbath show night.

Fact — Privately owned shibari rope venue in Central Jutland, Denmark, active since January 2023. ### Nagare Studio

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/nagare-studio-brno/

Fact — Nagare Studio, 2026-, is a Czech studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2026-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Brno shibari studio and local rope-learning space operating publicly as Nagare Studio / `@nagarebrno`. The first-party site characterizes Nagare as a shibari studio in central Brno and frames it as a safe, respectful place for people developing their love of rope, with communication, mutual respect, creative freedom, consent and safety emphasized. The current workshop calendar shows recurring rope jams and miniworkshops at the Nagare venue, and a June 2026 event page confirms the Hybešova 40 address and live workshop-programming surface. Defkongice's official workshop page says her 2026 studio workshops moved to Nagare after the Brno shibari studio was established, while the public Instagram profile characterizes workshops, rope jams, private lessons and a beginner-safe space.

### NekoNeko Studio

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/nekoneko-studio/

Fact — NekoNeko Studio, 2020s-, is a Serbian studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Belgrade shibari and photography studio run by Ksenia and Seva. NekoNeko frames itself as a safe space for rope practice, regular workshops, open rope jams, private shibari photography sessions and creative community work; its event/gallery and schedule traces include an intensive with Ludvig Rigger and Angie Ziv plus a scheduled July 17-19, 2026 Vik & Gala performance / workshop weekend. Its shop page also sells untreated 6 mm Ogawa jute rope directly at the studio. The Eastern European war / migration / diaspora context is based on a current project context: its organizers are Eastern European emigrants in Belgrade.

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/oblige-rope/

Fact — Oblige Rope, Kink, Art Boutique, 2020-, is documented as an American studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Detroit / Metro Detroit rope, kink and art studio established by Paul / Serene_X in 2020 and operated with Flynn Racer. Official pages describe Oblige as a 7,000 sqft second-floor shibari studio / dungeon / lounge with 25 suspension hard points, the recurring L.E.A.P. Education series, Open Rope / Sunday Rope Jam, Performance & Play, private memberships, venue amenities and city-level Detroit location only. The subMERGE podcast independently corroborates Paul and Flynn as co-owners who created the Metro Detroit BDSM / shibari studio together.

### Peony Studio

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/peony-studio/

Fact — Peony Studio, 2024–, is an Italian studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2024–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Turin kinbaku / shibari studio established by Kurogami and Shiawase and began operating in July 2024. Peony Studio frames itself as a venue for Japanese bondage technique, connection and aesthetics, with beginner via advanced classes, rope jams, bottom-focused meetings and international workshops. Its teaching program is inspired by Kinbaku LuXuria and the Naka-ryu / semenawa line via Riccardo Wildties and Red Sabbath. The studio reports a 100 m2 main workshop area, lounge / kitchen, 16 suspension points and classes in Italian, English and French; the name is dedicated to Naka Akira's symbolic peony tattoo.

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/place-des-cordes/

Fact — Place des Cordes, 2013–2018, is a French studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2013–2018

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Historical Paris shibari studio / community venue established by Cyril Grillon in 2013 after a monthly jam origin. Public profiles describe it as a dedicated rope practice, workshop, class and performance venue with a tatami-equipped loft in north Paris / rue du Docteur-Potain; the official About page says the physical studio closed in June 2018 and the company continued as Place des Cordes / SOPRACO around rope and accessory expansion-export.

### Qibari

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/qibari-berlin/

Fact — Qibari, 2019–, is documented as a German studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2019–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Berlin rope / bodywork studio and training project around René von Qibari. The first-party site frames Qibari as `Rope - Bodywork`, combining classical Shiatsu, floorwork-oriented shibari, consent and embodied partner practice. René's official bio says he began Rope-Shiatsu workshops in 2019, took over the Lübbener Straße 26 rooms in 2023 to preserve them as a place for bodywork and shibari, and has taught within the Rope-Shiatsu training since 2024. Current official pages expose active 2026 beginner and advanced workshops, a six-weekend Rope-Shiatsu training track, one-to-one sessions and a small Open Floor rope-and-bodywork jam in Berlin-Kreuzberg.

Fact — Queertopia, 2026–, is documented as a German studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2026–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Cologne queer community studio and coaching space with shibari, kink, bodywork and polyamory education. The official bodywork / shibari page supports 1:1 shibari lessons, small-group courses, regular course structure and private shibari bodywork. The public studio surface names Mona Lecia Schweegmann as owner, with the earlier Seilgruppe Koeln / Fushicho origin preserved as reference-supported predecessor context rather than a separate entry expansion.

### Reisei Studio

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/reisei-studio/

Fact — Reisei Studio, 2020s–, is a Spanish studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Valencia shibari studio / practice space visible via public local rope-jam and workshop listings. Entrapolis and local event pages place introductory shibari workshops and rope jams at Reisei Studio in Valencia. Public January-June 2026 calendar posts show a recurring programme of rope jams, study groups, foundation / suspension classes, Akai Ito rope practice, acroyoga, performances and focused workshops such as reverse prayer and summer initiation intensives. Public Telegram reposts, the SVF Instagram profile and the current Reisei Studio Linktree identify it as the organizer behind Shibari Valencia Fest, with the 2026 festival and the announced May 21-23, 2027 continuation both routed via the same public studio / festival channel family.

### Rope Jam Studio

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/rope-jam-studio-london/

Fact — Rope Jam Studio, 2024-, is a British studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2024-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — North London shibari / kinbaku studio and community space. The first-party site characterizes Rope Jam Studio as a sanctuary for practitioners at every level, with daily, weekly, bi-weekly and monthly workshops and courses, private lessons, private sessions and holistic one-to-one rope experiences. Public pages place the current expanded studio at Unit 2, 7 Fountayne Road in Tottenham / Seven Sisters and describe the 2025 move as a new chapter after returning to the building where the Rope Jam Studio journey began. Ticket Tailor shows a live 2026 programme of beginner rope jams, Thursday open-studio rope salons, study labs, themed rope-play jams, intensive courses and shibari play-party programming.

### RopeLounge Amsterdam

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/ropelounge-amsterdam/

Fact — RopeLounge Amsterdam, 2013-, is a Dutch studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2013-

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — Amsterdam-centered Japanese rope / shibari / kinbaku event organizer, workshop and class platform established in May 2013. Asiana is the founder and principal teacher; the current record treats Door is only occasionally her co-teacher / model and that Asiana works with a range of models as rope partners. Prague Shibari Festival noted Asiana had been running RopeLounge for almost six years by 2019, the official RopeLounge link hub points to the Asiana / Door podcast, and 2026 Hipsy listings still show RopeLounge running Japanese rope-bondage courses in Amsterdam. The current record also includes Amsterdam-hosting context for Otonawa & Tsubasa in 2013, Ren Yagami in 2016 and Kasumi Hourai in 2019.

### Ropevision

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/ropevision/

Fact — Ropevision, 2020s-, is a Finnish studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Oulu-centered shibari studio and artistic workgroup established by Solina and Theokino. Ropevision's official site says the project was created to increase the visibility of shibari inside the broader cultural field and to offer services and education around rope. The same public pages say the duo began via rope performances, later expanded into photography and other rope-art forms, and currently teach shibari, organize workshops and their own events, create rope art, and run a studio space began operating in spring 2026 in Hiukkavaara.

Fact — Sakura Rope, 2021-, is documented as a German studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2021-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Transmission — Stuttgart / Ludwigsburg-area shibari teaching project by Franky and Mel, established at the end of 2021. Sakura Rope says its style has been strongly influenced by Ren Yagami, Sarca Martello and Naka Akira, and that it became an official instructor of the Iyasu Ropu method after Sarca Martello in 09/2023. BoundCon 2026 records Franky / Mr. Sakura and Mel / Mrs. Sakura as Sakura Rope in a shared outdoor-stage performance with Phieli in Ropes.

### Sala de Luz

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/sala-de-luz/

Fact — Sala de Luz, 2022-, is a Spanish studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2022-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Barcelona / Poblenou arts, body-movement and sexuality space opened by Glü Wür / Paola in 2022. Sala de Luz's official team page characterizes Gluwur as a Chilean fine-arts / gemmology-trained artist centered in Barcelona, active in aerial circus from 2009 and the Barcelona shibari community from 2013, teaching self-tying and shibari from 2016, organizing Hitchin Bitches BCN, and opening Sala de Luz to generate synergies between art and sexuality. Current official home, location and Linktree surfaces frame Sala de Luz as an `Espacio de Co-Working / Cuerpo & Movimiento` in Poblenou between Marina and Bogatell, with workshops, talks, play parties and current rope-facing activity listings; the official venue pages also describe suspension points, tatami / padded-floor options and wheelchair-accessible lift / bathroom infrastructure.

### Salon Kitagawa / salonK2

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/salon-kitagawa-k2/

Fact — Salon Kitagawa / salonK2, 2003–, is documented as a Japanese studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2003–

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — Japanese rope salon / lesson context organized by Kitagawa / 喜多川, later salonK2 with kei. Nawapedia characterizes Salon Kitagawa as a Tokyo gathering of asanawa lovers from 2003 to 2009 that later continued behind closed doors and influenced semenawa and the styles of Kasumi Hourai and Otonawa. The official salonK2 page gives the 2003 Salon Kitagawa start, 2005 Kitagawa / kei partnership and 2009 salonK2 start, and frames current Yokohama gatherings and lessons around Kitagawa's semenawa / asanawa approach.

Fact — Kyoto private kinbaku salon run by Yamamoto and Momo. The official salonY site characterizes it as a private salon for people with shared interests, says it began in 2021 after Yamamoto had attended salonK2 monthly since 2016, and reports that the project was created to spread salonK2-style semenawa to Kansai. ### schwelle7

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/schwelle7/

Fact — Schwelle7, 2007-2016, is documented as a German studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2007-2016

Fact — Era: 2000s

Fact — Berlin/Wedding experimental performance, BDSM, body-practice and shibari venue opened by Felix Ruckert with Dasniya Sommer at Uferstrasse 6 at the end of March 2007. Surviving official programmes document Dasniya's weekly Bondage Jam and beginner shibari course, Midori's Hands-on Rope Bondage workshop in November 2007 and Matthias T. J. Grimme's three-day Japanese Bondage workshop in March 2008. Ruckert's biography frames schwelle7 as an international shibari hub whose programming helped popularize Japanese rope bondage in Berlin and spanning Europe. The venue closed in 2016 after nine years and remains a key context for the Berlin rope research scene that produced EURIX.

- [Felix Ruckert biography](https://felixruckert.de/news/biography/)

### Seilhafen

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/seilhafen/

Fact — Seilhafen, 2020s-, is documented as a German studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Berlin contemporary-rope education project and small teaching team. The first-party site frames Seilhafen as creating high-quality education and friendly learning environments for contemporary rope based on shibari / kinbaku, body awareness, intimacy and human connection. Its current beginner-resource page names Arto and Jessa as the public team behind the project, while the contact page exposes current Telegram, Instagram, FetLife and JOYclub surfaces and reports that event addresses are shared per event rather than via a fixed permanent venue. Seilhafen's `Other rope venues in Berlin` resource page also explicitly positions the project inside the wider Berlin rope ecosystem rather than as an isolated private shell.

### She.She.Shibari Studio

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/she-she-shibari-studio/

Fact — She.She.Shibari Studio, 2021–, is a Ukrainian studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2021–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Lviv shibari studio and school established by Marta Skinnysticks. Gal-Info reported that She.She.Shibari opened on December 27, 2021 on the territory of the Rema factory, with Marta as the artist, rigger and project author. The studio teaches via theory, demonstration and practice, with emphasis on voluntary participation and safety; later Ukrainian event listings and the studio's Telegram channel show continuing workshops, rope evenings, private sessions and related sex-positive / body-practice events.

### Shibari ARTlanta

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/shibari-artlanta/

Fact — Shibari ARTlanta, 2024–, is documented as an American studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2024–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Decatur / Atlanta shibari and kinbaku studio at ARTlanta Studios. The first-party site frames Shibari ARTlanta as a safe, inclusive, neutral learning space for Atlanta's shibari community, with beginner-to-advanced classes, rope labs, member nights, workshops, private lessons, studio rental, yoga, sound healing, life drawing and photography-oriented events. The current site characterizes a 700-square-foot Decatur studio designed for rope practice with multiple load-tested suspension points. Voudou Ropes' 2026 retreat page says Knot Posh and Kat VanWylder established Shibari ARTlanta in March 2024, and ShibariNews records it as Georgia's most prominent dedicated rope studio.

Fact — Active: 2024-

Fact — Graz shibari studio shell active in the mid-2020s. Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Fort Worth / north Texas rope and photography studio operating publicly as Shibari Studios. The official home page characterizes a 4,000 square foot, air-conditioned, two-story multifunctional studio for creative work and shibari, with more than 20 suspension points, six hashira, ground-rope space, learning equipment, performance stage, social room and photo / video studio equipment. The official About page frames it as an all-level shibari hub with workshops led by local leaders, advanced intensives, themed open sessions, community gatherings, private lessons and rentals.

Fact — Active: 2023–

Fact — Marrickville / Sydney shibari studio and community rope space. The first-party site characterizes Shibari Sydney as a welcoming, volunteer-run studio with certified suspension points, mats, space hire, social practice, memberships, performance nights and classes from local and overseas teachers. Its current classes page records a weekly Tuesday / Wednesday teaching structure, Thursday rope practice, monthly intensives, private lessons and recurring guest-teacher / performance, play-party, rope-social and focus-workshop formats. Humanitix and the contact page corroborate the Marrickville location and Shibari Sydney-hosted beginner programming.

Fact — India shibari studio / teaching project led by Amiya Bhanushali. Fact — Stonewall MN, 2020s–, is documented as an American studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Northeast Minneapolis queer and trans-led kink and rope studio run as a member collective. Its public Humanitix host page characterizes StonewallMN as a democratically run studio for kink education and safety, with a Thursday Open Ropes / Shibari community hangout, a bamboo rig and additional hardpoints. The Shibari Study profile adds Thursday and Sunday jams, monthly parties, visiting-instructor workshops, loaner ropes and an LGBTQIA+-centered member-run studio frame.

### Studio Allegory

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/studio-allegory/

Fact — Studio Allegory, 2010s–, is a Canadian studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Transmission — Vancouver rope / intimacy studio formerly known as TheSpace2 and now operating as Studio Allegory with the same core people and influence lineage. TheSpace2 was documented as Vancouver's first dedicated rope studio, established by Barkas and Addie; current Studio Allegory is run by Barkas with partners Addie and Emmy and continues the Vancouver rope / kinbaku education context with classes and events involving Georg Barkas and Addie Tahl.

### Studio Friction

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/studio-friction/

Fact — Studio Friction, 2018–, is documented as an American studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2018–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Denver rope and aerial art studio and 501(c)(7) nonprofit social club. Current official pages frame Studio Friction as Denver's home for rope and aerial art, with rope-bondage curriculum tracks, beginner primers, open rope labs, performances, member parties, curriculum grants and a community-elected board / volunteer-committee operating model. The public surface also confirms the 740 Lipan Street Denver location, office and incident-reporting contacts, and a community-first educational frame for rope, kinbaku and aerial-art practice in Colorado.

### Studio K Shanghai

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/studio-k-shanghai/

Fact — Studio K Shanghai, 2010s, is a Chinese studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Shanghai shibari studio / TIE UP! Scene location began operating in 2018 by Davide LaoDai and Ayame after TIE UP! Began in 2017. Shibari Study's official 2024 Laodai podcast bio characterizes Studio K as the first shibari studio in Shanghai and credits Laodai with creating Mainland China's first public rope party. Studio Yukari's Laodai / Ayame event bio says Studio K hosted Japanese and international teachers such as Murasaki Haruan, Naoko / Subay, Avalon, Hajime Kinoko, Zima and Ren Yagami. Current @studiokshibari profile wording spans Shanghai and Bangkok, but the reviewed public references do not yet establish a separate Bangkok successor studio.

### Studio Kink Sydney

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/studio-kink-sydney/

Fact — Studio Kink Sydney, 2015–, is an Australian studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2015–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Sydney kink and shibari education studio formed in 2015 as an educational cooperative by Mark De Viate, Aleni De Viate, Lani De Viate, Nawarei and Precipice. The first-party site characterizes Studio Kink as a kink / queer education space with a strong focus on Japanese-style rope bondage, regular shibari classes, a structured six-level syllabus, online shibari courses and national / international guest presenters. The location page places the venue in Caroline Lane, St Peters.

### Studio Kokoro UK

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/studio-kokoro-uk/

Fact — Studio Kokoro UK, 2019-2021, is a British studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2019-2021

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Mansfield, UK rope studio / education project opened by WykD Dave and Clover. Nawapedia records the physical studio as opening in 2019 and closing in 2021, while the official Studio Kokoro site remains as a tutorial and resource archive with rope-care / knot / hitch videos, Clover's Rope Bottom Guide, Natasha NawaTaNeko's Somatics for Rope Bottoms and the sold-out TEN anniversary book. Rope Podcast EP58 and EP63 frame Studio Kokoro as the Mansfield rope space WykD Dave and Clover began operating in 2019, with EP58 giving visitor / community-space context and EP63 tying it to their long-term rope partnership.

### Studio Ma

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/studio-ma-london/

Fact — Studio Ma, 2025-, is a British studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2025-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — London shibari studio and Ma Collective teaching space active from November 2025 in the former Studio Yukari physical location. Official Studio Ma pages frame the project as the continuation of that London rope space after Studio Yukari ceased operation, carrying forward shibari / kinbaku classes and events while expanding into complementary practices. Public pages show a structured beginner-to-suspension teaching programme, named jams and a live 2026 event calendar. The core-team map connection follows public Studio Ma team roles for M / Gordian_ties, Marsh, Anna Noctuelle and Rocco Grigio. Naoko and Mistress Chiaki context stays with bounded study / influence connections on the relevant people; the Studio Ma transition wording does not establish lineage, deshi, rank or studio-successor connection.

Fact — Active: 2021–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Tokyo private shibari studio created by Louis Kordexe in 2021 near Sasazuka / Shinjuku. Official pages frame Studio Y7 as a dedicated rope-arts space for private beginner and intermediate lessons in English / French / Japanese, photoshoots with assistant support, rope shows, model collaborations and related bookings. ### Studio Yukari

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/studio-yukari/

Fact — Studio Yukari, 2022–2025, is a British studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2022–2025

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — London shibari studio established by Naoko / formerly Subay in 2022 and active as a physical Studio Yukari space via October 2025. Studio Yukari / Nawa Jyou Wa sources present Naoko as a Hong Kong-born London-centered shibari artist, educator and Nawa Naka Kai member recognized as Naka Akira's third deshi in 2023; the studio offered private lessons, group classes, performances and filming / art collaboration. Its 2025 Nawa-Ato anniversary program hosted Akane and Nuitdetokyo for shows, sharing and a Yukimura-style model-focused class. The official farewell listing and Studio Ma site state that Studio Yukari ceased operation from November 1, 2025, with the physical space continued by Ma Collective as Studio Ma.

#### Reference links

### Suzaku Studio CR

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/suzaku-studio-cr/

Fact — Suzaku Studio CR, 2020s–, is a studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Costa Rica rope / shibari studio shell active in the mid-2020s. Public Instagram profile confirms a live `Suzaku Studio CR` / `@shibaristudiocostarica` identity with stable follower / post continuity, while public 2025-2026 Instagram snippets preserve recurring-programming traces such as a beginner `101 Shibari / Kinbaku Workshop`, a nine-class progressive course, and a `Rope Jam de Inicio de Ano`. The public trail is still social-first and does not yet expose a release-safe city, venue or organizer page, so the entry is listed conservatively as a Costa Rica country-location studio with only one bounded facilitator-shell connection from `maleku-kinbaku`.

### Temple New York

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/temple-new-york/

Fact — Temple New York, 2019–, is documented as an American studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2019–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Bushwick / Brooklyn BDSM and rope-bondage play space, education studio and rental venue established by William / TieMeTightly in 2019. Temple's official About page frames it as a place for people interested in or active in BDSM and rope bondage to assemble, practice, learn, host special-interest gatherings and work within consent-centered community agreements, and says the space was specifically built to accommodate suspension work. Public profile surfaces describe Temple as a consent-driven private event space focused on connection, education, performance, immersive events, workshops, suspension, alternative lifestyle, movement and visual arts. William's official profile names Temple as his organizer / educator base since its 2019 founding within the New York rope scene.

### Tengu Shibari Munich

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/tengu-shibari-munich/

Fact — Tengu Shibari Munich, 2010s-, is documented as a German studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2010s-

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Munich shibari teaching, performance and studio project by Frank and Felia. Their own site frames Tengu Shibari Munich as traditional rope art, shows and instruction, with exclusive 1:1 coaching by Frank and Felia as a teaching unit. The official bio says their path led them to Vinciens / Kinbaku Studio Vienna, where they found their shibari home and follow the Osada-ryu Kyu system as students, with further workshops or lessons with Ranki Kazami, UnkownRigger, RopEmotion and Japanese masters such as Osada Steve and Ren Yagami. BoundCon 2026 records Tengu Shibari Munich for main-stage and outdoor-stage performances.

### Tension MTL

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/tension-mtl/

Fact — Tension MTL, 2020s-, is a Canadian studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Montreal shibari / kinbaku education studio and community space operating publicly as Tension MTL. The first-party site frames a structured shibari curriculum spanning Fundamentals, Floorplay, The L.A.B., Nawajutsu, suspension fundamentals, partials / suspension / play and advanced dynamic work, alongside privates, workshops and open-space programming. The room-rentals page records a physical studio with a main hall, multiple suspension points, private room, therapy room, dojo space and lounge areas. Rope Social and Soiree Shibari pages corroborate recurring community and performance formats, while the contact page gives a public Montreal address and safer-space / accountability framing.

### That Rope Place

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/that-rope-place/

Fact — That Rope Place, 2019–, is a studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2019–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Singapore shibari studio established by Rosa and Python in 2019, formerly operating publicly as 0101 Studio Singapore / Studio 1010. The current official site frames That Rope Place as Singapore's first queer-focused, female-owned shibari studio at Pearl's Hill Terrace, with classes, rope jams, private bookings, photoshoots and guest-teaching / performance infrastructure in a consent-centered, community-driven frame. Earlier Rope Podcast and Singapore media traces under the 0101 / Studio 1010 names support the same Singapore rope-jam / practice-space continuity and the founders' four-year Anatomie Studio study context; the current record treats That Rope Place as the rebrand / continuation of that project. The official 2026 Meet the Team page says the studio began in Rosa and Python's living room, grew into a permanent central Singapore space, and is now run as a collective with Rosa as owner, Python / Pyth01 as Head of Rope Education, Jules as co-owner, and house-cat / operations roles for Summer, Bear, Rabbit and Banana.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Bio.site and the Humanitix host profile frame The Bindery as a Boorloo / Perth shibari community rope space for jams, beginner classes, workshops and performances. Humanitix listings also show The Bindery hosting a November 2025 Akechi Kanna / Akechi Kagura weekend with assistance from Nawashi Supaku.

### The Studio NW

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/the-studio-nw/

Fact — The Studio NW, 2025–, is a British studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2025–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Private, purpose-built rope and event space in Bacup, northwest England, owned and facilitated by Mystrix. The Studio NW runs regular classes and events for newcomers via experienced practitioners, supports natural- and synthetic-rope practice spanning varied bodies, and programming includes private hire. Its teaching approach emphasizes explaining how and why structures work so ties can be adapted to the person in rope.

Fact — Active: 2026–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Leuven home studio for rope education, community jams and private lessons. Maison Tsuri Neko runs small, approximately fortnightly jams with beginner support, tailored lessons for individuals, pairs and small groups, and separate floor-work, self-tying and suspension areas. Its public programme emphasizes consent, communication, safety, accessible learning and a queer-owned, disability-owned, nonprofit community environment; DoodleMe / Pez is publicly named as a co-host and teacher.

Fact — VoxBody Studio, 2017–, is documented as an American studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2017–

Fact — Bay Area rope-dedicated studio in Oakland, California, began operating in spring 2017. Programming includes monthly rope classes at all levels, rope jams, labtime, performance events, and an in-house curriculum taking students from absolute beginner to suspension over one year. VoxBody event listings also place OtaKinbaku's Oakland Rope Salon in the studio's current class / event ecosystem.

### Wisimi Studio

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/wisimi-studio/

Fact — Wisimi Studio, 2020s–, is a Polish studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Warsaw shibari / rope studio and community space visible via 2025 public event listings. A Ryhor Shabeka workshop listing places his semi-suspension class at Wisimi Studio in Warsaw and notes weekly practical rope classes led by Arkadiusz Hezler plus Tuesday rope jams. A separate Play With Ropes listing characterizes an open weekly Wisimi rope jam with floor and suspension practice, mats, six bamboo suspension points, one ring and shared ropes.

### Yanari Studio

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/yanari-studio/

Fact — Yanari Studio, 2025–, is a Polish studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2025–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Warsaw shibari / rope studio at Wybrzeze Kosciuszkowskie 21A. Public event listings and Rafko's current atlas context place Yanari's start on October 4, 2025 and describe the space as hosting classes, rope jams and invited workshops. The same context names Rafko, Dancingwithropes and Feeling_Blue as founders, with Rafko and Dancingwithropes running the studio now, and confirms public Instagram, Facebook, Telegram and FetLife contact surfaces. A StayHappening opening-party listing for October 4-5, 2025 characterizes Yanari as a two-room space with a studio area dedicated to tying and an upstairs social area. Krajownik and Joitin course pages place Shibari for Beginners / Shibari Basics classes there and describe Ada and Tomek from Yanari Studio as course teachers who co-organize jams and workshops. AllEvents and SimplyGo listings separately support recurring rope-jam context at the same address, with SimplyGo naming Tomek / DancingWithRopes as the beginner-facing jam contact. Public Instagram screenshots further document 2026 guest programming with Tati Limati and Vik & Gala.

### Yoridokoro (よりどころ)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/yoridokoro-ikebukuro/

Fact — Yoridokoro (よりどころ), 2023-, is documented as a Japanese studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2023-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Ikebukuro / Tokyo members' kinbaku salon began operating in 2023. The first-party site frames Yoridokoro as a place for people to practice, experience, view and exchange around Japanese rope regardless of top/bottom role, school or nationality, with capacity limits, three bamboo suspension beams, thirteen anchors, two-pair suspension capacity and mat-covered rope floor. The site records beginner-friendly recurring formats such as Haya Nawa Kai, Aoharu Nawa Kai, Yossy Salon and tea-rope gatherings, plus Yossy & Airi outside appearances. The public Bluesky profile names Yossy Balpoa and Airi as representatives and exposes current contact routing.

### Yugen Studio

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/studios/yugen-studio/

Fact — Yugen Studio, 2020–, is an Italian studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Kinbaku studio in Milan, Italy, run by Federico Kirigami and Marta Tenshiko via Kiri Nawa Kai APS. Yugen Studio's official site says the studio began operating in 2020 after Federico and Marta had worked together since 2011 and run a smaller Milan venue from 2016 to 2020. The current space has a social area, 40-tatami practice area, 16 double hard points and 4 hashira, and hosts classes, shootings, bondage parties and cultural / editorial projects. A 2023 Bologna YSM presentation says Federico and Marta built Yugen Studio with valuable help from Ema Kuroko. Its Rope Tales / School of Rope roots connect it to Kirigami and Tenshiko's independent Milan teaching line, shaped by Naka Akira / Sugiura Norio references and Kirigami's earlier Kinbaku LuXuria certification period, while remaining an independent studio rather than a formal Japanese branch. TicketTailor / Rope Tales pages document a 2023-2026 collaboration line with Japanese kinbaku model Akane via live streaming, photoshoots and a 2026 photography study group.

Fact — Zwielicht Studio, 2020–, is documented as a German studio in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020–

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Wuppertal rope studio / school / community space. The first-party site characterizes Zwielicht as an international rope studio with an atmospheric studio loft, private shibari training, recurring community gatherings, workshops, photography-space context and After-Work-Fesseln meetings. The current About and Impressum pages tighten the team chronology: founder Lennart Schaeferling established the dedicated shibari school in 2020, and Nina Wiebusch joined as coach / model and co-founder in 2022 via the legal `Wiebusch & Schaeferling GbR` operator surface.

Fact — Alternative nightclub and kink-friendly event venue in Sofia, Bulgaria. The first-party site frames Club Asylum as a safe-space venue for different community nights and names KinkCorp as one of its resident event brands. Its 2026 Rope Jam + Bar Night listing is a shibari-focused social with rope basics, open tying and safety rules at the start of the event. Resident Advisor separately records a 2025 KinkCorp party at Asylum with a live shibari performance by Ludvig, and Intimacy Bulgaria characterizes International Munch / Open Doors Sofia meetings at Club Asylum.

### Birdhaus

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/venues/birdhaus/

Fact — Birdhaus, 2020s-, is a Canadian venue in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2020s-

Fact — Era: 2020s

Fact — Toronto alternative-arts venue, event space and rope-education studio co-owned by Tifereth and Miranda Tempest. Birdhaus began operating in 2021 and now operates from a historic Queen Street West space with shibari classes, jams, intensives, performances, play parties and a live-event programme. Its Shibari Study profile frames the rope programme around varied teaching methodologies, component-centered progression and learning toward play, while The first-party site characterizes Birdhaus as a dedicated alt-community events venue.

Fact — Tokyo / Okubo multipurpose rental, photo and event studio began operating in 2006. The official current site characterizes a 24-hour rental / photo / film / event space with a 100sqm floor, 4m height, ceiling chain blocks and cage / lounge / bar setups. It is lineage view-relevant here because independent rope / kink sources tie the venue to Yaminawakai: Clubberia names MIRA KURUMI as `Yaminawakai / The Owner of MK Studio` in a 2018 Midnight★Mess rope-performance listing, iFLYER preserves the same MIRA KURUMI / Yaminawakai / MK Studio rope-show shell, and MyShibari reports that Miura Kurumi runs Yaminawakai at MK Studio.

### Shibari Lounge

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/venues/shibari-lounge/

Fact — Shibari Lounge, 2016–, is a Belgian venue in the bakushi / kinbaku ecosystem.

Fact — Active: 2016–

Fact — Era: 2010s

Fact — Antwerp, Belgium shibari venue and host of Onawa Asobi Europe. Established by Marc BeShibari and Shibarista Jess after they discovered the former rehearsal studio in 2016 and began hosting private Shibari Sundays there. Official venue pages place it at Sergeyselsstraat 42 in Borgerhout / Antwerp, about 10 to 15 minutes on foot from Antwerp Central Station. Shibari Lounge characterizes itself as the beating heart of the Belgian rope scene: 350 m2 total, 150 m2 heated workfloor, at least 12 suspension points, bamboos, rings, a hashira, a bar / lounge area, regular classes, rope parties, workshops, festivals and international presenters. Its 2023-2026 programme includes Yagami Convention performance nights, Kazami Ranki and Tati Limati event pages, and a Kasumi Hourai / Akane weekend framed as their first collaboration outside Japan.

## Dated event occurrences

### BoundCon Europe 2004 (BoundCon I)

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/events/2004/boundcon-munich-i-2004/

Fact — 11–13 Jun · Munich, Germany 🇩🇪 · Kleine Olympiahalle, Olympiapark · Past

Fact — BoundCon Europe 2004 (BoundCon I) runs 11–13 Jun in Munich, Germany.

Fact — Founding-decade slice of BoundCon Munich, the international bondage and fetish convention first held at Kleine Olympiahalle in 2004. Official archived pages document BoundCon I-V via 2008, such as BoundCon III — Revenge of the Ropes, BoundCon IV — Back to the Future at Tonhalle / Kultfabrik and BoundCon V — Destination Bizarre at Zenith. Early rope programmes connected German and international show, workshop and exhibitor circuits via figures and projects such as Matthias T. J. Grimme / Bondage Project, Lew Rubens / BoundnDetermined and RopeMarks / Bob Roos. The first documented public convention was held in 2004; later 2003 origin wording likely refers to the project's foundation or planning phase.

### ShibariCon 2004

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/events/2004/shibaricon-2004/

Fact — 28–31 May · Rosemont, IL, United reports 🇺🇸 · Holiday Inn O'Hare International Hotel · Past

Fact — ShibariCon 2004 runs 28–31 May in Rosemont, IL, United reports.

Fact — Historic Chicagoland rope-bondage conference focused on education and information exchange for lovers of Japanese rope bondage. The inaugural May 2004 edition was hosted in Rosemont, followed by documented Naperville and Lincolnwood editions in 2005 and 2006. ShibariCon became one of the key North American meeting points for international rope teachers and practitioners; the early official programmes connect Midori, Dov / Washi Nawashi, Bridgett / Lee Harrington, Lochai, Claire Adams, Graydancer, Mark of DV8House and other established educators to its formative years.

### Austin Ropecraft Symposium 2005

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/events/2005/austin-ropecraft-symposium-2005/

Fact — 3–4 Dec · Austin, TX, United reports 🇺🇸 · Past

Fact — Austin Ropecraft Symposium 2005 runs 3–4 Dec in Austin, TX, United reports.

Fact — Historical rope-education symposium held in Austin in 2005 and 2006. The inaugural December 2005 edition described itself as unaffiliated and not-for-profit, sold out and brought together an international teaching roster; Austin Ropecraft Symposium 2 followed on September 1-3, 2006. Public programmes connect the symposium to Bridgett / Lee Harrington, Lochai, Mark of DV8House, Lew Rubens and Graydancer. This two-edition symposium is distinct from Graydancer's later ROPECRAFT convention brand launched in 2016.

Fact — Austin Ropecraft Symposium 2 runs 1–3 Sep in Austin, TX, United reports.

Fact — Historical rope-education symposium held in Austin in 2005 and 2006. The inaugural December 2005 edition described itself as unaffiliated and not-for-profit, sold out and brought together an international teaching roster; Austin Ropecraft Symposium 2 followed on September 1-3, 2006. Public programmes connect the symposium to Bridgett / Lee Harrington, Lochai, Mark of DV8House, Lew Rubens and Graydancer. This two-edition symposium is distinct from Graydancer's later ROPECRAFT convention brand launched in 2016.

### BondageFest 2007

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/events/2007/bondagefest-moscow-2007/

Fact — 3 Mar · Moscow, Russia 🇷🇺 · Club-restaurant Europe and Asia · Past

Fact — BondageFest 2007 runs 3 Mar in Moscow, Russia.

Fact — Early Moscow / Russian-language bondage arts festival held in 2007 and 2008. The first edition ran on March 3, 2007 at the club-restaurant Europe and Asia, with two podiums, demonstrations and a photo exhibition; its official programme records Mihail_A from Minsk for shibari. The 2008 archive frames the second open all-Russian festival as a public binding-art event with masters from Russia, Ukraine and Belarus, shibari / hojo-jutsu among the represented forms, master classes during breaks and a thematic photo exhibition. The event is a pre-RopeFest public trace for post-Soviet shibari / bondage exchange.

#### Line-up

#### Programme

##### Official two-podium programme

- First all-Russian festival of the art of binding; two podiums and a photo exhibition
- Mihail_A from Minsk — Shibari
  - Linked evidence or record: [Mihail_A](https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/mihail-balabanchik/)

### London Festival of the Art of Japanese Bondage 2009

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/events/2009/lfra-2009/

Fact — 25–28 Jun · London, United Kingdom 🇬🇧 · Resistance Gallery · Past

Fact — London Festival of the Art of Japanese Bondage runs 25–28 Jun in London, United Kingdom.

Transmission — Historical London rope-art festival lineage spanning the London Festival of the Art of Japanese Bondage and later London Festival of Rope Art. Early Resistance Gallery editions in 2009 / 2010 helped introduce international kinbaku performance and teaching to the UK scene, with Esinem and BeShibari among early participants and Hajime Kinoko appearing in the 2010 edition. The 2015 LFRA edition at Resistance Gallery framed itself as a celebration of shibari, performance art, cultural exchange and international cooperative learning, with performers, teachers, exhibitions, workshops and masterclasses from many countries.

#### Line-up

#### Programme

##### Selected artist / programme traces

- [Esinem](https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/esinem/)
- [BeShibari](https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/beshibari/)
- [Nawashi Murakawa](https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/nawashi-murakawa/)
- [Mark Varley](https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/mark-varley/)

### Onawa Asobi 2009

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/events/2009/onawa-asobi-tokyo-2009-june/

Fact — 19 Jun · Tokyo, Japan 🇯🇵 · Good-Studio · Past

Fact — Onawa Asobi runs 19 Jun in Tokyo, Japan.

Fact — Tokyo-centered annual female bakushi festival / high-skilled amateur and professional kinbaku stage established and produced by Yoi Yoshida since 2009. Kinbaku Today's Yoi interview says the first June 2009 Onawa Asobi grew from the Good-Studio orbit and featured Hajime Kinoko, Kasumi Hourai, Shigonawa Bingo, Ero Ouji and others; later interview traces place Ranki Kazami at Onawa Asobi 2010, while Shibari Lounge sources document BeShibari and Shibarista Jess performing at Tokyo Onawa Asobi in 2019. The European edition (Onawa Asobi Europe) was launched from this Tokyo format as a partnership with Shibari Lounge.

#### Line-up

#### Programme

##### Performer roster

- [Hajime Kinoko](https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/hajime-kinoko/)
- [Kasumi Hourai](https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/kasumi-hourai/)
- [Shigonawa Bingo](https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/shigonawa-bingo/)

Kinbaku Today's Yoi interview says the first June 2009 Onawa Asobi grew from the Good-Studio orbit and featured Hajime Kinoko, Kasumi Hourai, Shigonawa Bingo, Ero Ouji and others; later interview traces place Ranki Kazami at Onawa Asobi 2010, while Shibari Lounge sources document BeShibari and Shibarista Jess performing at Tokyo Onawa Asobi in 2019. The European edition (Onawa Asobi Europe) was launched from this Tokyo format as a partnership with Shibari Lounge.

### ShibariCon 2010

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/events/2010/shibaricon-2010/

Fact — 28–31 May · Chicago, IL, United reports 🇺🇸 · Past

Fact — ShibariCon 2010 runs 28–31 May in Chicago, IL, United reports.

Fact — Historic Chicagoland rope-bondage conference focused on education and information exchange for lovers of Japanese rope bondage. The inaugural May 2004 edition was hosted in Rosemont, followed by documented Naperville and Lincolnwood editions in 2005 and 2006. ShibariCon became one of the key North American meeting points for international rope teachers and practitioners; the early official programmes connect Midori, Dov / Washi Nawashi, Bridgett / Lee Harrington, Lochai, Claire Adams, Graydancer, Mark of DV8House and other established educators to its formative years.

#### Line-up

#### Programme

##### Archive card and class-folder trace

- 2010 Shibaricon bondage event in Chicago, Illinois
  - Linked evidence or record: [Shibaricon](https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/shibaricon/)
- Lord Morpheous class handouts: Microbondage 201; Aesthetics of Rope Bondage

### The 2011 London Festival of the Art of Japanese Bondage

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/events/2011/lfra-2011/

Fact — 23–26 Sep · London, United Kingdom 🇬🇧 · Resistance Gallery · Past

Fact — The 2011 London Festival of the Art of Japanese Bondage runs 23–26 Sep in London, United Kingdom.

Transmission — Historical London rope-art festival lineage spanning the London Festival of the Art of Japanese Bondage and later London Festival of Rope Art. Early Resistance Gallery editions in 2009 / 2010 helped introduce international kinbaku performance and teaching to the UK scene, with Esinem and BeShibari among early participants and Hajime Kinoko appearing in the 2010 edition. The 2015 LFRA edition at Resistance Gallery framed itself as a celebration of shibari, performance art, cultural exchange and international cooperative learning, with performers, teachers, exhibitions, workshops and masterclasses from many countries.

### Seattle Erotic Art Festival 2011

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/events/2011/seattle-erotic-art-festival-2011/

Fact — 20–22 May · Seattle, United reports 🇺🇸 · Fremont Studios · Past

Fact — Seattle Erotic Art Festival 2011 runs 20–22 May in Seattle, United reports.

Fact — Long-running Seattle erotic-art and performance festival with a durable rope-relevant scene connection. The official history page says the first Seattle Erotic Art Festival was produced in 2003 by the Pan Eros Foundation, grew into a multi-format festival spanning visual art, performance, literature and film, and introduced an Aerial Stage for aerialists and rope performances in 2018. The official photo archive preserves dated 2011-2021 festival venue traces, while the current home page advertises the 2026 edition at Seattle Center Exhibition Hall.

### Toubaku 2011

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/events/2011/toubaku-tokyo-2011/

Fact — 29–30 Jan · Tokyo, Japan 🇯🇵 · Past

Fact — Toubaku 2011 runs 29–30 Jan in Tokyo, Japan.

Fact — Tokyo international shibari festival organized by Hajime Kinoko and Ichinawakai. Expanded from the 2010 Winter Bondage event into an international festival in 2011, with performers such as Naka Akira and visiting Western practitioners such as Esinem. Nawapedia's Hebari chronology preserves a September 28, 2012 Petit in Toubaku performance trace at KunKun, documented as a bounded later occurrence rather than a full 2012 festival roster.

Fact — 4 May · London, United Kingdom 🇬🇧 · The Flying Dutchman · Past

Fact — BOUND London at The Flying Dutchman runs 4 May in London, United Kingdom.

Fact — London shibari night created to support Japanese rope bondage enthusiasts, public awareness, skill exchange, new performers and rope shows. Venue and ticket archives trace BOUND at The Flying Dutchman from 2012 via the mid-2010s; a 2013 WeGotTickets listing credits Esinem, Nina Russ, Gorgone and MoSaru as creators in collaboration with Flying Dutchman London, and ShibariClasses characterizes BOUND as London's pioneering shibari night established in 2012.

### RopeFest Saint Petersburg 2012

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/events/2012/ropefest-peterburg-2012/

Fact — 14–15 Jul · Saint Petersburg, Russia 🇷🇺 · Past

Fact — RopeFest Saint Petersburg 2012 runs 14–15 Jul in Saint Petersburg, Russia.

Fact — 2010s slice of Russia's first and largest shibari festival, first held in Saint Petersburg in 2012 and developed via the ArtCo / RopeSchool ecosystem before the pandemic-era break. ShibariNews characterizes Rope Fest as the first Russian Shibari Festival in St Petersburg, supported by Shibari Club Yugen, RopeSchool, RopeShop.Guru and KinkAfisha, with multi-day performances, seminars, peer rope, escape events and rope jams. The 2010s branch locations the early Saint Petersburg / Moscow / Baltia festival family and its international guest-master roster.

Fact — Athens Shibari Festival Volume 1 runs 11 Oct in Athens, Greece.

Art&Life preserves Athens Shibari Festival Volume 1 as an October 11, 2012 Faust event and names Rope Shadow / Rope Studies plus Alexis-D on the performer bill, while VICE later characterizes Ropegasms / Athens Shibari Festival as the 2012 Rope Studies event format.

### Bound In Boston XIII 2013

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/events/2013/bound-in-boston-xiii-2013/

Fact — 23–24 Mar · Boston, MA area, United reports 🇺🇸 · Past

Fact — Bound In Boston XIII runs 23–24 Mar in Boston, MA area, United reports.

Fact — New England / Boston-area rope-bondage convention and workshop series centered on Shibari / Kinbaku-style rope education. Official archived pages document Bound In Boston XIII for March 23-24, 2013, Sched records Bound In Boston XX for March 24-26, 2017 at Crowne Plaza Hotel, Warwick, RI, and later official pages describe the March 16-18, 2018 Warwick edition as a large-format con with hundreds of people, classes and parties. Matthew / RopeRider's 2018 farewell says the series grew from a January 2009 30-person Sunday education session into 21 cons over nine years at four hotels, with Tethered Together emerging from the same organizer / staff continuity after the final Bound In Boston.

#### Line-up

#### Programme

##### Official archive trace

  - Linked evidence or record: [Bound In Boston](https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/events/bound-in-boston/)

Kinbaku Today's Yoi interview says the first June 2009 Onawa Asobi grew from the Good-Studio orbit and featured Hajime Kinoko, Kasumi Hourai, Shigonawa Bingo, Ero Ouji and others; later interview traces place Ranki Kazami at Onawa Asobi 2010, while Shibari Lounge sources document BeShibari and Shibarista Jess performing at Tokyo Onawa Asobi in 2019. The European edition (Onawa Asobi Europe) was launched from this Tokyo format as a partnership with Shibari Lounge.

### Bound In Boston XV 2014

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/events/2014/bound-in-boston-xv-2014/

Fact — 22–23 Mar · Norwood, MA, United reports 🇺🇸 · F.P.S.N. · Past

Fact — Bound In Boston XV runs 22–23 Mar in Norwood, MA, United reports.

Fact — New England / Boston-area rope-bondage convention and workshop series centered on Shibari / Kinbaku-style rope education. Official archived pages document Bound In Boston XIII for March 23-24, 2013, Sched records Bound In Boston XX for March 24-26, 2017 at Crowne Plaza Hotel, Warwick, RI, and later official pages describe the March 16-18, 2018 Warwick edition as a large-format con with hundreds of people, classes and parties. Matthew / RopeRider's 2018 farewell says the series grew from a January 2009 30-person Sunday education session into 21 cons over nine years at four hotels, with Tethered Together emerging from the same organizer / staff continuity after the final Bound In Boston.

### ShibariCon 2014

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/events/2014/shibaricon-2014/

Fact — 22–28 May · Chicago, IL, United reports 🇺🇸 · Past

Fact — ShibariCon 2014 runs 22–28 May in Chicago, IL, United reports.

Fact — Historic Chicagoland rope-bondage conference focused on education and information exchange for lovers of Japanese rope bondage. The inaugural May 2004 edition was hosted in Rosemont, followed by documented Naperville and Lincolnwood editions in 2005 and 2006. ShibariCon became one of the key North American meeting points for international rope teachers and practitioners; the early official programmes connect Midori, Dov / Washi Nawashi, Bridgett / Lee Harrington, Lochai, Claire Adams, Graydancer, Mark of DV8House and other established educators to its formative years.

Fact — 16 Jan · London, United Kingdom 🇬🇧 · Past

Fact — BOUND London: Andrea Ropes and Gestalta runs 16 Jan in London, United Kingdom.

Fact — London shibari night created to support Japanese rope bondage enthusiasts, public awareness, skill exchange, new performers and rope shows. Venue and ticket archives trace BOUND at The Flying Dutchman from 2012 via the mid-2010s; a 2013 WeGotTickets listing credits Esinem, Nina Russ, Gorgone and MoSaru as creators in collaboration with Flying Dutchman London, and ShibariClasses characterizes BOUND as London's pioneering shibari night established in 2012.

### La Nuit des Cordes 2015

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/events/2015/la-nuit-des-cordes-2015/

Fact — 28 Feb · Montreal, Canada 🇨🇦 · Espace des Arts · Past

Fact — La Nuit des Cordes 2015 runs 28 Feb in Montreal, Canada.

Fact — Montreal annual rope-bondage performance event established by Ebi McKnotty and Dunter in 2014, inspired by Morpheous' Bondage Extravaganza and tied to Nuit Blanche / Montréal en Lumière. The official current site says NDC has been part of Nuit Blanche every year since, with the 2021 edition virtual and a 2024 return in person at the usual Espace des Arts. Public listings and archives place the 2015, 2016, 2017 and 2019 editions at Espace des Arts / 9 Sainte-Catherine Est; the 2021 page frames the event as a seven-hour digital edition with 100+ artists, simultaneous performances, circus, erotic bondage, acrobatic and decorative rope, and photography exhibition context.

### London Festival of Rope Art 2015

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/events/2015/lfra-2015/

Fact — 25–27 Sep · London, United Kingdom 🇬🇧 · Resistance Gallery · Past

Fact — London Festival of Rope Art runs 25–27 Sep in London, United Kingdom.

Transmission — Historical London rope-art festival lineage spanning the London Festival of the Art of Japanese Bondage and later London Festival of Rope Art. Early Resistance Gallery editions in 2009 / 2010 helped introduce international kinbaku performance and teaching to the UK scene, with Esinem and BeShibari among early participants and Hajime Kinoko appearing in the 2010 edition. The 2015 LFRA edition at Resistance Gallery framed itself as a celebration of shibari, performance art, cultural exchange and international cooperative learning, with performers, teachers, exhibitions, workshops and masterclasses from many countries.

### MoscowKnot 3 2015

Source URL: https://shibariatlas.org/events/2015/moscowknot-2015/

Fact — 17–19 Apr · Moscow, Russia 🇷🇺 · Mona Club · Past

Fact — MoscowKnot 3 runs 17–19 Apr in Moscow, Russia.

Transmission — Moscow shibari / bondage festival lineage associated with Vlada Vedmovskaya's Russian-scene organizing work. Public traces use the Moscow Knot name for bondage-festival performances by 2015 / 2016, such as Andrea Ropes and Xa Na, while BalkanBound's history frames MoscowKnot International as the 2018-present international event that connected practitioners and audiences and popularized shibari in Russia and abroad. RopeMarks' own archive records a 2018 Moscow Knot edition at Mona Club with RopeMarks shows, a RopeMarks Ryu workshop and Yagami Ren in the same program, plus an October 2018 follow-up RopeMarks Ryu weekend at Club Predel organized by Vlada and Falco. Ka.sure characterizes Vlada as one of the founders and producers of Moscow Knot for many years. This entry keeps the early Moscow Knot performance traces and the later International format together because they belong to the same 2010s Moscow event lineage rather than separate visual eras.

#### Line-up

#### Programme

##### Selected performance trace

- Andrea Ropes and Ishemia performance
  - Linked evidence or record: [Andrea Ropes](https://shibariatlas.org/lineage/people/andrea-ropes/)

Fact — Historic Chicagoland rope-bondage conference focused on education and information exchange for lovers of Japanese rope bondage. The inaugural May 2004 edition was hosted in Rosemont, followed by documented Naperville and Lincolnwood editions in 2005 and 2006. ShibariCon became one of the key North American meeting points for international rope teachers and practitioners; the early official programmes connect Midori, Dov / Washi Nawashi, Bridgett / Lee Harrington, Lochai, Claire Adams, Graydancer, Mark of DV8House and other established educators to its formative years.

Venue and ticket archives trace BOUND at The Flying Dutchman from 2012 via the mid-2010s; a 2013 WeGotTickets listing credits Esinem, Nina Russ, Gorgone and MoSaru as creators in collaboration with Flying Dutchman London, and ShibariClasses characterizes BOUND as London's pioneering shibari night established in 2012.
